sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततस्ते सहिता वीराः प्रयाता दक्षिणामुखाः ।उपास्तमयवेलायां शिबिराभ्याशमागताः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataste sahitā vīrāḥ prayātā dakṣiṇāmukhāḥ ,upāstamayavelāyāṁ śibirābhyāśamāgatāḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, those united warriors, having proceeded southwards, arrived near the camp at dusk.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then, those united warriors, having proceeded southwards, arrived near the camp at dusk.

sanskrit: विमुच्य वाहांस्त्वरिता भीताः समभवंस्तदा ।गहनं देशमासाद्य प्रच्छन्ना न्यविशन्त ते ॥२॥
transliteration: vimucya vāhāṁstvaritā bhītāḥ samabhavaṁstadā ,gahanaṁ deśamāsādya pracchannā nyaviśanta te.
english: Then, having abandoned their horses, they became panicked and hurried. Reaching a dense region, they hid themselves and settled there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Then, having abandoned their horses, they became panicked and hurried. Reaching a dense region, they hid themselves and settled there.

sanskrit: सेनानिवेशमभितो नातिदूरमवस्थिताः ।निकृत्ता निशितैः शस्त्रैः समन्तात्क्षतविक्षताः ॥३॥
transliteration: senāniveśamabhito nātidūramavasthitāḥ ,nikṛttā niśitaiḥ śastraiḥ samantātkṣatavikṣatāḥ.
english: They were situated not very far from the military encampment, having been deeply cut by sharp weapons and completely mangled all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. They were situated not very far from the military encampment, having been deeply cut by sharp weapons and completely mangled all around.

sanskrit: दीर्घमुष्णं च निःश्वस्य पाण्डवानन्वचिन्तयन् ।श्रुत्वा च निनदं घोरं पाण्डवानां जयैषिणाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca niḥśvasya pāṇḍavānanvacintayan ,śrutvā ca ninadaṁ ghoraṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ jayaiṣiṇām.
english: And sighing deeply and hotly, they reflected upon the Pandavas. After hearing the terrible roar of the Pandavas, who were eager for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. And sighing deeply and hotly, they reflected upon the Pandavas. After hearing the terrible roar of the Pandavas, who were eager for victory.

sanskrit: अनुसारभयाद्भीताः प्राङ्मुखाः प्राद्रवन्पुनः ।ते मुहूर्तं ततो गत्वा श्रान्तवाहाः पिपासिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: anusārabhayādbhītāḥ prāṅmukhāḥ prādravanpunaḥ ,te muhūrtaṁ tato gatvā śrāntavāhāḥ pipāsitāḥ.
english: Frightened by the fear of pursuit, they fled forward again, facing that direction. After going a short distance from there, they became thirsty, their horses exhausted.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Frightened by the fear of pursuit, they fled forward again, facing that direction. After going a short distance from there, they became thirsty, their horses exhausted.

sanskrit: नामृष्यन्त महेष्वासाः क्रोधामर्षवशं गताः ।राज्ञो वधेन संतप्ता मुहूर्तं समवस्थिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: nāmṛṣyanta maheṣvāsāḥ krodhāmarṣavaśaṁ gatāḥ ,rājño vadhena saṁtaptā muhūrtaṁ samavasthitāḥ.
english: The great archers, overwhelmed by anger and indignation, and greatly tormented by the killing of the king, stood motionless for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. The great archers, overwhelmed by anger and indignation, and greatly tormented by the killing of the king, stood motionless for a moment.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अश्रद्धेयमिदं कर्म कृतं भीमेन संजय ।यत्स नागायुतप्राणः पुत्रो मम निपातितः ॥७॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,aśraddheyamidaṁ karma kṛtaṁ bhīmena saṁjaya ,yatsa nāgāyutaprāṇaḥ putro mama nipātitaḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: Sanjaya, this unbelievable act (karma) done by Bhima is that my son, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Dhritarashtra said: Sanjaya, this unbelievable act (karma) done by Bhima is that my son, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, was slain.

sanskrit: अवध्यः सर्वभूतानां वज्रसंहननो युवा ।पाण्डवैः समरे पुत्रो निहतो मम संजय ॥८॥
transliteration: avadhyaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ vajrasaṁhanano yuvā ,pāṇḍavaiḥ samare putro nihato mama saṁjaya.
english: Sanjaya, my son, who was invincible by all beings, young, and possessed a body firm as a thunderbolt, has been slain in battle by the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Sanjaya, my son, who was invincible by all beings, young, and possessed a body firm as a thunderbolt, has been slain in battle by the Pandavas.

sanskrit: न दिष्टमभ्यतिक्रान्तुं शक्यं गावल्गणे नरैः ।यत्समेत्य रणे पार्थैः पुत्रो मम निपातितः ॥९॥
transliteration: na diṣṭamabhyatikrāntuṁ śakyaṁ gāvalgaṇe naraiḥ ,yatsametya raṇe pārthaiḥ putro mama nipātitaḥ.
english: O son of Gavalgana (Sanjaya), it is not possible for humans to overcome destiny (diṣṭa), for my son, having confronted the Pandavas in battle, was slain by them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. O son of Gavalgana (Sanjaya), it is not possible for humans to overcome destiny (diṣṭa), for my son, having confronted the Pandavas in battle, was slain by them.

sanskrit: अद्रिसारमयं नूनं हृदयं मम संजय ।हतं पुत्रशतं श्रुत्वा यन्न दीर्णं सहस्रधा ॥१०॥
transliteration: adrisāramayaṁ nūnaṁ hṛdayaṁ mama saṁjaya ,hataṁ putraśataṁ śrutvā yanna dīrṇaṁ sahasradhā.
english: O Saṃjaya, my heart must certainly be made of stone, since, even after hearing that a hundred of my sons have been slain, it has not shattered into a thousand pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. O Saṃjaya, my heart must certainly be made of stone, since, even after hearing that a hundred of my sons have been slain, it has not shattered into a thousand pieces.

sanskrit: कथं हि वृद्धमिथुनं हतपुत्रं भविष्यति ।न ह्यहं पाण्डवेयस्य विषये वस्तुमुत्सहे ॥११॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hi vṛddhamithunaṁ hataputraṁ bhaviṣyati ,na hyahaṁ pāṇḍaveyasya viṣaye vastumutsahe.
english: How will an aged couple, whose sons have been killed, possibly survive? I certainly cannot bear to live in the dominion of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. How will an aged couple, whose sons have been killed, possibly survive? I certainly cannot bear to live in the dominion of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: कथं राज्ञः पिता भूत्वा स्वयं राजा च संजय ।प्रेष्यभूतः प्रवर्तेयं पाण्डवेयस्य शासनात् ॥१२॥
transliteration: kathaṁ rājñaḥ pitā bhūtvā svayaṁ rājā ca saṁjaya ,preṣyabhūtaḥ pravarteyaṁ pāṇḍaveyasya śāsanāt.
english: O Saṃjaya, how could I, having been the father of a king and a king myself, behave as a mere servant under the command of the Pāṇḍava?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. O Saṃjaya, how could I, having been the father of a king and a king myself, behave as a mere servant under the command of the Pāṇḍava?

sanskrit: आज्ञाप्य पृथिवीं सर्वां स्थित्वा मूर्ध्नि च संजय ।कथमद्य भविष्यामि प्रेष्यभूतो दुरन्तकृत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ājñāpya pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ sthitvā mūrdhni ca saṁjaya ,kathamadya bhaviṣyāmi preṣyabhūto durantakṛt.
english: O Saṃjaya, how can I, who once commanded the entire earth and stood at its pinnacle, become a servant today, especially after bringing about such a dreadful outcome?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. O Saṃjaya, how can I, who once commanded the entire earth and stood at its pinnacle, become a servant today, especially after bringing about such a dreadful outcome?

sanskrit: कथं भीमस्य वाक्यानि श्रोतुं शक्ष्यामि संजय ।येन पुत्रशतं पूर्णमेकेन निहतं मम ॥१४॥
transliteration: kathaṁ bhīmasya vākyāni śrotuṁ śakṣyāmi saṁjaya ,yena putraśataṁ pūrṇamekena nihataṁ mama.
english: Saṃjaya, how shall I be able to hear the words of Bhīma, by whom my hundred sons were completely slain by that single individual?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Saṃjaya, how shall I be able to hear the words of Bhīma, by whom my hundred sons were completely slain by that single individual?

sanskrit: कृतं सत्यं वचस्तस्य विदुरस्य महात्मनः ।अकुर्वता वचस्तेन मम पुत्रेण संजय ॥१५॥
transliteration: kṛtaṁ satyaṁ vacastasya vidurasya mahātmanaḥ ,akurvatā vacastena mama putreṇa saṁjaya.
english: Saṃjaya, the words of that great-souled (mahātman) Vidura became true because my son did not heed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. Saṃjaya, the words of that great-souled (mahātman) Vidura became true because my son did not heed them.

sanskrit: अधर्मेण हते तात पुत्रे दुर्योधने मम ।कृतवर्मा कृपो द्रौणिः किमकुर्वत संजय ॥१६॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa hate tāta putre duryodhane mama ,kṛtavarmā kṛpo drauṇiḥ kimakurvata saṁjaya.
english: O dear Saṃjaya, when my son Duryodhana was unjustly slain (adharma), what did Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and Drauṇi do?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. O dear Saṃjaya, when my son Duryodhana was unjustly slain (adharma), what did Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and Drauṇi do?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।गत्वा तु तावका राजन्नातिदूरमवस्थिताः ।अपश्यन्त वनं घोरं नानाद्रुमलताकुलम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,gatvā tu tāvakā rājannātidūramavasthitāḥ ,apaśyanta vanaṁ ghoraṁ nānādrumalatākulam.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O King, your people, having gone not very far, observed a dreadful forest dense with various trees and creepers."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. Saṃjaya said: "O King, your people, having gone not very far, observed a dreadful forest dense with various trees and creepers."

sanskrit: ते मुहूर्तं तु विश्रम्य लब्धतोयैर्हयोत्तमैः ।सूर्यास्तमयवेलायामासेदुः सुमहद्वनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: te muhūrtaṁ tu viśramya labdhatoyairhayottamaiḥ ,sūryāstamayavelāyāmāseduḥ sumahadvanam.
english: After resting for a moment, and once their excellent horses had obtained water, they reached a very vast forest at sunset.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. After resting for a moment, and once their excellent horses had obtained water, they reached a very vast forest at sunset.

sanskrit: नानामृगगणैर्जुष्टं नानापक्षिसमाकुलम् ।नानाद्रुमलताच्छन्नं नानाव्यालनिषेवितम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: nānāmṛgagaṇairjuṣṭaṁ nānāpakṣisamākulam ,nānādrumalatācchannaṁ nānāvyālaniṣevitam.
english: [The forest was] teeming with various herds of animals, bustling with diverse flocks of birds, covered with different trees and creepers, and frequented by many kinds of venomous creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. [The forest was] teeming with various herds of animals, bustling with diverse flocks of birds, covered with different trees and creepers, and frequented by many kinds of venomous creatures.

sanskrit: नानातोयसमाकीर्णं तडागैरुपशोभितम् ।पद्मिनीशतसंछन्नं नीलोत्पलसमायुतम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: nānātoyasamākīrṇaṁ taḍāgairupaśobhitam ,padminīśatasaṁchannaṁ nīlotpalasamāyutam.
english: [The forest was also] teeming with diverse bodies of water, graced by ponds, covered with hundreds of lotus plants, and adorned with blue water lilies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. [The forest was also] teeming with diverse bodies of water, graced by ponds, covered with hundreds of lotus plants, and adorned with blue water lilies.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य तद्वनं घोरं वीक्षमाणाः समन्ततः ।शाखासहस्रसंछन्नं न्यग्रोधं ददृशुस्ततः ॥२१॥
transliteration: praviśya tadvanaṁ ghoraṁ vīkṣamāṇāḥ samantataḥ ,śākhāsahasrasaṁchannaṁ nyagrodhaṁ dadṛśustataḥ.
english: After entering that formidable forest and looking all around, they then saw a Banyan tree (nyagrodha) covered with thousands of branches.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. After entering that formidable forest and looking all around, they then saw a Banyan tree (nyagrodha) covered with thousands of branches.

sanskrit: उपेत्य तु तदा राजन्न्यग्रोधं ते महारथाः ।ददृशुर्द्विपदां श्रेष्ठाः श्रेष्ठं तं वै वनस्पतिम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: upetya tu tadā rājannyagrodhaṁ te mahārathāḥ ,dadṛśurdvipadāṁ śreṣṭhāḥ śreṣṭhaṁ taṁ vai vanaspatim.
english: Then, O King, those great charioteers, who were the foremost among men, approached that banyan tree and beheld that supreme tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. Then, O King, those great charioteers, who were the foremost among men, approached that banyan tree and beheld that supreme tree.

sanskrit: तेऽवतीर्य रथेभ्यस्तु विप्रमुच्य च वाजिनः ।उपस्पृश्य यथान्यायं संध्यामन्वासत प्रभो ॥२३॥
transliteration: te'vatīrya rathebhyastu vipramucya ca vājinaḥ ,upaspṛśya yathānyāyaṁ saṁdhyāmanvāsata prabho.
english: Then, O lord, having descended from their chariots and unyoked their horses, they performed their evening prayers (sandhyā) according to custom and sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. Then, O lord, having descended from their chariots and unyoked their horses, they performed their evening prayers (sandhyā) according to custom and sat down.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्तं पर्वतश्रेष्ठमनुप्राप्ते दिवाकरे ।सर्वस्य जगतो धात्री शर्वरी समपद्यत ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato'staṁ parvataśreṣṭhamanuprāpte divākare ,sarvasya jagato dhātrī śarvarī samapadyata.
english: Then, when the sun reached the chief of mountains (the western mountain of sunset), night, the nourisher of the entire world, came upon them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. Then, when the sun reached the chief of mountains (the western mountain of sunset), night, the nourisher of the entire world, came upon them.

sanskrit: ग्रहनक्षत्रताराभिः प्रकीर्णाभिरलंकृतम् ।नभोंऽशुकमिवाभाति प्रेक्षणीयं समन्ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: grahanakṣatratārābhiḥ prakīrṇābhiralaṁkṛtam ,nabhoṁ'śukamivābhāti prekṣaṇīyaṁ samantataḥ.
english: Adorned with scattered planets, constellations, and stars, the sky appeared like a splendid garment, beautiful to behold from every direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. Adorned with scattered planets, constellations, and stars, the sky appeared like a splendid garment, beautiful to behold from every direction.

sanskrit: ईषच्चापि प्रवल्गन्ति ये सत्त्वा रात्रिचारिणः ।दिवाचराश्च ये सत्त्वास्ते निद्रावशमागताः ॥२६॥
transliteration: īṣaccāpi pravalganti ye sattvā rātricāriṇaḥ ,divācarāśca ye sattvāste nidrāvaśamāgatāḥ.
english: And to some extent, those beings that move about at night are frolicking, while those beings that move about by day have fallen under the sway of sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. And to some extent, those beings that move about at night are frolicking, while those beings that move about by day have fallen under the sway of sleep.

sanskrit: रात्रिंचराणां सत्त्वानां निनादोऽभूत्सुदारुणः ।क्रव्यादाश्च प्रमुदिता घोरा प्राप्ता च शर्वरी ॥२७॥
transliteration: rātriṁcarāṇāṁ sattvānāṁ ninādo'bhūtsudāruṇaḥ ,kravyādāśca pramuditā ghorā prāptā ca śarvarī.
english: A very terrible sound arose from the night-roaming creatures, and the carnivores were delighted. A dreadful night had indeed arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 27. A very terrible sound arose from the night-roaming creatures, and the carnivores were delighted. A dreadful night had indeed arrived.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्रात्रिमुखे घोरे दुःखशोकसमन्विताः ।कृतवर्मा कृपो द्रौणिरुपोपविविशुः समम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasminrātrimukhe ghore duḥkhaśokasamanvitāḥ ,kṛtavarmā kṛpo drauṇirupopaviviśuḥ samam.
english: In that terrible onset of night, Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman), filled with sorrow and grief, sat down together nearby.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 28. In that terrible onset of night, Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman), filled with sorrow and grief, sat down together nearby.

sanskrit: तत्रोपविष्टाः शोचन्तो न्यग्रोधस्य समन्ततः ।तमेवार्थमतिक्रान्तं कुरुपाण्डवयोः क्षयम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatropaviṣṭāḥ śocanto nyagrodhasya samantataḥ ,tamevārthamatikrāntaṁ kurupāṇḍavayoḥ kṣayam.
english: Seated there, grieving all around the banyan tree, they pondered that very past event: the destruction of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 29. Seated there, grieving all around the banyan tree, they pondered that very past event: the destruction of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: निद्रया च परीताङ्गा निषेदुर्धरणीतले ।श्रमेण सुदृढं युक्ता विक्षता विविधैः शरैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: nidrayā ca parītāṅgā niṣedurdharaṇītale ,śrameṇa sudṛḍhaṁ yuktā vikṣatā vividhaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Their bodies, afflicted by sleep (nidrā), sat down on the ground. They were firmly exhausted by fatigue and wounded by various arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 30. Their bodies, afflicted by sleep (nidrā), sat down on the ground. They were firmly exhausted by fatigue and wounded by various arrows.

sanskrit: ततो निद्रावशं प्राप्तौ कृपभोजौ महारथौ ।सुखोचितावदुःखार्हौ निषण्णौ धरणीतले ।तौ तु सुप्तौ महाराज श्रमशोकसमन्वितौ ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato nidrāvaśaṁ prāptau kṛpabhojau mahārathau ,sukhocitāvaduḥkhārhau niṣaṇṇau dharaṇītale ,tau tu suptau mahārāja śramaśokasamanvitau.
english: Then, Kṛpa and Bhoja, the great charioteers, accustomed to comfort and not deserving of distress, fell under the sway of sleep (nidrā) and lay down on the ground. But those two, O great king, sleeping, were overwhelmed by fatigue and sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 31. Then, Kṛpa and Bhoja, the great charioteers, accustomed to comfort and not deserving of distress, fell under the sway of sleep (nidrā) and lay down on the ground. But those two, O great king, sleeping, were overwhelmed by fatigue and sorrow.

sanskrit: क्रोधामर्षवशं प्राप्तो द्रोणपुत्रस्तु भारत ।नैव स्म स जगामाथ निद्रां सर्प इव श्वसन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: krodhāmarṣavaśaṁ prāpto droṇaputrastu bhārata ,naiva sma sa jagāmātha nidrāṁ sarpa iva śvasan.
english: But the son of Droṇa, O Bhārata, having fallen under the sway of wrath and indignation, did not go to sleep (nidrā) at all, breathing like a snake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 32. But the son of Droṇa, O Bhārata, having fallen under the sway of wrath and indignation, did not go to sleep (nidrā) at all, breathing like a snake.

sanskrit: न लेभे स तु निद्रां वै दह्यमानोऽतिमन्युना ।वीक्षां चक्रे महाबाहुस्तद्वनं घोरदर्शनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: na lebhe sa tu nidrāṁ vai dahyamāno'timanyunā ,vīkṣāṁ cakre mahābāhustadvanaṁ ghoradarśanam.
english: But being consumed by extreme rage, he certainly did not obtain sleep (nidrā). The mighty-armed one surveyed that terrible-looking forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 33. But being consumed by extreme rage, he certainly did not obtain sleep (nidrā). The mighty-armed one surveyed that terrible-looking forest.

sanskrit: वीक्षमाणो वनोद्देशं नानासत्त्वैर्निषेवितम् ।अपश्यत महाबाहुर्न्यग्रोधं वायसायुतम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: vīkṣamāṇo vanoddeśaṁ nānāsattvairniṣevitam ,apaśyata mahābāhurnyagrodhaṁ vāyasāyutam.
english: As he surveyed that forest region, which was frequented by various creatures, the mighty-armed hero (mahābāhu) spotted a banyan tree teeming with ten thousand crows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 34. As he surveyed that forest region, which was frequented by various creatures, the mighty-armed hero (mahābāhu) spotted a banyan tree teeming with ten thousand crows.

sanskrit: तत्र काकसहस्राणि तां निशां पर्यणामयन् ।सुखं स्वपन्तः कौरव्य पृथक्पृथगपाश्रयाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatra kākasahasrāṇi tāṁ niśāṁ paryaṇāmayan ,sukhaṁ svapantaḥ kauravya pṛthakpṛthagapāśrayāḥ.
english: O descendant of Kuru (Kauravya), thousands of crows spent that night there, sleeping comfortably and each having found their own separate resting place.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 35. O descendant of Kuru (Kauravya), thousands of crows spent that night there, sleeping comfortably and each having found their own separate resting place.

sanskrit: सुप्तेषु तेषु काकेषु विस्रब्धेषु समन्ततः ।सोऽपश्यत्सहसायान्तमुलूकं घोरदर्शनम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: supteṣu teṣu kākeṣu visrabdheṣu samantataḥ ,so'paśyatsahasāyāntamulūkaṁ ghoradarśanam.
english: While those crows were sleeping soundly and confidently all around, he suddenly saw an owl (ulūka) of terrifying appearance approaching.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 36. While those crows were sleeping soundly and confidently all around, he suddenly saw an owl (ulūka) of terrifying appearance approaching.

sanskrit: महास्वनं महाकायं हर्यक्षं बभ्रुपिङ्गलम् ।सुदीर्घघोणानखरं सुपर्णमिव वेगिनम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: mahāsvanaṁ mahākāyaṁ haryakṣaṁ babhrupiṅgalam ,sudīrghaghoṇānakharaṁ suparṇamiva veginam.
english: It was characterized by a mighty cry, a colossal body, tawny eyes, and a brownish-yellow color. It had a very long beak and claws, and was as swift as Garuḍa (suparṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 37. It was characterized by a mighty cry, a colossal body, tawny eyes, and a brownish-yellow color. It had a very long beak and claws, and was as swift as Garuḍa (suparṇa).

sanskrit: सोऽथ शब्दं मृदुं कृत्वा लीयमान इवाण्डजः ।न्यग्रोधस्य ततः शाखां प्रार्थयामास भारत ॥३८॥
transliteration: so'tha śabdaṁ mṛduṁ kṛtvā līyamāna ivāṇḍajaḥ ,nyagrodhasya tataḥ śākhāṁ prārthayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, then, making a soft sound, like a bird preparing to hide, he approached the branch of the banyan tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 38. O Bhārata, then, making a soft sound, like a bird preparing to hide, he approached the branch of the banyan tree.

sanskrit: संनिपत्य तु शाखायां न्यग्रोधस्य विहंगमः ।सुप्ताञ्जघान सुबहून्वायसान्वायसान्तकः ॥३९॥
transliteration: saṁnipatya tu śākhāyāṁ nyagrodhasya vihaṁgamaḥ ,suptāñjaghāna subahūnvāyasānvāyasāntakaḥ.
english: Having settled on the banyan tree's branch, the bird – the killer of crows – then killed many sleeping crows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 39. Having settled on the banyan tree's branch, the bird – the killer of crows – then killed many sleeping crows.

sanskrit: केषांचिदच्छिनत्पक्षाञ्शिरांसि च चकर्त ह ।चरणांश्चैव केषांचिद्बभञ्ज चरणायुधः ॥४०॥
transliteration: keṣāṁcidacchinatpakṣāñśirāṁsi ca cakarta ha ,caraṇāṁścaiva keṣāṁcidbabhañja caraṇāyudhaḥ.
english: He cut off the wings of some (crows), and indeed severed their heads. And the bird, whose weapon is its feet, broke the feet of some others.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 40. He cut off the wings of some (crows), and indeed severed their heads. And the bird, whose weapon is its feet, broke the feet of some others.

sanskrit: क्षणेनाहन्स बलवान्येऽस्य दृष्टिपथे स्थिताः ।तेषां शरीरावयवैः शरीरैश्च विशां पते ।न्यग्रोधमण्डलं सर्वं संछन्नं सर्वतोऽभवत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: kṣaṇenāhansa balavānye'sya dṛṣṭipathe sthitāḥ ,teṣāṁ śarīrāvayavaiḥ śarīraiśca viśāṁ pate ,nyagrodhamaṇḍalaṁ sarvaṁ saṁchannaṁ sarvato'bhavat.
english: O Lord of men, that powerful bird instantly killed all those (crows) who were within his sight. With their body parts and their bodies, the entire circular area around the banyan tree became completely covered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 41. O Lord of men, that powerful bird instantly killed all those (crows) who were within his sight. With their body parts and their bodies, the entire circular area around the banyan tree became completely covered.

sanskrit: तांस्तु हत्वा ततः काकान्कौशिको मुदितोऽभवत् ।प्रतिकृत्य यथाकामं शत्रूणां शत्रुसूदनः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tāṁstu hatvā tataḥ kākānkauśiko mudito'bhavat ,pratikṛtya yathākāmaṁ śatrūṇāṁ śatrusūdanaḥ.
english: Having thus killed those crows, Kauśika, the slayer of enemies (śatrusūdana), became joyful. He had taken revenge on his enemies as he desired.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 42. Having thus killed those crows, Kauśika, the slayer of enemies (śatrusūdana), became joyful. He had taken revenge on his enemies as he desired.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा सोपधं कर्म कौशिकेन कृतं निशि ।तद्भावकृतसंकल्पो द्रौणिरेको व्यचिन्तयत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā sopadhaṁ karma kauśikena kṛtaṁ niśi ,tadbhāvakṛtasaṁkalpo drauṇireko vyacintayat.
english: Seeing that deceptive act (karma) performed by Kauśika in the night, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) alone, whose resolve was fixed on that very idea, pondered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 43. Seeing that deceptive act (karma) performed by Kauśika in the night, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) alone, whose resolve was fixed on that very idea, pondered.

sanskrit: उपदेशः कृतोऽनेन पक्षिणा मम संयुगे ।शत्रूणां क्षपणे युक्तः प्राप्तकालश्च मे मतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: upadeśaḥ kṛto'nena pakṣiṇā mama saṁyuge ,śatrūṇāṁ kṣapaṇe yuktaḥ prāptakālaśca me mataḥ.
english: This bird has provided an instruction to me for combat: it is suitable for the destruction of enemies, and I consider it to be the opportune moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 44. This bird has provided an instruction to me for combat: it is suitable for the destruction of enemies, and I consider it to be the opportune moment.

sanskrit: नाद्य शक्या मया हन्तुं पाण्डवा जितकाशिनः ।बलवन्तः कृतोत्साहा लब्धलक्षाः प्रहारिणः ।राज्ञः सकाशे तेषां च प्रतिज्ञातो वधो मया ॥४५॥
transliteration: nādya śakyā mayā hantuṁ pāṇḍavā jitakāśinaḥ ,balavantaḥ kṛtotsāhā labdhalakṣāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,rājñaḥ sakāśe teṣāṁ ca pratijñāto vadho mayā.
english: Today, the Pāṇḍavas, who are accustomed to victory, strong, highly resolute, have achieved their objective, and are formidable attackers, cannot be killed by me. Furthermore, their slaying was vowed by me in the presence of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 45. Today, the Pāṇḍavas, who are accustomed to victory, strong, highly resolute, have achieved their objective, and are formidable attackers, cannot be killed by me. Furthermore, their slaying was vowed by me in the presence of the king.

sanskrit: पतंगाग्निसमां वृत्तिमास्थायात्मविनाशिनीम् ।न्यायतो युध्यमानस्य प्राणत्यागो न संशयः ।छद्मना तु भवेत्सिद्धिः शत्रूणां च क्षयो महान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: pataṁgāgnisamāṁ vṛttimāsthāyātmavināśinīm ,nyāyato yudhyamānasya prāṇatyāgo na saṁśayaḥ ,chadmanā tu bhavetsiddhiḥ śatrūṇāṁ ca kṣayo mahān.
english: When one adopts a self-destructive attitude, akin to a moth drawn to a fire, there is no doubt that one fighting righteously, according to natural law (dharma), will sacrifice their life. However, success and a great destruction of enemies can be achieved through deceit.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 46. When one adopts a self-destructive attitude, akin to a moth drawn to a fire, there is no doubt that one fighting righteously, according to natural law (dharma), will sacrifice their life. However, success and a great destruction of enemies can be achieved through deceit.

sanskrit: तत्र संशयितादर्थाद्योऽर्थो निःसंशयो भवेत् ।तं जना बहु मन्यन्ते येऽर्थशास्त्रविशारदाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tatra saṁśayitādarthādyo'rtho niḥsaṁśayo bhavet ,taṁ janā bahu manyante ye'rthaśāstraviśāradāḥ.
english: From doubtful matters, whichever meaning or outcome becomes certain, that is highly esteemed by people who are experts in the science of polity (arthaśāstra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 47. From doubtful matters, whichever meaning or outcome becomes certain, that is highly esteemed by people who are experts in the science of polity (arthaśāstra).

sanskrit: यच्चाप्यत्र भवेद्वाच्यं गर्हितं लोकनिन्दितम् ।कर्तव्यं तन्मनुष्येण क्षत्रधर्मेण वर्तता ॥४८॥
transliteration: yaccāpyatra bhavedvācyaṁ garhitaṁ lokaninditam ,kartavyaṁ tanmanuṣyeṇa kṣatradharmeṇa vartatā.
english: And whatever here may be censurable, condemned, and despised by people, that must be done by a man who acts in accordance with the natural law (dharma) of a warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 48. And whatever here may be censurable, condemned, and despised by people, that must be done by a man who acts in accordance with the natural law (dharma) of a warrior.

sanskrit: निन्दितानि च सर्वाणि कुत्सितानि पदे पदे ।सोपधानि कृतान्येव पाण्डवैरकृतात्मभिः ॥४९॥
transliteration: ninditāni ca sarvāṇi kutsitāni pade pade ,sopadhāni kṛtānyeva pāṇḍavairakṛtātmabhiḥ.
english: Indeed, all those actions, censured and despicable at every turn, were performed with stratagem by the Pāṇḍavas, who had not disciplined their inner self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 49. Indeed, all those actions, censured and despicable at every turn, were performed with stratagem by the Pāṇḍavas, who had not disciplined their inner self (ātman).

sanskrit: अस्मिन्नर्थे पुरा गीतौ श्रूयेते धर्मचिन्तकैः ।श्लोकौ न्यायमवेक्षद्भिस्तत्त्वार्थं तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: asminnarthe purā gītau śrūyete dharmacintakaiḥ ,ślokau nyāyamavekṣadbhistattvārthaṁ tattvadarśibhiḥ.
english: Regarding this matter, in the past, two verses were sung and are heard, composed by those who ponder natural law (dharma), by those who observe justice, and by seers of truth who understand its essence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 50. Regarding this matter, in the past, two verses were sung and are heard, composed by those who ponder natural law (dharma), by those who observe justice, and by seers of truth who understand its essence.

sanskrit: परिश्रान्ते विदीर्णे च भुञ्जाने चापि शत्रुभिः ।प्रस्थाने च प्रवेशे च प्रहर्तव्यं रिपोर्बलम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: pariśrānte vidīrṇe ca bhuñjāne cāpi śatrubhiḥ ,prasthāne ca praveśe ca prahartavyaṁ riporbalam.
english: The enemy's force should be attacked when it is exhausted, scattered, when eating, or even when suffering losses at the hands of enemies, as well as when it is on the march or entering a place.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 51. The enemy's force should be attacked when it is exhausted, scattered, when eating, or even when suffering losses at the hands of enemies, as well as when it is on the march or entering a place.

sanskrit: निद्रार्तमर्धरात्रे च तथा नष्टप्रणायकम् ।भिन्नयोधं बलं यच्च द्विधा युक्तं च यद्भवेत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: nidrārtamardharātre ca tathā naṣṭapraṇāyakam ,bhinnayodhaṁ balaṁ yacca dvidhā yuktaṁ ca yadbhavet.
english: Furthermore, an army should be attacked if it is drowsy, in the middle of the night, if it has lost its leader, if its warriors are divided, or if it is deployed in two separate divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 52. Furthermore, an army should be attacked if it is drowsy, in the middle of the night, if it has lost its leader, if its warriors are divided, or if it is deployed in two separate divisions.

sanskrit: इत्येवं निश्चयं चक्रे सुप्तानां युधि मारणे ।पाण्डूनां सह पाञ्चालैर्द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ॥५३॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ niścayaṁ cakre suptānāṁ yudhi māraṇe ,pāṇḍūnāṁ saha pāñcālairdroṇaputraḥ pratāpavān.
english: Thus, the mighty son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) decided to kill the sleeping Pāṇḍavas along with the Pañcālas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 53. Thus, the mighty son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) decided to kill the sleeping Pāṇḍavas along with the Pañcālas in battle.

sanskrit: स क्रूरां मतिमास्थाय विनिश्चित्य मुहुर्मुहुः ।सुप्तौ प्राबोधयत्तौ तु मातुलं भोजमेव च ॥५४॥
transliteration: sa krūrāṁ matimāsthāya viniścitya muhurmuhuḥ ,suptau prābodhayattau tu mātulaṁ bhojameva ca.
english: Having repeatedly made a firm decision and adopted a cruel intention, he awoke those two who were sleeping: his maternal uncle and Bhoja.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 54. Having repeatedly made a firm decision and adopted a cruel intention, he awoke those two who were sleeping: his maternal uncle and Bhoja.

sanskrit: नोत्तरं प्रतिपेदे च तत्र युक्तं ह्रिया वृतः ।स मुहूर्तमिव ध्यात्वा बाष्पविह्वलमब्रवीत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: nottaraṁ pratipede ca tatra yuktaṁ hriyā vṛtaḥ ,sa muhūrtamiva dhyātvā bāṣpavihvalamabravīt.
english: Overwhelmed by shame, he could not give a suitable reply in that situation. After thinking for a moment, he spoke, agitated by tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 55. Overwhelmed by shame, he could not give a suitable reply in that situation. After thinking for a moment, he spoke, agitated by tears.

sanskrit: हतो दुर्योधनो राजा एकवीरो महाबलः ।यस्यार्थे वैरमस्माभिरासक्तं पाण्डवैः सह ॥५६॥
transliteration: hato duryodhano rājā ekavīro mahābalaḥ ,yasyārthe vairamasmābhirāsaktaṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha.
english: King Duryodhana, that unique and mighty hero, has been slain – he for whose sake we engaged in hostility with the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 56. King Duryodhana, that unique and mighty hero, has been slain – he for whose sake we engaged in hostility with the Pandavas.

sanskrit: एकाकी बहुभिः क्षुद्रैराहवे शुद्धविक्रमः ।पातितो भीमसेनेन एकादशचमूपतिः ॥५७॥
transliteration: ekākī bahubhiḥ kṣudrairāhave śuddhavikramaḥ ,pātito bhīmasenena ekādaśacamūpatiḥ.
english: Though he was a solitary warrior (ekākī) of unblemished prowess (śuddhavikrama), the commander of eleven armies was struck down in battle by the many petty individuals, by Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 57. Though he was a solitary warrior (ekākī) of unblemished prowess (śuddhavikrama), the commander of eleven armies was struck down in battle by the many petty individuals, by Bhimasena.

sanskrit: वृकोदरेण क्षुद्रेण सुनृशंसमिदं कृतम् ।मूर्धाभिषिक्तस्य शिरः पादेन परिमृद्नता ॥५८॥
transliteration: vṛkodareṇa kṣudreṇa sunṛśaṁsamidaṁ kṛtam ,mūrdhābhiṣiktasya śiraḥ pādena parimṛdnatā.
english: This extremely cruel act was performed by the base Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who trampled the head of the crowned king with his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 58. This extremely cruel act was performed by the base Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who trampled the head of the crowned king with his foot.

sanskrit: विनर्दन्ति स्म पाञ्चालाः क्ष्वेडन्ति च हसन्ति च ।धमन्ति शङ्खाञ्शतशो हृष्टा घ्नन्ति च दुन्दुभीन् ॥५९॥
transliteration: vinardanti sma pāñcālāḥ kṣveḍanti ca hasanti ca ,dhamanti śaṅkhāñśataśo hṛṣṭā ghnanti ca dundubhīn.
english: The Pāñcālas roared, shouted, and laughed. Delighted, they blew hundreds of conch shells and beat the drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 59. The Pāñcālas roared, shouted, and laughed. Delighted, they blew hundreds of conch shells and beat the drums.

sanskrit: वादित्रघोषस्तुमुलो विमिश्रः शङ्खनिस्वनैः ।अनिलेनेरितो घोरो दिशः पूरयतीव हि ॥६०॥
transliteration: vāditraghoṣastumulo vimiśraḥ śaṅkhanisvanaiḥ ,anilenerito ghoro diśaḥ pūrayatīva hi.
english: The tumultuous sound of musical instruments, mingled with the blasts of conch shells, dreadful and driven by the wind, truly seems to fill all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 60. The tumultuous sound of musical instruments, mingled with the blasts of conch shells, dreadful and driven by the wind, truly seems to fill all directions.

sanskrit: अश्वानां हेषमाणानां गजानां चैव बृंहताम् ।सिंहनादश्च शूराणां श्रूयते सुमहानयम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: aśvānāṁ heṣamāṇānāṁ gajānāṁ caiva bṛṁhatām ,siṁhanādaśca śūrāṇāṁ śrūyate sumahānayam.
english: This very great neighing of horses, trumpeting of elephants, and lion's roar (battle cry) of heroes is heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 61. This very great neighing of horses, trumpeting of elephants, and lion's roar (battle cry) of heroes is heard.

sanskrit: दिशं प्राचीं समाश्रित्य हृष्टानां गर्जतां भृशम् ।रथनेमिस्वनाश्चैव श्रूयन्ते लोमहर्षणाः ॥६२॥
transliteration: diśaṁ prācīṁ samāśritya hṛṣṭānāṁ garjatāṁ bhṛśam ,rathanemisvanāścaiva śrūyante lomaharṣaṇāḥ.
english: From the eastern direction, where they are joyfully and loudly roaring, the thrilling sounds of chariot wheels are heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 62. From the eastern direction, where they are joyfully and loudly roaring, the thrilling sounds of chariot wheels are heard.

sanskrit: पाण्डवैर्धार्तराष्ट्राणां यदिदं कदनं कृतम् ।वयमेव त्रयः शिष्टास्तस्मिन्महति वैशसे ॥६३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavairdhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ yadidaṁ kadanaṁ kṛtam ,vayameva trayaḥ śiṣṭāstasminmahati vaiśase.
english: Considering this great destruction inflicted by the Pāṇḍavas upon the Dhārtarāṣṭras, only we three remain from that immense slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 63. Considering this great destruction inflicted by the Pāṇḍavas upon the Dhārtarāṣṭras, only we three remain from that immense slaughter.

sanskrit: केचिन्नागशतप्राणाः केचित्सर्वास्त्रकोविदाः ।निहताः पाण्डवेयैः स्म मन्ये कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: kecinnāgaśataprāṇāḥ kecitsarvāstrakovidāḥ ,nihatāḥ pāṇḍaveyaiḥ sma manye kālasya paryayam.
english: Some possessed the strength of a hundred elephants, and others were experts in all weaponry. Yet, they were slain by the Pāṇḍavas. I consider this to be the turning point of (kāla) time or destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 64. Some possessed the strength of a hundred elephants, and others were experts in all weaponry. Yet, they were slain by the Pāṇḍavas. I consider this to be the turning point of (kāla) time or destiny.

sanskrit: एवमेतेन भाव्यं हि नूनं कार्येण तत्त्वतः ।यथा ह्यस्येदृशी निष्ठा कृते कार्येऽपि दुष्करे ॥६५॥
transliteration: evametena bhāvyaṁ hi nūnaṁ kāryeṇa tattvataḥ ,yathā hyasyedṛśī niṣṭhā kṛte kārye'pi duṣkare.
english: Indeed, it must certainly be that this event has truly come to pass this way, for such is the final outcome (niṣṭhā) for him, even after a most difficult task (kārya) has been accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 65. Indeed, it must certainly be that this event has truly come to pass this way, for such is the final outcome (niṣṭhā) for him, even after a most difficult task (kārya) has been accomplished.

sanskrit: भवतोस्तु यदि प्रज्ञा न मोहादपचीयते ।व्यापन्नेऽस्मिन्महत्यर्थे यन्नः श्रेयस्तदुच्यताम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: bhavatostu yadi prajñā na mohādapacīyate ,vyāpanne'sminmahatyarthe yannaḥ śreyastaducyatām.
english: But if your wisdom is not diminished by delusion, then regarding this great matter that has gone awry, please tell us what is most beneficial for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 66. But if your wisdom is not diminished by delusion, then regarding this great matter that has gone awry, please tell us what is most beneficial for us.

sanskrit: कृप उवाच ।श्रुतं ते वचनं सर्वं हेतुयुक्तं मया विभो ।ममापि तु वचः किंचिच्छृणुष्वाद्य महाभुज ॥१॥
transliteration: kṛpa uvāca ,śrutaṁ te vacanaṁ sarvaṁ hetuyuktaṁ mayā vibho ,mamāpi tu vacaḥ kiṁcicchṛṇuṣvādya mahābhuja.
english: Kṛpa said: "All your logical speech, O Lord, has been heard by me. But now, O mighty-armed one, please listen to some words from me as well."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Kṛpa said: "All your logical speech, O Lord, has been heard by me. But now, O mighty-armed one, please listen to some words from me as well."

sanskrit: आबद्धा मानुषाः सर्वे निर्बन्धाः कर्मणोर्द्वयोः ।दैवे पुरुषकारे च परं ताभ्यां न विद्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: ābaddhā mānuṣāḥ sarve nirbandhāḥ karmaṇordvayoḥ ,daive puruṣakāre ca paraṁ tābhyāṁ na vidyate.
english: All human beings are bound and constrained by two factors: destiny and human effort (puruṣakāra). Nothing superior to these two is known to exist.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. All human beings are bound and constrained by two factors: destiny and human effort (puruṣakāra). Nothing superior to these two is known to exist.

sanskrit: न हि दैवेन सिध्यन्ति कर्माण्येकेन सत्तम ।न चापि कर्मणैकेन द्वाभ्यां सिद्धिस्तु योगतः ॥३॥
transliteration: na hi daivena sidhyanti karmāṇyekena sattama ,na cāpi karmaṇaikena dvābhyāṁ siddhistu yogataḥ.
english: Indeed, O best of righteous ones, actions (karma) are not accomplished by destiny alone. Nor are they achieved by human effort (karma) alone. But success (siddhi) comes through the proper combination (yoga) of both.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Indeed, O best of righteous ones, actions (karma) are not accomplished by destiny alone. Nor are they achieved by human effort (karma) alone. But success (siddhi) comes through the proper combination (yoga) of both.

sanskrit: ताभ्यामुभाभ्यां सर्वार्था निबद्धा ह्यधमोत्तमाः ।प्रवृत्ताश्चैव दृश्यन्ते निवृत्ताश्चैव सर्वशः ॥४॥
transliteration: tābhyāmubhābhyāṁ sarvārthā nibaddhā hyadhamottamāḥ ,pravṛttāścaiva dṛśyante nivṛttāścaiva sarvaśaḥ.
english: By both of these (principles of engagement and withdrawal), all purposes, whether low or high, are bound. And indeed, they are seen to be both engaged and withdrawn in every respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. By both of these (principles of engagement and withdrawal), all purposes, whether low or high, are bound. And indeed, they are seen to be both engaged and withdrawn in every respect.

sanskrit: पर्जन्यः पर्वते वर्षन्किं नु साधयते फलम् ।कृष्टे क्षेत्रे तथावर्षन्किं नु साधयते फलम् ॥५॥
transliteration: parjanyaḥ parvate varṣankiṁ nu sādhayate phalam ,kṛṣṭe kṣetre tathāvarṣankiṁ nu sādhayate phalam.
english: What fruit does the rain god (parjanya) achieve by raining on a mountain? And likewise, what fruit does it achieve by not raining on a cultivated field?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. What fruit does the rain god (parjanya) achieve by raining on a mountain? And likewise, what fruit does it achieve by not raining on a cultivated field?

sanskrit: उत्थानं चाप्यदैवस्य ह्यनुत्थानस्य दैवतम् ।व्यर्थं भवति सर्वत्र पूर्वं कस्तत्र निश्चयः ॥६॥
transliteration: utthānaṁ cāpyadaivasya hyanutthānasya daivatam ,vyarthaṁ bhavati sarvatra pūrvaṁ kastatra niścayaḥ.
english: The effort of one who lacks divine aid, and the divine aid for one who makes no effort - both become useless everywhere. What then is the primary determination regarding this?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. The effort of one who lacks divine aid, and the divine aid for one who makes no effort - both become useless everywhere. What then is the primary determination regarding this?

sanskrit: प्रवृष्टे च यथा देवे सम्यक्क्षेत्रे च कर्षिते ।बीजं महागुणं भूयात्तथा सिद्धिर्हि मानुषी ॥७॥
transliteration: pravṛṣṭe ca yathā deve samyakkṣetre ca karṣite ,bījaṁ mahāguṇaṁ bhūyāttathā siddhirhi mānuṣī.
english: Just as when the divine (rain) has fallen and the field is well-cultivated, the seed becomes highly fruitful; similarly, human success (siddhi) is indeed achieved.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Just as when the divine (rain) has fallen and the field is well-cultivated, the seed becomes highly fruitful; similarly, human success (siddhi) is indeed achieved.

sanskrit: तयोर्दैवं विनिश्चित्य स्ववशेनैव वर्तते ।प्राज्ञाः पुरुषकारं तु घटन्ते दाक्ष्यमास्थिताः ॥८॥
transliteration: tayordaivaṁ viniścitya svavaśenaiva vartate ,prājñāḥ puruṣakāraṁ tu ghaṭante dākṣyamāsthitāḥ.
english: Among these two (fate and human effort), even after ascertaining destiny (daivam), one acts by one's own will. But the wise, having resorted to diligence, indeed strive for human effort (puruṣakāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. Among these two (fate and human effort), even after ascertaining destiny (daivam), one acts by one's own will. But the wise, having resorted to diligence, indeed strive for human effort (puruṣakāra).

sanskrit: ताभ्यां सर्वे हि कार्यार्था मनुष्याणां नरर्षभ ।विचेष्टन्तश्च दृश्यन्ते निवृत्ताश्च तथैव हि ॥९॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ sarve hi kāryārthā manuṣyāṇāṁ nararṣabha ,viceṣṭantaśca dṛśyante nivṛttāśca tathaiva hi.
english: O best among men, by means of these two (fate and human effort), indeed all those among humans who undertake actions are seen both striving and similarly desisting.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. O best among men, by means of these two (fate and human effort), indeed all those among humans who undertake actions are seen both striving and similarly desisting.

sanskrit: कृतः पुरुषकारः सन्सोऽपि दैवेन सिध्यति ।तथास्य कर्मणः कर्तुरभिनिर्वर्तते फलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛtaḥ puruṣakāraḥ sanso'pi daivena sidhyati ,tathāsya karmaṇaḥ karturabhinirvartate phalam.
english: Even when human effort (puruṣakāra) is performed, that too succeeds through destiny (daivam). Similarly, the fruit (phalam) of an action (karma) results for its doer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. Even when human effort (puruṣakāra) is performed, that too succeeds through destiny (daivam). Similarly, the fruit (phalam) of an action (karma) results for its doer.

sanskrit: उत्थानं तु मनुष्याणां दक्षाणां दैववर्जितम् ।अफलं दृश्यते लोके सम्यगप्युपपादितम् ॥११॥
transliteration: utthānaṁ tu manuṣyāṇāṁ dakṣāṇāṁ daivavarjitam ,aphalaṁ dṛśyate loke samyagapyupapāditam.
english: But the exertion of diligent people, when devoid of (favorable) destiny (daivam), is seen in the world to be fruitless, even if it is perfectly accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. But the exertion of diligent people, when devoid of (favorable) destiny (daivam), is seen in the world to be fruitless, even if it is perfectly accomplished.

sanskrit: तत्रालसा मनुष्याणां ये भवन्त्यमनस्विनः ।उत्थानं ते विगर्हन्ति प्राज्ञानां तन्न रोचते ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatrālasā manuṣyāṇāṁ ye bhavantyamanasvinaḥ ,utthānaṁ te vigarhanti prājñānāṁ tanna rocate.
english: Those among men who are lazy and mindless condemn effort. That (condemnation) does not please the wise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. Those among men who are lazy and mindless condemn effort. That (condemnation) does not please the wise.

sanskrit: प्रायशो हि कृतं कर्म अफलं दृश्यते भुवि ।अकृत्वा च पुनर्दुःखं कर्म दृश्येन्महाफलम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: prāyaśo hi kṛtaṁ karma aphalaṁ dṛśyate bhuvi ,akṛtvā ca punarduḥkhaṁ karma dṛśyenmahāphalam.
english: Indeed, an action (karma) that has been performed is often seen to be fruitless on earth. On the other hand, a difficult action (karma) that one did not undertake may be seen (in hindsight) to have yielded great results.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Indeed, an action (karma) that has been performed is often seen to be fruitless on earth. On the other hand, a difficult action (karma) that one did not undertake may be seen (in hindsight) to have yielded great results.

sanskrit: चेष्टामकुर्वँल्लभते यदि किंचिद्यदृच्छया ।यो वा न लभते कृत्वा दुर्दशौ तावुभावपि ॥१४॥
transliteration: ceṣṭāmakurvaँllabhate yadi kiṁcidyadṛcchayā ,yo vā na labhate kṛtvā durdaśau tāvubhāvapi.
english: If one obtains something by chance without making an effort, or if one does not obtain (anything) despite making an effort, both of those individuals are indeed in an unfortunate situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. If one obtains something by chance without making an effort, or if one does not obtain (anything) despite making an effort, both of those individuals are indeed in an unfortunate situation.

sanskrit: शक्नोति जीवितुं दक्षो नालसः सुखमेधते ।दृश्यन्ते जीवलोकेऽस्मिन्दक्षाः प्रायो हितैषिणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śaknoti jīvituṁ dakṣo nālasaḥ sukhamedhate ,dṛśyante jīvaloke'smindakṣāḥ prāyo hitaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: A capable person is able to sustain their life, whereas a lazy person does not thrive in comfort. In this world of living beings (jīvaloka), the capable are generally seen as benevolent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. A capable person is able to sustain their life, whereas a lazy person does not thrive in comfort. In this world of living beings (jīvaloka), the capable are generally seen as benevolent.

sanskrit: यदि दक्षः समारम्भात्कर्मणां नाश्नुते फलम् ।नास्य वाच्यं भवेत्किंचित्तत्त्वं चाप्यधिगच्छति ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadi dakṣaḥ samārambhātkarmaṇāṁ nāśnute phalam ,nāsya vācyaṁ bhavetkiṁcittattvaṁ cāpyadhigacchati.
english: If a capable person (dakṣa), despite undertaking actions (karma), does not obtain their results (phalam), no blame should be attached to him, and he also understands the fundamental truth (tattvam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. If a capable person (dakṣa), despite undertaking actions (karma), does not obtain their results (phalam), no blame should be attached to him, and he also understands the fundamental truth (tattvam).

sanskrit: अकृत्वा कर्म यो लोके फलं विन्दति विष्टितः ।स तु वक्तव्यतां याति द्वेष्यो भवति प्रायशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: akṛtvā karma yo loke phalaṁ vindati viṣṭitaḥ ,sa tu vaktavyatāṁ yāti dveṣyo bhavati prāyaśaḥ.
english: The person who, in this world, obtains results (phalam) without performing actions (karma) and merely being supported, he indeed becomes liable to criticism and is generally disliked.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. The person who, in this world, obtains results (phalam) without performing actions (karma) and merely being supported, he indeed becomes liable to criticism and is generally disliked.

sanskrit: एवमेतदनादृत्य वर्तते यस्त्वतोऽन्यथा ।स करोत्यात्मनोऽनर्थान्नैष बुद्धिमतां नयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: evametadanādṛtya vartate yastvato'nyathā ,sa karotyātmano'narthānnaiṣa buddhimatāṁ nayaḥ.
english: But whoever acts otherwise than this, disregarding this principle (of effort), he brings misfortunes upon himself. This is not the policy (naya) of the intelligent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. But whoever acts otherwise than this, disregarding this principle (of effort), he brings misfortunes upon himself. This is not the policy (naya) of the intelligent.

sanskrit: हीनं पुरुषकारेण यदा दैवेन वा पुनः ।कारणाभ्यामथैताभ्यामुत्थानमफलं भवेत् ।हीनं पुरुषकारेण कर्म त्विह न सिध्यति ॥१९॥
transliteration: hīnaṁ puruṣakāreṇa yadā daivena vā punaḥ ,kāraṇābhyāmathaitābhyāmutthānamaphalaṁ bhavet ,hīnaṁ puruṣakāreṇa karma tviha na sidhyati.
english: When an endeavor (utthānam) is lacking either in human effort (puruṣakāra) or, moreover, in divine agency (fate), then, by these two very causes, that endeavor becomes fruitless. Indeed, an action (karma) deficient in human effort (puruṣakāra) does not succeed in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. When an endeavor (utthānam) is lacking either in human effort (puruṣakāra) or, moreover, in divine agency (fate), then, by these two very causes, that endeavor becomes fruitless. Indeed, an action (karma) deficient in human effort (puruṣakāra) does not succeed in this world.

sanskrit: दैवतेभ्यो नमस्कृत्य यस्त्वर्थान्सम्यगीहते ।दक्षो दाक्षिण्यसंपन्नो न स मोघं विहन्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: daivatebhyo namaskṛtya yastvarthānsamyagīhate ,dakṣo dākṣiṇyasaṁpanno na sa moghaṁ vihanyate.
english: A person who, after offering respects to the deities, strives effectively for his objectives, and is both skillful and courteous, will not have his efforts thwarted in vain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. A person who, after offering respects to the deities, strives effectively for his objectives, and is both skillful and courteous, will not have his efforts thwarted in vain.

sanskrit: सम्यगीहा पुनरियं यो वृद्धानुपसेवते ।आपृच्छति च यच्छ्रेयः करोति च हितं वचः ॥२१॥
transliteration: samyagīhā punariyaṁ yo vṛddhānupasevate ,āpṛcchati ca yacchreyaḥ karoti ca hitaṁ vacaḥ.
english: This, moreover, is proper endeavor: the person who serves the elders, asks them about what is beneficial, and acts upon their wholesome advice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. This, moreover, is proper endeavor: the person who serves the elders, asks them about what is beneficial, and acts upon their wholesome advice.

sanskrit: उत्थायोत्थाय हि सदा प्रष्टव्या वृद्धसंमताः ।तेऽस्य योगे परं मूलं तन्मूला सिद्धिरुच्यते ॥२२॥
transliteration: utthāyotthāya hi sadā praṣṭavyā vṛddhasaṁmatāḥ ,te'sya yoge paraṁ mūlaṁ tanmūlā siddhirucyate.
english: Indeed, one should always repeatedly rise and consult those esteemed by the elders. They are considered the ultimate foundation in one's discipline (yoga), and success (siddhi) is said to be rooted in them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. Indeed, one should always repeatedly rise and consult those esteemed by the elders. They are considered the ultimate foundation in one's discipline (yoga), and success (siddhi) is said to be rooted in them.

sanskrit: वृद्धानां वचनं श्रुत्वा यो ह्युत्थानं प्रयोजयेत् ।उत्थानस्य फलं सम्यक्तदा स लभतेऽचिरात् ॥२३॥
transliteration: vṛddhānāṁ vacanaṁ śrutvā yo hyutthānaṁ prayojayet ,utthānasya phalaṁ samyaktadā sa labhate'cirāt.
english: The person who, having listened to the advice of the elders, applies himself to exertion, he then quickly obtains the full fruit of that exertion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. The person who, having listened to the advice of the elders, applies himself to exertion, he then quickly obtains the full fruit of that exertion.

sanskrit: रागात्क्रोधाद्भयाल्लोभाद्योऽर्थानीहेत मानवः ।अनीशश्चावमानी च स शीघ्रं भ्रश्यते श्रियः ॥२४॥
transliteration: rāgātkrodhādbhayāllobhādyo'rthānīheta mānavaḥ ,anīśaścāvamānī ca sa śīghraṁ bhraśyate śriyaḥ.
english: A human being who strives for material gain (artha) out of passion, anger, fear, or greed, and who is both powerless and contemptuous, quickly falls from prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 24. A human being who strives for material gain (artha) out of passion, anger, fear, or greed, and who is both powerless and contemptuous, quickly falls from prosperity.

sanskrit: सोऽयं दुर्योधनेनार्थो लुब्धेनादीर्घदर्शिना ।असमर्थ्य समारब्धो मूढत्वादविचिन्तितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ duryodhanenārtho lubdhenādīrghadarśinā ,asamarthya samārabdho mūḍhatvādavicintitaḥ.
english: This undertaking, started by the greedy and short-sighted Duryodhana, was commenced without proper preparation or capability, and was unconsidered due to his foolishness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 25. This undertaking, started by the greedy and short-sighted Duryodhana, was commenced without proper preparation or capability, and was unconsidered due to his foolishness.

sanskrit: हितबुद्धीननादृत्य संमन्त्र्यासाधुभिः सह ।वार्यमाणोऽकरोद्वैरं पाण्डवैर्गुणवत्तरैः ॥२६॥
transliteration: hitabuddhīnanādṛtya saṁmantryāsādhubhiḥ saha ,vāryamāṇo'karodvairaṁ pāṇḍavairguṇavattaraiḥ.
english: Disregarding those with good intentions, and consulting with evil persons, he (Duryodhana), even while being restrained, created enmity with the Pāṇḍavas, who were more virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 26. Disregarding those with good intentions, and consulting with evil persons, he (Duryodhana), even while being restrained, created enmity with the Pāṇḍavas, who were more virtuous.

sanskrit: पूर्वमप्यतिदुःशीलो न दैन्यं कर्तुमर्हति ।तपत्यर्थे विपन्ने हि मित्राणामकृतं वचः ॥२७॥
transliteration: pūrvamapyatiduḥśīlo na dainyaṁ kartumarhati ,tapatyarthe vipanne hi mitrāṇāmakṛtaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Even if someone was extremely ill-behaved previously, they should not resort to wretchedness or despair now. For, when an objective (artha) is lost, the unheeded advice of friends truly pains.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 27. Even if someone was extremely ill-behaved previously, they should not resort to wretchedness or despair now. For, when an objective (artha) is lost, the unheeded advice of friends truly pains.

sanskrit: अन्वावर्तामहि वयं यत्तु तं पापपूरुषम् ।अस्मानप्यनयस्तस्मात्प्राप्तोऽयं दारुणो महान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: anvāvartāmahi vayaṁ yattu taṁ pāpapūruṣam ,asmānapyanayastasmātprāpto'yaṁ dāruṇo mahān.
english: Indeed, because we aligned ourselves with that wicked person (puruṣa), this great and terrible misfortune has also befallen us because of him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 28. Indeed, because we aligned ourselves with that wicked person (puruṣa), this great and terrible misfortune has also befallen us because of him.

sanskrit: अनेन तु ममाद्यापि व्यसनेनोपतापिता ।बुद्धिश्चिन्तयतः किंचित्स्वं श्रेयो नावबुध्यते ॥२९॥
transliteration: anena tu mamādyāpi vyasanenopatāpitā ,buddhiścintayataḥ kiṁcitsvaṁ śreyo nāvabudhyate.
english: But even today, my intellect, while reflecting and afflicted by this misfortune, does not discern anything of its own welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 29. But even today, my intellect, while reflecting and afflicted by this misfortune, does not discern anything of its own welfare.

sanskrit: मुह्यता तु मनुष्येण प्रष्टव्याः सुहृदो बुधाः ।ते च पृष्टा यथा ब्रूयुस्तत्कर्तव्यं तथा भवेत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: muhyatā tu manuṣyeṇa praṣṭavyāḥ suhṛdo budhāḥ ,te ca pṛṣṭā yathā brūyustatkartavyaṁ tathā bhavet.
english: Indeed, a deluded person should consult wise and well-wishing friends. And whatever they say when asked, that should be done accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 30. Indeed, a deluded person should consult wise and well-wishing friends. And whatever they say when asked, that should be done accordingly.

sanskrit: ते वयं धृतराष्ट्रं च गान्धारीं च समेत्य ह ।उपपृच्छामहे गत्वा विदुरं च महामतिम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca gāndhārīṁ ca sametya ha ,upapṛcchāmahe gatvā viduraṁ ca mahāmatim.
english: We shall indeed go and, having met Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, we shall also inquire of the greatly wise Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 31. We shall indeed go and, having met Dhritarashtra and Gandhari, we shall also inquire of the greatly wise Vidura.

sanskrit: ते पृष्टाश्च वदेयुर्यच्छ्रेयो नः समनन्तरम् ।तदस्माभिः पुनः कार्यमिति मे नैष्ठिकी मतिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: te pṛṣṭāśca vadeyuryacchreyo naḥ samanantaram ,tadasmābhiḥ punaḥ kāryamiti me naiṣṭhikī matiḥ.
english: And when questioned, they should declare what is immediately beneficial for us. That, in turn, should be undertaken by us again; this is my firm conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 32. And when questioned, they should declare what is immediately beneficial for us. That, in turn, should be undertaken by us again; this is my firm conviction.

sanskrit: अनारम्भात्तु कार्याणां नार्थः संपद्यते क्वचित् ।कृते पुरुषकारे च येषां कार्यं न सिध्यति ।दैवेनोपहतास्ते तु नात्र कार्या विचारणा ॥३३॥
transliteration: anārambhāttu kāryāṇāṁ nārthaḥ saṁpadyate kvacit ,kṛte puruṣakāre ca yeṣāṁ kāryaṁ na sidhyati ,daivenopahatāste tu nātra kāryā vicāraṇā.
english: Indeed, by not initiating undertakings, no objective is ever achieved. And for those whose endeavors do not succeed even after they have exerted personal effort (puruṣakāra), they are simply afflicted by destiny (daiva). In their case, there is no need for further deliberation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 33. Indeed, by not initiating undertakings, no objective is ever achieved. And for those whose endeavors do not succeed even after they have exerted personal effort (puruṣakāra), they are simply afflicted by destiny (daiva). In their case, there is no need for further deliberation.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कृपस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा धर्मार्थसहितं शुभम् ।अश्वत्थामा महाराज दुःखशोकसमन्वितः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kṛpasya vacanaṁ śrutvā dharmārthasahitaṁ śubham ,aśvatthāmā mahārāja duḥkhaśokasamanvitaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, having heard Kṛpa's auspicious and beneficial counsel, which was endowed with both natural law (dharma) and practical purpose (artha), Aśvatthāmā became overwhelmed with pain and sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, having heard Kṛpa's auspicious and beneficial counsel, which was endowed with both natural law (dharma) and practical purpose (artha), Aśvatthāmā became overwhelmed with pain and sorrow.

sanskrit: दह्यमानस्तु शोकेन प्रदीप्तेनाग्निना यथा ।क्रूरं मनस्ततः कृत्वा तावुभौ प्रत्यभाषत ॥२॥
transliteration: dahyamānastu śokena pradīptenāgninā yathā ,krūraṁ manastataḥ kṛtvā tāvubhau pratyabhāṣata.
english: Indeed, tormented by grief as if by a blazing fire, he then hardened his mind and spoke in reply to both of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. Indeed, tormented by grief as if by a blazing fire, he then hardened his mind and spoke in reply to both of them.

sanskrit: पुरुषे पुरुषे बुद्धिः सा सा भवति शोभना ।तुष्यन्ति च पृथक्सर्वे प्रज्ञया ते स्वया स्वया ॥३॥
transliteration: puruṣe puruṣe buddhiḥ sā sā bhavati śobhanā ,tuṣyanti ca pṛthaksarve prajñayā te svayā svayā.
english: In every person, their own intellect (buddhi) seems excellent. And all individuals are separately satisfied with their own particular understanding (prajñā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. In every person, their own intellect (buddhi) seems excellent. And all individuals are separately satisfied with their own particular understanding (prajñā).

sanskrit: सर्वो हि मन्यते लोक आत्मानं बुद्धिमत्तरम् ।सर्वस्यात्मा बहुमतः सर्वात्मानं प्रशंसति ॥४॥
transliteration: sarvo hi manyate loka ātmānaṁ buddhimattaram ,sarvasyātmā bahumataḥ sarvātmānaṁ praśaṁsati.
english: Indeed, everyone (loka) considers their own self (ātman) to be more intelligent. The self (ātman) of every person is highly esteemed by them, and each praises their whole self (sarvātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. Indeed, everyone (loka) considers their own self (ātman) to be more intelligent. The self (ātman) of every person is highly esteemed by them, and each praises their whole self (sarvātman).

sanskrit: सर्वस्य हि स्वका प्रज्ञा साधुवादे प्रतिष्ठिता ।परबुद्धिं च निन्दन्ति स्वां प्रशंसन्ति चासकृत् ॥५॥
transliteration: sarvasya hi svakā prajñā sādhuvāde pratiṣṭhitā ,parabuddhiṁ ca nindanti svāṁ praśaṁsanti cāsakṛt.
english: Indeed, everyone's own wisdom (prajñā) is firmly established in self-praise. They repeatedly censure the intellect (buddhi) of others and praise their own.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Indeed, everyone's own wisdom (prajñā) is firmly established in self-praise. They repeatedly censure the intellect (buddhi) of others and praise their own.

sanskrit: कारणान्तरयोगेन योगे येषां समा मतिः ।तेऽन्योन्येन च तुष्यन्ति बहु मन्यन्ति चासकृत् ॥६॥
transliteration: kāraṇāntarayogena yoge yeṣāṁ samā matiḥ ,te'nyonyena ca tuṣyanti bahu manyanti cāsakṛt.
english: However, those whose understanding (mati) is similar due to a different kind of connection (yoga), they become mutually satisfied and repeatedly hold each other in high esteem.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. However, those whose understanding (mati) is similar due to a different kind of connection (yoga), they become mutually satisfied and repeatedly hold each other in high esteem.

sanskrit: तस्यैव तु मनुष्यस्य सा सा बुद्धिस्तदा तदा ।कालयोगविपर्यासं प्राप्यान्योन्यं विपद्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: tasyaiva tu manuṣyasya sā sā buddhistadā tadā ,kālayogaviparyāsaṁ prāpyānyonyaṁ vipadyate.
english: However, the very intellect of that person, at various times, upon experiencing an inversion of the influences of time, becomes mutually confused.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. However, the very intellect of that person, at various times, upon experiencing an inversion of the influences of time, becomes mutually confused.

sanskrit: अचिन्त्यत्वाद्धि चित्तानां मनुष्याणां विशेषतः ।चित्तवैकल्यमासाद्य सा सा बुद्धिः प्रजायते ॥८॥
transliteration: acintyatvāddhi cittānāṁ manuṣyāṇāṁ viśeṣataḥ ,cittavaikalyamāsādya sā sā buddhiḥ prajāyate.
english: Indeed, due to the inconceivable nature of minds (citta), especially among human beings, that very intellect, upon experiencing mental disturbance, manifests.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. Indeed, due to the inconceivable nature of minds (citta), especially among human beings, that very intellect, upon experiencing mental disturbance, manifests.

sanskrit: यथा हि वैद्यः कुशलो ज्ञात्वा व्याधिं यथाविधि ।भेषजं कुरुते योगात्प्रशमार्थमिहाभिभो ॥९॥
transliteration: yathā hi vaidyaḥ kuśalo jñātvā vyādhiṁ yathāvidhi ,bheṣajaṁ kurute yogātpraśamārthamihābhibho.
english: Indeed, just as a skillful physician, having properly diagnosed a disease, prepares medicine according to the prescribed method for its alleviation by means of application (yoga), O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. Indeed, just as a skillful physician, having properly diagnosed a disease, prepares medicine according to the prescribed method for its alleviation by means of application (yoga), O lord.

sanskrit: एवं कार्यस्य योगार्थं बुद्धिं कुर्वन्ति मानवाः ।प्रज्ञया हि स्वया युक्तास्तां च निन्दन्ति मानवाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ kāryasya yogārthaṁ buddhiṁ kurvanti mānavāḥ ,prajñayā hi svayā yuktāstāṁ ca nindanti mānavāḥ.
english: Thus, human beings apply their intellect for the proper accomplishment of a task. Indeed, though endowed with their own wisdom, these very human beings also criticize it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. Thus, human beings apply their intellect for the proper accomplishment of a task. Indeed, though endowed with their own wisdom, these very human beings also criticize it.

sanskrit: अन्यया यौवने मर्त्यो बुद्ध्या भवति मोहितः ।मध्येऽन्यया जरायां तु सोऽन्यां रोचयते मतिम् ॥११॥
transliteration: anyayā yauvane martyo buddhyā bhavati mohitaḥ ,madhye'nyayā jarāyāṁ tu so'nyāṁ rocayate matim.
english: In youth, a mortal becomes deluded by one kind of intellect (buddhi). In middle age, he is swayed by yet another, and in old age, he indeed develops a preference for a completely different mental disposition (mati).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. In youth, a mortal becomes deluded by one kind of intellect (buddhi). In middle age, he is swayed by yet another, and in old age, he indeed develops a preference for a completely different mental disposition (mati).

sanskrit: व्यसनं वा पुनर्घोरं समृद्धिं वापि तादृशीम् ।अवाप्य पुरुषो भोज कुरुते बुद्धिवैकृतम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyasanaṁ vā punarghoraṁ samṛddhiṁ vāpi tādṛśīm ,avāpya puruṣo bhoja kurute buddhivaikṛtam.
english: O Bhoja, having attained either a terrible calamity or such great prosperity, a person then develops a distortion of intellect (buddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. O Bhoja, having attained either a terrible calamity or such great prosperity, a person then develops a distortion of intellect (buddhi).

sanskrit: एकस्मिन्नेव पुरुषे सा सा बुद्धिस्तदा तदा ।भवत्यनित्यप्रज्ञत्वात्सा तस्यैव न रोचते ॥१३॥
transliteration: ekasminneva puruṣe sā sā buddhistadā tadā ,bhavatyanityaprajñatvātsā tasyaiva na rocate.
english: In one and the same person, various intellects (buddhi) arise from time to time. Because of the impermanent nature of his understanding (prajñā), that very (intellect) does not remain pleasing to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. In one and the same person, various intellects (buddhi) arise from time to time. Because of the impermanent nature of his understanding (prajñā), that very (intellect) does not remain pleasing to him.

sanskrit: निश्चित्य तु यथाप्रज्ञं यां मतिं साधु पश्यति ।तस्यां प्रकुरुते भावं सा तस्योद्योगकारिका ॥१४॥
transliteration: niścitya tu yathāprajñaṁ yāṁ matiṁ sādhu paśyati ,tasyāṁ prakurute bhāvaṁ sā tasyodyogakārikā.
english: Having ascertained, according to his understanding (prajñā), which particular thought (mati) he considers to be correct, he then cultivates an intention (bhāva) towards it. That very (thought or intention) becomes the cause of his endeavor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Having ascertained, according to his understanding (prajñā), which particular thought (mati) he considers to be correct, he then cultivates an intention (bhāva) towards it. That very (thought or intention) becomes the cause of his endeavor.

sanskrit: सर्वो हि पुरुषो भोज साध्वेतदिति निश्चितः ।कर्तुमारभते प्रीतो मरणादिषु कर्मसु ॥१५॥
transliteration: sarvo hi puruṣo bhoja sādhvetaditi niścitaḥ ,kartumārabhate prīto maraṇādiṣu karmasu.
english: Indeed, every person (puruṣa), O Bhoja, being convinced that 'this is proper,' delightedly undertakes various actions (karma), such as those related to death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Indeed, every person (puruṣa), O Bhoja, being convinced that 'this is proper,' delightedly undertakes various actions (karma), such as those related to death.

sanskrit: सर्वे हि युक्तिं विज्ञाय प्रज्ञां चापि स्वकां नराः ।चेष्टन्ते विविधाश्चेष्टा हितमित्येव जानते ॥१६॥
transliteration: sarve hi yuktiṁ vijñāya prajñāṁ cāpi svakāṁ narāḥ ,ceṣṭante vividhāśceṣṭā hitamityeva jānate.
english: Indeed, all people, having understood proper method and also their own wisdom, undertake various efforts, knowing only that 'this is beneficial'.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Indeed, all people, having understood proper method and also their own wisdom, undertake various efforts, knowing only that 'this is beneficial'.

sanskrit: उपजाता व्यसनजा येयमद्य मतिर्मम ।युवयोस्तां प्रवक्ष्यामि मम शोकविनाशिनीम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: upajātā vyasanajā yeyamadya matirmama ,yuvayostāṁ pravakṣyāmi mama śokavināśinīm.
english: This thought of mine, which has arisen today from distress, I will declare to you two - it is that which dispels my sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. This thought of mine, which has arisen today from distress, I will declare to you two - it is that which dispels my sorrow.

sanskrit: प्रजापतिः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा कर्म तासु विधाय च ।वर्णे वर्णे समाधत्त एकैकं गुणवत्तरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: prajāpatiḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā karma tāsu vidhāya ca ,varṇe varṇe samādhatta ekaikaṁ guṇavattaram.
english: Having created beings and established their respective duties (karma) among them, Prajāpati ordained for each social class (varṇa) a unique, more excellent quality.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. Having created beings and established their respective duties (karma) among them, Prajāpati ordained for each social class (varṇa) a unique, more excellent quality.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणे दममव्यग्रं क्षत्रिये तेज उत्तमम् ।दाक्ष्यं वैश्ये च शूद्रे च सर्ववर्णानुकूलताम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇe damamavyagraṁ kṣatriye teja uttamam ,dākṣyaṁ vaiśye ca śūdre ca sarvavarṇānukūlatām.
english: Unwavering self-control in a Brahmin, excellent valor in a Kshatriya, skill and efficiency in a Vaishya, and helpfulness towards all social classes in a Shudra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Unwavering self-control in a Brahmin, excellent valor in a Kshatriya, skill and efficiency in a Vaishya, and helpfulness towards all social classes in a Shudra.

sanskrit: अदान्तो ब्राह्मणोऽसाधुर्निस्तेजाः क्षत्रियोऽधमः ।अदक्षो निन्द्यते वैश्यः शूद्रश्च प्रतिकूलवान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: adānto brāhmaṇo'sādhurnistejāḥ kṣatriyo'dhamaḥ ,adakṣo nindyate vaiśyaḥ śūdraśca pratikūlavān.
english: An unrestrained Brahmin is unvirtuous. A Kshatriya devoid of valor is inferior. An unskilled Vaishya is censured, and a Shudra who is hostile is also criticized.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. An unrestrained Brahmin is unvirtuous. A Kshatriya devoid of valor is inferior. An unskilled Vaishya is censured, and a Shudra who is hostile is also criticized.

sanskrit: सोऽस्मि जातः कुले श्रेष्ठे ब्राह्मणानां सुपूजिते ।मन्दभाग्यतयास्म्येतं क्षत्रधर्ममनु ष्ठितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: so'smi jātaḥ kule śreṣṭhe brāhmaṇānāṁ supūjite ,mandabhāgyatayāsmyetaṁ kṣatradharmamanu ṣṭhitaḥ.
english: I was born into an excellent and highly revered family of Brahmins, but due to misfortune, I have undertaken this warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. I was born into an excellent and highly revered family of Brahmins, but due to misfortune, I have undertaken this warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).

sanskrit: क्षत्रधर्मं विदित्वाहं यदि ब्राह्मण्यसंश्रितम् ।प्रकुर्यां सुमहत्कर्म न मे तत्साधु संमतम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: kṣatradharmaṁ viditvāhaṁ yadi brāhmaṇyasaṁśritam ,prakuryāṁ sumahatkarma na me tatsādhu saṁmatam.
english: If, having understood the natural law (kṣatradharma) of a Kshatriya, I were to perform a very great deed (karma) that is based on Brahminhood (brāhmaṇya), that would not be deemed appropriate by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. If, having understood the natural law (kṣatradharma) of a Kshatriya, I were to perform a very great deed (karma) that is based on Brahminhood (brāhmaṇya), that would not be deemed appropriate by me.

sanskrit: धारयित्वा धनुर्दिव्यं दिव्यान्यस्त्राणि चाहवे ।पितरं निहतं दृष्ट्वा किं नु वक्ष्यामि संसदि ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhārayitvā dhanurdivyaṁ divyānyastrāṇi cāhave ,pitaraṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā kiṁ nu vakṣyāmi saṁsadi.
english: After bearing a divine bow and divine weapons in battle, having witnessed my father's death, what indeed will I say in the assembly?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 23. After bearing a divine bow and divine weapons in battle, having witnessed my father's death, what indeed will I say in the assembly?

sanskrit: सोऽहमद्य यथाकामं क्षत्रधर्ममुपास्य तम् ।गन्तास्मि पदवीं राज्ञः पितुश्चापि महाद्युतेः ॥२४॥
transliteration: so'hamadya yathākāmaṁ kṣatradharmamupāsya tam ,gantāsmi padavīṁ rājñaḥ pituścāpi mahādyuteḥ.
english: Today, having fulfilled that warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) as I wished, I shall follow the path of my greatly resplendent father, the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 24. Today, having fulfilled that warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) as I wished, I shall follow the path of my greatly resplendent father, the king.

sanskrit: अद्य स्वप्स्यन्ति पाञ्चाला विश्वस्ता जितकाशिनः ।विमुक्तयुग्यकवचा हर्षेण च समन्विताः ।वयं जिता मताश्चैषां श्रान्ता व्यायमनेन च ॥२५॥
transliteration: adya svapsyanti pāñcālā viśvastā jitakāśinaḥ ,vimuktayugyakavacā harṣeṇa ca samanvitāḥ ,vayaṁ jitā matāścaiṣāṁ śrāntā vyāyamanena ca.
english: Today, the Pāñcālas will sleep confidently, appearing victorious, having abandoned their chariots and armor, and filled with joy. They consider us defeated and exhausted from the battle (vyāyamana).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 25. Today, the Pāñcālas will sleep confidently, appearing victorious, having abandoned their chariots and armor, and filled with joy. They consider us defeated and exhausted from the battle (vyāyamana).

sanskrit: तेषां निशि प्रसुप्तानां स्वस्थानां शिबिरे स्वके ।अवस्कन्दं करिष्यामि शिबिरस्याद्य दुष्करम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ niśi prasuptānāṁ svasthānāṁ śibire svake ,avaskandaṁ kariṣyāmi śibirasyādya duṣkaram.
english: Tonight, I will launch a formidable assault on their own camp, against them who are fast asleep and at ease in their own quarters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 26. Tonight, I will launch a formidable assault on their own camp, against them who are fast asleep and at ease in their own quarters.

sanskrit: तानवस्कन्द्य शिबिरे प्रेतभूतान्विचेतसः ।सूदयिष्यामि विक्रम्य मघवानिव दानवान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tānavaskandya śibire pretabhūtānvicetasaḥ ,sūdayiṣyāmi vikramya maghavāniva dānavān.
english: Assaulting them in their camp, who are senseless and like ghosts, I will destroy them with my valor, just as Indra (Maghavan) destroys the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 27. Assaulting them in their camp, who are senseless and like ghosts, I will destroy them with my valor, just as Indra (Maghavan) destroys the Dānavas.

sanskrit: अद्य तान्सहितान्सर्वान्धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमान् ।सूदयिष्यामि विक्रम्य कक्षं दीप्त इवानलः ।निहत्य चैव पाञ्चालाञ्शान्तिं लब्धास्मि सत्तम ॥२८॥
transliteration: adya tānsahitānsarvāndhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamān ,sūdayiṣyāmi vikramya kakṣaṁ dīpta ivānalaḥ ,nihatya caiva pāñcālāñśāntiṁ labdhāsmi sattama.
english: Today, O best of men, I will destroy all of them, including Dhṛṣṭadyumna, with my valor, just as a blazing fire consumes dry grass. And having killed the Pāñcālas, I shall attain peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 28. Today, O best of men, I will destroy all of them, including Dhṛṣṭadyumna, with my valor, just as a blazing fire consumes dry grass. And having killed the Pāñcālas, I shall attain peace.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालेषु चरिष्यामि सूदयन्नद्य संयुगे ।पिनाकपाणिः संक्रुद्धः स्वयं रुद्रः पशुष्विव ॥२९॥
transliteration: pāñcāleṣu cariṣyāmi sūdayannadya saṁyuge ,pinākapāṇiḥ saṁkruddhaḥ svayaṁ rudraḥ paśuṣviva.
english: Today in battle, I will move among the Pāñcālas, destroying them, just as Rudra himself, greatly enraged and holding his Pināka bow, moves among beasts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 29. Today in battle, I will move among the Pāñcālas, destroying them, just as Rudra himself, greatly enraged and holding his Pināka bow, moves among beasts.

sanskrit: अद्याहं सर्वपाञ्चालान्निहत्य च निकृत्य च ।अर्दयिष्यामि संक्रुद्धो रणे पाण्डुसुतांस्तथा ॥३०॥
transliteration: adyāhaṁ sarvapāñcālānnihatya ca nikṛtya ca ,ardayiṣyāmi saṁkruddho raṇe pāṇḍusutāṁstathā.
english: Today, greatly enraged, I will kill and mutilate all the Pāñcālas, and similarly crush the sons of Pāṇḍu in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 30. Today, greatly enraged, I will kill and mutilate all the Pāñcālas, and similarly crush the sons of Pāṇḍu in battle.

sanskrit: अद्याहं सर्वपाञ्चालैः कृत्वा भूमिं शरीरिणीम् ।प्रहृत्यैकैकशस्तेभ्यो भविष्याम्यनृणः पितुः ॥३१॥
transliteration: adyāhaṁ sarvapāñcālaiḥ kṛtvā bhūmiṁ śarīriṇīm ,prahṛtyaikaikaśastebhyo bhaviṣyāmyanṛṇaḥ pituḥ.
english: Today, having filled the earth with the bodies of all the Pañcālas and having struck them down one by one, I will become free from debt to my father.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 31. Today, having filled the earth with the bodies of all the Pañcālas and having struck them down one by one, I will become free from debt to my father.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य कर्णस्य भीष्मसैन्धवयोरपि ।गमयिष्यामि पाञ्चालान्पदवीमद्य दुर्गमाम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya karṇasya bhīṣmasaindhavayorapi ,gamayiṣyāmi pāñcālānpadavīmadya durgamām.
english: Today, I will send the Pañcālas to the inaccessible state (death) that Duryodhana, Karṇa, Bhīṣma, and Saindhava have reached.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 32. Today, I will send the Pañcālas to the inaccessible state (death) that Duryodhana, Karṇa, Bhīṣma, and Saindhava have reached.

sanskrit: अद्य पाञ्चालराजस्य धृष्टद्युम्नस्य वै निशि ।विरात्रे प्रमथिष्यामि पशोरिव शिरो बलात् ॥३३॥
transliteration: adya pāñcālarājasya dhṛṣṭadyumnasya vai niśi ,virātre pramathiṣyāmi paśoriva śiro balāt.
english: Today, indeed, in the dead of night, I will crush the head of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the king of Pañcāla, by force, just as one crushes an animal's head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 33. Today, indeed, in the dead of night, I will crush the head of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the king of Pañcāla, by force, just as one crushes an animal's head.

sanskrit: अद्य पाञ्चालपाण्डूनां शयितानात्मजान्निशि ।खड्गेन निशितेनाजौ प्रमथिष्यामि गौतम ॥३४॥
transliteration: adya pāñcālapāṇḍūnāṁ śayitānātmajānniśi ,khaḍgena niśitenājau pramathiṣyāmi gautama.
english: Today, O Gautama, I will destroy the sleeping sons of the Pañcālas and Pāṇḍavas on the battlefield tonight with a sharp sword.
