search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 34. Today, O Gautama, I will destroy the sleeping sons of the Pañcālas and Pāṇḍavas on the battlefield tonight with a sharp sword.

sanskrit: अद्य पाञ्चालसेनां तां निहत्य निशि सौप्तिके ।कृतकृत्यः सुखी चैव भविष्यामि महामते ॥३५॥
transliteration: adya pāñcālasenāṁ tāṁ nihatya niśi sauptike ,kṛtakṛtyaḥ sukhī caiva bhaviṣyāmi mahāmate.
english: O great-minded one, once I have destroyed that Pañcāla army tonight in the sleep-attack, I will indeed be content and happy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 35. O great-minded one, once I have destroyed that Pañcāla army tonight in the sleep-attack, I will indeed be content and happy.

sanskrit: कृप उवाच ।दिष्ट्या ते प्रतिकर्तव्ये मतिर्जातेयमच्युत ।न त्वा वारयितुं शक्तो वज्रपाणिरपि स्वयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: kṛpa uvāca ,diṣṭyā te pratikartavye matirjāteyamacyuta ,na tvā vārayituṁ śakto vajrapāṇirapi svayam.
english: Kṛpa said: "O Acyuta, it is fortunate that this resolve to retaliate has arisen in you. Not even Vajrapāṇi (Indra) himself would be able to stop you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Kṛpa said: "O Acyuta, it is fortunate that this resolve to retaliate has arisen in you. Not even Vajrapāṇi (Indra) himself would be able to stop you."

sanskrit: अनुयास्यावहे त्वां तु प्रभाते सहितावुभौ ।अद्य रात्रौ विश्रमस्व विमुक्तकवचध्वजः ॥२॥
transliteration: anuyāsyāvahe tvāṁ tu prabhāte sahitāvubhau ,adya rātrau viśramasva vimuktakavacadhvajaḥ.
english: However, both of us will follow you together in the morning. Tonight, please rest, having set aside your armor and banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. However, both of us will follow you together in the morning. Tonight, please rest, having set aside your armor and banner.

sanskrit: अहं त्वामनुयास्यामि कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।परानभिमुखं यान्तं रथावास्थाय दंशितौ ॥३॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tvāmanuyāsyāmi kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,parānabhimukhaṁ yāntaṁ rathāvāsthāya daṁśitau.
english: I will follow you, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, will also. Both of us will be armored and will mount our chariots as you advance to face the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. I will follow you, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, will also. Both of us will be armored and will mount our chariots as you advance to face the enemies.

sanskrit: आवाभ्यां सहितः शत्रूञ्श्वोऽसि हन्ता समागमे ।विक्रम्य रथिनां श्रेष्ठ पाञ्चालान्सपदानुगान् ॥४॥
transliteration: āvābhyāṁ sahitaḥ śatrūñśvo'si hantā samāgame ,vikramya rathināṁ śreṣṭha pāñcālānsapadānugān.
english: O best among charioteers, tomorrow, accompanied by us both, you will be the slayer of the Panchalas and their followers in battle, after having shown great valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. O best among charioteers, tomorrow, accompanied by us both, you will be the slayer of the Panchalas and their followers in battle, after having shown great valor.

sanskrit: शक्तस्त्वमसि विक्रान्तुं विश्रमस्व निशामिमाम् ।चिरं ते जाग्रतस्तात स्वप तावन्निशामिमाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śaktastvamasi vikrāntuṁ viśramasva niśāmimām ,ciraṁ te jāgratastāta svapa tāvanniśāmimām.
english: You are capable of showing valor. Rest this night. O dear one, since you have been awake for a long time, sleep this night now.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. You are capable of showing valor. Rest this night. O dear one, since you have been awake for a long time, sleep this night now.

sanskrit: विश्रान्तश्च विनिद्रश्च स्वस्थचित्तश्च मानद ।समेत्य समरे शत्रून्वधिष्यसि न संशयः ॥६॥
transliteration: viśrāntaśca vinidraśca svasthacittaśca mānada ,sametya samare śatrūnvadhiṣyasi na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O giver of honor, having rested, being awake, and with a composed mind, you will kill the enemies in battle; there is no doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. O giver of honor, having rested, being awake, and with a composed mind, you will kill the enemies in battle; there is no doubt.

sanskrit: न हि त्वा रथिनां श्रेष्ठ प्रगृहीतवरायुधम् ।जेतुमुत्सहते कश्चिदपि देवेषु पावकिः ॥७॥
transliteration: na hi tvā rathināṁ śreṣṭha pragṛhītavarāyudham ,jetumutsahate kaścidapi deveṣu pāvakiḥ.
english: Indeed, O best among charioteers, no one, not even among the gods, nor even Pāvaki (Kartikeya), is able to conquer you, who have grasped excellent weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Indeed, O best among charioteers, no one, not even among the gods, nor even Pāvaki (Kartikeya), is able to conquer you, who have grasped excellent weapons.

sanskrit: कृपेण सहितं यान्तं युक्तं च कृतवर्मणा ।को द्रौणिं युधि संरब्धं योधयेदपि देवराट् ॥८॥
transliteration: kṛpeṇa sahitaṁ yāntaṁ yuktaṁ ca kṛtavarmaṇā ,ko drauṇiṁ yudhi saṁrabdhaṁ yodhayedapi devarāṭ.
english: Who, even among the gods, would fight Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), enraged in battle, when he is accompanied by Kṛpa and joined by Kṛtavarman?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Who, even among the gods, would fight Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), enraged in battle, when he is accompanied by Kṛpa and joined by Kṛtavarman?

sanskrit: ते वयं परिविश्रान्ता विनिद्रा विगतज्वराः ।प्रभातायां रजन्यां वै निहनिष्याम शात्रवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ pariviśrāntā vinidrā vigatajvarāḥ ,prabhātāyāṁ rajanyāṁ vai nihaniṣyāma śātravān.
english: Once fully rested, free from sleepiness and anxiety, we will indeed destroy the enemies when dawn breaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. Once fully rested, free from sleepiness and anxiety, we will indeed destroy the enemies when dawn breaks.

sanskrit: तव ह्यस्त्राणि दिव्यानि मम चैव न संशयः ।सात्वतोऽपि महेष्वासो नित्यं युद्धेषु कोविदः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tava hyastrāṇi divyāni mama caiva na saṁśayaḥ ,sātvato'pi maheṣvāso nityaṁ yuddheṣu kovidaḥ.
english: Indeed, there is no doubt about your divine weapons and mine. Sātvata (Kṛtavarman) is also a great archer, always proficient in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Indeed, there is no doubt about your divine weapons and mine. Sātvata (Kṛtavarman) is also a great archer, always proficient in battles.

sanskrit: ते वयं सहितास्तात सर्वाञ्शत्रून्समागतान् ।प्रसह्य समरे हत्वा प्रीतिं प्राप्स्याम पुष्कलाम् ।विश्रमस्व त्वमव्यग्रः स्वप चेमां निशां सुखम् ॥११॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ sahitāstāta sarvāñśatrūnsamāgatān ,prasahya samare hatvā prītiṁ prāpsyāma puṣkalām ,viśramasva tvamavyagraḥ svapa cemāṁ niśāṁ sukham.
english: Dear father, we will attain immense joy after forcibly defeating and slaying all the gathered enemies in battle. As for you, remain undisturbed, rest, and sleep peacefully this night.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Dear father, we will attain immense joy after forcibly defeating and slaying all the gathered enemies in battle. As for you, remain undisturbed, rest, and sleep peacefully this night.

sanskrit: अहं च कृतवर्मा च प्रयान्तं त्वां नरोत्तम ।अनुयास्याव सहितौ धन्विनौ परतापिनौ ।रथिनं त्वरया यान्तं रथावास्थाय दंशितौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: ahaṁ ca kṛtavarmā ca prayāntaṁ tvāṁ narottama ,anuyāsyāva sahitau dhanvinau paratāpinau ,rathinaṁ tvarayā yāntaṁ rathāvāsthāya daṁśitau.
english: O best among men, Kṛtavarmā and I, both armored, carrying bows, and capable of tormenting enemies, will follow you together. We will mount our chariots and accompany you as you, a charioteer, swiftly proceed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. O best among men, Kṛtavarmā and I, both armored, carrying bows, and capable of tormenting enemies, will follow you together. We will mount our chariots and accompany you as you, a charioteer, swiftly proceed.

sanskrit: स गत्वा शिबिरं तेषां नाम विश्राव्य चाहवे ।ततः कर्तासि शत्रूणां युध्यतां कदनं महत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa gatvā śibiraṁ teṣāṁ nāma viśrāvya cāhave ,tataḥ kartāsi śatrūṇāṁ yudhyatāṁ kadanaṁ mahat.
english: Once you have gone to their camp and proclaimed your name, both there and in battle, you will then bring about a great slaughter of the fighting enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Once you have gone to their camp and proclaimed your name, both there and in battle, you will then bring about a great slaughter of the fighting enemies.

sanskrit: कृत्वा च कदनं तेषां प्रभाते विमलेऽहनि ।विहरस्व यथा शक्रः सूदयित्वा महासुरान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: kṛtvā ca kadanaṁ teṣāṁ prabhāte vimale'hani ,viharasva yathā śakraḥ sūdayitvā mahāsurān.
english: And after you have inflicted a slaughter upon them on a clear morning, you may revel, just as Śakra (Indra) rejoices after destroying the great asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. And after you have inflicted a slaughter upon them on a clear morning, you may revel, just as Śakra (Indra) rejoices after destroying the great asuras.

sanskrit: त्वं हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं पाञ्चालानां वरूथिनीम् ।दैत्यसेनामिव क्रुद्धः सर्वदानवसूदनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ pāñcālānāṁ varūthinīm ,daityasenāmiva kruddhaḥ sarvadānavasūdanaḥ.
english: Indeed, you are capable of conquering the Pañcāla army in battle, just as you, enraged and the destroyer of all Dānavas (demons), would conquer the Daitya army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. Indeed, you are capable of conquering the Pañcāla army in battle, just as you, enraged and the destroyer of all Dānavas (demons), would conquer the Daitya army.

sanskrit: मया त्वां सहितं संख्ये गुप्तं च कृतवर्मणा ।न सहेत विभुः साक्षाद्वज्रपाणिरपि स्वयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: mayā tvāṁ sahitaṁ saṁkhye guptaṁ ca kṛtavarmaṇā ,na saheta vibhuḥ sākṣādvajrapāṇirapi svayam.
english: Even the mighty Indra (Vajrapāṇi) himself, appearing in person, would not be able to withstand you, accompanied and protected by me and Kṛtavarman, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. Even the mighty Indra (Vajrapāṇi) himself, appearing in person, would not be able to withstand you, accompanied and protected by me and Kṛtavarman, in battle.

sanskrit: न चाहं समरे तात कृतवर्मा तथैव च ।अनिर्जित्य रणे पाण्डून्व्यपयास्याव कर्हिचित् ॥१७॥
transliteration: na cāhaṁ samare tāta kṛtavarmā tathaiva ca ,anirjitya raṇe pāṇḍūnvyapayāsyāva karhicit.
english: Dear one, neither I nor Kṛtavarman will ever withdraw from battle without first conquering the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. Dear one, neither I nor Kṛtavarman will ever withdraw from battle without first conquering the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: हत्वा च समरे क्षुद्रान्पाञ्चालान्पाण्डुभिः सह ।निवर्तिष्यामहे सर्वे हता वा स्वर्गगा वयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: hatvā ca samare kṣudrānpāñcālānpāṇḍubhiḥ saha ,nivartiṣyāmahe sarve hatā vā svargagā vayam.
english: Having slain the insignificant Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas in battle, all of us shall return. Or, if we are killed, we shall attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. Having slain the insignificant Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas in battle, all of us shall return. Or, if we are killed, we shall attain heaven.

sanskrit: सर्वोपायैः सहायास्ते प्रभाते वयमेव हि ।सत्यमेतन्महाबाहो प्रब्रवीमि तवानघ ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarvopāyaiḥ sahāyāste prabhāte vayameva hi ,satyametanmahābāho prabravīmi tavānagha.
english: O mighty-armed and sinless one, I truly declare to you that we ourselves will be your helpers by all means tomorrow morning.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. O mighty-armed and sinless one, I truly declare to you that we ourselves will be your helpers by all means tomorrow morning.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततो द्रौणिर्मातुलेन हितं वचः ।अब्रवीन्मातुलं राजन्क्रोधादुद्वृत्य लोचने ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamuktastato drauṇirmātulena hitaṁ vacaḥ ,abravīnmātulaṁ rājankrodhādudvṛtya locane.
english: O King, thus addressed with beneficial words by his maternal uncle, Drauṇi, rolling his eyes in anger, spoke to his maternal uncle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. O King, thus addressed with beneficial words by his maternal uncle, Drauṇi, rolling his eyes in anger, spoke to his maternal uncle.

sanskrit: आतुरस्य कुतो निद्रा नरस्यामर्षितस्य च ।अर्थांश्चिन्तयतश्चापि कामयानस्य वा पुनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: āturasya kuto nidrā narasyāmarṣitasya ca ,arthāṁścintayataścāpi kāmayānasya vā punaḥ.
english: From where can sleep come for a distressed person, or for an enraged man? And also, how can it come for one who is constantly contemplating his goals, or for one who is yearning for something?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. From where can sleep come for a distressed person, or for an enraged man? And also, how can it come for one who is constantly contemplating his goals, or for one who is yearning for something?

sanskrit: तदिदं समनुप्राप्तं पश्य मेऽद्य चतुष्टयम् ।यस्य भागश्चतुर्थो मे स्वप्नमह्नाय नाशयेत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ samanuprāptaṁ paśya me'dya catuṣṭayam ,yasya bhāgaścaturtho me svapnamahnāya nāśayet.
english: Behold, this set of four (things) has befallen me today, the fourth component of which is enough to quickly destroy my sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. Behold, this set of four (things) has befallen me today, the fourth component of which is enough to quickly destroy my sleep.

sanskrit: किं नाम दुःखं लोकेऽस्मिन्पितुर्वधमनुस्मरन् ।हृदयं निर्दहन्मेऽद्य रात्र्यहानि न शाम्यति ॥२३॥
transliteration: kiṁ nāma duḥkhaṁ loke'sminpiturvadhamanusmaran ,hṛdayaṁ nirdahanme'dya rātryahāni na śāmyati.
english: What suffering in this world can compare to remembering my father's murder? Burning my heart, it does not subside day or night.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. What suffering in this world can compare to remembering my father's murder? Burning my heart, it does not subside day or night.

sanskrit: यथा च निहतः पापैः पिता मम विशेषतः ।प्रत्यक्षमपि ते सर्वं तन्मे मर्माणि कृन्तति ॥२४॥
transliteration: yathā ca nihataḥ pāpaiḥ pitā mama viśeṣataḥ ,pratyakṣamapi te sarvaṁ tanme marmāṇi kṛntati.
english: And how my father was especially killed by the sinful, even though all of that is directly visible to you, it pierces my very vitals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. And how my father was especially killed by the sinful, even though all of that is directly visible to you, it pierces my very vitals.

sanskrit: कथं हि मादृशो लोके मुहूर्तमपि जीवति ।द्रोणो हतेति यद्वाचः पाञ्चालानां शृणोम्यहम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hi mādṛśo loke muhūrtamapi jīvati ,droṇo hateti yadvācaḥ pāñcālānāṁ śṛṇomyaham.
english: Indeed, how can one like me live even for a moment in this world, when I hear the words of the Pāñcālas proclaiming, 'Droṇa is killed'?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. Indeed, how can one like me live even for a moment in this world, when I hear the words of the Pāñcālas proclaiming, 'Droṇa is killed'?

sanskrit: दृष्टद्युम्नमहत्वाजौ नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ।स मे पितृवधाद्वध्यः पाञ्चाला ये च संगताः ॥२६॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭadyumnamahatvājau nāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe ,sa me pitṛvadhādvadhyaḥ pāñcālā ye ca saṁgatāḥ.
english: I have no desire to live without killing Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle. He is certainly worthy of being killed by me because he caused my father's death, and so are all those Pāñcālas who have allied with him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. I have no desire to live without killing Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle. He is certainly worthy of being killed by me because he caused my father's death, and so are all those Pāñcālas who have allied with him.

sanskrit: विलापो भग्नसक्थस्य यस्तु राज्ञो मया श्रुतः ।स पुनर्हृदयं कस्य क्रूरस्यापि न निर्दहेत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vilāpo bhagnasakthasya yastu rājño mayā śrutaḥ ,sa punarhṛdayaṁ kasya krūrasyāpi na nirdahet.
english: But the lamentation of the king with broken thighs, which I heard, whose heart, even of a cruel person, would it not burn anew?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. But the lamentation of the king with broken thighs, which I heard, whose heart, even of a cruel person, would it not burn anew?

sanskrit: कस्य ह्यकरुणस्यापि नेत्राभ्यामश्रु नाव्रजेत् ।नृपतेर्भग्नसक्थस्य श्रुत्वा तादृग्वचः पुनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: kasya hyakaruṇasyāpi netrābhyāmaśru nāvrajet ,nṛpaterbhagnasakthasya śrutvā tādṛgvacaḥ punaḥ.
english: Indeed, whose eyes, even those of a heartless person, would not shed tears upon hearing such words again from the king whose thighs were shattered?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. Indeed, whose eyes, even those of a heartless person, would not shed tears upon hearing such words again from the king whose thighs were shattered?

sanskrit: यश्चायं मित्रपक्षो मे मयि जीवति निर्जितः ।शोकं मे वर्धयत्येष वारिवेग इवार्णवम् ।एकाग्रमनसो मेऽद्य कुतो निद्रा कुतः सुखम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: yaścāyaṁ mitrapakṣo me mayi jīvati nirjitaḥ ,śokaṁ me vardhayatyeṣa vārivega ivārṇavam ,ekāgramanaso me'dya kuto nidrā kutaḥ sukham.
english: And this faction of my friends, defeated while I am still alive, only increases my sorrow, just as a surge of water swells the ocean. With my mind focused today, how can there be sleep for me? How can there be happiness?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. And this faction of my friends, defeated while I am still alive, only increases my sorrow, just as a surge of water swells the ocean. With my mind focused today, how can there be sleep for me? How can there be happiness?

sanskrit: वासुदेवार्जुनाभ्यां हि तानहं परिरक्षितान् ।अविषह्यतमान्मन्ये महेन्द्रेणापि मातुल ॥३०॥
transliteration: vāsudevārjunābhyāṁ hi tānahaṁ parirakṣitān ,aviṣahyatamānmanye mahendreṇāpi mātula.
english: Indeed, those protected by Vāsudeva and Arjuna, I consider them to be utterly invincible, O uncle, even by Mahendra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. Indeed, those protected by Vāsudeva and Arjuna, I consider them to be utterly invincible, O uncle, even by Mahendra (Indra).

sanskrit: न चास्मि शक्यः संयन्तुमस्मात्कार्यात्कथंचन ।न तं पश्यामि लोकेऽस्मिन्यो मां कार्यान्निवर्तयेत् ।इति मे निश्चिता बुद्धिरेषा साधुमता च मे ॥३१॥
transliteration: na cāsmi śakyaḥ saṁyantumasmātkāryātkathaṁcana ,na taṁ paśyāmi loke'sminyo māṁ kāryānnivartayet ,iti me niścitā buddhireṣā sādhumatā ca me.
english: And I cannot possibly be restrained from this task in any way. I do not see anyone in this world who could divert me from my duty (dharma). This is my firm resolve, and it is also deemed proper by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. And I cannot possibly be restrained from this task in any way. I do not see anyone in this world who could divert me from my duty (dharma). This is my firm resolve, and it is also deemed proper by me.

sanskrit: वार्त्तिकैः कथ्यमानस्तु मित्राणां मे पराभवः ।पाण्डवानां च विजयो हृदयं दहतीव मे ॥३२॥
transliteration: vārttikaiḥ kathyamānastu mitrāṇāṁ me parābhavaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ ca vijayo hṛdayaṁ dahatīva me.
english: Indeed, the news reported by messengers of my friends' defeat and the Pāṇḍavas' victory, burns my heart as if with fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. Indeed, the news reported by messengers of my friends' defeat and the Pāṇḍavas' victory, burns my heart as if with fire.

sanskrit: अहं तु कदनं कृत्वा शत्रूणामद्य सौप्तिके ।ततो विश्रमिता चैव स्वप्ता च विगतज्वरः ॥३३॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tu kadanaṁ kṛtvā śatrūṇāmadya sauptike ,tato viśramitā caiva svaptā ca vigatajvaraḥ.
english: Indeed, having accomplished the slaughter of the enemies tonight in the night attack, I shall then be rested and shall sleep, free from anxiety.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. Indeed, having accomplished the slaughter of the enemies tonight in the night attack, I shall then be rested and shall sleep, free from anxiety.

sanskrit: कृप उवाच ।शुश्रूषुरपि दुर्मेधाः पुरुषोऽनियतेन्द्रियः ।नालं वेदयितुं कृत्स्नौ धर्मार्थाविति मे मतिः ॥१॥
transliteration: kṛpa uvāca ,śuśrūṣurapi durmedhāḥ puruṣo'niyatendriyaḥ ,nālaṁ vedayituṁ kṛtsnau dharmārthāviti me matiḥ.
english: Kṛpa said: It is my opinion that even a person desirous of learning, who is dull-witted and whose senses are uncontrolled, is not capable of understanding the entirety of both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Kṛpa said: It is my opinion that even a person desirous of learning, who is dull-witted and whose senses are uncontrolled, is not capable of understanding the entirety of both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: तथैव तावन्मेधावी विनयं यो न शिक्षति ।न च किंचन जानाति सोऽपि धर्मार्थनिश्चयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tathaiva tāvanmedhāvī vinayaṁ yo na śikṣati ,na ca kiṁcana jānāti so'pi dharmārthaniścayam.
english: Similarly, even an intelligent person who does not learn discipline and understands nothing whatsoever, he too cannot ascertain the true nature of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Similarly, even an intelligent person who does not learn discipline and understands nothing whatsoever, he too cannot ascertain the true nature of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: शुश्रूषुस्त्वेव मेधावी पुरुषो नियतेन्द्रियः ।जानीयादागमान्सर्वान्ग्राह्यं च न विरोधयेत् ॥३॥
transliteration: śuśrūṣustveva medhāvī puruṣo niyatendriyaḥ ,jānīyādāgamānsarvāngrāhyaṁ ca na virodhayet.
english: However, an intelligent person (puruṣa) who is eager to learn and has controlled their senses should understand all sacred traditions (āgama) and should not contradict what is acceptable.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. However, an intelligent person (puruṣa) who is eager to learn and has controlled their senses should understand all sacred traditions (āgama) and should not contradict what is acceptable.

sanskrit: अनेयस्त्ववमानी यो दुरात्मा पापपूरुषः ।दिष्टमुत्सृज्य कल्याणं करोति बहुपापकम् ॥४॥
transliteration: aneyastvavamānī yo durātmā pāpapūruṣaḥ ,diṣṭamutsṛjya kalyāṇaṁ karoti bahupāpakam.
english: But a dishonorable (avamanin), evil-minded, sinful person (puruṣa) who is not to be led [by good advice], abandoning what is destined to be auspicious, commits many sinful acts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. But a dishonorable (avamanin), evil-minded, sinful person (puruṣa) who is not to be led [by good advice], abandoning what is destined to be auspicious, commits many sinful acts.

sanskrit: नाथवन्तं तु सुहृदः प्रतिषेधन्ति पातकात् ।निवर्तते तु लक्ष्मीवान्नालक्ष्मीवान्निवर्तते ॥५॥
transliteration: nāthavantaṁ tu suhṛdaḥ pratiṣedhanti pātakāt ,nivartate tu lakṣmīvānnālakṣmīvānnivartate.
english: Indeed, friends protect one who has a protector from sin. But the fortunate person (lakṣmīvant) turns away (from sin), while the unfortunate person does not turn away.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Indeed, friends protect one who has a protector from sin. But the fortunate person (lakṣmīvant) turns away (from sin), while the unfortunate person does not turn away.

sanskrit: यथा ह्युच्चावचैर्वाक्यैः क्षिप्तचित्तो नियम्यते ।तथैव सुहृदा शक्यो नशक्यस्त्ववसीदति ॥६॥
transliteration: yathā hyuccāvacairvākyaiḥ kṣiptacitto niyamyate ,tathaiva suhṛdā śakyo naśakyastvavasīdati.
english: Just as a distraught person (kṣiptacitta) is restrained by various words, in the same way, one who is capable [of being restrained] by a friend can be disciplined; but one who is not capable [of being restrained], however, succumbs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Just as a distraught person (kṣiptacitta) is restrained by various words, in the same way, one who is capable [of being restrained] by a friend can be disciplined; but one who is not capable [of being restrained], however, succumbs.

sanskrit: तथैव सुहृदं प्राज्ञं कुर्वाणं कर्म पापकम् ।प्राज्ञाः संप्रतिषेधन्ते यथाशक्ति पुनः पुनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tathaiva suhṛdaṁ prājñaṁ kurvāṇaṁ karma pāpakam ,prājñāḥ saṁpratiṣedhante yathāśakti punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Similarly, wise people repeatedly restrain a wise friend who is performing a sinful act, doing so to the best of their ability.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Similarly, wise people repeatedly restrain a wise friend who is performing a sinful act, doing so to the best of their ability.

sanskrit: स कल्याणे मतिं कृत्वा नियम्यात्मानमात्मना ।कुरु मे वचनं तात येन पश्चान्न तप्यसे ॥८॥
transliteration: sa kalyāṇe matiṁ kṛtvā niyamyātmānamātmanā ,kuru me vacanaṁ tāta yena paścānna tapyase.
english: Having focused his mind on what is beneficial and having controlled his own self (ātman) by his self (ātman), O dear one, obey my words so that you will not suffer later.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. Having focused his mind on what is beneficial and having controlled his own self (ātman) by his self (ātman), O dear one, obey my words so that you will not suffer later.

sanskrit: न वधः पूज्यते लोके सुप्तानामिह धर्मतः ।तथैव न्यस्तशस्त्राणां विमुक्तरथवाजिनाम् ॥९॥
transliteration: na vadhaḥ pūjyate loke suptānāmiha dharmataḥ ,tathaiva nyastaśastrāṇāṁ vimuktarathavājinām.
english: Killing those who are sleeping is not considered proper in this world according to natural law (dharma). Similarly, it is not proper to kill those who have laid down their weapons or those who have abandoned their chariots and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. Killing those who are sleeping is not considered proper in this world according to natural law (dharma). Similarly, it is not proper to kill those who have laid down their weapons or those who have abandoned their chariots and horses.

sanskrit: ये च ब्रूयुस्तवास्मीति ये च स्युः शरणागताः ।विमुक्तमूर्धजा ये च ये चापि हतवाहनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ye ca brūyustavāsmīti ye ca syuḥ śaraṇāgatāḥ ,vimuktamūrdhajā ye ca ye cāpi hatavāhanāḥ.
english: Also, (it is not proper to kill) those who declare, 'I am yours,' and those who have sought refuge, as well as those with disheveled hair, and those whose conveyances have been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. Also, (it is not proper to kill) those who declare, 'I am yours,' and those who have sought refuge, as well as those with disheveled hair, and those whose conveyances have been destroyed.

sanskrit: अद्य स्वप्स्यन्ति पाञ्चाला विमुक्तकवचा विभो ।विश्वस्ता रजनीं सर्वे प्रेता इव विचेतसः ॥११॥
transliteration: adya svapsyanti pāñcālā vimuktakavacā vibho ,viśvastā rajanīṁ sarve pretā iva vicetasaḥ.
english: O lord, today all the Pāñcālas, having removed their armor and feeling secure, will sleep through the night like departed spirits (pretāḥ), completely unaware.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. O lord, today all the Pāñcālas, having removed their armor and feeling secure, will sleep through the night like departed spirits (pretāḥ), completely unaware.

sanskrit: यस्तेषां तदवस्थानां द्रुह्येत पुरुषोऽनृजुः ।व्यक्तं स नरके मज्जेदगाधे विपुलेऽप्लवे ॥१२॥
transliteration: yasteṣāṁ tadavasthānāṁ druhyeta puruṣo'nṛjuḥ ,vyaktaṁ sa narake majjedagādhe vipule'plave.
english: Whoever, being a dishonest (anṛju) person (puruṣa), would harm them while they are in such a state, he would surely sink into a hell (naraka) that is unfathomable, vast, and without any means of support.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Whoever, being a dishonest (anṛju) person (puruṣa), would harm them while they are in such a state, he would surely sink into a hell (naraka) that is unfathomable, vast, and without any means of support.

sanskrit: सर्वास्त्रविदुषां लोके श्रेष्ठस्त्वमसि विश्रुतः ।न च ते जातु लोकेऽस्मिन्सुसूक्ष्ममपि किल्बिषम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sarvāstraviduṣāṁ loke śreṣṭhastvamasi viśrutaḥ ,na ca te jātu loke'sminsusūkṣmamapi kilbiṣam.
english: You are renowned as the foremost in the world among those who know all weapons. And never in this world is there even the slightest fault in you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. You are renowned as the foremost in the world among those who know all weapons. And never in this world is there even the slightest fault in you.

sanskrit: त्वं पुनः सूर्यसंकाशः श्वोभूत उदिते रवौ ।प्रकाशे सर्वभूतानां विजेता युधि शात्रवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tvaṁ punaḥ sūryasaṁkāśaḥ śvobhūta udite ravau ,prakāśe sarvabhūtānāṁ vijetā yudhi śātravān.
english: But you, shining like the sun, will be the conqueror of enemies in battle when the sun rises tomorrow, visible to all beings (sarvabhūtānām).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. But you, shining like the sun, will be the conqueror of enemies in battle when the sun rises tomorrow, visible to all beings (sarvabhūtānām).

sanskrit: असंभावितरूपं हि त्वयि कर्म विगर्हितम् ।शुक्ले रक्तमिव न्यस्तं भवेदिति मतिर्मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: asaṁbhāvitarūpaṁ hi tvayi karma vigarhitam ,śukle raktamiva nyastaṁ bhavediti matirmama.
english: Indeed, an action of such an unthinkable and blameworthy nature coming from you would be like placing a red stain on a white cloth. This is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. Indeed, an action of such an unthinkable and blameworthy nature coming from you would be like placing a red stain on a white cloth. This is my opinion.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामोवाच ।एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमनुशास्मीह मातुल ।तैस्तु पूर्वमयं सेतुः शतधा विदलीकृतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmovāca ,evametadyathāttha tvamanuśāsmīha mātula ,taistu pūrvamayaṁ setuḥ śatadhā vidalīkṛtaḥ.
english: Ashvatthama said: 'This is indeed as you have stated, O uncle (mātula). I declare this here: But this moral boundary (setu) was shattered a hundredfold by them long ago.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. Ashvatthama said: 'This is indeed as you have stated, O uncle (mātula). I declare this here: But this moral boundary (setu) was shattered a hundredfold by them long ago.'

sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षं भूमिपालानां भवतां चापि संनिधौ ।न्यस्तशस्त्रो मम पिता धृष्टद्युम्नेन पातितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pratyakṣaṁ bhūmipālānāṁ bhavatāṁ cāpi saṁnidhau ,nyastaśastro mama pitā dhṛṣṭadyumnena pātitaḥ.
english: My father, who had laid down his weapons, was slain by Dhristadyumna right before the eyes of the kings and also in your presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. My father, who had laid down his weapons, was slain by Dhristadyumna right before the eyes of the kings and also in your presence.

sanskrit: कर्णश्च पतिते चक्रे रथस्य रथिनां वरः ।उत्तमे व्यसने सन्नो हतो गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥१८॥
transliteration: karṇaśca patite cakre rathasya rathināṁ varaḥ ,uttame vyasane sanno hato gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: And Karna, the foremost among charioteers, was slain by the wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow (Arjuna) when his chariot's wheel had fallen, while he was overwhelmed by supreme misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. And Karna, the foremost among charioteers, was slain by the wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow (Arjuna) when his chariot's wheel had fallen, while he was overwhelmed by supreme misfortune.

sanskrit: तथा शांतनवो भीष्मो न्यस्तशस्त्रो निरायुधः ।शिखण्डिनं पुरस्कृत्य हतो गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥१९॥
transliteration: tathā śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo nyastaśastro nirāyudhaḥ ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ puraskṛtya hato gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: Similarly, Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, having laid down his weapons and being weaponless, was killed by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, who had placed Śikhaṇḍin in front.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. Similarly, Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, having laid down his weapons and being weaponless, was killed by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, who had placed Śikhaṇḍin in front.

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवा महेष्वासस्तथा प्रायगतो रणे ।क्रोशतां भूमिपालानां युयुधानेन पातितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhūriśravā maheṣvāsastathā prāyagato raṇe ,krośatāṁ bhūmipālānāṁ yuyudhānena pātitaḥ.
english: Similarly, Bhūriśravas, a great archer, who was observing a fast unto death (prāyagata) on the battlefield, was struck down by Yuyudhāna (Satyaki) while the kings were crying out in protest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. Similarly, Bhūriśravas, a great archer, who was observing a fast unto death (prāyagata) on the battlefield, was struck down by Yuyudhāna (Satyaki) while the kings were crying out in protest.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनश्च भीमेन समेत्य गदया मृधे ।पश्यतां भूमिपालानामधर्मेण निपातितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: duryodhanaśca bhīmena sametya gadayā mṛdhe ,paśyatāṁ bhūmipālānāmadharmeṇa nipātitaḥ.
english: And Duryodhana, having confronted Bhīma in battle, was struck down by his mace, unlawfully (adharmeṇa), as the kings watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. And Duryodhana, having confronted Bhīma in battle, was struck down by his mace, unlawfully (adharmeṇa), as the kings watched.

sanskrit: एकाकी बहुभिस्तत्र परिवार्य महारथैः ।अधर्मेण नरव्याघ्रो भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ekākī bahubhistatra parivārya mahārathaiḥ ,adharmeṇa naravyāghro bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: There, that tiger among men (Duryodhana), being alone and surrounded by many great charioteers, was unlawfully (adharmeṇa) struck down by Bhīmasena (Bhīma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. There, that tiger among men (Duryodhana), being alone and surrounded by many great charioteers, was unlawfully (adharmeṇa) struck down by Bhīmasena (Bhīma).

sanskrit: विलापो भग्नसक्थस्य यो मे राज्ञः परिश्रुतः ।वार्त्तिकानां कथयतां स मे मर्माणि कृन्तति ॥२३॥
transliteration: vilāpo bhagnasakthasya yo me rājñaḥ pariśrutaḥ ,vārttikānāṁ kathayatāṁ sa me marmāṇi kṛntati.
english: The lamentation of the king whose thighs were broken, which I heard from those who recounted the news, that indeed pierces my vital spots.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. The lamentation of the king whose thighs were broken, which I heard from those who recounted the news, that indeed pierces my vital spots.

sanskrit: एवमधार्मिकाः पापाः पाञ्चाला भिन्नसेतवः ।तानेवं भिन्नमर्यादान्किं भवान्न विगर्हति ॥२४॥
transliteration: evamadhārmikāḥ pāpāḥ pāñcālā bhinnasetavaḥ ,tānevaṁ bhinnamaryādānkiṁ bhavānna vigarhati.
english: Why do you not censure these Pāñcālas, who are thus unrighteous (adhārmikāḥ) and wicked, having broken all bounds and transgressed all limits?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. Why do you not censure these Pāñcālas, who are thus unrighteous (adhārmikāḥ) and wicked, having broken all bounds and transgressed all limits?

sanskrit: पितृहन्तॄनहं हत्वा पाञ्चालान्निशि सौप्तिके ।कामं कीटः पतंगो वा जन्म प्राप्य भवामि वै ॥२५॥
transliteration: pitṛhantṝnahaṁ hatvā pāñcālānniśi sauptike ,kāmaṁ kīṭaḥ pataṁgo vā janma prāpya bhavāmi vai.
english: After killing the Pāñcālas, who were my father's murderers, during the night attack (sauptike), let me indeed be born as an insect or a moth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. After killing the Pāñcālas, who were my father's murderers, during the night attack (sauptike), let me indeed be born as an insect or a moth.

sanskrit: त्वरे चाहमनेनाद्य यदिदं मे चिकीर्षितम् ।तस्य मे त्वरमाणस्य कुतो निद्रा कुतः सुखम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tvare cāhamanenādya yadidaṁ me cikīrṣitam ,tasya me tvaramāṇasya kuto nidrā kutaḥ sukham.
english: I am eager right now to accomplish this task that I intend. For me, who is so intent on hastening, how can there be any sleep? How can there be any happiness?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. I am eager right now to accomplish this task that I intend. For me, who is so intent on hastening, how can there be any sleep? How can there be any happiness?

sanskrit: न स जातः पुमाँल्लोके कश्चिन्न च भविष्यति ।यो मे व्यावर्तयेदेतां वधे तेषां कृतां मतिम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: na sa jātaḥ pumāँlloke kaścinna ca bhaviṣyati ,yo me vyāvartayedetāṁ vadhe teṣāṁ kṛtāṁ matim.
english: There is no man born in this world, nor will there be anyone, who could turn away this resolve of mine, which is fixed upon killing them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. There is no man born in this world, nor will there be anyone, who could turn away this resolve of mine, which is fixed upon killing them.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा महाराज द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।एकान्ते योजयित्वाश्वान्प्रायादभिमुखः परान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamuktvā mahārāja droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,ekānte yojayitvāśvānprāyādabhimukhaḥ parān.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O great king, having thus spoken, Drona's powerful son (Aśvatthāman), having harnessed the horses aside, departed, facing the enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 28. Saṃjaya said: "O great king, having thus spoken, Drona's powerful son (Aśvatthāman), having harnessed the horses aside, departed, facing the enemies."

sanskrit: तमब्रूतां महात्मानौ भोजशारद्वतावुभौ ।किमयं स्यन्दनो युक्तः किं च कार्यं चिकीर्षितम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamabrūtāṁ mahātmānau bhojaśāradvatāvubhau ,kimayaṁ syandano yuktaḥ kiṁ ca kāryaṁ cikīrṣitam.
english: The two great souls, Bhoja (Kṛpa) and Śāradvata (Kṛtavarman), both spoke to him: "Why is this chariot harnessed? And what action is intended to be done?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 29. The two great souls, Bhoja (Kṛpa) and Śāradvata (Kṛtavarman), both spoke to him: "Why is this chariot harnessed? And what action is intended to be done?"

sanskrit: एकसार्थं प्रयातौ स्वस्त्वया सह नरर्षभ ।समदुःखसुखौ चैव नावां शङ्कितुमर्हसि ॥३०॥
transliteration: ekasārthaṁ prayātau svastvayā saha nararṣabha ,samaduḥkhasukhau caiva nāvāṁ śaṅkitumarhasi.
english: "O best of men, we two have set out with a common purpose, along with you. And indeed, you ought not to doubt us, who share equally in pain and pleasure."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 30. "O best of men, we two have set out with a common purpose, along with you. And indeed, you ought not to doubt us, who share equally in pain and pleasure."

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु संक्रुद्धः पितुर्वधमनुस्मरन् ।ताभ्यां तथ्यं तदाचख्यौ यदस्यात्मचिकीर्षितम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu saṁkruddhaḥ piturvadhamanusmaran ,tābhyāṁ tathyaṁ tadācakhyau yadasyātmacikīrṣitam.
english: Ashwatthama, greatly enraged and remembering his father's killing, then revealed to them his own intention.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 31. Ashwatthama, greatly enraged and remembering his father's killing, then revealed to them his own intention.

sanskrit: हत्वा शतसहस्राणि योधानां निशितैः शरैः ।न्यस्तशस्त्रो मम पिता धृष्टद्युम्नेन पातितः ॥३२॥
transliteration: hatvā śatasahasrāṇi yodhānāṁ niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,nyastaśastro mama pitā dhṛṣṭadyumnena pātitaḥ.
english: My father, after killing hundreds of thousands of warriors with sharp arrows, was then struck down by Dhristadyumna while he had laid aside his weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 32. My father, after killing hundreds of thousands of warriors with sharp arrows, was then struck down by Dhristadyumna while he had laid aside his weapons.

sanskrit: तं तथैव हनिष्यामि न्यस्तवर्माणमद्य वै ।पुत्रं पाञ्चालराजस्य पापं पापेन कर्मणा ॥३३॥
transliteration: taṁ tathaiva haniṣyāmi nyastavarmāṇamadya vai ,putraṁ pāñcālarājasya pāpaṁ pāpena karmaṇā.
english: Today, I will certainly kill him, that wicked son of the Pañcāla king, who has cast off his armor, with a wicked deed (karma), just as he killed my father.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 33. Today, I will certainly kill him, that wicked son of the Pañcāla king, who has cast off his armor, with a wicked deed (karma), just as he killed my father.

sanskrit: कथं च निहतः पापः पाञ्चालः पशुवन्मया ।शस्त्राहवजितां लोकान्प्राप्नुयादिति मे मतिः ॥३४॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca nihataḥ pāpaḥ pāñcālaḥ paśuvanmayā ,śastrāhavajitāṁ lokānprāpnuyāditi me matiḥ.
english: And if that wicked Pañcāla is killed by me like an animal, how could he attain the worlds earned by those who die in battle (śastra-āhava)? This is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 34. And if that wicked Pañcāla is killed by me like an animal, how could he attain the worlds earned by those who die in battle (śastra-āhava)? This is my opinion.

sanskrit: क्षिप्रं संनद्धकवचौ सखड्गावात्तकार्मुकौ ।समास्थाय प्रतीक्षेतां रथवर्यौ परंतपौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: kṣipraṁ saṁnaddhakavacau sakhaḍgāvāttakārmukau ,samāsthāya pratīkṣetāṁ rathavaryau paraṁtapau.
english: Quickly, those two tormentors of foes, having donned their armor, grasped their swords, and taken up their bows, should mount their excellent chariots and wait.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 35. Quickly, those two tormentors of foes, having donned their armor, grasped their swords, and taken up their bows, should mount their excellent chariots and wait.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा रथमास्थाय प्रायादभिमुखः परान् ।तमन्वगात्कृपो राजन्कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā rathamāsthāya prāyādabhimukhaḥ parān ,tamanvagātkṛpo rājankṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, he mounted his chariot and advanced, facing the enemies. Kṛpa, O King, and Kṛtavarmā, a Sātvata, followed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 36. Having spoken thus, he mounted his chariot and advanced, facing the enemies. Kṛpa, O King, and Kṛtavarmā, a Sātvata, followed him.

sanskrit: ते प्रयाता व्यरोचन्त परानभिमुखास्त्रयः ।हूयमाना यथा यज्ञे समिद्धा हव्यवाहनाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: te prayātā vyarocanta parānabhimukhāstrayaḥ ,hūyamānā yathā yajñe samiddhā havyavāhanāḥ.
english: The three, having advanced and facing their enemies, shone brilliantly, just like kindled fires receiving offerings in a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 37. The three, having advanced and facing their enemies, shone brilliantly, just like kindled fires receiving offerings in a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ययुश्च शिबिरं तेषां संप्रसुप्तजनं विभो ।द्वारदेशं तु संप्राप्य द्रौणिस्तस्थौ रथोत्तमे ॥३८॥
transliteration: yayuśca śibiraṁ teṣāṁ saṁprasuptajanaṁ vibho ,dvāradeśaṁ tu saṁprāpya drauṇistasthau rathottame.
english: And, O lord, they approached their camp, where the people were deeply asleep. But Drauṇi, having reached the entrance, stood in his excellent chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 38. And, O lord, they approached their camp, where the people were deeply asleep. But Drauṇi, having reached the entrance, stood in his excellent chariot.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।द्वारदेशे ततो द्रौणिमवस्थितमवेक्ष्य तौ ।अकुर्वतां भोजकृपौ किं संजय वदस्व मे ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,dvāradeśe tato drauṇimavasthitamavekṣya tau ,akurvatāṁ bhojakṛpau kiṁ saṁjaya vadasva me.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: Saṃjaya, tell me, when Bhoja (Kṛtavarmā) and Kṛpa saw Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) standing at the entrance to the camp, what did they do?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: Saṃjaya, tell me, when Bhoja (Kṛtavarmā) and Kṛpa saw Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) standing at the entrance to the camp, what did they do?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कृतवर्माणमामन्त्र्य कृपं च स महारथम् ।द्रौणिर्मन्युपरीतात्मा शिबिरद्वारमासदत् ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kṛtavarmāṇamāmantrya kṛpaṁ ca sa mahāratham ,drauṇirmanyuparītātmā śibiradvāramāsadat.
english: Saṃjaya said: Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), his soul (ātman) consumed by rage, approached the camp's entrance after addressing Kṛtavarmā, the great chariot warrior, and Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. Saṃjaya said: Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), his soul (ātman) consumed by rage, approached the camp's entrance after addressing Kṛtavarmā, the great chariot warrior, and Kṛpa.

sanskrit: तत्र भूतं महाकायं चन्द्रार्कसदृशद्युतिम् ।सोऽपश्यद्द्वारमावृत्य तिष्ठन्तं लोमहर्षणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tatra bhūtaṁ mahākāyaṁ candrārkasadṛśadyutim ,so'paśyaddvāramāvṛtya tiṣṭhantaṁ lomaharṣaṇam.
english: There, he saw a colossal, terrifying being, whose radiance was like the moon and sun, standing and blocking the entrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. There, he saw a colossal, terrifying being, whose radiance was like the moon and sun, standing and blocking the entrance.

sanskrit: वसानं चर्म वैयाघ्रं महारुधिरविस्रवम् ।कृष्णाजिनोत्तरासङ्गं नागयज्ञोपवीतिनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vasānaṁ carma vaiyāghraṁ mahārudhiravisravam ,kṛṣṇājinottarāsaṅgaṁ nāgayajñopavītinam.
english: (He saw him) wearing a tiger skin, drenched with great streams of blood, and with a black antelope hide as his upper garment, and a snake serving as his sacred thread (yajñopavīta).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. (He saw him) wearing a tiger skin, drenched with great streams of blood, and with a black antelope hide as his upper garment, and a snake serving as his sacred thread (yajñopavīta).

sanskrit: बाहुभिः स्वायतैः पीनैर्नानाप्रहरणोद्यतैः ।बद्धाङ्गदमहासर्पं ज्वालामालाकुलाननम् ॥५॥
transliteration: bāhubhiḥ svāyataiḥ pīnairnānāpraharaṇodyataiḥ ,baddhāṅgadamahāsarpaṁ jvālāmālākulānanam.
english: [He possessed] long, stout arms, uplifted with various weapons; [he had] great serpents bound as armlets; and his face was covered with a garland of flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. [He possessed] long, stout arms, uplifted with various weapons; [he had] great serpents bound as armlets; and his face was covered with a garland of flames.

sanskrit: दंष्ट्राकरालवदनं व्यादितास्यं भयावहम् ।नयनानां सहस्रैश्च विचित्रैरभिभूषितम् ॥६॥
transliteration: daṁṣṭrākarālavadanaṁ vyāditāsyaṁ bhayāvaham ,nayanānāṁ sahasraiśca vicitrairabhibhūṣitam.
english: [He had] a face made dreadful by tusks, with a wide-open mouth, and was terrifying; he was also adorned with thousands of variegated eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. [He had] a face made dreadful by tusks, with a wide-open mouth, and was terrifying; he was also adorned with thousands of variegated eyes.

sanskrit: नैव तस्य वपुः शक्यं प्रवक्तुं वेष एव वा ।सर्वथा तु तदालक्ष्य स्फुटेयुरपि पर्वताः ॥७॥
transliteration: naiva tasya vapuḥ śakyaṁ pravaktuṁ veṣa eva vā ,sarvathā tu tadālakṣya sphuṭeyurapi parvatāḥ.
english: Indeed, it is not possible to fully describe his body or even his attire; but in any case, having seen that, even mountains would split apart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. Indeed, it is not possible to fully describe his body or even his attire; but in any case, having seen that, even mountains would split apart.

sanskrit: तस्यास्यान्नासिकाभ्यां च श्रवणाभ्यां च सर्वशः ।तेभ्यश्चाक्षिसहस्रेभ्यः प्रादुरासन्महार्चिषः ॥८॥
transliteration: tasyāsyānnāsikābhyāṁ ca śravaṇābhyāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,tebhyaścākṣisahasrebhyaḥ prādurāsanmahārciṣaḥ.
english: From his mouth, and from his two nostrils, and from his two ears, everywhere; and from those thousands of eyes, great flames appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. From his mouth, and from his two nostrils, and from his two ears, everywhere; and from those thousands of eyes, great flames appeared.

sanskrit: तथा तेजोमरीचिभ्यः शङ्खचक्रगदाधराः ।प्रादुरासन्हृषीकेशाः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā tejomarīcibhyaḥ śaṅkhacakragadādharāḥ ,prādurāsanhṛṣīkeśāḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, from rays of splendor, hundreds and thousands of Hṛṣīkeśas (Lord of the senses), bearing conches, discuses, and maces, appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. Then, from rays of splendor, hundreds and thousands of Hṛṣīkeśas (Lord of the senses), bearing conches, discuses, and maces, appeared.

sanskrit: तदत्यद्भुतमालोक्य भूतं लोकभयंकरम् ।द्रौणिरव्यथितो दिव्यैरस्त्रवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tadatyadbhutamālokya bhūtaṁ lokabhayaṁkaram ,drauṇiravyathito divyairastravarṣairavākirat.
english: Having seen that extremely astonishing and world-terrifying being, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), undeterred, showered it with divine weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Having seen that extremely astonishing and world-terrifying being, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), undeterred, showered it with divine weapons.

sanskrit: द्रौणिमुक्ताञ्शरांस्तांस्तु तद्भूतं महदग्रसत् ।उदधेरिव वार्योघान्पावको वडवामुखः ॥११॥
transliteration: drauṇimuktāñśarāṁstāṁstu tadbhūtaṁ mahadagrasat ,udadheriva vāryoghānpāvako vaḍavāmukhaḥ.
english: But that great being swallowed those arrows released by Droṇi, just as the submarine fire (vaḍavāmukha) consumes the floods of water from the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. But that great being swallowed those arrows released by Droṇi, just as the submarine fire (vaḍavāmukha) consumes the floods of water from the ocean.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु संप्रेक्ष्य ताञ्शरौघान्निरर्थकान् ।रथशक्तिं मुमोचास्मै दीप्तामग्निशिखामिव ॥१२॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu saṁprekṣya tāñśaraughānnirarthakān ,rathaśaktiṁ mumocāsmai dīptāmagniśikhāmiva.
english: But Aśvatthāmā, having seen those floods of arrows rendered useless, released his chariot-weapon at it, like a blazing flame of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. But Aśvatthāmā, having seen those floods of arrows rendered useless, released his chariot-weapon at it, like a blazing flame of fire.

sanskrit: सा तदाहत्य दीप्ताग्रा रथशक्तिरशीर्यत ।युगान्ते सूर्यमाहत्य महोल्केव दिवश्च्युता ॥१३॥
transliteration: sā tadāhatya dīptāgrā rathaśaktiraśīryata ,yugānte sūryamāhatya maholkeva divaścyutā.
english: The chariot-missile, with its blazing tip, shattered upon impact, much like a great meteor detached from the sky (div) after striking the sun at the end of a cosmic age (yuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. The chariot-missile, with its blazing tip, shattered upon impact, much like a great meteor detached from the sky (div) after striking the sun at the end of a cosmic age (yuga).

sanskrit: अथ हेमत्सरुं दिव्यं खड्गमाकाशवर्चसम् ।कोशात्समुद्बबर्हाशु बिलाद्दीप्तमिवोरगम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: atha hematsaruṁ divyaṁ khaḍgamākāśavarcasam ,kośātsamudbabarhāśu bilāddīptamivoragam.
english: Then, he quickly drew from the sheath a divine sword, gold-hilted and shining like the sky, just as a blazing serpent emerges from its hole.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. Then, he quickly drew from the sheath a divine sword, gold-hilted and shining like the sky, just as a blazing serpent emerges from its hole.

sanskrit: ततः खड्गवरं धीमान्भूताय प्राहिणोत्तदा ।स तदासाद्य भूतं वै विलयं तूलवद्ययौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ khaḍgavaraṁ dhīmānbhūtāya prāhiṇottadā ,sa tadāsādya bhūtaṁ vai vilayaṁ tūlavadyayau.
english: Then, the wise (dhīmān) one hurled the excellent sword at the creature. When it (the sword) reached the creature, the creature indeed dissolved like cotton.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. Then, the wise (dhīmān) one hurled the excellent sword at the creature. When it (the sword) reached the creature, the creature indeed dissolved like cotton.

sanskrit: ततः स कुपितो द्रौणिरिन्द्रकेतुनिभां गदाम् ।ज्वलन्तीं प्राहिणोत्तस्मै भूतं तामपि चाग्रसत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa kupito drauṇirindraketunibhāṁ gadām ,jvalantīṁ prāhiṇottasmai bhūtaṁ tāmapi cāgrasat.
english: Then, enraged, Droṇi (Drauṇi) hurled at it (the creature) a blazing mace resembling Indra's banner. But the creature swallowed even that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Then, enraged, Droṇi (Drauṇi) hurled at it (the creature) a blazing mace resembling Indra's banner. But the creature swallowed even that.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वायुधाभावे वीक्षमाणस्ततस्ततः ।अपश्यत्कृतमाकाशमनाकाशं जनार्दनैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarvāyudhābhāve vīkṣamāṇastatastataḥ ,apaśyatkṛtamākāśamanākāśaṁ janārdanaiḥ.
english: Then, finding himself without any weapons, and looking everywhere, he saw the sky completely filled by the Janardanas, leaving no empty space.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. Then, finding himself without any weapons, and looking everywhere, he saw the sky completely filled by the Janardanas, leaving no empty space.

sanskrit: तदद्भुततमं दृष्ट्वा द्रोणपुत्रो निरायुधः ।अब्रवीदभिसंतप्तः कृपवाक्यमनुस्मरन् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tadadbhutatamaṁ dṛṣṭvā droṇaputro nirāyudhaḥ ,abravīdabhisaṁtaptaḥ kṛpavākyamanusmaran.
english: Having witnessed that exceedingly astonishing sight, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), now weaponless and deeply distressed, spoke, recalling the advice of Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. Having witnessed that exceedingly astonishing sight, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), now weaponless and deeply distressed, spoke, recalling the advice of Kṛpa.

sanskrit: ब्रुवतामप्रियं पथ्यं सुहृदां न शृणोति यः ।स शोचत्यापदं प्राप्य यथाहमतिवर्त्य तौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: bruvatāmapriyaṁ pathyaṁ suhṛdāṁ na śṛṇoti yaḥ ,sa śocatyāpadaṁ prāpya yathāhamativartya tau.
english: The one who does not heed the wholesome, even if unpleasant, counsel of friends, grieves after facing calamity, just as I am now, having disregarded the advice of those two.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 19. The one who does not heed the wholesome, even if unpleasant, counsel of friends, grieves after facing calamity, just as I am now, having disregarded the advice of those two.

sanskrit: शास्त्रदृष्टानवध्यान्यः समतीत्य जिघांसति ।स पथः प्रच्युतो धर्म्यात्कुपथं प्रतिपद्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: śāstradṛṣṭānavadhyānyaḥ samatītya jighāṁsati ,sa pathaḥ pracyuto dharmyātkupathaṁ pratipadyate.
english: Whoever, disregarding those declared inviolable by the scriptures, seeks to kill them, that person deviates from the righteous (dharma) path and takes a wicked course.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 20. Whoever, disregarding those declared inviolable by the scriptures, seeks to kill them, that person deviates from the righteous (dharma) path and takes a wicked course.

sanskrit: गोब्राह्मणनृपस्त्रीषु सख्युर्मातुर्गुरोस्तथा ।वृद्धबालजडान्धेषु सुप्तभीतोत्थितेषु च ॥२१॥
transliteration: gobrāhmaṇanṛpastrīṣu sakhyurmāturgurostathā ,vṛddhabālajaḍāndheṣu suptabhītotthiteṣu ca.
english: (One should not wield weapons) against cows, Brahmins, kings, or women; against a friend, a mother, or a preceptor (guru); similarly, against the old, children, the foolish, or the blind; and against those who are asleep, frightened, or have just awakened.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 21. (One should not wield weapons) against cows, Brahmins, kings, or women; against a friend, a mother, or a preceptor (guru); similarly, against the old, children, the foolish, or the blind; and against those who are asleep, frightened, or have just awakened.

sanskrit: मत्तोन्मत्तप्रमत्तेषु न शस्त्राण्युपधारयेत् ।इत्येवं गुरुभिः पूर्वमुपदिष्टं नृणां सदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: mattonmattapramatteṣu na śastrāṇyupadhārayet ,ityevaṁ gurubhiḥ pūrvamupadiṣṭaṁ nṛṇāṁ sadā.
english: ... nor against the intoxicated, the insane, or the heedless. This instruction was thus always given to people by preceptors (guru) in ancient times.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 22. ... nor against the intoxicated, the insane, or the heedless. This instruction was thus always given to people by preceptors (guru) in ancient times.

sanskrit: सोऽहमुत्क्रम्य पन्थानं शास्त्रदृष्टं सनातनम् ।अमार्गेणैवमारभ्य घोरामापदमागतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: so'hamutkramya panthānaṁ śāstradṛṣṭaṁ sanātanam ,amārgeṇaivamārabhya ghorāmāpadamāgataḥ.
english: So I, having transgressed the eternal path seen by the scriptures, and having commenced on a wrong path, have encountered a terrible calamity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 23. So I, having transgressed the eternal path seen by the scriptures, and having commenced on a wrong path, have encountered a terrible calamity.

sanskrit: तां चापदं घोरतरां प्रवदन्ति मनीषिणः ।यदुद्यम्य महत्कृत्यं भयादपि निवर्तते ॥२४॥
transliteration: tāṁ cāpadaṁ ghoratarāṁ pravadanti manīṣiṇaḥ ,yadudyamya mahatkṛtyaṁ bhayādapi nivartate.
english: And wise ones declare that calamity even more terrible when, having undertaken a great task, one turns back from it out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 24. And wise ones declare that calamity even more terrible when, having undertaken a great task, one turns back from it out of fear.

sanskrit: अशक्यं चैव कः कर्तुं शक्तः शक्तिबलादिह ।न हि दैवाद्गरीयो वै मानुषं कर्म कथ्यते ॥२५॥
transliteration: aśakyaṁ caiva kaḥ kartuṁ śaktaḥ śaktibalādiha ,na hi daivādgarīyo vai mānuṣaṁ karma kathyate.
english: Who, by the power of strength, is able to accomplish what is impossible here? Indeed, human action (karma) is not considered greater than divine will.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 25. Who, by the power of strength, is able to accomplish what is impossible here? Indeed, human action (karma) is not considered greater than divine will.

sanskrit: मानुषं कुर्वतः कर्म यदि दैवान्न सिध्यति ।स पथः प्रच्युतो धर्म्याद्विपदं प्रतिपद्यते ॥२६॥
transliteration: mānuṣaṁ kurvataḥ karma yadi daivānna sidhyati ,sa pathaḥ pracyuto dharmyādvipadaṁ pratipadyate.
english: If a person performing human action (karma) does not succeed due to divine will, then, having deviated from the path of his intrinsic nature (dharma), he encounters misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 26. If a person performing human action (karma) does not succeed due to divine will, then, having deviated from the path of his intrinsic nature (dharma), he encounters misfortune.

sanskrit: प्रतिघातं ह्यविज्ञातं प्रवदन्ति मनीषिणः ।यदारभ्य क्रियां कांचिद्भयादिह निवर्तते ॥२७॥
transliteration: pratighātaṁ hyavijñātaṁ pravadanti manīṣiṇaḥ ,yadārabhya kriyāṁ kāṁcidbhayādiha nivartate.
english: Indeed, the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) call it an unknown obstruction when someone, having begun a certain action, withdraws from it here out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 27. Indeed, the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) call it an unknown obstruction when someone, having begun a certain action, withdraws from it here out of fear.

sanskrit: तदिदं दुष्प्रणीतेन भयं मां समुपस्थितम् ।न हि द्रोणसुतः संख्ये निवर्तेत कथंचन ॥२८॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ duṣpraṇītena bhayaṁ māṁ samupasthitam ,na hi droṇasutaḥ saṁkhye nivarteta kathaṁcana.
english: This very fear has arisen in me due to ill-conceived action. For Drona's son would certainly never retreat from battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 28. This very fear has arisen in me due to ill-conceived action. For Drona's son would certainly never retreat from battle.

sanskrit: इदं च सुमहद्भूतं दैवदण्डमिवोद्यतम् ।न चैतदभिजानामि चिन्तयन्नपि सर्वथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca sumahadbhūtaṁ daivadaṇḍamivodyatam ,na caitadabhijānāmi cintayannapi sarvathā.
english: And this extremely great occurrence, raised up like a divine rod of punishment, I do not understand at all, even after contemplating it in every way.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 29. And this extremely great occurrence, raised up like a divine rod of punishment, I do not understand at all, even after contemplating it in every way.

sanskrit: ध्रुवं येयमधर्मे मे प्रवृत्ता कलुषा मतिः ।तस्याः फलमिदं घोरं प्रतिघाताय दृश्यते ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhruvaṁ yeyamadharme me pravṛttā kaluṣā matiḥ ,tasyāḥ phalamidaṁ ghoraṁ pratighātāya dṛśyate.
english: Certainly, this impure mind (mati) of mine, which has been inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), its dreadful consequence is seen as a retribution.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 30. Certainly, this impure mind (mati) of mine, which has been inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), its dreadful consequence is seen as a retribution.

sanskrit: तदिदं दैवविहितं मम संख्ये निवर्तनम् ।नान्यत्र दैवादुद्यन्तुमिह शक्यं कथंचन ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ daivavihitaṁ mama saṁkhye nivartanam ,nānyatra daivādudyantumiha śakyaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: This withdrawal of mine from battle is certainly ordained by divine will. It is not possible for it to arise here in any other way than by divine decree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 31. This withdrawal of mine from battle is certainly ordained by divine will. It is not possible for it to arise here in any other way than by divine decree.

sanskrit: सोऽहमद्य महादेवं प्रपद्ये शरणं प्रभुम् ।दैवदण्डमिमं घोरं स हि मे नाशयिष्यति ॥३२॥
transliteration: so'hamadya mahādevaṁ prapadye śaraṇaṁ prabhum ,daivadaṇḍamimaṁ ghoraṁ sa hi me nāśayiṣyati.
english: Therefore, I today take refuge in the great god (Mahādeva), the Lord. Indeed, he will destroy this dreadful divine punishment for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 32. Therefore, I today take refuge in the great god (Mahādeva), the Lord. Indeed, he will destroy this dreadful divine punishment for me.

sanskrit: कपर्दिनं प्रपद्याथ देवदेवमुमापतिम् ।कपालमालिनं रुद्रं भगनेत्रहरं हरम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: kapardinaṁ prapadyātha devadevamumāpatim ,kapālamālinaṁ rudraṁ bhaganetraharaṁ haram.
english: Then, I approach Hara (hara), the husband of Umā (umāpati), the God of gods (devadeva), who wears matted hair (kapardin), who is adorned with a garland of skulls (kapālamālin), who is Rudra, and who destroyed Bhaga's eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 33. Then, I approach Hara (hara), the husband of Umā (umāpati), the God of gods (devadeva), who wears matted hair (kapardin), who is adorned with a garland of skulls (kapālamālin), who is Rudra, and who destroyed Bhaga's eye.

sanskrit: स हि देवोऽत्यगाद्देवांस्तपसा विक्रमेण च ।तस्माच्छरणमभ्येष्ये गिरिशं शूलपाणिनम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa hi devo'tyagāddevāṁstapasā vikrameṇa ca ,tasmāccharaṇamabhyeṣye giriśaṁ śūlapāṇinam.
english: Indeed, that god surpassed the other deities through his asceticism (tapas) and valor. Therefore, I shall seek refuge in Girisha (giriśa), the trident-bearer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 34. Indeed, that god surpassed the other deities through his asceticism (tapas) and valor. Therefore, I shall seek refuge in Girisha (giriśa), the trident-bearer.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।स एवं चिन्तयित्वा तु द्रोणपुत्रो विशां पते ।अवतीर्य रथोपस्थाद्दध्यौ संप्रयतः स्थितः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,sa evaṁ cintayitvā tu droṇaputro viśāṁ pate ,avatīrya rathopasthāddadhyau saṁprayataḥ sthitaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: "O Lord of the people, Drona's son, having thus pondered, indeed, descended from the chariot platform and stood composed, engaged in deep meditation (dhyāna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "O Lord of the people, Drona's son, having thus pondered, indeed, descended from the chariot platform and stood composed, engaged in deep meditation (dhyāna)."

sanskrit: द्रौणिरुवाच ।उग्रं स्थाणुं शिवं रुद्रं शर्वमीशानमीश्वरम् ।गिरिशं वरदं देवं भवं भावनमव्ययम् ॥२॥
transliteration: drauṇiruvāca ,ugraṁ sthāṇuṁ śivaṁ rudraṁ śarvamīśānamīśvaram ,giriśaṁ varadaṁ devaṁ bhavaṁ bhāvanamavyayam.
english: Droni said: "I invoke the formidable, immovable (Sthāṇu), auspicious (Śiva), Rudra, Śarva, the ruler (Īśāna), the Lord (Īśvara), the Lord of the Mountain (Giriśa), the giver of boons, the divine, the source of existence (Bhava), the creator, and the imperishable."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. Droni said: "I invoke the formidable, immovable (Sthāṇu), auspicious (Śiva), Rudra, Śarva, the ruler (Īśāna), the Lord (Īśvara), the Lord of the Mountain (Giriśa), the giver of boons, the divine, the source of existence (Bhava), the creator, and the imperishable."

sanskrit: शितिकण्ठमजं शक्रं क्रथं क्रतुहरं हरम् ।विश्वरूपं विरूपाक्षं बहुरूपमुमापतिम् ॥३॥
transliteration: śitikaṇṭhamajaṁ śakraṁ krathaṁ kratuharaṁ haram ,viśvarūpaṁ virūpākṣaṁ bahurūpamumāpatim.
english: The dark-throated (Śitikaṇṭha), the unborn (aja), the powerful (Śakra), the crusher (Kratha), the one who takes away sacrifices (Kratuhara), the remover (Hara); the one with a universal form (Viśvarūpa), the odd-eyed (Virūpākṣa), the multi-formed (Bahurūpa), and the husband of Uma (Umāpati).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. The dark-throated (Śitikaṇṭha), the unborn (aja), the powerful (Śakra), the crusher (Kratha), the one who takes away sacrifices (Kratuhara), the remover (Hara); the one with a universal form (Viśvarūpa), the odd-eyed (Virūpākṣa), the multi-formed (Bahurūpa), and the husband of Uma (Umāpati).

sanskrit: श्मशानवासिनं दृप्तं महागणपतिं प्रभुम् ।खट्वाङ्गधारिणं मुण्डं जटिलं ब्रह्मचारिणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: śmaśānavāsinaṁ dṛptaṁ mahāgaṇapatiṁ prabhum ,khaṭvāṅgadhāriṇaṁ muṇḍaṁ jaṭilaṁ brahmacāriṇam.
english: The dweller of cremation grounds (śmaśānavāsin), the proud, the great lord of the Gaṇas (Mahāgaṇapati), the supreme master; the one bearing a skull-staff (khaṭvāṅga), the shaven-headed, the matted-haired, and the celibate (brahmacārin).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. The dweller of cremation grounds (śmaśānavāsin), the proud, the great lord of the Gaṇas (Mahāgaṇapati), the supreme master; the one bearing a skull-staff (khaṭvāṅga), the shaven-headed, the matted-haired, and the celibate (brahmacārin).

sanskrit: मनसाप्यसुचिन्त्येन दुष्करेणाल्पचेतसा ।सोऽहमात्मोपहारेण यक्ष्ये त्रिपुरघातिनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: manasāpyasucintyena duṣkareṇālpacetasā ,so'hamātmopahāreṇa yakṣye tripuraghātinam.
english: Even by my mind (manasā), with an inconceivable and arduous act - for I am of little intellect - I will worship the destroyer of Tripura (Tripuraghātin) through the offering of my very self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. Even by my mind (manasā), with an inconceivable and arduous act - for I am of little intellect - I will worship the destroyer of Tripura (Tripuraghātin) through the offering of my very self (ātman).

sanskrit: स्तुतं स्तुत्यं स्तूयमानममोघं चर्मवाससम् ।विलोहितं नीलकण्ठमपृक्तं दुर्निवारणम् ॥६॥
transliteration: stutaṁ stutyaṁ stūyamānamamoghaṁ carmavāsasam ,vilohitaṁ nīlakaṇṭhamapṛktaṁ durnivāraṇam.
english: The praised one (stuta), the praiseworthy one (stutya), the one being praised (stūyamāna), the unfailing, and the one wearing a skin garment; the deep-red (Vilohita), the blue-throated (Nīlakaṇṭha), the unattached, and the irresistible.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. The praised one (stuta), the praiseworthy one (stutya), the one being praised (stūyamāna), the unfailing, and the one wearing a skin garment; the deep-red (Vilohita), the blue-throated (Nīlakaṇṭha), the unattached, and the irresistible.

sanskrit: शुक्रं विश्वसृजं ब्रह्म ब्रह्मचारिणमेव च ।व्रतवन्तं तपोनित्यमनन्तं तपतां गतिम् ॥७॥
transliteration: śukraṁ viśvasṛjaṁ brahma brahmacāriṇameva ca ,vratavantaṁ taponityamanantaṁ tapatāṁ gatim.
english: He who is pure, the creator of the universe, sacred (brahman), and indeed a celibate student (brahmacārin); who observes vows, is ever devoted to austerities (tapas), is endless, and is the ultimate goal (gati) for those performing austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. He who is pure, the creator of the universe, sacred (brahman), and indeed a celibate student (brahmacārin); who observes vows, is ever devoted to austerities (tapas), is endless, and is the ultimate goal (gati) for those performing austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: बहुरूपं गणाध्यक्षं त्र्यक्षं पारिषदप्रियम् ।गणाध्यक्षेक्षितमुखं गौरीहृदयवल्लभम् ॥८॥
transliteration: bahurūpaṁ gaṇādhyakṣaṁ tryakṣaṁ pāriṣadapriyam ,gaṇādhyakṣekṣitamukhaṁ gaurīhṛdayavallabham.
english: He who has many forms, the chief of the gaṇas, three-eyed, beloved by his attendants; whose face is gazed upon by Gaṇādhyakṣa (Gaṇeśa), and who is the darling of Gauri's heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. He who has many forms, the chief of the gaṇas, three-eyed, beloved by his attendants; whose face is gazed upon by Gaṇādhyakṣa (Gaṇeśa), and who is the darling of Gauri's heart.

sanskrit: कुमारपितरं पिङ्गं गोवृषोत्तमवाहनम् ।तनुवाससमत्युग्रमुमाभूषणतत्परम् ॥९॥
transliteration: kumārapitaraṁ piṅgaṁ govṛṣottamavāhanam ,tanuvāsasamatyugramumābhūṣaṇatatparam.
english: He who is the father of Kumāra, tawny-colored, whose excellent bull is his vehicle; who wears a subtle (or thin) garment, is exceedingly fierce, and intent on adorning Uma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. He who is the father of Kumāra, tawny-colored, whose excellent bull is his vehicle; who wears a subtle (or thin) garment, is exceedingly fierce, and intent on adorning Uma.

sanskrit: परं परेभ्यः परमं परं यस्मान्न विद्यते ।इष्वस्त्रोत्तमभर्तारं दिगन्तं चैव दक्षिणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: paraṁ parebhyaḥ paramaṁ paraṁ yasmānna vidyate ,iṣvastrottamabhartāraṁ digantaṁ caiva dakṣiṇam.
english: He who is supreme over the supreme, the highest supreme, beyond whom nothing exists; the lord of the best arrows and missiles, extending to the ends of the quarters (diganta), and indeed benevolent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. He who is supreme over the supreme, the highest supreme, beyond whom nothing exists; the lord of the best arrows and missiles, extending to the ends of the quarters (diganta), and indeed benevolent.

sanskrit: हिरण्यकवचं देवं चन्द्रमौलिविभूषितम् ।प्रपद्ये शरणं देवं परमेण समाधिना ॥११॥
transliteration: hiraṇyakavacaṁ devaṁ candramaulivibhūṣitam ,prapadye śaraṇaṁ devaṁ parameṇa samādhinā.
english: I take refuge, with supreme concentration (samādhi), in the god who is adorned with a golden armor and the moon on his head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. I take refuge, with supreme concentration (samādhi), in the god who is adorned with a golden armor and the moon on his head.

sanskrit: इमां चाप्यापदं घोरां तराम्यद्य सुदुस्तराम् ।सर्वभूतोपहारेण यक्ष्येऽहं शुचिना शुचिम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: imāṁ cāpyāpadaṁ ghorāṁ tarāmyadya sudustarām ,sarvabhūtopahāreṇa yakṣye'haṁ śucinā śucim.
english: And today, I shall overcome this dreadful and extremely difficult calamity. I will offer a sacrifice of all beings, worshipping the pure one as a pure sacrificer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. And today, I shall overcome this dreadful and extremely difficult calamity. I will offer a sacrifice of all beings, worshipping the pure one as a pure sacrificer.

sanskrit: इति तस्य व्यवसितं ज्ञात्वा त्यागात्मकं मनः ।पुरस्तात्काञ्चनी वेदिः प्रादुरासीन्महात्मनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: iti tasya vyavasitaṁ jñātvā tyāgātmakaṁ manaḥ ,purastātkāñcanī vediḥ prādurāsīnmahātmanaḥ.
english: Knowing his mind, which was a firm resolve characterized by renunciation or sacrifice, a golden altar appeared before that great soul (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. Knowing his mind, which was a firm resolve characterized by renunciation or sacrifice, a golden altar appeared before that great soul (mahātman).

sanskrit: तस्यां वेद्यां तदा राजंश्चित्रभानुरजायत ।द्यां दिशो विदिशः खं च ज्वालाभिरभिपूरयन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ vedyāṁ tadā rājaṁścitrabhānurajāyata ,dyāṁ diśo vidiśaḥ khaṁ ca jvālābhirabhipūrayan.
english: Then, O king, on that altar, the fire god (Citrabhānu) appeared, filling the sky, the main directions, the intermediate directions, and the entire space with his flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. Then, O king, on that altar, the fire god (Citrabhānu) appeared, filling the sky, the main directions, the intermediate directions, and the entire space with his flames.

sanskrit: दीप्तास्यनयनाश्चात्र नैकपादशिरोभुजाः ।द्विपशैलप्रतीकाशाः प्रादुरासन्महाननाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dīptāsyanayanāścātra naikapādaśirobhujāḥ ,dvipaśailapratīkāśāḥ prādurāsanmahānanāḥ.
english: Here, beings appeared with blazing mouths and eyes, possessing not just one, but many feet, heads, and arms. They resembled elephants and mountains, and had enormous mouths.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. Here, beings appeared with blazing mouths and eyes, possessing not just one, but many feet, heads, and arms. They resembled elephants and mountains, and had enormous mouths.

sanskrit: श्ववराहोष्ट्ररूपाश्च हयगोमायुगोमुखाः ।ऋक्षमार्जारवदना व्याघ्रद्वीपिमुखास्तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: śvavarāhoṣṭrarūpāśca hayagomāyugomukhāḥ ,ṛkṣamārjāravadanā vyāghradvīpimukhāstathā.
english: They had the forms of dogs, boars, and camels, and faces like those of horses, jackals, and cows. Similarly, they possessed visages resembling bears, cats, tigers, and leopards.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. They had the forms of dogs, boars, and camels, and faces like those of horses, jackals, and cows. Similarly, they possessed visages resembling bears, cats, tigers, and leopards.

sanskrit: काकवक्त्राः प्लवमुखाः शुकवक्त्रास्तथैव च ।महाजगरवक्त्राश्च हंसवक्त्राः सितप्रभाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kākavaktrāḥ plavamukhāḥ śukavaktrāstathaiva ca ,mahājagaravaktrāśca haṁsavaktrāḥ sitaprabhāḥ.
english: And similarly, they had faces like crows, aquatic birds, and parrots. They also had faces like enormous pythons, and swans, appearing with a white radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. And similarly, they had faces like crows, aquatic birds, and parrots. They also had faces like enormous pythons, and swans, appearing with a white radiance.

sanskrit: दार्वाघाटमुखाश्चैव चाषवक्त्राश्च भारत ।कूर्मनक्रमुखाश्चैव शिशुमारमुखास्तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: dārvāghāṭamukhāścaiva cāṣavaktrāśca bhārata ,kūrmanakramukhāścaiva śiśumāramukhāstathā.
english: O Bhārata, they also had faces like woodpeckers and blue jays. And similarly, they possessed visages like tortoises, crocodiles, and dolphins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. O Bhārata, they also had faces like woodpeckers and blue jays. And similarly, they possessed visages like tortoises, crocodiles, and dolphins.

sanskrit: महामकरवक्त्राश्च तिमिवक्त्रास्तथैव च ।हरिवक्त्राः क्रौञ्चमुखाः कपोतेभमुखास्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: mahāmakaravaktrāśca timivaktrāstathaiva ca ,harivaktrāḥ krauñcamukhāḥ kapotebhamukhāstathā.
english: They had faces like great crocodiles and whales, as well as faces like lions, curlews, pigeons, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. They had faces like great crocodiles and whales, as well as faces like lions, curlews, pigeons, and elephants.

sanskrit: पारावतमुखाश्चैव मद्गुवक्त्रास्तथैव च ।पाणिकर्णाः सहस्राक्षास्तथैव च शतोदराः ॥२०॥
transliteration: pārāvatamukhāścaiva madguvaktrāstathaiva ca ,pāṇikarṇāḥ sahasrākṣāstathaiva ca śatodarāḥ.
english: And indeed, they had faces like doves, and similarly faces like cormorants. They had hands for ears, a thousand eyes, and likewise a hundred bellies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. And indeed, they had faces like doves, and similarly faces like cormorants. They had hands for ears, a thousand eyes, and likewise a hundred bellies.

sanskrit: निर्मांसाः कोकवक्त्राश्च श्येनवक्त्राश्च भारत ।तथैवाशिरसो राजन्नृक्षवक्त्राश्च भीषणाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: nirmāṁsāḥ kokavaktrāśca śyenavaktrāśca bhārata ,tathaivāśiraso rājannṛkṣavaktrāśca bhīṣaṇāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, some were fleshless, with faces like ruddy geese and hawks. And similarly, O King, others were headless, terrifying, and had faces like bears.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. O Bhārata, some were fleshless, with faces like ruddy geese and hawks. And similarly, O King, others were headless, terrifying, and had faces like bears.

sanskrit: प्रदीप्तनेत्रजिह्वाश्च ज्वालावक्त्रास्तथैव च ।मेषवक्त्रास्तथैवान्ये तथा छागमुखा नृप ॥२२॥
transliteration: pradīptanetrajihvāśca jvālāvaktrāstathaiva ca ,meṣavaktrāstathaivānye tathā chāgamukhā nṛpa.
english: O King, some had blazing eyes and tongues, and faces like flames. And likewise, others had faces like rams and goats.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. O King, some had blazing eyes and tongues, and faces like flames. And likewise, others had faces like rams and goats.

sanskrit: शङ्खाभाः शङ्खवक्त्राश्च शङ्खकर्णास्तथैव च ।शङ्खमालापरिकराः शङ्खध्वनिसमस्वनाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: śaṅkhābhāḥ śaṅkhavaktrāśca śaṅkhakarṇāstathaiva ca ,śaṅkhamālāparikarāḥ śaṅkhadhvanisamasvanāḥ.
english: They had the luster of conches, with conch-like faces and conch-like ears; they were adorned with garlands of conches and their voices resembled the sound of conches.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 23. They had the luster of conches, with conch-like faces and conch-like ears; they were adorned with garlands of conches and their voices resembled the sound of conches.

sanskrit: जटाधराः पञ्चशिखास्तथा मुण्डाः कृशोदराः ।चतुर्दंष्ट्राश्चतुर्जिह्वाः शङ्कुकर्णाः किरीटिनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: jaṭādharāḥ pañcaśikhāstathā muṇḍāḥ kṛśodarāḥ ,caturdaṁṣṭrāścaturjihvāḥ śaṅkukarṇāḥ kirīṭinaḥ.
english: Some wore matted locks, some had five top-knots, and some were shaven-headed with thin bellies. Others possessed four fangs, four tongues, cone-shaped ears, and wore crowns.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 24. Some wore matted locks, some had five top-knots, and some were shaven-headed with thin bellies. Others possessed four fangs, four tongues, cone-shaped ears, and wore crowns.

sanskrit: मौलीधराश्च राजेन्द्र तथाकुञ्चितमूर्धजाः ।उष्णीषिणो मुकुटिनश्चारुवक्त्राः स्वलंकृताः ॥२५॥
transliteration: maulīdharāśca rājendra tathākuñcitamūrdhajāḥ ,uṣṇīṣiṇo mukuṭinaścāruvaktrāḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ.
english: O great king, they wore crests and also had curly hair; some wore turbans, others crowns, all had beautiful faces and were well-adorned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 25. O great king, they wore crests and also had curly hair; some wore turbans, others crowns, all had beautiful faces and were well-adorned.

sanskrit: पद्मोत्पलापीडधरास्तथा कुमुदधारिणः ।माहात्म्येन च संयुक्ताः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥२६॥
transliteration: padmotpalāpīḍadharāstathā kumudadhāriṇaḥ ,māhātmyena ca saṁyuktāḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: They wore garlands of lotuses and water-lilies, and also bore kumuda flowers; endowed with great majesty (māhātmya), they were present in hundreds and thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 26. They wore garlands of lotuses and water-lilies, and also bore kumuda flowers; endowed with great majesty (māhātmya), they were present in hundreds and thousands.

sanskrit: शतघ्नीचक्रहस्ताश्च तथा मुसलपाणयः ।भुशुण्डीपाशहस्ताश्च गदाहस्ताश्च भारत ॥२७॥
transliteration: śataghnīcakrahastāśca tathā musalapāṇayaḥ ,bhuśuṇḍīpāśahastāśca gadāhastāśca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, some held "śataghnī" weapons and discuses in their hands, while others carried maces (musala) or "bhuśuṇḍīs" and nooses, and still others were armed with maces (gadā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 27. O Bhārata, some held "śataghnī" weapons and discuses in their hands, while others carried maces (musala) or "bhuśuṇḍīs" and nooses, and still others were armed with maces (gadā).

sanskrit: पृष्ठेषु बद्धेषुधयश्चित्रबाणा रणोत्कटाः ।सध्वजाः सपताकाश्च सघण्टाः सपरश्वधाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭheṣu baddheṣudhayaścitrabāṇā raṇotkaṭāḥ ,sadhvajāḥ sapatākāśca saghaṇṭāḥ saparaśvadhāḥ.
english: With quivers full of diverse arrows tied on their backs, fiercely eager for battle; they were also accompanied by banners, flags, bells, and battle-axes.
