search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 28. With quivers full of diverse arrows tied on their backs, fiercely eager for battle; they were also accompanied by banners, flags, bells, and battle-axes.

sanskrit: महापाशोद्यतकरास्तथा लगुडपाणयः ।स्थूणाहस्ताः खड्गहस्ताः सर्पोच्छ्रितकिरीटिनः ।महासर्पाङ्गदधराश्चित्राभरणधारिणः ॥२९॥
transliteration: mahāpāśodyatakarāstathā laguḍapāṇayaḥ ,sthūṇāhastāḥ khaḍgahastāḥ sarpocchritakirīṭinaḥ ,mahāsarpāṅgadadharāścitrābharaṇadhāriṇaḥ.
english: And also, they had great nooses held aloft in their hands, and some carried clubs (laguḍa) or heavy staves (sthūṇā), and others held swords. They wore crowns adorned with erect serpents, armlets fashioned from mighty serpents, and diverse, wondrous ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 29. And also, they had great nooses held aloft in their hands, and some carried clubs (laguḍa) or heavy staves (sthūṇā), and others held swords. They wore crowns adorned with erect serpents, armlets fashioned from mighty serpents, and diverse, wondrous ornaments.

sanskrit: रजोध्वस्ताः पङ्कदिग्धाः सर्वे शुक्लाम्बरस्रजः ।नीलाङ्गाः कमलाङ्गाश्च मुण्डवक्त्रास्तथैव च ॥३०॥
transliteration: rajodhvastāḥ paṅkadigdhāḥ sarve śuklāmbarasrajaḥ ,nīlāṅgāḥ kamalāṅgāśca muṇḍavaktrāstathaiva ca.
english: All were covered in dust and smeared with mud, yet they wore white garments and garlands. Some had blue bodies, others bodies the color of lotuses, and likewise, some possessed shaved heads or faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 30. All were covered in dust and smeared with mud, yet they wore white garments and garlands. Some had blue bodies, others bodies the color of lotuses, and likewise, some possessed shaved heads or faces.

sanskrit: भेरीशङ्खमृदङ्गांस्ते झर्झरानकगोमुखान् ।अवादयन्पारिषदाः प्रहृष्टाः कनकप्रभाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: bherīśaṅkhamṛdaṅgāṁste jharjharānakagomukhān ,avādayanpāriṣadāḥ prahṛṣṭāḥ kanakaprabhāḥ.
english: The delighted attendants, shining like gold, played drums, conchs, mridangas, jharjharas, anaka-drums, and gomukha horns.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 31. The delighted attendants, shining like gold, played drums, conchs, mridangas, jharjharas, anaka-drums, and gomukha horns.

sanskrit: गायमानास्तथैवान्ये नृत्यमानास्तथापरे ।लङ्घयन्तः प्लवन्तश्च वल्गन्तश्च महाबलाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: gāyamānāstathaivānye nṛtyamānāstathāpare ,laṅghayantaḥ plavantaśca valgantaśca mahābalāḥ.
english: While some others were singing and still others were dancing, mighty ones among them were leaping, jumping, and prancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 32. While some others were singing and still others were dancing, mighty ones among them were leaping, jumping, and prancing.

sanskrit: धावन्तो जवनाश्चण्डाः पवनोद्धूतमूर्धजाः ।मत्ता इव महानागा विनदन्तो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhāvanto javanāścaṇḍāḥ pavanoddhūtamūrdhajāḥ ,mattā iva mahānāgā vinadanto muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Running swiftly and fiercely, their hair blown by the wind, they roared again and again like intoxicated great elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 33. Running swiftly and fiercely, their hair blown by the wind, they roared again and again like intoxicated great elephants.

sanskrit: सुभीमा घोररूपाश्च शूलपट्टिशपाणयः ।नानाविरागवसनाश्चित्रमाल्यानुलेपनाः ॥३४॥
transliteration: subhīmā ghorarūpāśca śūlapaṭṭiśapāṇayaḥ ,nānāvirāgavasanāścitramālyānulepanāḥ.
english: They were exceedingly dreadful and of terrible appearance, carrying spears and paṭṭiśa weapons in their hands. They wore variously colored garments and were adorned with colorful garlands and unguents.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 34. They were exceedingly dreadful and of terrible appearance, carrying spears and paṭṭiśa weapons in their hands. They wore variously colored garments and were adorned with colorful garlands and unguents.

sanskrit: रत्नचित्राङ्गदधराः समुद्यतकरास्तथा ।हन्तारो द्विषतां शूराः प्रसह्यासह्यविक्रमाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: ratnacitrāṅgadadharāḥ samudyatakarāstathā ,hantāro dviṣatāṁ śūrāḥ prasahyāsahyavikramāḥ.
english: They wear armlets adorned with gems, have uplifted hands, are valiant heroes who slay enemies, and display irresistible might by force.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 35. They wear armlets adorned with gems, have uplifted hands, are valiant heroes who slay enemies, and display irresistible might by force.

sanskrit: पातारोऽसृग्वसाद्यानां मांसान्त्रकृतभोजनाः ।चूडालाः कर्णिकालाश्च प्रकृशाः पिठरोदराः ॥३६॥
transliteration: pātāro'sṛgvasādyānāṁ māṁsāntrakṛtabhojanāḥ ,cūḍālāḥ karṇikālāśca prakṛśāḥ piṭharodarāḥ.
english: They devour blood, fat, and similar substances, making flesh and entrails their food. They possess top-knots and large ears, appearing very lean yet having pot-bellies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 36. They devour blood, fat, and similar substances, making flesh and entrails their food. They possess top-knots and large ears, appearing very lean yet having pot-bellies.

sanskrit: अतिह्रस्वातिदीर्घाश्च प्रबलाश्चातिभैरवाः ।विकटाः काललम्बोष्ठा बृहच्छेफास्थिपिण्डिकाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: atihrasvātidīrghāśca prabalāścātibhairavāḥ ,vikaṭāḥ kālalamboṣṭhā bṛhacchephāsthipiṇḍikāḥ.
english: They are either excessively short or exceedingly tall, and are very powerful and utterly terrifying. They appear monstrous, with dark, pendulous lips, and possess prominent male organs and bony protuberances.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 37. They are either excessively short or exceedingly tall, and are very powerful and utterly terrifying. They appear monstrous, with dark, pendulous lips, and possess prominent male organs and bony protuberances.

sanskrit: महार्हनानामुकुटा मुण्डाश्च जटिलाः परे ।सार्केन्दुग्रहनक्षत्रां द्यां कुर्युर्ये महीतले ॥३८॥
transliteration: mahārhanānāmukuṭā muṇḍāśca jaṭilāḥ pare ,sārkendugrahanakṣatrāṁ dyāṁ kuryurye mahītale.
english: Some wear various crowns of great value, while others are shaven-headed or have matted hair. These are the beings who could make the sky, complete with its sun, moon, planets, and stars, appear on the surface of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 38. Some wear various crowns of great value, while others are shaven-headed or have matted hair. These are the beings who could make the sky, complete with its sun, moon, planets, and stars, appear on the surface of the earth.

sanskrit: उत्सहेरंश्च ये हन्तुं भूतग्रामं चतुर्विधम् ।ये च वीतभया नित्यं हरस्य भ्रुकुटीभटाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: utsaheraṁśca ye hantuṁ bhūtagrāmaṁ caturvidham ,ye ca vītabhayā nityaṁ harasya bhrukuṭībhaṭāḥ.
english: And those who would dare to kill the four-fold host of beings, and who are perpetually fearless warriors, serving the frown of Hara (Śiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 39. And those who would dare to kill the four-fold host of beings, and who are perpetually fearless warriors, serving the frown of Hara (Śiva).

sanskrit: कामकारकराः सिद्धास्त्रैलोक्यस्येश्वरेश्वराः ।नित्यानन्दप्रमुदिता वागीशा वीतमत्सराः ॥४०॥
transliteration: kāmakārakarāḥ siddhāstrailokyasyeśvareśvarāḥ ,nityānandapramuditā vāgīśā vītamatsarāḥ.
english: They are perfected beings (Siddhas) who act according to their own will, supreme lords even of the rulers of the three worlds, perpetually delighted by eternal bliss, masters of speech, and free from envy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 40. They are perfected beings (Siddhas) who act according to their own will, supreme lords even of the rulers of the three worlds, perpetually delighted by eternal bliss, masters of speech, and free from envy.

sanskrit: प्राप्याष्टगुणमैश्वर्यं ये न यान्ति च विस्मयम् ।येषां विस्मयते नित्यं भगवान्कर्मभिर्हरः ॥४१॥
transliteration: prāpyāṣṭaguṇamaiśvaryaṁ ye na yānti ca vismayam ,yeṣāṁ vismayate nityaṁ bhagavānkarmabhirharaḥ.
english: Having attained the eight-fold divine power (aiśvarya), they do not fall into amazement, while the venerable Lord Hara (Śiva) Himself is constantly amazed by their (karma) deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 41. Having attained the eight-fold divine power (aiśvarya), they do not fall into amazement, while the venerable Lord Hara (Śiva) Himself is constantly amazed by their (karma) deeds.

sanskrit: मनोवाक्कर्मभिर्भक्तैर्नित्यमाराधितश्च यैः ।मनोवाक्कर्मभिर्भक्तान्पाति पुत्रानिवौरसान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: manovākkarmabhirbhaktairnityamārādhitaśca yaiḥ ,manovākkarmabhirbhaktānpāti putrānivaurasān.
english: And by whom (these perfected beings) Hara is constantly worshipped through mind, speech, and action (karma); He protects these devotees (bhaktas), just like His own legitimate sons, through mind, speech, and action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 42. And by whom (these perfected beings) Hara is constantly worshipped through mind, speech, and action (karma); He protects these devotees (bhaktas), just like His own legitimate sons, through mind, speech, and action (karma).

sanskrit: पिबन्तोऽसृग्वसास्त्वन्ये क्रुद्धा ब्रह्मद्विषां सदा ।चतुर्विंशात्मकं सोमं ये पिबन्ति च नित्यदा ॥४३॥
transliteration: pibanto'sṛgvasāstvanye kruddhā brahmadviṣāṁ sadā ,caturviṁśātmakaṁ somaṁ ye pibanti ca nityadā.
english: And others, who drink blood and fat, and are always enraged at those hostile to sacred knowledge (brahman), are the very ones who continuously partake of the twenty-four-fold soma (soma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 43. And others, who drink blood and fat, and are always enraged at those hostile to sacred knowledge (brahman), are the very ones who continuously partake of the twenty-four-fold soma (soma).

sanskrit: श्रुतेन ब्रह्मचर्येण तपसा च दमेन च ।ये समाराध्य शूलाङ्कं भवसायुज्यमागताः ॥४४॥
transliteration: śrutena brahmacaryeṇa tapasā ca damena ca ,ye samārādhya śūlāṅkaṁ bhavasāyujyamāgatāḥ.
english: Those who, by means of Vedic knowledge, celibacy (brahmacarya), austerity (tapas), and self-control, have profoundly worshipped Śūlāṅka (Shiva) and thereby achieved intimate union (sāyujya) with Bhava (Shiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 44. Those who, by means of Vedic knowledge, celibacy (brahmacarya), austerity (tapas), and self-control, have profoundly worshipped Śūlāṅka (Shiva) and thereby achieved intimate union (sāyujya) with Bhava (Shiva).

sanskrit: यैरात्मभूतैर्भगवान्पार्वत्या च महेश्वरः ।सह भूतगणान्भुङ्क्ते भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः ॥४५॥
transliteration: yairātmabhūtairbhagavānpārvatyā ca maheśvaraḥ ,saha bhūtagaṇānbhuṅkte bhūtabhavyabhavatprabhuḥ.
english: Through whom, having become identical with the divine self (ātman), the venerable Maheśvara (Shiva)  -  the Lord of all past, present, and future beings  -  enjoys together with Pārvatī and the hordes of attendant spirits.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 45. Through whom, having become identical with the divine self (ātman), the venerable Maheśvara (Shiva)  -  the Lord of all past, present, and future beings  -  enjoys together with Pārvatī and the hordes of attendant spirits.

sanskrit: नानाविचित्रहसितक्ष्वेडितोत्क्रुष्टगर्जितैः ।संनादयन्तस्ते विश्वमश्वत्थामानमभ्ययुः ॥४६॥
transliteration: nānāvicitrahasitakṣveḍitotkruṣṭagarjitaiḥ ,saṁnādayantaste viśvamaśvatthāmānamabhyayuḥ.
english: With their varied, wondrous laughter, shouts, loud cries, and roars, they, making the entire world resound, approached Aśvatthāmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 46. With their varied, wondrous laughter, shouts, loud cries, and roars, they, making the entire world resound, approached Aśvatthāmā.

sanskrit: संस्तुवन्तो महादेवं भाः कुर्वाणाः सुवर्चसः ।विवर्धयिषवो द्रौणेर्महिमानं महात्मनः ॥४७॥
transliteration: saṁstuvanto mahādevaṁ bhāḥ kurvāṇāḥ suvarcasaḥ ,vivardhayiṣavo drauṇermahimānaṁ mahātmanaḥ.
english: They, praising Mahadeva, possessing great splendor and manifesting light, were desirous of augmenting the glory of the great-souled Drona's son (Ashvatthama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 47. They, praising Mahadeva, possessing great splendor and manifesting light, were desirous of augmenting the glory of the great-souled Drona's son (Ashvatthama).

sanskrit: जिज्ञासमानास्तत्तेजः सौप्तिकं च दिदृक्षवः ।भीमोग्रपरिघालातशूलपट्टिशपाणयः ।घोररूपाः समाजग्मुर्भूतसंघाः समन्ततः ॥४८॥
transliteration: jijñāsamānāstattejaḥ sauptikaṁ ca didṛkṣavaḥ ,bhīmograparighālātaśūlapaṭṭiśapāṇayaḥ ,ghorarūpāḥ samājagmurbhūtasaṁghāḥ samantataḥ.
english: They, inquisitive about that (Ashvatthama's) power, and eager to witness the (carnage of the) Souptika (attack on the sleepers), arrived from all sides. These hosts of spirits (bhūtas) were of terrifying forms, with formidable and fierce iron maces, burning brands, lances, and sharp spears in their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 48. They, inquisitive about that (Ashvatthama's) power, and eager to witness the (carnage of the) Souptika (attack on the sleepers), arrived from all sides. These hosts of spirits (bhūtas) were of terrifying forms, with formidable and fierce iron maces, burning brands, lances, and sharp spears in their hands.

sanskrit: जनयेयुर्भयं ये स्म त्रैलोक्यस्यापि दर्शनात् ।तान्प्रेक्षमाणोऽपि व्यथां न चकार महाबलः ॥४९॥
transliteration: janayeyurbhayaṁ ye sma trailokyasyāpi darśanāt ,tānprekṣamāṇo'pi vyathāṁ na cakāra mahābalaḥ.
english: These (ghostly) hosts, whose mere sight would instill fear even in the three worlds, yet the mighty Ashvatthama (mahābalaḥ), even observing them, felt no distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 49. These (ghostly) hosts, whose mere sight would instill fear even in the three worlds, yet the mighty Ashvatthama (mahābalaḥ), even observing them, felt no distress.

sanskrit: अथ द्रौणिर्धनुष्पाणिर्बद्धगोधाङ्गुलित्रवान् ।स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानमुपहारमुपाहरत् ॥५०॥
transliteration: atha drauṇirdhanuṣpāṇirbaddhagodhāṅgulitravān ,svayamevātmanātmānamupahāramupāharat.
english: Then Ashvatthama (drauṇiḥ), with bow in hand and having fastened his leather finger-guard (for archery), truly offered himself as a sacrifice (upahāra) with his own (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 50. Then Ashvatthama (drauṇiḥ), with bow in hand and having fastened his leather finger-guard (for archery), truly offered himself as a sacrifice (upahāra) with his own (ātman).

sanskrit: धनूंषि समिधस्तत्र पवित्राणि शिताः शराः ।हविरात्मवतश्चात्मा तस्मिन्भारत कर्मणि ॥५१॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi samidhastatra pavitrāṇi śitāḥ śarāḥ ,havirātmavataścātmā tasminbhārata karmaṇi.
english: O Bhārata, in that action (karma), bows are the sacrificial fuel, sharpened arrows are the purifiers, and the self (ātman) of the self-controlled person is the oblation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 51. O Bhārata, in that action (karma), bows are the sacrificial fuel, sharpened arrows are the purifiers, and the self (ātman) of the self-controlled person is the oblation.

sanskrit: ततः सौम्येन मन्त्रेण द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।उपहारं महामन्युरथात्मानमुपाहरत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: tataḥ saumyena mantreṇa droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,upahāraṁ mahāmanyurathātmānamupāharat.
english: Then, the mighty son of Droṇa, filled with great wrath, offered himself (ātman) as an oblation, using a gentle sacred formula (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 52. Then, the mighty son of Droṇa, filled with great wrath, offered himself (ātman) as an oblation, using a gentle sacred formula (mantra).

sanskrit: तं रुद्रं रौद्रकर्माणं रौद्रैः कर्मभिरच्युतम् ।अभिष्टुत्य महात्मानमित्युवाच कृताञ्जलिः ॥५३॥
transliteration: taṁ rudraṁ raudrakarmāṇaṁ raudraiḥ karmabhiracyutam ,abhiṣṭutya mahātmānamityuvāca kṛtāñjaliḥ.
english: Having praised that Rudra, the great soul (ātman), who is unchanging (acyuta) and whose terrible deeds are performed with fierce actions, Aśvatthāmā spoke thus with folded hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 53. Having praised that Rudra, the great soul (ātman), who is unchanging (acyuta) and whose terrible deeds are performed with fierce actions, Aśvatthāmā spoke thus with folded hands.

sanskrit: इममात्मानमद्याहं जातमाङ्गिरसे कुले ।अग्नौ जुहोमि भगवन्प्रतिगृह्णीष्व मां बलिम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: imamātmānamadyāhaṁ jātamāṅgirase kule ,agnau juhomi bhagavanpratigṛhṇīṣva māṁ balim.
english: O Lord (Bhagavan), today I offer this very self (ātman) of mine, born in the Angirasa lineage, into the fire. Please accept me as an offering.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 54. O Lord (Bhagavan), today I offer this very self (ātman) of mine, born in the Angirasa lineage, into the fire. Please accept me as an offering.

sanskrit: भवद्भक्त्या महादेव परमेण समाधिना ।अस्यामापदि विश्वात्मन्नुपाकुर्मि तवाग्रतः ॥५५॥
transliteration: bhavadbhaktyā mahādeva parameṇa samādhinā ,asyāmāpadi viśvātmannupākurmi tavāgrataḥ.
english: O Great God (mahādeva), O Soul of the Universe (viśvātman), in this calamity, I offer myself before you with supreme concentration (samādhi) and devotion (bhakti) to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 55. O Great God (mahādeva), O Soul of the Universe (viśvātman), in this calamity, I offer myself before you with supreme concentration (samādhi) and devotion (bhakti) to you.

sanskrit: त्वयि सर्वाणि भूतानि सर्वभूतेषु चासि वै ।गुणानां हि प्रधानानामेकत्वं त्वयि तिष्ठति ॥५६॥
transliteration: tvayi sarvāṇi bhūtāni sarvabhūteṣu cāsi vai ,guṇānāṁ hi pradhānānāmekatvaṁ tvayi tiṣṭhati.
english: All beings reside in you, and indeed, you are present in all beings. The unity of the primary qualities (guṇas) truly exists within you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 56. All beings reside in you, and indeed, you are present in all beings. The unity of the primary qualities (guṇas) truly exists within you.

sanskrit: सर्वभूताशय विभो हविर्भूतमुपस्थितम् ।प्रतिगृहाण मां देव यद्यशक्याः परे मया ॥५७॥
transliteration: sarvabhūtāśaya vibho havirbhūtamupasthitam ,pratigṛhāṇa māṁ deva yadyaśakyāḥ pare mayā.
english: O all-pervading Lord (vibho), O abode within all beings (sarvabhūtāśaya), I stand before you, having become an oblation. O God, accept me, for my enemies are impossible for me to overcome.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 57. O all-pervading Lord (vibho), O abode within all beings (sarvabhūtāśaya), I stand before you, having become an oblation. O God, accept me, for my enemies are impossible for me to overcome.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा द्रौणिरास्थाय तां वेदीं दीप्तपावकाम् ।संत्यक्तात्मा समारुह्य कृष्णवर्त्मन्युपाविशत् ॥५८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā drauṇirāsthāya tāṁ vedīṁ dīptapāvakām ,saṁtyaktātmā samāruhya kṛṣṇavartmanyupāviśat.
english: Having spoken thus, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) approached and mounted that altar with blazing fire. Having given up his self (ātman), he climbed onto it and sat down upon the path of the fire (kṛṣṇavartman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 58. Having spoken thus, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) approached and mounted that altar with blazing fire. Having given up his self (ātman), he climbed onto it and sat down upon the path of the fire (kṛṣṇavartman).

sanskrit: तमूर्ध्वबाहुं निश्चेष्टं दृष्ट्वा हविरुपस्थितम् ।अब्रवीद्भगवान्साक्षान्महादेवो हसन्निव ॥५९॥
transliteration: tamūrdhvabāhuṁ niśceṣṭaṁ dṛṣṭvā havirupasthitam ,abravīdbhagavānsākṣānmahādevo hasanniva.
english: Having seen him standing motionless with uplifted arms, and the offering presented, the divine Mahadeva himself spoke, as if smiling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 59. Having seen him standing motionless with uplifted arms, and the offering presented, the divine Mahadeva himself spoke, as if smiling.

sanskrit: सत्यशौचार्जवत्यागैस्तपसा नियमेन च ।क्षान्त्या भक्त्या च धृत्या च बुद्ध्या च वचसा तथा ॥६०॥
transliteration: satyaśaucārjavatyāgaistapasā niyamena ca ,kṣāntyā bhaktyā ca dhṛtyā ca buddhyā ca vacasā tathā.
english: By means of truth, purity, sincerity, and renunciation; by means of austerity (tapas) and self-discipline; and by means of patience, devotion (bhakti), fortitude, intelligence, and also by speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 60. By means of truth, purity, sincerity, and renunciation; by means of austerity (tapas) and self-discipline; and by means of patience, devotion (bhakti), fortitude, intelligence, and also by speech.

sanskrit: यथावदहमाराद्धः कृष्णेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।तस्मादिष्टतमः कृष्णादन्यो मम न विद्यते ॥६१॥
transliteration: yathāvadahamārāddhaḥ kṛṣṇenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,tasmādiṣṭatamaḥ kṛṣṇādanyo mama na vidyate.
english: I have been duly worshipped by Krishna, whose deeds are effortless. Therefore, no one else is dearer to me than Krishna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 61. I have been duly worshipped by Krishna, whose deeds are effortless. Therefore, no one else is dearer to me than Krishna.

sanskrit: कुर्वता तस्य संमानं त्वां च जिज्ञासता मया ।पाञ्चालाः सहसा गुप्ता मायाश्च बहुशः कृताः ॥६२॥
transliteration: kurvatā tasya saṁmānaṁ tvāṁ ca jijñāsatā mayā ,pāñcālāḥ sahasā guptā māyāśca bahuśaḥ kṛtāḥ.
english: While honoring him and wishing to understand you, I suddenly protected the Pañcālas and created many illusions (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 62. While honoring him and wishing to understand you, I suddenly protected the Pañcālas and created many illusions (māyā).

sanskrit: कृतस्तस्यैष संमानः पाञ्चालान्रक्षता मया ।अभिभूतास्तु कालेन नैषामद्यास्ति जीवितम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: kṛtastasyaiṣa saṁmānaḥ pāñcālānrakṣatā mayā ,abhibhūtāstu kālena naiṣāmadyāsti jīvitam.
english: This honor (sammānaḥ) has been performed for him by me, the protector of the Pañcālas. But now they (the Pañcālas) are overcome by time; their life (jīvitam) no longer exists.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 63. This honor (sammānaḥ) has been performed for him by me, the protector of the Pañcālas. But now they (the Pañcālas) are overcome by time; their life (jīvitam) no longer exists.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महेष्वासं भगवानात्मनस्तनुम् ।आविवेश ददौ चास्मै विमलं खड्गमुत्तमम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: evamuktvā maheṣvāsaṁ bhagavānātmanastanum ,āviveśa dadau cāsmai vimalaṁ khaḍgamuttamam.
english: Having thus spoken to the great archer, the divine one (bhagavān) entered his (the archer's) body (ātman). And he gave to him an excellent, stainless sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 64. Having thus spoken to the great archer, the divine one (bhagavān) entered his (the archer's) body (ātman). And he gave to him an excellent, stainless sword.

sanskrit: अथाविष्टो भगवता भूयो जज्वाल तेजसा ।वर्ष्मवांश्चाभवद्युद्धे देवसृष्टेन तेजसा ॥६५॥
transliteration: athāviṣṭo bhagavatā bhūyo jajvāla tejasā ,varṣmavāṁścābhavadyuddhe devasṛṣṭena tejasā.
english: Then, possessed by the divine one (bhagavān), he blazed forth again with splendor. And in battle, he became mighty with the god-created splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 65. Then, possessed by the divine one (bhagavān), he blazed forth again with splendor. And in battle, he became mighty with the god-created splendor.

sanskrit: तमदृश्यानि भूतानि रक्षांसि च समाद्रवन् ।अभितः शत्रुशिबिरं यान्तं साक्षादिवेश्वरम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: tamadṛśyāni bhūtāni rakṣāṁsi ca samādravan ,abhitaḥ śatruśibiraṁ yāntaṁ sākṣādiveśvaram.
english: Invisible beings (bhūtāni) and Rākṣasas rushed towards him as he moved around the enemy camp, as if he were the Lord (īśvara) himself, manifest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 66. Invisible beings (bhūtāni) and Rākṣasas rushed towards him as he moved around the enemy camp, as if he were the Lord (īśvara) himself, manifest.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।तथा प्रयाते शिबिरं द्रोणपुत्रे महारथे ।कच्चित्कृपश्च भोजश्च भयार्तौ न न्यवर्तताम् ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tathā prayāte śibiraṁ droṇaputre mahārathe ,kaccitkṛpaśca bhojaśca bhayārtau na nyavartatām.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "After Droṇa's son, the great charioteer, had thus gone to the camp, tell me, did Kṛpa and Bhoja, afflicted by fear, not turn back?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "After Droṇa's son, the great charioteer, had thus gone to the camp, tell me, did Kṛpa and Bhoja, afflicted by fear, not turn back?"

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न वारितौ क्षुद्रै रक्षिभिर्नोपलक्षितौ ।असह्यमिति वा मत्वा न निवृत्तौ महारथौ ॥२॥
transliteration: kaccinna vāritau kṣudrai rakṣibhirnopalakṣitau ,asahyamiti vā matvā na nivṛttau mahārathau.
english: Were they (Kṛpa and Bhoja) surely not stopped by lowly guards, nor noticed (by them)? Or, considering it unbearable, did those two great charioteers not turn back?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Were they (Kṛpa and Bhoja) surely not stopped by lowly guards, nor noticed (by them)? Or, considering it unbearable, did those two great charioteers not turn back?

sanskrit: कच्चित्प्रमथ्य शिबिरं हत्वा सोमकपाण्डवान् ।दुर्योधनस्य पदवीं गतौ परमिकां रणे ॥३॥
transliteration: kaccitpramathya śibiraṁ hatvā somakapāṇḍavān ,duryodhanasya padavīṁ gatau paramikāṁ raṇe.
english: Did they (Kṛpa and Bhoja), after crushing the camp and killing the Somakas and Pāṇḍavas, attain Duryodhana's supreme fate on the battlefield?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. Did they (Kṛpa and Bhoja), after crushing the camp and killing the Somakas and Pāṇḍavas, attain Duryodhana's supreme fate on the battlefield?

sanskrit: पाञ्चालैर्वा विनिहतौ कच्चिन्नास्वपतां क्षितौ ।कच्चित्ताभ्यां कृतं कर्म तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥४॥
transliteration: pāñcālairvā vinihatau kaccinnāsvapatāṁ kṣitau ,kaccittābhyāṁ kṛtaṁ karma tanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: Or, were they (Kṛpa and Bhoja) surely not killed by the Pāñcālas and laid down on the ground? O Saṃjaya, tell me whether that deed was accomplished by them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. Or, were they (Kṛpa and Bhoja) surely not killed by the Pāñcālas and laid down on the ground? O Saṃjaya, tell me whether that deed was accomplished by them.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तस्मिन्प्रयाते शिबिरं द्रोणपुत्रे महात्मनि ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च शिबिरद्वार्यतिष्ठताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tasminprayāte śibiraṁ droṇaputre mahātmani ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca śibiradvāryatiṣṭhatām.
english: Sañjaya said: After that noble-minded son of Droṇa (Ashvatthama) had entered the camp, Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā remained standing at the camp's entrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. Sañjaya said: After that noble-minded son of Droṇa (Ashvatthama) had entered the camp, Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā remained standing at the camp's entrance.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु तौ दृष्ट्वा यत्नवन्तौ महारथौ ।प्रहृष्टः शनकै राजन्निदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu tau dṛṣṭvā yatnavantau mahārathau ,prahṛṣṭaḥ śanakai rājannidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: O King, seeing those two diligent great chariot-warriors (Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā), Ashvatthama was greatly pleased and slowly spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. O King, seeing those two diligent great chariot-warriors (Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā), Ashvatthama was greatly pleased and slowly spoke these words.

sanskrit: यत्तौ भवन्तौ पर्याप्तौ सर्वक्षत्रस्य नाशने ।किं पुनर्योधशेषस्य प्रसुप्तस्य विशेषतः ॥७॥
transliteration: yattau bhavantau paryāptau sarvakṣatrasya nāśane ,kiṁ punaryodhaśeṣasya prasuptasya viśeṣataḥ.
english: Since you two are perfectly capable of destroying the entire Kṣatriya (kṣatra) class, how much more so for the remnant of warriors, especially those who are asleep?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Since you two are perfectly capable of destroying the entire Kṣatriya (kṣatra) class, how much more so for the remnant of warriors, especially those who are asleep?

sanskrit: अहं प्रवेक्ष्ये शिबिरं चरिष्यामि च कालवत् ।यथा न कश्चिदपि मे जीवन्मुच्येत मानवः ॥८॥
transliteration: ahaṁ pravekṣye śibiraṁ cariṣyāmi ca kālavat ,yathā na kaścidapi me jīvanmucyeta mānavaḥ.
english: I will enter the camp and move about like Time (Kāla) itself, so that no human being will escape me alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. I will enter the camp and move about like Time (Kāla) itself, so that no human being will escape me alive.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा प्राविशद्द्रौणिः पार्थानां शिबिरं महत् ।अद्वारेणाभ्यवस्कन्द्य विहाय भयमात्मनः ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktvā prāviśaddrauṇiḥ pārthānāṁ śibiraṁ mahat ,advāreṇābhyavaskandya vihāya bhayamātmanaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Drauṇi entered the large camp of the Pārthas, assailing it not through its entrance and having abandoned his own fear (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. Having spoken thus, Drauṇi entered the large camp of the Pārthas, assailing it not through its entrance and having abandoned his own fear (ātman).

sanskrit: स प्रविश्य महाबाहुरुद्देशज्ञश्च तस्य ह ।धृष्टद्युम्नस्य निलयं शनकैरभ्युपागमत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa praviśya mahābāhuruddeśajñaśca tasya ha ,dhṛṣṭadyumnasya nilayaṁ śanakairabhyupāgamat.
english: Having entered, he, the mighty-armed one, who indeed knew the exact location, slowly approached the dwelling of Dhṛṣṭadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. Having entered, he, the mighty-armed one, who indeed knew the exact location, slowly approached the dwelling of Dhṛṣṭadyumna.

sanskrit: ते तु कृत्वा महत्कर्म श्रान्ताश्च बलवद्रणे ।प्रसुप्ता वै सुविश्वस्ताः स्वसैन्यपरिवारिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: te tu kṛtvā mahatkarma śrāntāśca balavadraṇe ,prasuptā vai suviśvastāḥ svasainyaparivāritāḥ.
english: But they, having accomplished a great deed (karma) and being greatly fatigued from battle, were fast asleep, truly unsuspecting, surrounded by their own army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. But they, having accomplished a great deed (karma) and being greatly fatigued from battle, were fast asleep, truly unsuspecting, surrounded by their own army.

sanskrit: अथ प्रविश्य तद्वेश्म धृष्टद्युम्नस्य भारत ।पाञ्चाल्यं शयने द्रौणिरपश्यत्सुप्तमन्तिकात् ॥१२॥
transliteration: atha praviśya tadveśma dhṛṣṭadyumnasya bhārata ,pāñcālyaṁ śayane drauṇirapaśyatsuptamantikāt.
english: Then, O Bhārata, having entered that dwelling of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Drauṇi saw the Pāñcāla prince sleeping nearby on his bed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. Then, O Bhārata, having entered that dwelling of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Drauṇi saw the Pāñcāla prince sleeping nearby on his bed.

sanskrit: क्षौमावदाते महति स्पर्ध्यास्तरणसंवृते ।माल्यप्रवरसंयुक्ते धूपैश्चूर्णैश्च वासिते ॥१३॥
transliteration: kṣaumāvadāte mahati spardhyāstaraṇasaṁvṛte ,mālyapravarasaṁyukte dhūpaiścūrṇaiśca vāsite.
english: Upon a vast, pure white bed, made of fine linen, covered with a splendid spread, adorned with excellent garlands, and perfumed with incense and powders.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. Upon a vast, pure white bed, made of fine linen, covered with a splendid spread, adorned with excellent garlands, and perfumed with incense and powders.

sanskrit: तं शयानं महात्मानं विस्रब्धमकुतोभयम् ।प्राबोधयत पादेन शयनस्थं महीपते ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ śayānaṁ mahātmānaṁ visrabdhamakutobhayam ,prābodhayata pādena śayanasthaṁ mahīpate.
english: O king, (he) woke that great-souled one (mahātman), who was sleeping on the bed, utterly fearless and confident, by (touching him with) his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. O king, (he) woke that great-souled one (mahātman), who was sleeping on the bed, utterly fearless and confident, by (touching him with) his foot.

sanskrit: स बुद्ध्वा चरणस्पर्शमुत्थाय रणदुर्मदः ।अभ्यजानदमेयात्मा द्रोणपुत्रं महारथम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa buddhvā caraṇasparśamutthāya raṇadurmadaḥ ,abhyajānadameyātmā droṇaputraṁ mahāratham.
english: Having perceived the touch of a foot and having risen, he, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was fierce in battle, recognized Drona's son, the great chariot-warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. Having perceived the touch of a foot and having risen, he, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was fierce in battle, recognized Drona's son, the great chariot-warrior.

sanskrit: तमुत्पतन्तं शयनादश्वत्थामा महाबलः ।केशेष्वालम्ब्य पाणिभ्यां निष्पिपेष महीतले ॥१६॥
transliteration: tamutpatantaṁ śayanādaśvatthāmā mahābalaḥ ,keśeṣvālambya pāṇibhyāṁ niṣpipeṣa mahītale.
english: The mighty Aśvatthāmā, seizing him by the hair with both hands as he sprang up from the bed, crushed him onto the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. The mighty Aśvatthāmā, seizing him by the hair with both hands as he sprang up from the bed, crushed him onto the ground.

sanskrit: स बलात्तेन निष्पिष्टः साध्वसेन च भारत ।निद्रया चैव पाञ्चाल्यो नाशकच्चेष्टितुं तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa balāttena niṣpiṣṭaḥ sādhvasena ca bhārata ,nidrayā caiva pāñcālyo nāśakacceṣṭituṁ tadā.
english: O Bhārata, crushed by him with force, and simultaneously overwhelmed by fear and sleep, that Pāñcāla prince (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) was then unable to stir.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. O Bhārata, crushed by him with force, and simultaneously overwhelmed by fear and sleep, that Pāñcāla prince (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) was then unable to stir.

sanskrit: तमाक्रम्य तदा राजन्कण्ठे चोरसि चोभयोः ।नदन्तं विस्फुरन्तं च पशुमारममारयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tamākramya tadā rājankaṇṭhe corasi cobhayoḥ ,nadantaṁ visphurantaṁ ca paśumāramamārayat.
english: Then, O king, having overpowered him, he killed him – who was crying out and struggling – in the manner of an animal's slaughter (paśumāram), striking him on both his neck and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. Then, O king, having overpowered him, he killed him – who was crying out and struggling – in the manner of an animal's slaughter (paśumāram), striking him on both his neck and chest.

sanskrit: तुदन्नखैस्तु स द्रौणिं नातिव्यक्तमुदाहरत् ।आचार्यपुत्र शस्त्रेण जहि मा मा चिरं कृथाः ।त्वत्कृते सुकृताँल्लोकान्गच्छेयं द्विपदां वर ॥१९॥
transliteration: tudannakhaistu sa drauṇiṁ nātivyaktamudāharat ,ācāryaputra śastreṇa jahi mā mā ciraṁ kṛthāḥ ,tvatkṛte sukṛtāँllokāngaccheyaṁ dvipadāṁ vara.
english: Indeed, even as he pierced him with his nails, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) spoke to Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), though not very clearly: 'O son of the teacher, kill me with a weapon! Do not delay! For your sake, O best among two-footed beings, may I attain the worlds (loka) of the virtuous.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. Indeed, even as he pierced him with his nails, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) spoke to Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), though not very clearly: 'O son of the teacher, kill me with a weapon! Do not delay! For your sake, O best among two-footed beings, may I attain the worlds (loka) of the virtuous.'

sanskrit: तस्याव्यक्तां तु तां वाचं संश्रुत्य द्रौणिरब्रवीत् ।आचार्यघातिनां लोका न सन्ति कुलपांसन ।तस्माच्छस्त्रेण निधनं न त्वमर्हसि दुर्मते ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasyāvyaktāṁ tu tāṁ vācaṁ saṁśrutya drauṇirabravīt ,ācāryaghātināṁ lokā na santi kulapāṁsana ,tasmācchastreṇa nidhanaṁ na tvamarhasi durmate.
english: However, upon hearing his (Dhṛṣṭadyumna's) indistinct voice, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) replied: 'O disgracer of your family, there are no good worlds (loka) for those who kill their teacher! Therefore, O evil-minded one, you do not deserve to die by a weapon!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. However, upon hearing his (Dhṛṣṭadyumna's) indistinct voice, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) replied: 'O disgracer of your family, there are no good worlds (loka) for those who kill their teacher! Therefore, O evil-minded one, you do not deserve to die by a weapon!'

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवाणस्तं वीरं सिंहो मत्तमिव द्विपम् ।मर्मस्वभ्यवधीत्क्रुद्धः पादाष्ठीलैः सुदारुणैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvāṇastaṁ vīraṁ siṁho mattamiva dvipam ,marmasvabhyavadhītkruddhaḥ pādāṣṭhīlaiḥ sudāruṇaiḥ.
english: Speaking thus, the enraged man, like a lion attacking an intoxicated elephant, struck that hero in his vital points with extremely terrible heel strikes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. Speaking thus, the enraged man, like a lion attacking an intoxicated elephant, struck that hero in his vital points with extremely terrible heel strikes.

sanskrit: तस्य वीरस्य शब्देन मार्यमाणस्य वेश्मनि ।अबुध्यन्त महाराज स्त्रियो ये चास्य रक्षिणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tasya vīrasya śabdena māryamāṇasya veśmani ,abudhyanta mahārāja striyo ye cāsya rakṣiṇaḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), by the sound of that hero being struck down in the house, the women and his guards awoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. O great king (mahārāja), by the sound of that hero being struck down in the house, the women and his guards awoke.

sanskrit: ते दृष्ट्वा वर्ष्मवन्तं तमतिमानुषविक्रमम् ।भूतमेव व्यवस्यन्तो न स्म प्रव्याहरन्भयात् ॥२३॥
transliteration: te dṛṣṭvā varṣmavantaṁ tamatimānuṣavikramam ,bhūtameva vyavasyanto na sma pravyāharanbhayāt.
english: Seeing him, who was corporeal and possessed of superhuman valor, they, concluding that he was a supernatural being, did not speak out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. Seeing him, who was corporeal and possessed of superhuman valor, they, concluding that he was a supernatural being, did not speak out of fear.

sanskrit: तं तु तेनाभ्युपायेन गमयित्वा यमक्षयम् ।अध्यतिष्ठत्स तेजस्वी रथं प्राप्य सुदर्शनम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: taṁ tu tenābhyupāyena gamayitvā yamakṣayam ,adhyatiṣṭhatsa tejasvī rathaṁ prāpya sudarśanam.
english: But that lustrous and powerful one (tejasvin), having dispatched him to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam) by that very means, then obtained and mounted the splendid chariot (sudarśanam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. But that lustrous and powerful one (tejasvin), having dispatched him to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam) by that very means, then obtained and mounted the splendid chariot (sudarśanam).

sanskrit: स तस्य भवनाद्राजन्निष्क्रम्यानादयन्दिशः ।रथेन शिबिरं प्रायाज्जिघांसुर्द्विषतो बली ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa tasya bhavanādrājanniṣkramyānādayandiśaḥ ,rathena śibiraṁ prāyājjighāṁsurdviṣato balī.
english: O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), that mighty one, having emerged from his dwelling and making the directions resound, proceeded by chariot to the camp, intending to kill his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), that mighty one, having emerged from his dwelling and making the directions resound, proceeded by chariot to the camp, intending to kill his enemies.

sanskrit: अपक्रान्ते ततस्तस्मिन्द्रोणपुत्रे महारथे ।सह तै रक्षिभिः सर्वैः प्रणेदुर्योषितस्तदा ॥२६॥
transliteration: apakrānte tatastasmindroṇaputre mahārathe ,saha tai rakṣibhiḥ sarvaiḥ praṇeduryoṣitastadā.
english: After that great chariot-warrior, the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman), had then departed, the women, along with all those guards, then wailed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. After that great chariot-warrior, the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman), had then departed, the women, along with all those guards, then wailed.

sanskrit: राजानं निहतं दृष्ट्वा भृशं शोकपरायणाः ।व्याक्रोशन्क्षत्रियाः सर्वे धृष्टद्युम्नस्य भारत ॥२७॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā bhṛśaṁ śokaparāyaṇāḥ ,vyākrośankṣatriyāḥ sarve dhṛṣṭadyumnasya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), all the Kṣatriyas, having seen king Dhṛṣṭadyumna slain, and deeply overwhelmed by grief, cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), all the Kṣatriyas, having seen king Dhṛṣṭadyumna slain, and deeply overwhelmed by grief, cried out.

sanskrit: तासां तु तेन शब्देन समीपे क्षत्रियर्षभाः ।क्षिप्रं च समनह्यन्त किमेतदिति चाब्रुवन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tāsāṁ tu tena śabdena samīpe kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ ,kṣipraṁ ca samanahyanta kimetaditi cābruvan.
english: But by that sound of theirs, the chief Kṣatriyas nearby quickly prepared themselves and said, "What is this?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. But by that sound of theirs, the chief Kṣatriyas nearby quickly prepared themselves and said, "What is this?"

sanskrit: स्त्रियस्तु राजन्वित्रस्ता भारद्वाजं निरीक्ष्य तम् ।अब्रुवन्दीनकण्ठेन क्षिप्रमाद्रवतेति वै ॥२९॥
transliteration: striyastu rājanvitrastā bhāradvājaṁ nirīkṣya tam ,abruvandīnakaṇṭhena kṣipramādravateti vai.
english: But, O King, the terrified women, seeing him, the son of Bharadvāja, exclaimed with a mournful voice, 'Quickly flee!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. But, O King, the terrified women, seeing him, the son of Bharadvāja, exclaimed with a mournful voice, 'Quickly flee!'

sanskrit: राक्षसो वा मनुष्यो वा नैनं जानीमहे वयम् ।हत्वा पाञ्चालराजं यो रथमारुह्य तिष्ठति ॥३०॥
transliteration: rākṣaso vā manuṣyo vā nainaṁ jānīmahe vayam ,hatvā pāñcālarājaṁ yo rathamāruhya tiṣṭhati.
english: We do not know if this one is a demon or a human, he who, having killed the king of Pañcāla, stands on the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. We do not know if this one is a demon or a human, he who, having killed the king of Pañcāla, stands on the chariot.

sanskrit: ततस्ते योधमुख्यास्तं सहसा पर्यवारयन् ।स तानापततः सर्वान्रुद्रास्त्रेण व्यपोथयत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tataste yodhamukhyāstaṁ sahasā paryavārayan ,sa tānāpatataḥ sarvānrudrāstreṇa vyapothayat.
english: Then, those chief warriors suddenly surrounded him. He, in turn, annihilated all of them who were attacking with a Rudra weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. Then, those chief warriors suddenly surrounded him. He, in turn, annihilated all of them who were attacking with a Rudra weapon.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं च हत्वा स तांश्चैवास्य पदानुगान् ।अपश्यच्छयने सुप्तमुत्तमौजसमन्तिके ॥३२॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca hatvā sa tāṁścaivāsya padānugān ,apaśyacchayane suptamuttamaujasamantike.
english: And having killed Dhṛṣṭadyumna, he then saw his followers and Uttamaujas, who was sleeping on his bed nearby.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. And having killed Dhṛṣṭadyumna, he then saw his followers and Uttamaujas, who was sleeping on his bed nearby.

sanskrit: तमप्याक्रम्य पादेन कण्ठे चोरसि चौजसा ।तथैव मारयामास विनर्दन्तमरिंदमम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tamapyākramya pādena kaṇṭhe corasi caujasā ,tathaiva mārayāmāsa vinardantamariṁdamam.
english: Stepping on the neck and chest of that roaring vanquisher of enemies with his foot and with great might, he killed him in just that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. Stepping on the neck and chest of that roaring vanquisher of enemies with his foot and with great might, he killed him in just that manner.

sanskrit: युधामन्युस्तु संप्राप्तो मत्त्वा तं रक्षसा हतम् ।गदामुद्यम्य वेगेन हृदि द्रौणिमताडयत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: yudhāmanyustu saṁprāpto mattvā taṁ rakṣasā hatam ,gadāmudyamya vegena hṛdi drauṇimatāḍayat.
english: But Yudhamanyu, having arrived and thinking that (the fallen warrior) had been killed by a demon (rakṣas), quickly raised his mace and struck Droṇi's son (Drauṇi) on the chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. But Yudhamanyu, having arrived and thinking that (the fallen warrior) had been killed by a demon (rakṣas), quickly raised his mace and struck Droṇi's son (Drauṇi) on the chest.

sanskrit: तमभिद्रुत्य जग्राह क्षितौ चैनमपातयत् ।विस्फुरन्तं च पशुवत्तथैवैनममारयत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tamabhidrutya jagrāha kṣitau cainamapātayat ,visphurantaṁ ca paśuvattathaivainamamārayat.
english: Rushing towards him, he seized him and threw him to the ground. And as he was writhing like an animal, he killed him in just that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. Rushing towards him, he seized him and threw him to the ground. And as he was writhing like an animal, he killed him in just that manner.

sanskrit: तथा स वीरो हत्वा तं ततोऽन्यान्समुपाद्रवत् ।संसुप्तानेव राजेन्द्र तत्र तत्र महारथान् ।स्फुरतो वेपमानांश्च शमितेव पशून्मखे ॥३६॥
transliteration: tathā sa vīro hatvā taṁ tato'nyānsamupādravat ,saṁsuptāneva rājendra tatra tatra mahārathān ,sphurato vepamānāṁśca śamiteva paśūnmakhe.
english: Thus, O king (rājendra), that hero, having killed him, then rushed towards the other deeply sleeping great charioteers (mahārathas) here and there. He killed them, though they were struggling and trembling, just as a sacrificer (śamitā) kills animals (paśus) in a ritual sacrifice (makha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. Thus, O king (rājendra), that hero, having killed him, then rushed towards the other deeply sleeping great charioteers (mahārathas) here and there. He killed them, though they were struggling and trembling, just as a sacrificer (śamitā) kills animals (paśus) in a ritual sacrifice (makha).

sanskrit: ततो निस्त्रिंशमादाय जघानान्यान्पृथग्जनान् ।भागशो विचरन्मार्गानसियुद्धविशारदः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tato nistriṁśamādāya jaghānānyānpṛthagjanān ,bhāgaśo vicaranmārgānasiyuddhaviśāradaḥ.
english: Then, taking his sword, he, an expert in sword-fighting, moving along the roads, killed others, the common people, group by group.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. Then, taking his sword, he, an expert in sword-fighting, moving along the roads, killed others, the common people, group by group.

sanskrit: तथैव गुल्मे संप्रेक्ष्य शयानान्मध्यगौल्मिकान् ।श्रान्तान्न्यस्तायुधान्सर्वान्क्षणेनैव व्यपोथयत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tathaiva gulme saṁprekṣya śayānānmadhyagaulmikān ,śrāntānnyastāyudhānsarvānkṣaṇenaiva vyapothayat.
english: Similarly, having seen all the middle guards sleeping in the encampment, exhausted and having put down their weapons, he destroyed them in an instant.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. Similarly, having seen all the middle guards sleeping in the encampment, exhausted and having put down their weapons, he destroyed them in an instant.

sanskrit: योधानश्वान्द्विपांश्चैव प्राच्छिनत्स वरासिना ।रुधिरोक्षितसर्वाङ्गः कालसृष्ट इवान्तकः ॥३९॥
transliteration: yodhānaśvāndvipāṁścaiva prācchinatsa varāsinā ,rudhirokṣitasarvāṅgaḥ kālasṛṣṭa ivāntakaḥ.
english: With his excellent sword, he cut down warriors, horses, and elephants. His entire body was drenched in blood, as if he were Antaka (the Destroyer) manifested by Kāla (Time/Death).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 39. With his excellent sword, he cut down warriors, horses, and elephants. His entire body was drenched in blood, as if he were Antaka (the Destroyer) manifested by Kāla (Time/Death).

sanskrit: विस्फुरद्भिश्च तैर्द्रौणिर्निस्त्रिंशस्योद्यमेन च ।आक्षेपेण तथैवासेस्त्रिधा रक्तोक्षितोऽभवत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: visphuradbhiśca tairdrauṇirnistriṁśasyodyamena ca ,ākṣepeṇa tathaivāsestridhā raktokṣito'bhavat.
english: Drauṇi became drenched in blood in three ways: by the writhing (bodies) and by them, by the exertion of wielding his long sword, and similarly by the forceful blows of the sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 40. Drauṇi became drenched in blood in three ways: by the writhing (bodies) and by them, by the exertion of wielding his long sword, and similarly by the forceful blows of the sword.

sanskrit: तस्य लोहितसिक्तस्य दीप्तखड्गस्य युध्यतः ।अमानुष इवाकारो बभौ परमभीषणः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tasya lohitasiktasya dīptakhaḍgasya yudhyataḥ ,amānuṣa ivākāro babhau paramabhīṣaṇaḥ.
english: As he fought, his form, soaked in blood and with a gleaming sword, appeared superhuman and exceedingly terrifying.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 41. As he fought, his form, soaked in blood and with a gleaming sword, appeared superhuman and exceedingly terrifying.

sanskrit: ये त्वजाग्रत कौरव्य तेऽपि शब्देन मोहिताः ।निरीक्ष्यमाणा अन्योन्यं द्रौणिं दृष्ट्वा प्रविव्यथुः ॥४२॥
transliteration: ye tvajāgrata kauravya te'pi śabdena mohitāḥ ,nirīkṣyamāṇā anyonyaṁ drauṇiṁ dṛṣṭvā pravivyathuḥ.
english: But O Kauravya, even those who were awake were bewildered by the sound. Looking at each other, they became greatly distressed upon seeing Drona's son (Drauṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 42. But O Kauravya, even those who were awake were bewildered by the sound. Looking at each other, they became greatly distressed upon seeing Drona's son (Drauṇi).

sanskrit: तद्रूपं तस्य ते दृष्ट्वा क्षत्रियाः शत्रुकर्शनाः ।राक्षसं मन्यमानास्तं नयनानि न्यमीलयन् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tadrūpaṁ tasya te dṛṣṭvā kṣatriyāḥ śatrukarśanāḥ ,rākṣasaṁ manyamānāstaṁ nayanāni nyamīlayan.
english: Having seen that form of his, those Kṣatriya warriors, tormentors of their enemies, thinking him to be a demon, closed their eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 43. Having seen that form of his, those Kṣatriya warriors, tormentors of their enemies, thinking him to be a demon, closed their eyes.

sanskrit: स घोररूपो व्यचरत्कालवच्छिबिरे ततः ।अपश्यद्द्रौपदीपुत्रानवशिष्टांश्च सोमकान् ॥४४॥
transliteration: sa ghorarūpo vyacaratkālavacchibire tataḥ ,apaśyaddraupadīputrānavaśiṣṭāṁśca somakān.
english: Then, he, of dreadful form, wandered in the camp like (the god of) Death. He saw the sons of Draupadi and the other remaining Somaka warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 44. Then, he, of dreadful form, wandered in the camp like (the god of) Death. He saw the sons of Draupadi and the other remaining Somaka warriors.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन वित्रस्ता धनुर्हस्ता महारथाः ।धृष्टद्युम्नं हतं श्रुत्वा द्रौपदेया विशां पते ।अवाकिरञ्शरव्रातैर्भारद्वाजमभीतवत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tena śabdena vitrastā dhanurhastā mahārathāḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ hataṁ śrutvā draupadeyā viśāṁ pate ,avākirañśaravrātairbhāradvājamabhītavat.
english: O ruler of men, the great chariot warriors, holding bows in their hands, became terrified by that sound. Hearing that Dhṛṣṭadyumna had been killed, the sons of Draupad then fearlessly showered Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 45. O ruler of men, the great chariot warriors, holding bows in their hands, became terrified by that sound. Hearing that Dhṛṣṭadyumna had been killed, the sons of Draupad then fearlessly showered Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तेन निनादेन संप्रबुद्धाः प्रभद्रकाः ।शिलीमुखैः शिखण्डी च द्रोणपुत्रं समार्दयन् ॥४६॥
transliteration: tatastena ninādena saṁprabuddhāḥ prabhadrakāḥ ,śilīmukhaiḥ śikhaṇḍī ca droṇaputraṁ samārdayan.
english: Then, awakened by that sound, the Prabhadrakas and Shikhandi tormented Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 46. Then, awakened by that sound, the Prabhadrakas and Shikhandi tormented Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) with arrows.

sanskrit: भारद्वाजस्तु तान्दृष्ट्वा शरवर्षाणि वर्षतः ।ननाद बलवन्नादं जिघांसुस्तान्सुदुर्जयान् ॥४७॥
transliteration: bhāradvājastu tāndṛṣṭvā śaravarṣāṇi varṣataḥ ,nanāda balavannādaṁ jighāṁsustānsudurjayān.
english: But Bhāradvāja (Droṇa), seeing them raining volleys of arrows, roared a mighty roar, desiring to kill those who were very difficult to conquer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 47. But Bhāradvāja (Droṇa), seeing them raining volleys of arrows, roared a mighty roar, desiring to kill those who were very difficult to conquer.

sanskrit: ततः परमसंक्रुद्धः पितुर्वधमनुस्मरन् ।अवरुह्य रथोपस्थात्त्वरमाणोऽभिदुद्रुवे ॥४८॥
transliteration: tataḥ paramasaṁkruddhaḥ piturvadhamanusmaran ,avaruhya rathopasthāttvaramāṇo'bhidudruve.
english: Then, exceedingly enraged and remembering his father's death, he quickly descended from the chariot's platform and rushed forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 48. Then, exceedingly enraged and remembering his father's death, he quickly descended from the chariot's platform and rushed forward.

sanskrit: सहस्रचन्द्रं विपुलं गृहीत्वा चर्म संयुगे ।खड्गं च विपुलं दिव्यं जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ।द्रौपदेयानभिद्रुत्य खड्गेन व्यचरद्बली ॥४९॥
transliteration: sahasracandraṁ vipulaṁ gṛhītvā carma saṁyuge ,khaḍgaṁ ca vipulaṁ divyaṁ jātarūpapariṣkṛtam ,draupadeyānabhidrutya khaḍgena vyacaradbalī.
english: The powerful warrior, grasping a large shield adorned with a thousand moon-like patterns and a vast, divine sword embellished with gold, attacked the sons of Draupadī and fought fiercely with his sword in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 49. The powerful warrior, grasping a large shield adorned with a thousand moon-like patterns and a vast, divine sword embellished with gold, attacked the sons of Draupadī and fought fiercely with his sword in battle.

sanskrit: ततः स नरशार्दूलः प्रतिविन्ध्यं तमाहवे ।कुक्षिदेशेऽवधीद्राजन्स हतो न्यपतद्भुवि ॥५०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa naraśārdūlaḥ prativindhyaṁ tamāhave ,kukṣideśe'vadhīdrājansa hato nyapatadbhuvi.
english: O king, then that foremost among men struck down Prativindhya in battle, hitting him in the stomach region. Having been slain, he fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 50. O king, then that foremost among men struck down Prativindhya in battle, hitting him in the stomach region. Having been slain, he fell to the ground.

sanskrit: प्रासेन विद्ध्वा द्रौणिं तु सुतसोमः प्रतापवान् ।पुनश्चासिं समुद्यम्य द्रोणपुत्रमुपाद्रवत् ॥५१॥
transliteration: prāsena viddhvā drauṇiṁ tu sutasomaḥ pratāpavān ,punaścāsiṁ samudyamya droṇaputramupādravat.
english: But the powerful Sutasoma, having struck Droṇi (Ashvatthama) with a spear, then again raised his sword and rushed towards Drona's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 51. But the powerful Sutasoma, having struck Droṇi (Ashvatthama) with a spear, then again raised his sword and rushed towards Drona's son.

sanskrit: सुतसोमस्य सासिं तु बाहुं छित्त्वा नरर्षभः ।पुनरभ्यहनत्पार्श्वे स भिन्नहृदयोऽपतत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: sutasomasya sāsiṁ tu bāhuṁ chittvā nararṣabhaḥ ,punarabhyahanatpārśve sa bhinnahṛdayo'patat.
english: But that foremost among men, having cut off Sutasoma's sword-wielding arm, again struck him in the flank. With his heart shattered, he fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 52. But that foremost among men, having cut off Sutasoma's sword-wielding arm, again struck him in the flank. With his heart shattered, he fell.

sanskrit: नाकुलिस्तु शतानीको रथचक्रेण वीर्यवान् ।दोर्भ्यामुत्क्षिप्य वेगेन वक्षस्येनमताडयत् ॥५३॥
transliteration: nākulistu śatānīko rathacakreṇa vīryavān ,dorbhyāmutkṣipya vegena vakṣasyenamatāḍayat.
english: The valorous Nakula, lifting a chariot wheel with his two arms, forcefully struck Śatānīka on the chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 53. The valorous Nakula, lifting a chariot wheel with his two arms, forcefully struck Śatānīka on the chest.

sanskrit: अताडयच्छतानीकं मुक्तचक्रं द्विजस्तु सः ।स विह्वलो ययौ भूमिं ततोऽस्यापाहरच्छिरः ॥५४॥
transliteration: atāḍayacchatānīkaṁ muktacakraṁ dvijastu saḥ ,sa vihvalo yayau bhūmiṁ tato'syāpāharacchiraḥ.
english: But that Brahmin (Aśvatthāman) struck Śatānīka, who had lost his wheel. Bewildered, he (Śatānīka) fell to the ground. Then (Aśvatthāman) cut off his head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 54. But that Brahmin (Aśvatthāman) struck Śatānīka, who had lost his wheel. Bewildered, he (Śatānīka) fell to the ground. Then (Aśvatthāman) cut off his head.

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्मा तु परिघं गृहीत्वा समताडयत् ।अभिद्रुत्य ततो द्रौणिं सव्ये स फलके भृशम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: śrutakarmā tu parighaṁ gṛhītvā samatāḍayat ,abhidrutya tato drauṇiṁ savye sa phalake bhṛśam.
english: But Śrutakarmā, having seized an iron club and then having rushed towards Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), struck him severely on his left shield.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 55. But Śrutakarmā, having seized an iron club and then having rushed towards Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), struck him severely on his left shield.

sanskrit: स तु तं श्रुतकर्माणमास्ये जघ्ने वरासिना ।स हतो न्यपतद्भूमौ विमूढो विकृताननः ॥५६॥
transliteration: sa tu taṁ śrutakarmāṇamāsye jaghne varāsinā ,sa hato nyapatadbhūmau vimūḍho vikṛtānanaḥ.
english: But he (Drauṇi/Aśvatthāman) struck Śrutakarmā on the face with an excellent sword. He (Śrutakarmā), thus struck and bewildered, fell to the ground with a distorted face.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 56. But he (Drauṇi/Aśvatthāman) struck Śrutakarmā on the face with an excellent sword. He (Śrutakarmā), thus struck and bewildered, fell to the ground with a distorted face.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन वीरस्तु श्रुतकीर्तिर्महाधनुः ।अश्वत्थामानमासाद्य शरवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tena śabdena vīrastu śrutakīrtirmahādhanuḥ ,aśvatthāmānamāsādya śaravarṣairavākirat.
english: Hearing that sound, the hero Śrutakīrti, wielding his great bow, approached Ashwatthaman and showered him with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 57. Hearing that sound, the hero Śrutakīrti, wielding his great bow, approached Ashwatthaman and showered him with arrows.

sanskrit: तस्यापि शरवर्षाणि चर्मणा प्रतिवार्य सः ।सकुण्डलं शिरः कायाद्भ्राजमानमपाहरत् ॥५८॥
transliteration: tasyāpi śaravarṣāṇi carmaṇā prativārya saḥ ,sakuṇḍalaṁ śiraḥ kāyādbhrājamānamapāharat.
english: Ashwatthaman, having warded off those showers of arrows with his shield, then severed from the body the shining head, adorned with earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 58. Ashwatthaman, having warded off those showers of arrows with his shield, then severed from the body the shining head, adorned with earrings.

sanskrit: ततो भीष्मनिहन्ता तं सह सर्वैः प्रभद्रकैः ।अहनत्सर्वतो वीरं नानाप्रहरणैर्बली ।शिलीमुखेन चाप्येनं भ्रुवोर्मध्ये समार्दयत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tato bhīṣmanihantā taṁ saha sarvaiḥ prabhadrakaiḥ ,ahanatsarvato vīraṁ nānāpraharaṇairbalī ,śilīmukhena cāpyenaṁ bhruvormadhye samārdayat.
english: Then, the powerful slayer of Bhishma (Shikhaṇḍī), along with all the Prabhadrkas, attacked that hero (Ashwatthaman) from all sides with various weapons. He also severely wounded him between the eyebrows with an arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 59. Then, the powerful slayer of Bhishma (Shikhaṇḍī), along with all the Prabhadrkas, attacked that hero (Ashwatthaman) from all sides with various weapons. He also severely wounded him between the eyebrows with an arrow.

sanskrit: स तु क्रोधसमाविष्टो द्रोणपुत्रो महाबलः ।शिखण्डिनं समासाद्य द्विधा चिच्छेद सोऽसिना ॥६०॥
transliteration: sa tu krodhasamāviṣṭo droṇaputro mahābalaḥ ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ samāsādya dvidhā ciccheda so'sinā.
english: But that extremely powerful son of Drona (Ashwatthaman), overcome with intense anger, approached Shikhaṇḍī and cut him into two with his sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 60. But that extremely powerful son of Drona (Ashwatthaman), overcome with intense anger, approached Shikhaṇḍī and cut him into two with his sword.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं ततो हत्वा क्रोधाविष्टः परंतपः ।प्रभद्रकगणान्सर्वानभिदुद्राव वेगवान् ।यच्च शिष्टं विराटस्य बलं तच्च समाद्रवत् ॥६१॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ tato hatvā krodhāviṣṭaḥ paraṁtapaḥ ,prabhadrakagaṇānsarvānabhidudrāva vegavān ,yacca śiṣṭaṁ virāṭasya balaṁ tacca samādravat.
english: Then, after killing Shikhandin, that tormentor of foes, inflamed with rage, swiftly charged towards all the Prabhadrka troops. And whatever remained of Virata's army, he also attacked that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 61. Then, after killing Shikhandin, that tormentor of foes, inflamed with rage, swiftly charged towards all the Prabhadrka troops. And whatever remained of Virata's army, he also attacked that.

sanskrit: द्रुपदस्य च पुत्राणां पौत्राणां सुहृदामपि ।चकार कदनं घोरं दृष्ट्वा दृष्ट्वा महाबलः ॥६२॥
transliteration: drupadasya ca putrāṇāṁ pautrāṇāṁ suhṛdāmapi ,cakāra kadanaṁ ghoraṁ dṛṣṭvā dṛṣṭvā mahābalaḥ.
english: And the immensely mighty one, repeatedly encountering them, brought about a dreadful slaughter of Drupada's sons, grandsons, and even his friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 62. And the immensely mighty one, repeatedly encountering them, brought about a dreadful slaughter of Drupada's sons, grandsons, and even his friends.

sanskrit: अन्यानन्यांश्च पुरुषानभिसृत्याभिसृत्य च ।न्यकृन्तदसिना द्रौणिरसिमार्गविशारदः ॥६३॥
transliteration: anyānanyāṁśca puruṣānabhisṛtyābhisṛtya ca ,nyakṛntadasinā drauṇirasimārgaviśāradaḥ.
english: And Drauṇi, Drona's son, who was skilled in sword fighting, repeatedly attacked and cut down other men (puruṣa) with his sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 63. And Drauṇi, Drona's son, who was skilled in sword fighting, repeatedly attacked and cut down other men (puruṣa) with his sword.

sanskrit: कालीं रक्तास्यनयनां रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनाम् ।रक्ताम्बरधरामेकां पाशहस्तां शिखण्डिनीम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: kālīṁ raktāsyanayanāṁ raktamālyānulepanām ,raktāmbaradharāmekāṁ pāśahastāṁ śikhaṇḍinīm.
english: (He beheld) a singular dark-skinned goddess, (like) Kālī, whose face and eyes were red, adorned with red garlands and smeared with red ointments, dressed in red garments, holding a noose in her hand, and crowned with a crest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 64. (He beheld) a singular dark-skinned goddess, (like) Kālī, whose face and eyes were red, adorned with red garlands and smeared with red ointments, dressed in red garments, holding a noose in her hand, and crowned with a crest.

sanskrit: ददृशुः कालरात्रिं ते स्मयमानामवस्थिताम् ।नराश्वकुञ्जरान्पाशैर्बद्ध्वा घोरैः प्रतस्थुषीम् ।हरन्तीं विविधान्प्रेतान्पाशबद्धान्विमूर्धजान् ॥६५॥
transliteration: dadṛśuḥ kālarātriṁ te smayamānāmavasthitām ,narāśvakuñjarānpāśairbaddhvā ghoraiḥ pratasthuṣīm ,harantīṁ vividhānpretānpāśabaddhānvimūrdhajān.
english: They saw Kalaratri, standing there and smiling. She was moving forth, having bound men, horses, and elephants with dreadful nooses, and carrying away various headless spirits who were also bound by her snares.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 65. They saw Kalaratri, standing there and smiling. She was moving forth, having bound men, horses, and elephants with dreadful nooses, and carrying away various headless spirits who were also bound by her snares.

sanskrit: स्वप्ने सुप्तान्नयन्तीं तां रात्रिष्वन्यासु मारिष ।ददृशुर्योधमुख्यास्ते घ्नन्तं द्रौणिं च नित्यदा ॥६६॥
transliteration: svapne suptānnayantīṁ tāṁ rātriṣvanyāsu māriṣa ,dadṛśuryodhamukhyāste ghnantaṁ drauṇiṁ ca nityadā.
english: O venerable one, on other nights, those chief warriors saw Kalaratri leading away the sleeping in their dreams. They also constantly saw Drauni (Ashvatthama) killing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 66. O venerable one, on other nights, those chief warriors saw Kalaratri leading away the sleeping in their dreams. They also constantly saw Drauni (Ashvatthama) killing.

sanskrit: यतः प्रवृत्तः संग्रामः कुरुपाण्डवसेनयोः ।ततः प्रभृति तां कृत्यामपश्यन्द्रौणिमेव च ॥६७॥
transliteration: yataḥ pravṛttaḥ saṁgrāmaḥ kurupāṇḍavasenayoḥ ,tataḥ prabhṛti tāṁ kṛtyāmapaśyandrauṇimeva ca.
english: From the moment the battle between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas commenced, from that point onwards, they beheld that Kṛtyā (Kalaratri) and Drauni (Ashvatthama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 67. From the moment the battle between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas commenced, from that point onwards, they beheld that Kṛtyā (Kalaratri) and Drauni (Ashvatthama).

sanskrit: तांस्तु दैवहतान्पूर्वं पश्चाद्द्रौणिर्न्यपातयत् ।त्रासयन्सर्वभूतानि विनदन्भैरवान्रवान् ॥६८॥
transliteration: tāṁstu daivahatānpūrvaṁ paścāddrauṇirnyapātayat ,trāsayansarvabhūtāni vinadanbhairavānravān.
english: But Drauni (Ashvatthama) struck down those who had previously been slain by divine will, terrifying all beings and uttering dreadful roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 68. But Drauni (Ashvatthama) struck down those who had previously been slain by divine will, terrifying all beings and uttering dreadful roars.

sanskrit: तदनुस्मृत्य ते वीरा दर्शनं पौर्वकालिकम् ।इदं तदित्यमन्यन्त दैवेनोपनिपीडिताः ॥६९॥
transliteration: tadanusmṛtya te vīrā darśanaṁ paurvakālikam ,idaṁ tadityamanyanta daivenopanipīḍitāḥ.
english: Remembering that prior vision, those warriors, oppressed by fate, believed, "This is that (predicted event)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 69. Remembering that prior vision, those warriors, oppressed by fate, believed, "This is that (predicted event)."

sanskrit: ततस्तेन निनादेन प्रत्यबुध्यन्त धन्विनः ।शिबिरे पाण्डवेयानां शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥७०॥
transliteration: tatastena ninādena pratyabudhyanta dhanvinaḥ ,śibire pāṇḍaveyānāṁ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, by that sound, hundreds and thousands of archers in the camp of the Pāṇḍavas awoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 70. Then, by that sound, hundreds and thousands of archers in the camp of the Pāṇḍavas awoke.

sanskrit: सोऽच्छिनत्कस्यचित्पादौ जघनं चैव कस्यचित् ।कांश्चिद्बिभेद पार्श्वेषु कालसृष्ट इवान्तकः ॥७१॥
transliteration: so'cchinatkasyacitpādau jaghanaṁ caiva kasyacit ,kāṁścidbibheda pārśveṣu kālasṛṣṭa ivāntakaḥ.
english: He cut off someone's feet, and someone else's hip, and he pierced some in their sides, like Death (antaka) created by time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 71. He cut off someone's feet, and someone else's hip, and he pierced some in their sides, like Death (antaka) created by time.

sanskrit: अत्युग्रप्रतिपिष्टैश्च नदद्भिश्च भृशातुरैः ।गजाश्वमथितैश्चान्यैर्मही कीर्णाभवत्प्रभो ॥७२॥
transliteration: atyugrapratipiṣṭaiśca nadadbhiśca bhṛśāturaiḥ ,gajāśvamathitaiścānyairmahī kīrṇābhavatprabho.
english: And by those crushed with extreme violence, and by those roaring and greatly distressed, and by others trampled by elephants and horses, the earth became covered, O Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 72. And by those crushed with extreme violence, and by those roaring and greatly distressed, and by others trampled by elephants and horses, the earth became covered, O Lord.

sanskrit: क्रोशतां किमिदं कोऽयं किं शब्दः किं नु किं कृतम् ।एवं तेषां तदा द्रौणिरन्तकः समपद्यत ॥७३॥
transliteration: krośatāṁ kimidaṁ ko'yaṁ kiṁ śabdaḥ kiṁ nu kiṁ kṛtam ,evaṁ teṣāṁ tadā drauṇirantakaḥ samapadyata.
english: As they cried out, asking, 'What is this? Who is this? What is this sound? What indeed? What has been done?', Drauṇi then became death (antaka) for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 73. As they cried out, asking, 'What is this? Who is this? What is this sound? What indeed? What has been done?', Drauṇi then became death (antaka) for them.

sanskrit: अपेतशस्त्रसंनाहान्संरब्धान्पाण्डुसृञ्जयान् ।प्राहिणोन्मृत्युलोकाय द्रौणिः प्रहरतां वरः ॥७४॥
transliteration: apetaśastrasaṁnāhānsaṁrabdhānpāṇḍusṛñjayān ,prāhiṇonmṛtyulokāya drauṇiḥ praharatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Drauṇi, the best among attackers, dispatched to the realm of death (mṛtyuloka) those Pāṇḍavas and Sṛñjayas who were disarmed and frantic.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 74. Drauṇi, the best among attackers, dispatched to the realm of death (mṛtyuloka) those Pāṇḍavas and Sṛñjayas who were disarmed and frantic.

sanskrit: ततस्तच्छस्त्रवित्रस्ता उत्पतन्तो भयातुराः ।निद्रान्धा नष्टसंज्ञाश्च तत्र तत्र निलिल्यिरे ॥७५॥
transliteration: tatastacchastravitrastā utpatanto bhayāturāḥ ,nidrāndhā naṣṭasaṁjñāśca tatra tatra nililyire.
english: Then, terrified by those weapons, panic-stricken and attempting to flee, those who were blinded by sleep and had lost their senses scattered and hid themselves here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 75. Then, terrified by those weapons, panic-stricken and attempting to flee, those who were blinded by sleep and had lost their senses scattered and hid themselves here and there.

sanskrit: ऊरुस्तम्भगृहीताश्च कश्मलाभिहतौजसः ।विनदन्तो भृशं त्रस्ताः संन्यपेषन्परस्परम् ॥७६॥
transliteration: ūrustambhagṛhītāśca kaśmalābhihataujasaḥ ,vinadanto bhṛśaṁ trastāḥ saṁnyapeṣanparasparam.
english: And, seized by paralysis of the thighs, with their strength destroyed by bewilderment, they cried out loudly, exceedingly terrified, and trampled each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 76. And, seized by paralysis of the thighs, with their strength destroyed by bewilderment, they cried out loudly, exceedingly terrified, and trampled each other.

sanskrit: ततो रथं पुनर्द्रौणिरास्थितो भीमनिस्वनम् ।धनुष्पाणिः शरैरन्यान्प्रेषयद्वै यमक्षयम् ॥७७॥
transliteration: tato rathaṁ punardrauṇirāsthito bhīmanisvanam ,dhanuṣpāṇiḥ śarairanyānpreṣayadvai yamakṣayam.
english: Droṇi, having mounted his chariot again, which produced a formidable sound, and holding his bow in hand, then sent other warriors to the abode of Yama (Yama-kṣaya) with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 77. Droṇi, having mounted his chariot again, which produced a formidable sound, and holding his bow in hand, then sent other warriors to the abode of Yama (Yama-kṣaya) with his arrows.

sanskrit: पुनरुत्पततः कांश्चिद्दूरादपि नरोत्तमान् ।शूरान्संपततश्चान्यान्कालरात्र्यै न्यवेदयत् ॥७८॥
transliteration: punarutpatataḥ kāṁściddūrādapi narottamān ,śūrānsaṁpatataścānyānkālarātryai nyavedayat.
english: Again, he offered some excellent men (narottama) who were leaping up, and other brave warriors (śūra) who were rushing forward, even from a distance, to Kalaratri, the night of cosmic dissolution.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 78. Again, he offered some excellent men (narottama) who were leaping up, and other brave warriors (śūra) who were rushing forward, even from a distance, to Kalaratri, the night of cosmic dissolution.

sanskrit: तथैव स्यन्दनाग्रेण प्रमथन्स विधावति ।शरवर्षैश्च विविधैरवर्षच्छात्रवांस्ततः ॥७९॥
transliteration: tathaiva syandanāgreṇa pramathansa vidhāvati ,śaravarṣaiśca vividhairavarṣacchātravāṁstataḥ.
english: In the same manner, he ran forward, crushing (pramathan) with the front of his chariot, and then, with diverse showers of arrows, he rained upon his enemies (śātravān).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 79. In the same manner, he ran forward, crushing (pramathan) with the front of his chariot, and then, with diverse showers of arrows, he rained upon his enemies (śātravān).

sanskrit: पुनश्च सुविचित्रेण शतचन्द्रेण चर्मणा ।तेन चाकाशवर्णेन तदाचरत सोऽसिना ॥८०॥
transliteration: punaśca suvicitreṇa śatacandreṇa carmaṇā ,tena cākāśavarṇena tadācarata so'sinā.
english: And again, he then fought with a very wonderfully embellished shield (carman) marked with a hundred moons (śatachandra), and with that sky-colored sword (asi).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 80. And again, he then fought with a very wonderfully embellished shield (carman) marked with a hundred moons (śatachandra), and with that sky-colored sword (asi).

sanskrit: तथा स शिबिरं तेषां द्रौणिराहवदुर्मदः ।व्यक्षोभयत राजेन्द्र महाह्रदमिव द्विपः ॥८१॥
transliteration: tathā sa śibiraṁ teṣāṁ drauṇirāhavadurmadaḥ ,vyakṣobhayata rājendra mahāhradamiva dvipaḥ.
english: O king, just as an elephant agitates a great lake, so did Drona's son (Drauṇi), intoxicated with battle, disturb their camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 81. O king, just as an elephant agitates a great lake, so did Drona's son (Drauṇi), intoxicated with battle, disturb their camp.

sanskrit: उत्पेतुस्तेन शब्देन योधा राजन्विचेतसः ।निद्रार्ताश्च भयार्ताश्च व्यधावन्त ततस्ततः ॥८२॥
transliteration: utpetustena śabdena yodhā rājanvicetasaḥ ,nidrārtāśca bhayārtāśca vyadhāvanta tatastataḥ.
english: O king, at that sound, the bewildered warriors sprang up, both afflicted by sleep and terrified, and ran about in every direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 82. O king, at that sound, the bewildered warriors sprang up, both afflicted by sleep and terrified, and ran about in every direction.

sanskrit: विस्वरं चुक्रुशुश्चान्ये बह्वबद्धं तथावदन् ।न च स्म प्रतिपद्यन्ते शस्त्राणि वसनानि च ॥८३॥
transliteration: visvaraṁ cukruśuścānye bahvabaddhaṁ tathāvadan ,na ca sma pratipadyante śastrāṇi vasanāni ca.
english: And others shrieked discordantly and spoke much nonsense. They were unable to find their weapons or their clothes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 83. And others shrieked discordantly and spoke much nonsense. They were unable to find their weapons or their clothes.

sanskrit: विमुक्तकेशाश्चाप्यन्ये नाभ्यजानन्परस्परम् ।उत्पतन्तः परे भीताः केचित्तत्र तथाभ्रमन् ।पुरीषमसृजन्केचित्केचिन्मूत्रं प्रसुस्रुवुः ॥८४॥
transliteration: vimuktakeśāścāpyanye nābhyajānanparasparam ,utpatantaḥ pare bhītāḥ kecittatra tathābhraman ,purīṣamasṛjankecitkecinmūtraṁ prasusruvuḥ.
english: And some others, with dishevelled hair, could not even recognize each other. Some of the terrified ones sprang up and wandered about there in confusion. Others discharged feces, and still others released urine.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 84. And some others, with dishevelled hair, could not even recognize each other. Some of the terrified ones sprang up and wandered about there in confusion. Others discharged feces, and still others released urine.

sanskrit: बन्धनानि च राजेन्द्र संछिद्य तुरगा द्विपाः ।समं पर्यपतंश्चान्ये कुर्वन्तो महदाकुलम् ॥८५॥
transliteration: bandhanāni ca rājendra saṁchidya turagā dvipāḥ ,samaṁ paryapataṁścānye kurvanto mahadākulam.
english: O best of kings, having broken their bonds, horses, elephants, and other creatures simultaneously ran about, causing great confusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 85. O best of kings, having broken their bonds, horses, elephants, and other creatures simultaneously ran about, causing great confusion.

sanskrit: तत्र केचिन्नरा भीता व्यलीयन्त महीतले ।तथैव तान्निपतितानपिंषन्गजवाजिनः ॥८६॥
transliteration: tatra kecinnarā bhītā vyalīyanta mahītale ,tathaiva tānnipatitānapiṁṣangajavājinaḥ.
english: There, some frightened men hid themselves on the ground. Similarly, elephants and horses crushed those who had fallen.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 86. There, some frightened men hid themselves on the ground. Similarly, elephants and horses crushed those who had fallen.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तथा वर्तमाने रक्षांसि पुरुषर्षभ ।तृप्तानि व्यनदन्नुच्चैर्मुदा भरतसत्तम ॥८७॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstathā vartamāne rakṣāṁsi puruṣarṣabha ,tṛptāni vyanadannuccairmudā bharatasattama.
english: O best among men, O foremost of Bharatas, while that situation was thus unfolding, the satisfied rākṣasas roared loudly with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 87. O best among men, O foremost of Bharatas, while that situation was thus unfolding, the satisfied rākṣasas roared loudly with joy.

sanskrit: स शब्दः प्रेरितो राजन्भूतसंघैर्मुदा युतैः ।अपूरयद्दिशः सर्वा दिवं चापि महास्वनः ॥८८॥
transliteration: sa śabdaḥ prerito rājanbhūtasaṁghairmudā yutaiḥ ,apūrayaddiśaḥ sarvā divaṁ cāpi mahāsvanaḥ.
english: O King, that great sound (mahāsvana), produced by the joyful hosts of beings, filled all directions and also the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 88. O King, that great sound (mahāsvana), produced by the joyful hosts of beings, filled all directions and also the sky.

sanskrit: तेषामार्तस्वरं श्रुत्वा वित्रस्ता गजवाजिनः ।मुक्ताः पर्यपतन्राजन्मृद्नन्तः शिबिरे जनम् ॥८९॥
transliteration: teṣāmārtasvaraṁ śrutvā vitrastā gajavājinaḥ ,muktāḥ paryapatanrājanmṛdnantaḥ śibire janam.
english: O king, having heard their cry of distress, the terrified elephants and horses, now freed, rushed about, trampling the people in the camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 89. O king, having heard their cry of distress, the terrified elephants and horses, now freed, rushed about, trampling the people in the camp.

sanskrit: तैस्तत्र परिधावद्भिश्चरणोदीरितं रजः ।अकरोच्छिबिरे तेषां रजन्यां द्विगुणं तमः ॥९०॥
transliteration: taistatra paridhāvadbhiścaraṇodīritaṁ rajaḥ ,akarocchibire teṣāṁ rajanyāṁ dviguṇaṁ tamaḥ.
english: The dust, stirred up by the feet of those (animals) running about there, made the darkness in their camp twice as dense during the night.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 90. The dust, stirred up by the feet of those (animals) running about there, made the darkness in their camp twice as dense during the night.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तमसि संजाते प्रमूढाः सर्वतो जनाः ।नाजानन्पितरः पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्भ्रातर एव च ॥९१॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstamasi saṁjāte pramūḍhāḥ sarvato janāḥ ,nājānanpitaraḥ putrānbhrātṝnbhrātara eva ca.
english: With that darkness having arisen, people everywhere became utterly bewildered. Fathers could not recognize their sons, nor could brothers recognize their brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 91. With that darkness having arisen, people everywhere became utterly bewildered. Fathers could not recognize their sons, nor could brothers recognize their brothers.

sanskrit: गजा गजानतिक्रम्य निर्मनुष्या हया हयान् ।अताडयंस्तथाभञ्जंस्तथामृद्नंश्च भारत ॥९२॥
transliteration: gajā gajānatikramya nirmanuṣyā hayā hayān ,atāḍayaṁstathābhañjaṁstathāmṛdnaṁśca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the elephants trampled other elephants, and the riderless horses trampled other horses. They struck them, broke them, and crushed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 92. O Bhārata, the elephants trampled other elephants, and the riderless horses trampled other horses. They struck them, broke them, and crushed them.

sanskrit: ते भग्नाः प्रपतन्तश्च निघ्नन्तश्च परस्परम् ।न्यपातयन्त च परान्पातयित्वा तथापिषन् ॥९३॥
transliteration: te bhagnāḥ prapatantaśca nighnantaśca parasparam ,nyapātayanta ca parānpātayitvā tathāpiṣan.
english: Those who were routed fell down, striking one another. They also caused others to fall and, having made them fall, they similarly crushed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 93. Those who were routed fell down, striking one another. They also caused others to fall and, having made them fall, they similarly crushed them.

sanskrit: विचेतसः सनिद्राश्च तमसा चावृता नराः ।जघ्नुः स्वानेव तत्राथ कालेनाभिप्रचोदिताः ॥९४॥
transliteration: vicetasaḥ sanidrāśca tamasā cāvṛtā narāḥ ,jaghnuḥ svāneva tatrātha kālenābhipracoditāḥ.
english: Bewildered, drowsy, and overwhelmed by darkness (tamas), those men then killed their own people, driven by destiny (kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 94. Bewildered, drowsy, and overwhelmed by darkness (tamas), those men then killed their own people, driven by destiny (kāla).

sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा द्वाराणि च द्वाःस्थास्तथा गुल्मांश्च गौल्मिकाः ।प्राद्रवन्त यथाशक्ति कांदिशीका विचेतसः ॥९५॥
transliteration: tyaktvā dvārāṇi ca dvāḥsthāstathā gulmāṁśca gaulmikāḥ ,prādravanta yathāśakti kāṁdiśīkā vicetasaḥ.
english: The gatekeepers, abandoning their posts at the gates, and likewise the commanders, abandoning their units, fled as fast as they could, bewildered and panic-stricken.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 95. The gatekeepers, abandoning their posts at the gates, and likewise the commanders, abandoning their units, fled as fast as they could, bewildered and panic-stricken.
