sanskrit: विप्रनष्टाश्च तेऽन्योन्यं नाजानन्त तदा विभो ।क्रोशन्तस्तात पुत्रेति दैवोपहतचेतसः ॥९६॥
transliteration: vipranaṣṭāśca te'nyonyaṁ nājānanta tadā vibho ,krośantastāta putreti daivopahatacetasaḥ.
english: And those men were utterly lost, O great one (vibhu), no longer recognizing one another at that time. With their minds overwhelmed by destiny, they cried out, 'Father!' and 'Son!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 96. And those men were utterly lost, O great one (vibhu), no longer recognizing one another at that time. With their minds overwhelmed by destiny, they cried out, 'Father!' and 'Son!'

sanskrit: पलायतां दिशस्तेषां स्वानप्युत्सृज्य बान्धवान् ।गोत्रनामभिरन्योन्यमाक्रन्दन्त ततो जनाः ॥९७॥
transliteration: palāyatāṁ diśasteṣāṁ svānapyutsṛjya bāndhavān ,gotranāmabhiranyonyamākrandanta tato janāḥ.
english: Then, abandoning even their own relatives, those people fled in all directions, crying out to each other by their family names.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 97. Then, abandoning even their own relatives, those people fled in all directions, crying out to each other by their family names.

sanskrit: हाहाकारं च कुर्वाणाः पृथिव्यां शेरते परे ।तान्बुद्ध्वा रणमत्तोऽसौ द्रोणपुत्रो व्यपोथयत् ॥९८॥
transliteration: hāhākāraṁ ca kurvāṇāḥ pṛthivyāṁ śerate pare ,tānbuddhvā raṇamatto'sau droṇaputro vyapothayat.
english: Others, crying out in lamentation, lay on the ground. Recognizing them, the son of Droṇa, who was intoxicated by battle, crushed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 98. Others, crying out in lamentation, lay on the ground. Recognizing them, the son of Droṇa, who was intoxicated by battle, crushed them.

sanskrit: तत्रापरे वध्यमाना मुहुर्मुहुरचेतसः ।शिबिरान्निष्पतन्ति स्म क्षत्रिया भयपीडिताः ॥९९॥
transliteration: tatrāpare vadhyamānā muhurmuhuracetasaḥ ,śibirānniṣpatanti sma kṣatriyā bhayapīḍitāḥ.
english: There, other warriors, repeatedly being slain, unconscious and afflicted by fear, were fleeing from the camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 99. There, other warriors, repeatedly being slain, unconscious and afflicted by fear, were fleeing from the camp.

sanskrit: तांस्तु निष्पततस्त्रस्ताञ्शिबिराञ्जीवितैषिणः ।कृतवर्मा कृपश्चैव द्वारदेशे निजघ्नतुः ॥१००॥
transliteration: tāṁstu niṣpatatastrastāñśibirāñjīvitaiṣiṇaḥ ,kṛtavarmā kṛpaścaiva dvāradeśe nijaghnatuḥ.
english: But Kṛtavarmā and Kṛpa struck down, at the gate, those terrified individuals who, desiring to live, were fleeing from the camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 100. But Kṛtavarmā and Kṛpa struck down, at the gate, those terrified individuals who, desiring to live, were fleeing from the camp.

sanskrit: विशस्त्रयन्त्रकवचान्मुक्तकेशान्कृताञ्जलीन् ।वेपमानान्क्षितौ भीतान्नैव कांश्चिदमुञ्चताम् ॥१०१॥
transliteration: viśastrayantrakavacānmuktakeśānkṛtāñjalīn ,vepamānānkṣitau bhītānnaiva kāṁścidamuñcatām.
english: They (the two) did not spare anyone who was without weapons, devices, or armor, with loose hair, hands folded in supplication (kṛtāñjalīn), trembling, and terrified on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 101. They (the two) did not spare anyone who was without weapons, devices, or armor, with loose hair, hands folded in supplication (kṛtāñjalīn), trembling, and terrified on the ground.

sanskrit: नामुच्यत तयोः कश्चिन्निष्क्रान्तः शिबिराद्बहिः ।कृपस्य च महाराज हार्दिक्यस्य च दुर्मतेः ॥१०२॥
transliteration: nāmucyata tayoḥ kaścinniṣkrāntaḥ śibirādbahiḥ ,kṛpasya ca mahārāja hārdikyasya ca durmateḥ.
english: O great king, no one who had left the camp was spared by those two, Kṛpa and the evil-minded Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 102. O great king, no one who had left the camp was spared by those two, Kṛpa and the evil-minded Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman).

sanskrit: भूयश्चैव चिकीर्षन्तौ द्रोणपुत्रस्य तौ प्रियम् ।त्रिषु देशेषु ददतुः शिबिरस्य हुताशनम् ॥१०३॥
transliteration: bhūyaścaiva cikīrṣantau droṇaputrasya tau priyam ,triṣu deśeṣu dadatuḥ śibirasya hutāśanam.
english: Moreover, those two, intending to please Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), set fire to the camp in three places.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 103. Moreover, those two, intending to please Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), set fire to the camp in three places.

sanskrit: ततः प्रकाशे शिबिरे खड्गेन पितृनन्दनः ।अश्वत्थामा महाराज व्यचरत्कृतहस्तवत् ॥१०४॥
transliteration: tataḥ prakāśe śibire khaḍgena pitṛnandanaḥ ,aśvatthāmā mahārāja vyacaratkṛtahastavat.
english: Then, O great king, in the illuminated camp, Aśvatthāmā, the son who delights his father, moved about with his sword like a skilled warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 104. Then, O great king, in the illuminated camp, Aśvatthāmā, the son who delights his father, moved about with his sword like a skilled warrior.

sanskrit: कांश्चिदापततो वीरानपरांश्च प्रधावतः ।व्ययोजयत खड्गेन प्राणैर्द्विजवरो नरान् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: kāṁścidāpatato vīrānaparāṁśca pradhāvataḥ ,vyayojayata khaḍgena prāṇairdvijavaro narān.
english: The excellent Brahmin (dvijavara) with his sword separated some attacking warriors and others who were fleeing from their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 105. The excellent Brahmin (dvijavara) with his sword separated some attacking warriors and others who were fleeing from their lives.

sanskrit: कांश्चिद्योधान्स खड्गेन मध्ये संछिद्य वीर्यवान् ।अपातयद्द्रोणसुतः संरब्धस्तिलकाण्डवत् ॥१०६॥
transliteration: kāṁścidyodhānsa khaḍgena madhye saṁchidya vīryavān ,apātayaddroṇasutaḥ saṁrabdhastilakāṇḍavat.
english: That valorous and enraged son of Droṇa (Droṇasuta), having cut some warriors in the middle with his sword, made them fall like sesame stalks.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 106. That valorous and enraged son of Droṇa (Droṇasuta), having cut some warriors in the middle with his sword, made them fall like sesame stalks.

sanskrit: विनदद्भिर्भृशायस्तैर्नराश्वद्विरदोत्तमैः ।पतितैरभवत्कीर्णा मेदिनी भरतर्षभ ॥१०७॥
transliteration: vinadadbhirbhṛśāyastairnarāśvadviradottamaiḥ ,patitairabhavatkīrṇā medinī bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, the earth (medinī) became strewn with fallen men, horses, and excellent elephants, who were roaring and utterly exhausted.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 107. O best of Bharatas, the earth (medinī) became strewn with fallen men, horses, and excellent elephants, who were roaring and utterly exhausted.

sanskrit: मानुषाणां सहस्रेषु हतेषु पतितेषु च ।उदतिष्ठन्कबन्धानि बहून्युत्थाय चापतन् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: mānuṣāṇāṁ sahasreṣu hateṣu patiteṣu ca ,udatiṣṭhankabandhāni bahūnyutthāya cāpatan.
english: Among the thousands of men who were slain and fallen, many headless trunks (kabandhas) rose up, and having stood up, they then fell down.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 108. Among the thousands of men who were slain and fallen, many headless trunks (kabandhas) rose up, and having stood up, they then fell down.

sanskrit: सायुधान्साङ्गदान्बाहून्निचकर्त शिरांसि च ।हस्तिहस्तोपमानूरून्हस्तान्पादांश्च भारत ॥१०९॥
transliteration: sāyudhānsāṅgadānbāhūnnicakarta śirāṁsi ca ,hastihastopamānūrūnhastānpādāṁśca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, he cut off arms that were equipped with weapons and armlets, along with heads, and also thighs resembling elephant trunks, hands, and feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 109. O Bhārata, he cut off arms that were equipped with weapons and armlets, along with heads, and also thighs resembling elephant trunks, hands, and feet.

sanskrit: पृष्ठच्छिन्नाञ्शिरश्छिन्नान्पार्श्वच्छिन्नांस्तथापरान् ।समासाद्याकरोद्द्रौणिः कांश्चिच्चापि पराङ्मुखान् ॥११०॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhacchinnāñśiraśchinnānpārśvacchinnāṁstathāparān ,samāsādyākaroddrauṇiḥ kāṁściccāpi parāṅmukhān.
english: Having approached them, Drauṇi cut some at their backs, some at their heads, and others at their sides. He also made some turn and flee.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 110. Having approached them, Drauṇi cut some at their backs, some at their heads, and others at their sides. He also made some turn and flee.

sanskrit: मध्यकायान्नरानन्यांश्चिच्छेदान्यांश्च कर्णतः ।अंसदेशे निहत्यान्यान्काये प्रावेशयच्छिरः ॥१११॥
transliteration: madhyakāyānnarānanyāṁścicchedānyāṁśca karṇataḥ ,aṁsadeśe nihatyānyānkāye prāveśayacchiraḥ.
english: He cut other men through their midsections, and others at their ears. Striking some others in the shoulder region, he drove their heads into their bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 111. He cut other men through their midsections, and others at their ears. Striking some others in the shoulder region, he drove their heads into their bodies.

sanskrit: एवं विचरतस्तस्य निघ्नतः सुबहून्नरान् ।तमसा रजनी घोरा बभौ दारुणदर्शना ॥११२॥
transliteration: evaṁ vicaratastasya nighnataḥ subahūnnarān ,tamasā rajanī ghorā babhau dāruṇadarśanā.
english: As he was thus ranging about, striking down very many men, the dreadful night appeared, terrifying to behold due to the darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 112. As he was thus ranging about, striking down very many men, the dreadful night appeared, terrifying to behold due to the darkness.

sanskrit: किंचित्प्राणैश्च पुरुषैर्हतैश्चान्यैः सहस्रशः ।बहुना च गजाश्वेन भूरभूद्भीमदर्शना ॥११३॥
transliteration: kiṁcitprāṇaiśca puruṣairhataiścānyaiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,bahunā ca gajāśvena bhūrabhūdbhīmadarśanā.
english: With many lives [lost], and by thousands of slain men (puruṣa) and others, and with numerous elephants and horses, the earth became a terrifying sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 113. With many lives [lost], and by thousands of slain men (puruṣa) and others, and with numerous elephants and horses, the earth became a terrifying sight.

sanskrit: यक्षरक्षःसमाकीर्णे रथाश्वद्विपदारुणे ।क्रुद्धेन द्रोणपुत्रेण संछिन्नाः प्रापतन्भुवि ॥११४॥
transliteration: yakṣarakṣaḥsamākīrṇe rathāśvadvipadāruṇe ,kruddhena droṇaputreṇa saṁchinnāḥ prāpatanbhuvi.
english: On that field, which was made terrifying by chariots, horses, and elephants, and filled with Yakṣas and Rākṣasas [who feast on the dead], [warriors], cut down by the enraged son of Droṇa, fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 114. On that field, which was made terrifying by chariots, horses, and elephants, and filled with Yakṣas and Rākṣasas [who feast on the dead], [warriors], cut down by the enraged son of Droṇa, fell to the ground.

sanskrit: मातॄरन्ये पितॄनन्ये भ्रातॄनन्ये विचुक्रुशुः ।केचिदूचुर्न तत्क्रुद्धैर्धार्तराष्ट्रैः कृतं रणे ॥११५॥
transliteration: mātṝranye pitṝnanye bhrātṝnanye vicukruśuḥ ,kecidūcurna tatkruddhairdhārtarāṣṭraiḥ kṛtaṁ raṇe.
english: Some wailed for their mothers, others for their fathers, and still others for their brothers. Some (among them) said, 'This was not done in battle by the enraged Dhṛtarāṣṭras (Dhārtarāṣṭras).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 115. Some wailed for their mothers, others for their fathers, and still others for their brothers. Some (among them) said, 'This was not done in battle by the enraged Dhṛtarāṣṭras (Dhārtarāṣṭras).'

sanskrit: यत्कृतं नः प्रसुप्तानां रक्षोभिः क्रूरकर्मभिः ।असांनिध्याद्धि पार्थानामिदं नः कदनं कृतम् ॥११६॥
transliteration: yatkṛtaṁ naḥ prasuptānāṁ rakṣobhiḥ krūrakarmabhiḥ ,asāṁnidhyāddhi pārthānāmidaṁ naḥ kadanaṁ kṛtam.
english: What was inflicted upon us while we were asleep, as if by cruel-acting Rākṣasas  -  this slaughter was indeed wrought upon us due to the absence of the Pārthas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 116. What was inflicted upon us while we were asleep, as if by cruel-acting Rākṣasas  -  this slaughter was indeed wrought upon us due to the absence of the Pārthas.

sanskrit: न देवासुरगन्धर्वैर्न यक्षैर्न च राक्षसैः ।शक्यो विजेतुं कौन्तेयो गोप्ता यस्य जनार्दनः ॥११७॥
transliteration: na devāsuragandharvairna yakṣairna ca rākṣasaiḥ ,śakyo vijetuṁ kaunteyo goptā yasya janārdanaḥ.
english: Arjuna (Kaunteya), whose protector is Janardana (Krishna), cannot be conquered by devas, asuras, gandharvas, yakṣas, or rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 117. Arjuna (Kaunteya), whose protector is Janardana (Krishna), cannot be conquered by devas, asuras, gandharvas, yakṣas, or rākṣasas.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्यः सत्यवाग्दान्तः सर्वभूतानुकम्पकः ।न च सुप्तं प्रमत्तं वा न्यस्तशस्त्रं कृताञ्जलिम् ।धावन्तं मुक्तकेशं वा हन्ति पार्थो धनंजयः ॥११८॥
transliteration: brahmaṇyaḥ satyavāgdāntaḥ sarvabhūtānukampakaḥ ,na ca suptaṁ pramattaṁ vā nyastaśastraṁ kṛtāñjalim ,dhāvantaṁ muktakeśaṁ vā hanti pārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Partha (Arjuna) Dhananjaya is devoted to the Absolute (brahman), truthful in speech, self-controlled, and compassionate to all beings. He does not kill one who is asleep, heedless, has laid down their weapons, has folded hands (in supplication), is fleeing, or has disheveled hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 118. Partha (Arjuna) Dhananjaya is devoted to the Absolute (brahman), truthful in speech, self-controlled, and compassionate to all beings. He does not kill one who is asleep, heedless, has laid down their weapons, has folded hands (in supplication), is fleeing, or has disheveled hair.

sanskrit: तदिदं नः कृतं घोरं रक्षोभिः क्रूरकर्मभिः ।इति लालप्यमानाः स्म शेरते बहवो जनाः ॥११९॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ naḥ kṛtaṁ ghoraṁ rakṣobhiḥ krūrakarmabhiḥ ,iti lālapyamānāḥ sma śerate bahavo janāḥ.
english: "This terrible act was committed against us by the cruel-acting rākṣasas!" Many people lay down, lamenting repeatedly in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 119. "This terrible act was committed against us by the cruel-acting rākṣasas!" Many people lay down, lamenting repeatedly in this manner.

sanskrit: स्तनतां च मनुष्याणामपरेषां च कूजताम् ।ततो मुहूर्तात्प्राशाम्यत्स शब्दस्तुमुलो महान् ॥१२०॥
transliteration: stanatāṁ ca manuṣyāṇāmapareṣāṁ ca kūjatām ,tato muhūrtātprāśāmyatsa śabdastumulo mahān.
english: Amidst the roaring of men and the cries of others, that great tumultuous sound then completely subsided within a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 120. Amidst the roaring of men and the cries of others, that great tumultuous sound then completely subsided within a moment.

sanskrit: शोणितव्यतिषिक्तायां वसुधायां च भूमिप ।तद्रजस्तुमुलं घोरं क्षणेनान्तरधीयत ॥१२१॥
transliteration: śoṇitavyatiṣiktāyāṁ vasudhāyāṁ ca bhūmipa ,tadrajastumulaṁ ghoraṁ kṣaṇenāntaradhīyata.
english: O king, that fierce and turbulent dust, [which had risen] on the blood-soaked earth, disappeared in a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 121. O king, that fierce and turbulent dust, [which had risen] on the blood-soaked earth, disappeared in a moment.

sanskrit: संवेष्टमानानुद्विग्नान्निरुत्साहान्सहस्रशः ।न्यपातयन्नरान्क्रुद्धः पशून्पशुपतिर्यथा ॥१२२॥
transliteration: saṁveṣṭamānānudvignānnirutsāhānsahasraśaḥ ,nyapātayannarānkruddhaḥ paśūnpaśupatiryathā.
english: The enraged one felled thousands of men who were rolling, agitated, and devoid of enthusiasm, just as the lord of animals (Paśupati) [destroys] animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 122. The enraged one felled thousands of men who were rolling, agitated, and devoid of enthusiasm, just as the lord of animals (Paśupati) [destroys] animals.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं संपरिष्वज्य शयानान्द्रवतोऽपरान् ।संलीनान्युध्यमानांश्च सर्वान्द्रौणिरपोथयत् ॥१२३॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ saṁpariṣvajya śayānāndravato'parān ,saṁlīnānyudhyamānāṁśca sarvāndrauṇirapothayat.
english: Droṇi crushed all of them: those embracing each other, others lying down, those fleeing, those hiding, and those fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 123. Droṇi crushed all of them: those embracing each other, others lying down, those fleeing, those hiding, and those fighting.

sanskrit: दह्यमाना हुताशेन वध्यमानाश्च तेन ते ।परस्परं तदा योधा अनयन्यमसादनम् ॥१२४॥
transliteration: dahyamānā hutāśena vadhyamānāśca tena te ,parasparaṁ tadā yodhā anayanyamasādanam.
english: Then, those warriors, [who were already] being consumed by fire and being slain by him (Droṇi), mutually sent each other to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 124. Then, those warriors, [who were already] being consumed by fire and being slain by him (Droṇi), mutually sent each other to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: तस्या रजन्यास्त्वर्धेन पाण्डवानां महद्बलम् ।गमयामास राजेन्द्र द्रौणिर्यमनिवेशनम् ॥१२५॥
transliteration: tasyā rajanyāstvardhena pāṇḍavānāṁ mahadbalam ,gamayāmāsa rājendra drauṇiryamaniveśanam.
english: O king of kings, in just half of that night, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) sent the mighty army of the Pāṇḍavas to the abode of Yama (death).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 125. O king of kings, in just half of that night, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) sent the mighty army of the Pāṇḍavas to the abode of Yama (death).

sanskrit: निशाचराणां सत्त्वानां स रात्रिर्हर्षवर्धिनी ।आसीन्नरगजाश्वानां रौद्री क्षयकरी भृशम् ॥१२६॥
transliteration: niśācarāṇāṁ sattvānāṁ sa rātrirharṣavardhinī ,āsīnnaragajāśvānāṁ raudrī kṣayakarī bhṛśam.
english: That night was indeed a joy-increasing one for the night-roaming beings, but for men, elephants, and horses, it was exceedingly dreadful and destructive.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 126. That night was indeed a joy-increasing one for the night-roaming beings, but for men, elephants, and horses, it was exceedingly dreadful and destructive.

sanskrit: तत्रादृश्यन्त रक्षांसि पिशाचाश्च पृथग्विधाः ।खादन्तो नरमांसानि पिबन्तः शोणितानि च ॥१२७॥
transliteration: tatrādṛśyanta rakṣāṁsi piśācāśca pṛthagvidhāḥ ,khādanto naramāṁsāni pibantaḥ śoṇitāni ca.
english: There, Rākṣasas (demons) and various kinds of Piśācas (goblins) were seen, eating human flesh and drinking blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 127. There, Rākṣasas (demons) and various kinds of Piśācas (goblins) were seen, eating human flesh and drinking blood.

sanskrit: करालाः पिङ्गला रौद्राः शैलदन्ता रजस्वलाः ।जटिला दीर्घसक्थाश्च पञ्चपादा महोदराः ॥१२८॥
transliteration: karālāḥ piṅgalā raudrāḥ śailadantā rajasvalāḥ ,jaṭilā dīrghasakthāśca pañcapādā mahodarāḥ.
english: (These Rākṣasas and Piśācas were) dreadful, tawny, fierce, with teeth like rocks, dusty or blood-stained, matted-haired, long-legged, five-footed, and huge-bellied.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 128. (These Rākṣasas and Piśācas were) dreadful, tawny, fierce, with teeth like rocks, dusty or blood-stained, matted-haired, long-legged, five-footed, and huge-bellied.

sanskrit: पश्चादङ्गुलयो रूक्षा विरूपा भैरवस्वनाः ।घटजानवोऽतिह्रस्वाश्च नीलकण्ठा विभीषणाः ॥१२९॥
transliteration: paścādaṅgulayo rūkṣā virūpā bhairavasvanāḥ ,ghaṭajānavo'tihrasvāśca nīlakaṇṭhā vibhīṣaṇāḥ.
english: Their fingers were rough and deformed, their roars dreadful. They had pot-like knees, were extremely short, and were blue-throated and terrifying.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 129. Their fingers were rough and deformed, their roars dreadful. They had pot-like knees, were extremely short, and were blue-throated and terrifying.

sanskrit: सपुत्रदाराः सुक्रूरा दुर्दर्शनसुनिर्घृणाः ।विविधानि च रूपाणि तत्रादृश्यन्त रक्षसाम् ॥१३०॥
transliteration: saputradārāḥ sukrūrā durdarśanasunirghṛṇāḥ ,vividhāni ca rūpāṇi tatrādṛśyanta rakṣasām.
english: They were with their wives and children, exceedingly cruel, unpleasant to behold, and utterly merciless. There, various forms of the rakshasas were seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 130. They were with their wives and children, exceedingly cruel, unpleasant to behold, and utterly merciless. There, various forms of the rakshasas were seen.

sanskrit: पीत्वा च शोणितं हृष्टाः प्रानृत्यन्गणशोऽपरे ।इदं वरमिदं मेध्यमिदं स्वाद्विति चाब्रुवन् ॥१३१॥
transliteration: pītvā ca śoṇitaṁ hṛṣṭāḥ prānṛtyangaṇaśo'pare ,idaṁ varamidaṁ medhyamidaṁ svādviti cābruvan.
english: Having drunk the blood, others, delighted, danced in groups. And they exclaimed, "This is excellent! This is pure! This is delicious!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 131. Having drunk the blood, others, delighted, danced in groups. And they exclaimed, "This is excellent! This is pure! This is delicious!"

sanskrit: मेदोमज्जास्थिरक्तानां वसानां च भृशाशिताः ।परमांसानि खादन्तः क्रव्यादा मांसजीविनः ॥१३२॥
transliteration: medomajjāsthiraktānāṁ vasānāṁ ca bhṛśāśitāḥ ,paramāṁsāni khādantaḥ kravyādā māṁsajīvinaḥ.
english: They were excessively gorged on fat, marrow, bones, blood, and vital fluids. Devouring the flesh of others, these carnivores lived by eating meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 132. They were excessively gorged on fat, marrow, bones, blood, and vital fluids. Devouring the flesh of others, these carnivores lived by eating meat.

sanskrit: वसां चाप्यपरे पीत्वा पर्यधावन्विकुक्षिलाः ।नानावक्त्रास्तथा रौद्राः क्रव्यादाः पिशिताशिनः ॥१३३॥
transliteration: vasāṁ cāpyapare pītvā paryadhāvanvikukṣilāḥ ,nānāvaktrāstathā raudrāḥ kravyādāḥ piśitāśinaḥ.
english: And others, having drunk the fat, ran around with distended bellies. They had various faces, were terrible, ate raw flesh, and consumed meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 133. And others, having drunk the fat, ran around with distended bellies. They had various faces, were terrible, ate raw flesh, and consumed meat.

sanskrit: अयुतानि च तत्रासन्प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।रक्षसां घोररूपाणां महतां क्रूरकर्मणाम् ॥१३४॥
transliteration: ayutāni ca tatrāsanprayutānyarbudāni ca ,rakṣasāṁ ghorarūpāṇāṁ mahatāṁ krūrakarmaṇām.
english: There were tens of thousands, millions, and hundreds of millions of rākṣasas there, who were of terrifying appearance, mighty, and performed cruel deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 134. There were tens of thousands, millions, and hundreds of millions of rākṣasas there, who were of terrifying appearance, mighty, and performed cruel deeds.

sanskrit: मुदितानां वितृप्तानां तस्मिन्महति वैशसे ।समेतानि बहून्यासन्भूतानि च जनाधिप ॥१३५॥
transliteration: muditānāṁ vitṛptānāṁ tasminmahati vaiśase ,sametāni bahūnyāsanbhūtāni ca janādhipa.
english: Among those who were delighted and thoroughly satisfied, many beings, O lord of men, were also assembled in that great slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 135. Among those who were delighted and thoroughly satisfied, many beings, O lord of men, were also assembled in that great slaughter.

sanskrit: प्रत्यूषकाले शिबिरात्प्रतिगन्तुमियेष सः ।नृशोणितावसिक्तस्य द्रौणेरासीदसित्सरुः ।पाणिना सह संश्लिष्ट एकीभूत इव प्रभो ॥१३६॥
transliteration: pratyūṣakāle śibirātpratigantumiyeṣa saḥ ,nṛśoṇitāvasiktasya drauṇerāsīdasitsaruḥ ,pāṇinā saha saṁśliṣṭa ekībhūta iva prabho.
english: At dawn, he (Aśvatthāman) wished to return from the camp. O lord, Drauṇi's (sword), which was soaked in human blood and had a black hilt, was stuck to his hand as if it had become one with it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 136. At dawn, he (Aśvatthāman) wished to return from the camp. O lord, Drauṇi's (sword), which was soaked in human blood and had a black hilt, was stuck to his hand as if it had become one with it.

sanskrit: स निःशेषानरीन्कृत्वा विरराज जनक्षये ।युगान्ते सर्वभूतानि भस्म कृत्वेव पावकः ॥१३७॥
transliteration: sa niḥśeṣānarīnkṛtvā virarāja janakṣaye ,yugānte sarvabhūtāni bhasma kṛtveva pāvakaḥ.
english: Having completely eliminated his enemies, he shone brightly amidst the destruction of people, just as fire (pāvaka) at the end of an eon (yugānta) reduces all beings to ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 137. Having completely eliminated his enemies, he shone brightly amidst the destruction of people, just as fire (pāvaka) at the end of an eon (yugānta) reduces all beings to ashes.

sanskrit: यथाप्रतिज्ञं तत्कर्म कृत्वा द्रौणायनिः प्रभो ।दुर्गमां पदवीं कृत्वा पितुरासीद्गतज्वरः ॥१३८॥
transliteration: yathāpratijñaṁ tatkarma kṛtvā drauṇāyaniḥ prabho ,durgamāṁ padavīṁ kṛtvā piturāsīdgatajvaraḥ.
english: O lord (prabho), the son of Droṇa (Drauṇāyaniḥ), having performed that deed as promised, and having secured an inaccessible path to heaven for his father, became free from distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 138. O lord (prabho), the son of Droṇa (Drauṇāyaniḥ), having performed that deed as promised, and having secured an inaccessible path to heaven for his father, became free from distress.

sanskrit: यथैव संसुप्तजने शिबिरे प्राविशन्निशि ।तथैव हत्वा निःशब्दे निश्चक्राम नरर्षभः ॥१३९॥
transliteration: yathaiva saṁsuptajane śibire prāviśanniśi ,tathaiva hatvā niḥśabde niścakrāma nararṣabhaḥ.
english: Just as he entered the camp at night when people were fast asleep, so too, having slain (the enemies), the bull among men (naraṛṣabha) exited in silence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 139. Just as he entered the camp at night when people were fast asleep, so too, having slain (the enemies), the bull among men (naraṛṣabha) exited in silence.

sanskrit: निष्क्रम्य शिबिरात्तस्मात्ताभ्यां संगम्य वीर्यवान् ।आचख्यौ कर्म तत्सर्वं हृष्टः संहर्षयन्विभो ॥१४०॥
transliteration: niṣkramya śibirāttasmāttābhyāṁ saṁgamya vīryavān ,ācakhyau karma tatsarvaṁ hṛṣṭaḥ saṁharṣayanvibho.
english: O lord (vibho), the mighty one (Aśvatthāman), having exited from that camp and having met with those two (Kṛpa and Kṛtavarman), narrated all of that deed, delighted and gladdening (them).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 140. O lord (vibho), the mighty one (Aśvatthāman), having exited from that camp and having met with those two (Kṛpa and Kṛtavarman), narrated all of that deed, delighted and gladdening (them).

sanskrit: तावप्याचख्यतुस्तस्मै प्रियं प्रियकरौ तदा ।पाञ्चालान्सृञ्जयांश्चैव विनिकृत्तान्सहस्रशः ।प्रीत्या चोच्चैरुदक्रोशंस्तथैवास्फोटयंस्तलान् ॥१४१॥
transliteration: tāvapyācakhyatustasmai priyaṁ priyakarau tadā ,pāñcālānsṛñjayāṁścaiva vinikṛttānsahasraśaḥ ,prītyā coccairudakrośaṁstathaivāsphoṭayaṁstalān.
english: Then, those two pleasing individuals recounted to him the pleasant news that the Pañcālas and Sṛñjayas had been annihilated by the thousands. With joy, they also cried out loudly and similarly clapped their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 141. Then, those two pleasing individuals recounted to him the pleasant news that the Pañcālas and Sṛñjayas had been annihilated by the thousands. With joy, they also cried out loudly and similarly clapped their hands.

sanskrit: एवंविधा हि सा रात्रिः सोमकानां जनक्षये ।प्रसुप्तानां प्रमत्तानामासीत्सुभृशदारुणा ॥१४२॥
transliteration: evaṁvidhā hi sā rātriḥ somakānāṁ janakṣaye ,prasuptānāṁ pramattānāmāsītsubhṛśadāruṇā.
english: Indeed, such was that night for the Somakas, a night marked by the annihilation of their people. For those who were asleep and heedless, it proved to be exceedingly dreadful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 142. Indeed, such was that night for the Somakas, a night marked by the annihilation of their people. For those who were asleep and heedless, it proved to be exceedingly dreadful.

sanskrit: असंशयं हि कालस्य पर्यायो दुरतिक्रमः ।तादृशा निहता यत्र कृत्वास्माकं जनक्षयम् ॥१४३॥
transliteration: asaṁśayaṁ hi kālasya paryāyo duratikramaḥ ,tādṛśā nihatā yatra kṛtvāsmākaṁ janakṣayam.
english: Indeed, without doubt, the course of time is irresistible, a turn of events where such powerful individuals, after causing the annihilation of our people, were themselves slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 143. Indeed, without doubt, the course of time is irresistible, a turn of events where such powerful individuals, after causing the annihilation of our people, were themselves slain.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।प्रागेव सुमहत्कर्म द्रौणिरेतन्महारथः ।नाकरोदीदृशं कस्मान्मत्पुत्रविजये धृतः ॥१४४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,prāgeva sumahatkarma drauṇiretanmahārathaḥ ,nākarodīdṛśaṁ kasmānmatputravijaye dhṛtaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Why did Droṇi, this great warrior (mahāratha), not perform such a magnificent deed earlier, when he was engaged in securing the victory of my sons?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 144. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Why did Droṇi, this great warrior (mahāratha), not perform such a magnificent deed earlier, when he was engaged in securing the victory of my sons?'

sanskrit: अथ कस्माद्धते क्षत्रे कर्मेदं कृतवानसौ ।द्रोणपुत्रो महेष्वासस्तन्मे शंसितुमर्हसि ॥१४५॥
transliteration: atha kasmāddhate kṣatre karmedaṁ kṛtavānasau ,droṇaputro maheṣvāsastanme śaṁsitumarhasi.
english: Now, why did Drona's son, the great archer, perform this deed (karma) after the warriors had been slain? You ought to explain that to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 145. Now, why did Drona's son, the great archer, perform this deed (karma) after the warriors had been slain? You ought to explain that to me.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तेषां नूनं भयान्नासौ कृतवान्कुरुनन्दन ।असांनिध्याद्धि पार्थानां केशवस्य च धीमतः ॥१४६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,teṣāṁ nūnaṁ bhayānnāsau kṛtavānkurunandana ,asāṁnidhyāddhi pārthānāṁ keśavasya ca dhīmataḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: O delight of the Kurus, he (Aśvatthāmā) surely did not perform that act out of fear of them. Indeed, it was due to the absence of the Pārthas and the intelligent Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 146. Saṃjaya said: O delight of the Kurus, he (Aśvatthāmā) surely did not perform that act out of fear of them. Indeed, it was due to the absence of the Pārthas and the intelligent Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: सात्यकेश्चापि कर्मेदं द्रोणपुत्रेण साधितम् ।न हि तेषां समक्षं तान्हन्यादपि मरुत्पतिः ॥१४७॥
transliteration: sātyakeścāpi karmedaṁ droṇaputreṇa sādhitam ,na hi teṣāṁ samakṣaṁ tānhanyādapi marutpatiḥ.
english: And this very deed (karma) was accomplished by Drona's son even in Sātyaki's absence. For indeed, not even the lord of the Maruts (Indra) would kill them in their presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 147. And this very deed (karma) was accomplished by Drona's son even in Sātyaki's absence. For indeed, not even the lord of the Maruts (Indra) would kill them in their presence.

sanskrit: एतदीदृशकं वृत्तं राजन्सुप्तजने विभो ।ततो जनक्षयं कृत्वा पाण्डवानां महात्ययम् ।दिष्ट्या दिष्ट्येति चान्योन्यं समेत्योचुर्महारथाः ॥१४८॥
transliteration: etadīdṛśakaṁ vṛttaṁ rājansuptajane vibho ,tato janakṣayaṁ kṛtvā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātyayam ,diṣṭyā diṣṭyeti cānyonyaṁ sametyocurmahārathāḥ.
english: O King, O mighty one, such was this event among sleeping people. Then, having caused the destruction of the people - a great calamity for the Pāṇḍavas - the great warriors, having come together, exclaimed to each other, 'Fortunately! Fortunately!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 148. O King, O mighty one, such was this event among sleeping people. Then, having caused the destruction of the people - a great calamity for the Pāṇḍavas - the great warriors, having come together, exclaimed to each other, 'Fortunately! Fortunately!'

sanskrit: पर्यष्वजत्ततो द्रौणिस्ताभ्यां च प्रतिनन्दितः ।इदं हर्षाच्च सुमहदाददे वाक्यमुत्तमम् ॥१४९॥
transliteration: paryaṣvajattato drauṇistābhyāṁ ca pratinanditaḥ ,idaṁ harṣācca sumahadādade vākyamuttamam.
english: Then Drauṇi embraced them, and having been greeted in return by them, he joyfully uttered these excellent and very significant words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 149. Then Drauṇi embraced them, and having been greeted in return by them, he joyfully uttered these excellent and very significant words.

sanskrit: पाञ्चाला निहताः सर्वे द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ।सोमका मत्स्यशेषाश्च सर्वे विनिहता मया ॥१५०॥
transliteration: pāñcālā nihatāḥ sarve draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,somakā matsyaśeṣāśca sarve vinihatā mayā.
english: All the Pañcālas have been killed, and all the sons of Draupadī entirely. All the Somakas and the remaining Matsyas have also been slain by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 150. All the Pañcālas have been killed, and all the sons of Draupadī entirely. All the Somakas and the remaining Matsyas have also been slain by me.

sanskrit: इदानीं कृतकृत्याः स्म याम तत्रैव माचिरम् ।यदि जीवति नो राजा तस्मै शंसामहे प्रियम् ॥१५१॥
transliteration: idānīṁ kṛtakṛtyāḥ sma yāma tatraiva māciram ,yadi jīvati no rājā tasmai śaṁsāmahe priyam.
english: Now we have accomplished our purpose; let us go there without delay. If our king is still alive, we shall announce the good news to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 151. Now we have accomplished our purpose; let us go there without delay. If our king is still alive, we shall announce the good news to him.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ते हत्वा सर्वपाञ्चालान्द्रौपदेयांश्च सर्वशः ।अगच्छन्सहितास्तत्र यत्र दुर्योधनो हतः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,te hatvā sarvapāñcālāndraupadeyāṁśca sarvaśaḥ ,agacchansahitāstatra yatra duryodhano hataḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Having slain all the Pañcālas and completely the sons of Draupadī, they went together to that place where Duryodhana had been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Having slain all the Pañcālas and completely the sons of Draupadī, they went together to that place where Duryodhana had been killed.

sanskrit: गत्वा चैनमपश्यंस्ते किंचित्प्राणं नराधिपम् ।ततो रथेभ्यः प्रस्कन्द्य परिवव्रुस्तवात्मजम् ॥२॥
transliteration: gatvā cainamapaśyaṁste kiṁcitprāṇaṁ narādhipam ,tato rathebhyaḥ praskandya parivavrustavātmajam.
english: O ruler of men, having arrived there, they saw him, the lord of men, with but a little life. Then, leaping from their chariots, they surrounded your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. O ruler of men, having arrived there, they saw him, the lord of men, with but a little life. Then, leaping from their chariots, they surrounded your son.

sanskrit: तं भग्नसक्थं राजेन्द्र कृच्छ्रप्राणमचेतसम् ।वमन्तं रुधिरं वक्त्रादपश्यन्वसुधातले ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ bhagnasakthaṁ rājendra kṛcchraprāṇamacetasam ,vamantaṁ rudhiraṁ vaktrādapaśyanvasudhātale.
english: O greatest of kings (rājendra), they saw him on the ground, his thighs broken, barely breathing (kṛcchraprāṇa), unconscious, and vomiting blood from his mouth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. O greatest of kings (rājendra), they saw him on the ground, his thighs broken, barely breathing (kṛcchraprāṇa), unconscious, and vomiting blood from his mouth.

sanskrit: वृतं समन्ताद्बहुभिः श्वापदैर्घोरदर्शनैः ।शालावृकगणैश्चैव भक्षयिष्यद्भिरन्तिकात् ॥४॥
transliteration: vṛtaṁ samantādbahubhiḥ śvāpadairghoradarśanaiḥ ,śālāvṛkagaṇaiścaiva bhakṣayiṣyadbhirantikāt.
english: He was surrounded on all sides by many terrifying-looking beasts of prey and packs of jackals (śālāvṛka) that were about to devour him from close by.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. He was surrounded on all sides by many terrifying-looking beasts of prey and packs of jackals (śālāvṛka) that were about to devour him from close by.

sanskrit: निवारयन्तं कृच्छ्रात्ताञ्श्वापदान्संचिखादिषून् ।विवेष्टमानं मह्यां च सुभृशं गाढवेदनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: nivārayantaṁ kṛcchrāttāñśvāpadānsaṁcikhādiṣūn ,viveṣṭamānaṁ mahyāṁ ca subhṛśaṁ gāḍhavedanam.
english: He was, with great difficulty, warding off those beasts of prey that desired to devour him, and he was writhing on the ground, in very severe, agonizing pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. He was, with great difficulty, warding off those beasts of prey that desired to devour him, and he was writhing on the ground, in very severe, agonizing pain.

sanskrit: तं शयानं महात्मानं भूमौ स्वरुधिरोक्षितम् ।हतशिष्टास्त्रयो वीराः शोकार्ताः पर्यवारयन् ।अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ॥६॥
transliteration: taṁ śayānaṁ mahātmānaṁ bhūmau svarudhirokṣitam ,hataśiṣṭāstrayo vīrāḥ śokārtāḥ paryavārayan ,aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ.
english: The three remaining heroes - Ashvatthama, Kripa, and Kritavarma the Satvata - afflicted by sorrow, surrounded that great-souled one who was lying on the ground, drenched in his own blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. The three remaining heroes - Ashvatthama, Kripa, and Kritavarma the Satvata - afflicted by sorrow, surrounded that great-souled one who was lying on the ground, drenched in his own blood.

sanskrit: तैस्त्रिभिः शोणितादिग्धैर्निःश्वसद्भिर्महारथैः ।शुशुभे संवृतो राजा वेदी त्रिभिरिवाग्निभिः ॥७॥
transliteration: taistribhiḥ śoṇitādigdhairniḥśvasadbhirmahārathaiḥ ,śuśubhe saṁvṛto rājā vedī tribhirivāgnibhiḥ.
english: Surrounded by those three great charioteers, who were smeared with blood and sighing deeply, the king shone like a sacrificial altar (vedī) surrounded by three fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. Surrounded by those three great charioteers, who were smeared with blood and sighing deeply, the king shone like a sacrificial altar (vedī) surrounded by three fires.

sanskrit: ते तं शयानं संप्रेक्ष्य राजानमतथोचितम् ।अविषह्येन दुःखेन ततस्ते रुरुदुस्त्रयः ॥८॥
transliteration: te taṁ śayānaṁ saṁprekṣya rājānamatathocitam ,aviṣahyena duḥkhena tataste rurudustrayaḥ.
english: Seeing him, the king, lying there in such an unfitting state, those three then wept with unbearable sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Seeing him, the king, lying there in such an unfitting state, those three then wept with unbearable sorrow.

sanskrit: ततस्ते रुधिरं हस्तैर्मुखान्निर्मृज्य तस्य ह ।रणे राज्ञः शयानस्य कृपणं पर्यदेवयन् ॥९॥
transliteration: tataste rudhiraṁ hastairmukhānnirmṛjya tasya ha ,raṇe rājñaḥ śayānasya kṛpaṇaṁ paryadevayan.
english: Then, wiping his blood from his face with their hands, they lamented piteously for the king who was lying there on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Then, wiping his blood from his face with their hands, they lamented piteously for the king who was lying there on the battlefield.

sanskrit: कृप उवाच ।न दैवस्यातिभारोऽस्ति यदयं रुधिरोक्षितः ।एकादशचमूभर्ता शेते दुर्योधनो हतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛpa uvāca ,na daivasyātibhāro'sti yadayaṁ rudhirokṣitaḥ ,ekādaśacamūbhartā śete duryodhano hataḥ.
english: Kṛpa said: There is no excessive burden for destiny, that this Duryodhana, the lord of eleven armies, lies slain, drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. Kṛpa said: There is no excessive burden for destiny, that this Duryodhana, the lord of eleven armies, lies slain, drenched in blood.

sanskrit: पश्य चामीकराभस्य चामीकरविभूषिताम् ।गदां गदाप्रियस्येमां समीपे पतितां भुवि ॥११॥
transliteration: paśya cāmīkarābhasya cāmīkaravibhūṣitām ,gadāṁ gadāpriyasyemāṁ samīpe patitāṁ bhuvi.
english: Behold this mace, adorned with gold, of him who shone like gold and loved the mace, lying fallen nearby on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. Behold this mace, adorned with gold, of him who shone like gold and loved the mace, lying fallen nearby on the ground.

sanskrit: इयमेनं गदा शूरं न जहाति रणे रणे ।स्वर्गायापि व्रजन्तं हि न जहाति यशस्विनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: iyamenaṁ gadā śūraṁ na jahāti raṇe raṇe ,svargāyāpi vrajantaṁ hi na jahāti yaśasvinam.
english: This mace does not abandon this hero in every battle; indeed, it does not abandon the glorious one even as he proceeds to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. This mace does not abandon this hero in every battle; indeed, it does not abandon the glorious one even as he proceeds to heaven.

sanskrit: पश्येमां सह वीरेण जाम्बूनदविभूषिताम् ।शयानां शयने धर्मे भार्यां प्रीतिमतीमिव ॥१३॥
transliteration: paśyemāṁ saha vīreṇa jāmbūnadavibhūṣitām ,śayānāṁ śayane dharme bhāryāṁ prītimatīmiva.
english: Behold this mace, adorned with gold, lying with the hero, as if it were a loving wife on a bed of duty (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. Behold this mace, adorned with gold, lying with the hero, as if it were a loving wife on a bed of duty (dharma).

sanskrit: यो वै मूर्धावसिक्तानामग्रे यातः परंतपः ।स हतो ग्रसते पांसून्पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yo vai mūrdhāvasiktānāmagre yātaḥ paraṁtapaḥ ,sa hato grasate pāṁsūnpaśya kālasya paryayam.
english: Indeed, he who was the foremost among anointed kings, the tormentor of enemies, now, slain, devours dust. Behold the reversal of time!
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. Indeed, he who was the foremost among anointed kings, the tormentor of enemies, now, slain, devours dust. Behold the reversal of time!

sanskrit: येनाजौ निहता भूमावशेरत पुरा द्विषः ।स भूमौ निहतः शेते कुरुराजः परैरयम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yenājau nihatā bhūmāvaśerata purā dviṣaḥ ,sa bhūmau nihataḥ śete kururājaḥ parairayam.
english: By whom enemies were formerly slain in battle and lay on the ground - that very king of the Kurus now lies slain on the ground by his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. By whom enemies were formerly slain in battle and lay on the ground - that very king of the Kurus now lies slain on the ground by his enemies.

sanskrit: भयान्नमन्ति राजानो यस्य स्म शतसंघशः ।स वीरशयने शेते क्रव्याद्भिः परिवारितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhayānnamanti rājāno yasya sma śatasaṁghaśaḥ ,sa vīraśayane śete kravyādbhiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: He, before whom kings formerly bowed in hundreds out of fear, now lies on the hero's bed, surrounded by carrion-eaters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. He, before whom kings formerly bowed in hundreds out of fear, now lies on the hero's bed, surrounded by carrion-eaters.

sanskrit: उपासत नृपाः पूर्वमर्थहेतोर्यमीश्वरम् ।धिक्सद्यो निहतः शेते पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: upāsata nṛpāḥ pūrvamarthahetoryamīśvaram ,dhiksadyo nihataḥ śete paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: Shame! He, whom kings formerly served for the sake of gain, now lies suddenly slain. Behold the reversal of time!
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. Shame! He, whom kings formerly served for the sake of gain, now lies suddenly slain. Behold the reversal of time!

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तं शयानं नृपश्रेष्ठं ततो भरतसत्तम ।अश्वत्थामा समालोक्य करुणं पर्यदेवयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,taṁ śayānaṁ nṛpaśreṣṭhaṁ tato bharatasattama ,aśvatthāmā samālokya karuṇaṁ paryadevayat.
english: Sañjaya said: O best among the Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Aśvatthāmā, having seen that best of kings (Duryodhana) lying there, lamented piteously.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. Sañjaya said: O best among the Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Aśvatthāmā, having seen that best of kings (Duryodhana) lying there, lamented piteously.

sanskrit: आहुस्त्वां राजशार्दूल मुख्यं सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।धनाध्यक्षोपमं युद्धे शिष्यं संकर्षणस्य ह ॥१९॥
transliteration: āhustvāṁ rājaśārdūla mukhyaṁ sarvadhanuṣmatām ,dhanādhyakṣopamaṁ yuddhe śiṣyaṁ saṁkarṣaṇasya ha.
english: O tiger among kings, indeed people called you the foremost of all archers, comparable to Kubera, the lord of wealth, in battle, and a disciple of Saṃkarṣaṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. O tiger among kings, indeed people called you the foremost of all archers, comparable to Kubera, the lord of wealth, in battle, and a disciple of Saṃkarṣaṇa.

sanskrit: कथं विवरमद्राक्षीद्भीमसेनस्तवानघ ।बलिनः कृतिनो नित्यं स च पापात्मवान्नृप ॥२०॥
transliteration: kathaṁ vivaramadrākṣīdbhīmasenastavānagha ,balinaḥ kṛtino nityaṁ sa ca pāpātmavānnṛpa.
english: O sinless one (Duryodhana), O king, how did Bhīmasena find a weak point in you, who were always mighty and accomplished? And that Bhīmasena is truly wicked.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. O sinless one (Duryodhana), O king, how did Bhīmasena find a weak point in you, who were always mighty and accomplished? And that Bhīmasena is truly wicked.

sanskrit: कालो नूनं महाराज लोकेऽस्मिन्बलवत्तरः ।पश्यामो निहतं त्वां चेद्भीमसेनेन संयुगे ॥२१॥
transliteration: kālo nūnaṁ mahārāja loke'sminbalavattaraḥ ,paśyāmo nihataṁ tvāṁ cedbhīmasenena saṁyuge.
english: O great king, if we see you struck down by Bhīmasena in battle, then indeed Time (kāla) is more powerful in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. O great king, if we see you struck down by Bhīmasena in battle, then indeed Time (kāla) is more powerful in this world.

sanskrit: कथं त्वां सर्वधर्मज्ञं क्षुद्रः पापो वृकोदरः ।निकृत्या हतवान्मन्दो नूनं कालो दुरत्ययः ॥२२॥
transliteration: kathaṁ tvāṁ sarvadharmajñaṁ kṣudraḥ pāpo vṛkodaraḥ ,nikṛtyā hatavānmando nūnaṁ kālo duratyayaḥ.
english: How could that mean, sinful, and foolish Vrikodara (vṛkodara) have killed you, the knower of all natural laws (dharma), through deceit? Surely, time is irresistible.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. How could that mean, sinful, and foolish Vrikodara (vṛkodara) have killed you, the knower of all natural laws (dharma), through deceit? Surely, time is irresistible.

sanskrit: धर्मयुद्धे ह्यधर्मेण समाहूयौजसा मृधे ।गदया भीमसेनेन निर्भिन्ने सक्थिनी तव ॥२३॥
transliteration: dharmayuddhe hyadharmeṇa samāhūyaujasā mṛdhe ,gadayā bhīmasenena nirbhinne sakthinī tava.
english: For in the battle, having challenged you with his might in a righteous war (dharmayuddha), your two thighs were shattered by Bhīmasena (bhīmasena) with his mace through unrighteousness (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. For in the battle, having challenged you with his might in a righteous war (dharmayuddha), your two thighs were shattered by Bhīmasena (bhīmasena) with his mace through unrighteousness (adharma).

sanskrit: अधर्मेण हतस्याजौ मृद्यमानं पदा शिरः ।यदुपेक्षितवान्क्षुद्रो धिक्तमस्तु युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa hatasyājau mṛdyamānaṁ padā śiraḥ ,yadupekṣitavānkṣudro dhiktamastu yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Because that contemptible Yudhishthira (yudhiṣṭhira) overlooked the head of the one killed by unrighteousness (adharma) in battle, being trampled by a foot, shame on him!
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. Because that contemptible Yudhishthira (yudhiṣṭhira) overlooked the head of the one killed by unrighteousness (adharma) in battle, being trampled by a foot, shame on him!

sanskrit: युद्धेष्वपवदिष्यन्ति योधा नूनं वृकोदरम् ।यावत्स्थास्यन्ति भूतानि निकृत्या ह्यसि पातितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yuddheṣvapavadiṣyanti yodhā nūnaṁ vṛkodaram ,yāvatsthāsyanti bhūtāni nikṛtyā hyasi pātitaḥ.
english: Surely, warriors will blame Vrikodara (vṛkodara) in battles as long as living beings exist, for you have been brought down by deceit.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. Surely, warriors will blame Vrikodara (vṛkodara) in battles as long as living beings exist, for you have been brought down by deceit.

sanskrit: ननु रामोऽब्रवीद्राजंस्त्वां सदा यदुनन्दनः ।दुर्योधनसमो नास्ति गदया इति वीर्यवान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: nanu rāmo'bravīdrājaṁstvāṁ sadā yadunandanaḥ ,duryodhanasamo nāsti gadayā iti vīryavān.
english: O King, indeed, Rama, the delight of the Yadus, always told you, "There is no one equal to Duryodhana in mace fighting, for he is mighty."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. O King, indeed, Rama, the delight of the Yadus, always told you, "There is no one equal to Duryodhana in mace fighting, for he is mighty."

sanskrit: श्लाघते त्वां हि वार्ष्णेयो राजन्संसत्सु भारत ।सुशिष्यो मम कौरव्यो गदायुद्ध इति प्रभो ॥२७॥
transliteration: ślāghate tvāṁ hi vārṣṇeyo rājansaṁsatsu bhārata ,suśiṣyo mama kauravyo gadāyuddha iti prabho.
english: O King, O Bhārata, indeed, the Vārṣṇeya praises you in assemblies, saying, "O Lord, this Kaurava is my excellent disciple in mace fighting."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 27. O King, O Bhārata, indeed, the Vārṣṇeya praises you in assemblies, saying, "O Lord, this Kaurava is my excellent disciple in mace fighting."

sanskrit: यां गतिं क्षत्रियस्याहुः प्रशस्तां परमर्षयः ।हतस्याभिमुखस्याजौ प्राप्तस्त्वमसि तां गतिम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: yāṁ gatiṁ kṣatriyasyāhuḥ praśastāṁ paramarṣayaḥ ,hatasyābhimukhasyājau prāptastvamasi tāṁ gatim.
english: The great sages declare that the most commendable destiny (gati) for a warrior (kṣatriya) is to be slain while facing the enemy in battle. You have indeed attained that very destiny (gati).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 28. The great sages declare that the most commendable destiny (gati) for a warrior (kṣatriya) is to be slain while facing the enemy in battle. You have indeed attained that very destiny (gati).

sanskrit: दुर्योधन न शोचामि त्वामहं पुरुषर्षभ ।हतपुत्रां तु शोचामि गान्धारीं पितरं च ते ।भिक्षुकौ विचरिष्येते शोचन्तौ पृथिवीमिमाम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: duryodhana na śocāmi tvāmahaṁ puruṣarṣabha ,hataputrāṁ tu śocāmi gāndhārīṁ pitaraṁ ca te ,bhikṣukau vicariṣyete śocantau pṛthivīmimām.
english: O Duryodhana, O bull among men, I do not mourn for you. However, I do mourn for your mother Gāndhārī, whose sons have been killed, and for your father. They both will wander as beggars, grieving over this earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 29. O Duryodhana, O bull among men, I do not mourn for you. However, I do mourn for your mother Gāndhārī, whose sons have been killed, and for your father. They both will wander as beggars, grieving over this earth.

sanskrit: धिगस्तु कृष्णं वार्ष्णेयमर्जुनं चापि दुर्मतिम् ।धर्मज्ञमानिनौ यौ त्वां वध्यमानमुपेक्षताम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhigastu kṛṣṇaṁ vārṣṇeyamarjunaṁ cāpi durmatim ,dharmajñamāninau yau tvāṁ vadhyamānamupekṣatām.
english: Shame upon Krishna of the Vṛṣṇi clan and also upon the wicked-minded Arjuna! Woe to those two who, considering themselves knowers of (natural) law (dharma), neglected you while you were being slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 30. Shame upon Krishna of the Vṛṣṇi clan and also upon the wicked-minded Arjuna! Woe to those two who, considering themselves knowers of (natural) law (dharma), neglected you while you were being slain.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्चापि ते सर्वे किं वक्ष्यन्ति नराधिपान् ।कथं दुर्योधनोऽस्माभिर्हत इत्यनपत्रपाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāścāpi te sarve kiṁ vakṣyanti narādhipān ,kathaṁ duryodhano'smābhirhata ityanapatrapāḥ.
english: And what will all those shameless Pāṇḍavas say to other rulers: "How was Duryodhana killed by us?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 31. And what will all those shameless Pāṇḍavas say to other rulers: "How was Duryodhana killed by us?"

sanskrit: धन्यस्त्वमसि गान्धारे यस्त्वमायोधने हतः ।प्रयातोऽभिमुखः शत्रून्धर्मेण पुरुषर्षभ ॥३२॥
transliteration: dhanyastvamasi gāndhāre yastvamāyodhane hataḥ ,prayāto'bhimukhaḥ śatrūndharmeṇa puruṣarṣabha.
english: O son of Gandhārī, O best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), blessed are you who were killed in battle, having gone forth facing your enemies righteously, according to (natural) law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 32. O son of Gandhārī, O best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), blessed are you who were killed in battle, having gone forth facing your enemies righteously, according to (natural) law (dharma).

sanskrit: हतपुत्रा हि गान्धारी निहतज्ञातिबान्धवा ।प्रज्ञाचक्षुश्च दुर्धर्षः कां गतिं प्रतिपत्स्यते ॥३३॥
transliteration: hataputrā hi gāndhārī nihatajñātibāndhavā ,prajñācakṣuśca durdharṣaḥ kāṁ gatiṁ pratipatsyate.
english: Indeed, what fate will Gandhārī, whose sons, kinsmen, and relatives have been killed, attain? And what path will the formidable Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose 'eyes' are wisdom, follow?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 33. Indeed, what fate will Gandhārī, whose sons, kinsmen, and relatives have been killed, attain? And what path will the formidable Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose 'eyes' are wisdom, follow?

sanskrit: धिगस्तु कृतवर्माणं मां कृपं च महारथम् ।ये वयं न गताः स्वर्गं त्वां पुरस्कृत्य पार्थिवम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: dhigastu kṛtavarmāṇaṁ māṁ kṛpaṁ ca mahāratham ,ye vayaṁ na gatāḥ svargaṁ tvāṁ puraskṛtya pārthivam.
english: Shame on Kṛtavarman, on me, and on Kṛpa, the great charioteer! We who did not go to heaven by making you, the king, our leader.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 34. Shame on Kṛtavarman, on me, and on Kṛpa, the great charioteer! We who did not go to heaven by making you, the king, our leader.

sanskrit: दातारं सर्वकामानां रक्षितारं प्रजाहितम् ।यद्वयं नानुगच्छामस्त्वां धिगस्मान्नराधमान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: dātāraṁ sarvakāmānāṁ rakṣitāraṁ prajāhitam ,yadvayaṁ nānugacchāmastvāṁ dhigasmānnarādhamān.
english: Shame on us, the lowest of men, because we did not follow you, who were the giver of all desires, the protector, and the benefactor of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 35. Shame on us, the lowest of men, because we did not follow you, who were the giver of all desires, the protector, and the benefactor of the people.

sanskrit: कृपस्य तव वीर्येण मम चैव पितुश्च मे ।सभृत्यानां नरव्याघ्र रत्नवन्ति गृहाणि च ॥३६॥
transliteration: kṛpasya tava vīryeṇa mama caiva pituśca me ,sabhṛtyānāṁ naravyāghra ratnavanti gṛhāṇi ca.
english: O tiger among men, it was by the valor of Kṛpa, by your own, and indeed by my father's, and by my own, that the jeweled houses of those with servants existed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 36. O tiger among men, it was by the valor of Kṛpa, by your own, and indeed by my father's, and by my own, that the jeweled houses of those with servants existed.

sanskrit: भवत्प्रसादादस्माभिः समित्रैः सहबान्धवैः ।अवाप्ताः क्रतवो मुख्या बहवो भूरिदक्षिणाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: bhavatprasādādasmābhiḥ samitraiḥ sahabāndhavaiḥ ,avāptāḥ kratavo mukhyā bahavo bhūridakṣiṇāḥ.
english: By your grace, we, along with our friends and relatives, have accomplished many important Vedic rituals (kratu) that involved generous fees (dakṣiṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 37. By your grace, we, along with our friends and relatives, have accomplished many important Vedic rituals (kratu) that involved generous fees (dakṣiṇā).

sanskrit: कुतश्चापीदृशं सार्थमुपलप्स्यामहे वयम् ।यादृशेन पुरस्कृत्य त्वं गतः सर्वपार्थिवान् ॥३८॥
transliteration: kutaścāpīdṛśaṁ sārthamupalapsyāmahe vayam ,yādṛśena puraskṛtya tvaṁ gataḥ sarvapārthivān.
english: From where else could we obtain such a fellowship (sārtha), by means of which you, having been esteemed, went before all other kings?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 38. From where else could we obtain such a fellowship (sārtha), by means of which you, having been esteemed, went before all other kings?

sanskrit: वयमेव त्रयो राजन्गच्छन्तं परमां गतिम् ।यद्वै त्वां नानुगच्छामस्तेन तप्स्यामहे वयम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: vayameva trayo rājangacchantaṁ paramāṁ gatim ,yadvai tvāṁ nānugacchāmastena tapsyāmahe vayam.
english: O King, because we three alone cannot follow you, who are proceeding to the supreme destination (gati), we shall certainly grieve on account of that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 39. O King, because we three alone cannot follow you, who are proceeding to the supreme destination (gati), we shall certainly grieve on account of that.

sanskrit: त्वत्स्वर्गहीना हीनार्थाः स्मरन्तः सुकृतस्य ते ।किं नाम तद्भवेत्कर्म येन त्वानुव्रजेम वै ॥४०॥
transliteration: tvatsvargahīnā hīnārthāḥ smarantaḥ sukṛtasya te ,kiṁ nāma tadbhavetkarma yena tvānuvrajema vai.
english: Bereft of your presence in heaven and having lost our purpose, as we remember your good deeds (sukṛta), what indeed would that action (karma) be by which we might truly follow you?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 40. Bereft of your presence in heaven and having lost our purpose, as we remember your good deeds (sukṛta), what indeed would that action (karma) be by which we might truly follow you?

sanskrit: दुःखं नूनं कुरुश्रेष्ठ चरिष्यामो महीमिमाम् ।हीनानां नस्त्वया राजन्कुतः शान्तिः कुतः सुखम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: duḥkhaṁ nūnaṁ kuruśreṣṭha cariṣyāmo mahīmimām ,hīnānāṁ nastvayā rājankutaḥ śāntiḥ kutaḥ sukham.
english: O best among the Kurus, we shall surely wander this earth in great sorrow. For us, O King, who are deprived of you, from where can there be peace (śānti)? From where can there be happiness (sukham)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 41. O best among the Kurus, we shall surely wander this earth in great sorrow. For us, O King, who are deprived of you, from where can there be peace (śānti)? From where can there be happiness (sukham)?

sanskrit: गत्वैतांस्तु महाराज समेत्य त्वं महारथान् ।यथाश्रेष्ठं यथाज्येष्ठं पूजयेर्वचनान्मम ॥४२॥
transliteration: gatvaitāṁstu mahārāja sametya tvaṁ mahārathān ,yathāśreṣṭhaṁ yathājyeṣṭhaṁ pūjayervacanānmama.
english: O great king, having gone to these great warriors and met them, you should honor them according to their excellence and seniority, by my command.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 42. O great king, having gone to these great warriors and met them, you should honor them according to their excellence and seniority, by my command.

sanskrit: आचार्यं पूजयित्वा च केतुं सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।हतं मयाद्य शंसेथा धृष्टद्युम्नं नराधिप ॥४३॥
transliteration: ācāryaṁ pūjayitvā ca ketuṁ sarvadhanuṣmatām ,hataṁ mayādya śaṁsethā dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ narādhipa.
english: And, O king of men, after honoring the preceptor (Droṇa), who is the foremost among all archers, you should then declare that Dhṛṣṭadyumna has been slain by me today.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 43. And, O king of men, after honoring the preceptor (Droṇa), who is the foremost among all archers, you should then declare that Dhṛṣṭadyumna has been slain by me today.

sanskrit: परिष्वजेथा राजानं बाह्लिकं सुमहारथम् ।सैन्धवं सोमदत्तं च भूरिश्रवसमेव च ॥४४॥
transliteration: pariṣvajethā rājānaṁ bāhlikaṁ sumahāratham ,saindhavaṁ somadattaṁ ca bhūriśravasameva ca.
english: You should embrace King Bāhlika, that very great warrior, and also Saṃdhava, Somadatta, and Bhūriśravas himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 44. You should embrace King Bāhlika, that very great warrior, and also Saṃdhava, Somadatta, and Bhūriśravas himself.

sanskrit: तथा पूर्वगतानन्यान्स्वर्गं पार्थिवसत्तमान् ।अस्मद्वाक्यात्परिष्वज्य पृच्छेथास्त्वमनामयम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tathā pūrvagatānanyānsvargaṁ pārthivasattamān ,asmadvākyātpariṣvajya pṛcchethāstvamanāmayam.
english: Likewise, you should embrace those other excellent kings who have gone to heaven and, by our command, inquire about their well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 45. Likewise, you should embrace those other excellent kings who have gone to heaven and, by our command, inquire about their well-being.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा राजानं भग्नसक्थमचेतसम् ।अश्वत्थामा समुद्वीक्ष्य पुनर्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥४६॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā rājānaṁ bhagnasakthamacetasam ,aśvatthāmā samudvīkṣya punarvacanamabravīt.
english: Having thus spoken to the king, who lay unconscious with his thighs broken, Aśvatthāman looked at him and spoke again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 46. Having thus spoken to the king, who lay unconscious with his thighs broken, Aśvatthāman looked at him and spoke again.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन जीवसि चेद्वाचं श्रोत्रसुखां शृणु ।सप्त पाण्डवतः शेषा धार्तराष्ट्रास्त्रयो वयम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: duryodhana jīvasi cedvācaṁ śrotrasukhāṁ śṛṇu ,sapta pāṇḍavataḥ śeṣā dhārtarāṣṭrāstrayo vayam.
english: O Duryodhana, if you are still alive, listen to these words that are pleasing to the ear: seven from the Pāṇḍava side remain, and we Dhārtarāṣṭras are three.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 47. O Duryodhana, if you are still alive, listen to these words that are pleasing to the ear: seven from the Pāṇḍava side remain, and we Dhārtarāṣṭras are three.

sanskrit: ते चैव भ्रातरः पञ्च वासुदेवोऽथ सात्यकिः ।अहं च कृतवर्मा च कृपः शारद्वतस्तथा ॥४८॥
transliteration: te caiva bhrātaraḥ pañca vāsudevo'tha sātyakiḥ ,ahaṁ ca kṛtavarmā ca kṛpaḥ śāradvatastathā.
english: And those five brothers (the Pāṇḍavas), along with Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Sātyaki, (are the seven from the Pāṇḍava side who remain). And I, Kṛtavarmā, and Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat, (are we three).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 48. And those five brothers (the Pāṇḍavas), along with Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Sātyaki, (are the seven from the Pāṇḍava side who remain). And I, Kṛtavarmā, and Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat, (are we three).

sanskrit: द्रौपदेया हताः सर्वे धृष्टद्युम्नस्य चात्मजाः ।पाञ्चाला निहताः सर्वे मत्स्यशेषं च भारत ॥४९॥
transliteration: draupadeyā hatāḥ sarve dhṛṣṭadyumnasya cātmajāḥ ,pāñcālā nihatāḥ sarve matsyaśeṣaṁ ca bhārata.
english: All the sons of Draupadī have been slain, and also the children of Dhṛṣṭadyumna. All the Pāñcālas have been annihilated, O (descendant of) Bhārata (Duryodhana), with only a remnant of the Matsyas remaining.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 49. All the sons of Draupadī have been slain, and also the children of Dhṛṣṭadyumna. All the Pāñcālas have been annihilated, O (descendant of) Bhārata (Duryodhana), with only a remnant of the Matsyas remaining.

sanskrit: कृते प्रतिकृतं पश्य हतपुत्रा हि पाण्डवाः ।सौप्तिके शिबिरं तेषां हतं सनरवाहनम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: kṛte pratikṛtaṁ paśya hataputrā hi pāṇḍavāḥ ,sauptike śibiraṁ teṣāṁ hataṁ sanaravāhanam.
english: See the retaliation for their actions! Indeed, the Pāṇḍavas have had their sons slain. Their camp, including men and vehicles, was destroyed in the night attack.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 50. See the retaliation for their actions! Indeed, the Pāṇḍavas have had their sons slain. Their camp, including men and vehicles, was destroyed in the night attack.

sanskrit: मया च पापकर्मासौ धृष्टद्युम्नो महीपते ।प्रविश्य शिबिरं रात्रौ पशुमारेण मारितः ॥५१॥
transliteration: mayā ca pāpakarmāsau dhṛṣṭadyumno mahīpate ,praviśya śibiraṁ rātrau paśumāreṇa māritaḥ.
english: And, O king, I entered their camp at night and killed that evil-doer Dhṛṣṭadyumna as if he were an animal.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 51. And, O king, I entered their camp at night and killed that evil-doer Dhṛṣṭadyumna as if he were an animal.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु तां वाचं निशम्य मनसः प्रियाम् ।प्रतिलभ्य पुनश्चेत इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu tāṁ vācaṁ niśamya manasaḥ priyām ,pratilabhya punaśceta idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: But Duryodhana, having heard those words that were pleasing to his mind, and having regained his consciousness, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 52. But Duryodhana, having heard those words that were pleasing to his mind, and having regained his consciousness, spoke these words.

sanskrit: न मेऽकरोत्तद्गाङ्गेयो न कर्णो न च ते पिता ।यत्त्वया कृपभोजाभ्यां सहितेनाद्य मे कृतम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: na me'karottadgāṅgeyo na karṇo na ca te pitā ,yattvayā kṛpabhojābhyāṁ sahitenādya me kṛtam.
english: Neither Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma), nor Karṇa, nor your father did for me what you, accompanied by Kṛpa and Bhoja, have done for me today.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 53. Neither Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma), nor Karṇa, nor your father did for me what you, accompanied by Kṛpa and Bhoja, have done for me today.

sanskrit: स चेत्सेनापतिः क्षुद्रो हतः सार्धं शिखण्डिना ।तेन मन्ये मघवता सममात्मानमद्य वै ॥५४॥
transliteration: sa cetsenāpatiḥ kṣudro hataḥ sārdhaṁ śikhaṇḍinā ,tena manye maghavatā samamātmānamadya vai.
english: If that contemptible commander has been killed along with Śikhaṇḍin, then today I certainly consider myself (ātman) equal to Indra (Maghavat).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 54. If that contemptible commander has been killed along with Śikhaṇḍin, then today I certainly consider myself (ātman) equal to Indra (Maghavat).

sanskrit: स्वस्ति प्राप्नुत भद्रं वः स्वर्गे नः संगमः पुनः ।इत्येवमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं स कुरुराजो महामनाः ।प्राणानुदसृजद्वीरः सुहृदां शोकमादधत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: svasti prāpnuta bhadraṁ vaḥ svarge naḥ saṁgamaḥ punaḥ ,ityevamuktvā tūṣṇīṁ sa kururājo mahāmanāḥ ,prāṇānudasṛjadvīraḥ suhṛdāṁ śokamādadhat.
english: "May you attain well-being; good fortune be upon you. In heaven (svarga), we shall meet again." Having spoken thus, that great-souled king of the Kurus fell silent. The hero then gave up his life-breaths (prāṇa), casting sorrow upon his friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 55. "May you attain well-being; good fortune be upon you. In heaven (svarga), we shall meet again." Having spoken thus, that great-souled king of the Kurus fell silent. The hero then gave up his life-breaths (prāṇa), casting sorrow upon his friends.

sanskrit: तथेति ते परिष्वक्ताः परिष्वज्य च तं नृपम् ।पुनः पुनः प्रेक्षमाणाः स्वकानारुरुहू रथान् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tatheti te pariṣvaktāḥ pariṣvajya ca taṁ nṛpam ,punaḥ punaḥ prekṣamāṇāḥ svakānāruruhū rathān.
english: "So be it!" they said, having been embraced by him. And having embraced that king in return, they mounted their own chariots, repeatedly looking back (at him).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 56. "So be it!" they said, having been embraced by him. And having embraced that king in return, they mounted their own chariots, repeatedly looking back (at him).

sanskrit: इत्येवं तव पुत्रस्य निशम्य करुणां गिरम् ।प्रत्यूषकाले शोकार्तः प्राधावं नगरं प्रति ॥५७॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ tava putrasya niśamya karuṇāṁ giram ,pratyūṣakāle śokārtaḥ prādhāvaṁ nagaraṁ prati.
english: Having heard such pitiful words of your son, I, distressed by grief, ran towards the city at dawn.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 57. Having heard such pitiful words of your son, I, distressed by grief, ran towards the city at dawn.

sanskrit: तव पुत्रे गते स्वर्गं शोकार्तस्य ममानघ ।ऋषिदत्तं प्रनष्टं तद्दिव्यदर्शित्वमद्य वै ॥५८॥
transliteration: tava putre gate svargaṁ śokārtasya mamānagha ,ṛṣidattaṁ pranaṣṭaṁ taddivyadarśitvamadya vai.
english: Oh sinless one, with your son (Duryodhana) having gone to heaven, for me, afflicted by sorrow, that divine vision, granted by the sage, has indeed been lost today.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 58. Oh sinless one, with your son (Duryodhana) having gone to heaven, for me, afflicted by sorrow, that divine vision, granted by the sage, has indeed been lost today.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इति श्रुत्वा स नृपतिः पुत्रज्ञातिवधं तदा ।निःश्वस्य दीर्घमुष्णं च ततश्चिन्तापरोऽभवत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,iti śrutvā sa nṛpatiḥ putrajñātivadhaṁ tadā ,niḥśvasya dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca tataścintāparo'bhavat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this report about the slaughter of his sons and kinsmen, that king (Dhritarashtra) at that time sighed deeply and warmly, and then became engrossed in anxiety.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 59. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this report about the slaughter of his sons and kinsmen, that king (Dhritarashtra) at that time sighed deeply and warmly, and then became engrossed in anxiety.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायां धृष्टद्युम्नस्य सारथिः ।शशंस धर्मराजाय सौप्तिके कदनं कृतम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasyāṁ rātryāṁ vyatītāyāṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnasya sārathiḥ ,śaśaṁsa dharmarājāya sauptike kadanaṁ kṛtam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When that night had passed, Dhṛṣṭadyumna's charioteer reported to Yudhishthira (the king of natural law (dharma)) the slaughter that had been committed in the night attack (sauptika).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When that night had passed, Dhṛṣṭadyumna's charioteer reported to Yudhishthira (the king of natural law (dharma)) the slaughter that had been committed in the night attack (sauptika).

sanskrit: द्रौपदेया महाराज द्रुपदस्यात्मजैः सह ।प्रमत्ता निशि विश्वस्ताः स्वपन्तः शिबिरे स्वके ॥२॥
transliteration: draupadeyā mahārāja drupadasyātmajaiḥ saha ,pramattā niśi viśvastāḥ svapantaḥ śibire svake.
english: O great king, Draupadī's sons, along with Drupada's sons, were heedless at night, trusting and sleeping in their own camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. O great king, Draupadī's sons, along with Drupada's sons, were heedless at night, trusting and sleeping in their own camp.

sanskrit: कृतवर्मणा नृशंसेन गौतमेन कृपेण च ।अश्वत्थाम्ना च पापेन हतं वः शिबिरं निशि ॥३॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmaṇā nṛśaṁsena gautamena kṛpeṇa ca ,aśvatthāmnā ca pāpena hataṁ vaḥ śibiraṁ niśi.
english: Your camp was destroyed at night by the cruel Kṛtavarman, by Gautama Kṛpa, and by the wicked Aśvatthāman.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. Your camp was destroyed at night by the cruel Kṛtavarman, by Gautama Kṛpa, and by the wicked Aśvatthāman.

sanskrit: एतैर्नरगजाश्वानां प्रासशक्तिपरश्वधैः ।सहस्राणि निकृन्तद्भिर्निःशेषं ते बलं कृतम् ॥४॥
transliteration: etairnaragajāśvānāṁ prāsaśaktiparaśvadhaiḥ ,sahasrāṇi nikṛntadbhirniḥśeṣaṁ te balaṁ kṛtam.
english: By these (three), who were cutting down thousands of men, elephants, and horses with their spears, lances, and axes, your army was completely destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. By these (three), who were cutting down thousands of men, elephants, and horses with their spears, lances, and axes, your army was completely destroyed.

sanskrit: छिद्यमानस्य महतो वनस्येव परश्वधैः ।शुश्रुवे सुमहाञ्शब्दो बलस्य तव भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: chidyamānasya mahato vanasyeva paraśvadhaiḥ ,śuśruve sumahāñśabdo balasya tava bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, a very great sound was heard from your army, just like that of a vast forest being cut down by axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. O Bhārata, a very great sound was heard from your army, just like that of a vast forest being cut down by axes.

sanskrit: अहमेकोऽवशिष्टस्तु तस्मात्सैन्यान्महीपते ।मुक्तः कथंचिद्धर्मात्मन्व्यग्रस्य कृतवर्मणः ॥६॥
transliteration: ahameko'vaśiṣṭastu tasmātsainyānmahīpate ,muktaḥ kathaṁciddharmātmanvyagrasya kṛtavarmaṇaḥ.
english: O King, I alone am left from that army; somehow I escaped from Kṛtavarman, who was preoccupied, O righteous soul (dharma-ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. O King, I alone am left from that army; somehow I escaped from Kṛtavarman, who was preoccupied, O righteous soul (dharma-ātman).

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा वाक्यमशिवं कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।पपात मह्यां दुर्धर्षः पुत्रशोकसमन्वितः ॥७॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā vākyamaśivaṁ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,papāta mahyāṁ durdharṣaḥ putraśokasamanvitaḥ.
english: Having heard that inauspicious news, the unassailable Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, fell to the ground, overcome with grief for his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. Having heard that inauspicious news, the unassailable Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, fell to the ground, overcome with grief for his sons.

sanskrit: तं पतन्तमभिक्रम्य परिजग्राह सात्यकिः ।भीमसेनोऽर्जुनश्चैव माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ॥८॥
transliteration: taṁ patantamabhikramya parijagrāha sātyakiḥ ,bhīmaseno'rjunaścaiva mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau.
english: Approaching him as he fell, Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and Arjuna, along with the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, supported him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. Approaching him as he fell, Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and Arjuna, along with the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, supported him.

sanskrit: लब्धचेतास्तु कौन्तेयः शोकविह्वलया गिरा ।जित्वा शत्रूञ्जितः पश्चात्पर्यदेवयदातुरः ॥९॥
transliteration: labdhacetāstu kaunteyaḥ śokavihvalayā girā ,jitvā śatrūñjitaḥ paścātparyadevayadāturaḥ.
english: But having regained consciousness, the distressed son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), who had conquered his enemies, yet was defeated afterwards, lamented with a voice trembling from sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. But having regained consciousness, the distressed son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), who had conquered his enemies, yet was defeated afterwards, lamented with a voice trembling from sorrow.

sanskrit: दुर्विदा गतिरर्थानामपि ये दिव्यचक्षुषः ।जीयमाना जयन्त्यन्ये जयमाना वयं जिताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: durvidā gatirarthānāmapi ye divyacakṣuṣaḥ ,jīyamānā jayantyanye jayamānā vayaṁ jitāḥ.
english: Difficult to comprehend is the course of events, even for those with divine vision. Some, though being defeated, conquer, while we, though conquering, are defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Difficult to comprehend is the course of events, even for those with divine vision. Some, though being defeated, conquer, while we, though conquering, are defeated.

sanskrit: हत्वा भ्रातॄन्वयस्यांश्च पितॄन्पुत्रान्सुहृद्गणान् ।बन्धूनमात्यान्पौत्रांश्च जित्वा सर्वाञ्जिता वयम् ॥११॥
transliteration: hatvā bhrātṝnvayasyāṁśca pitṝnputrānsuhṛdgaṇān ,bandhūnamātyānpautrāṁśca jitvā sarvāñjitā vayam.
english: Having killed brothers, friends, fathers, sons, well-wishers, relatives, ministers, and grandsons, and having conquered all of them, we ourselves would effectively be defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. Having killed brothers, friends, fathers, sons, well-wishers, relatives, ministers, and grandsons, and having conquered all of them, we ourselves would effectively be defeated.

sanskrit: अनर्थो ह्यर्थसंकाशस्तथार्थोऽनर्थदर्शनः ।जयोऽयमजयाकारो जयस्तस्मात्पराजयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: anartho hyarthasaṁkāśastathārtho'narthadarśanaḥ ,jayo'yamajayākāro jayastasmātparājayaḥ.
english: Indeed, a misfortune (anartha) often appears like a gain (artha), and similarly, a gain can appear like a misfortune. This victory has the nature of a defeat; therefore, this victory is truly a defeat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. Indeed, a misfortune (anartha) often appears like a gain (artha), and similarly, a gain can appear like a misfortune. This victory has the nature of a defeat; therefore, this victory is truly a defeat.

sanskrit: यं जित्वा तप्यते पश्चादापन्न इव दुर्मतिः ।कथं मन्येत विजयं ततो जिततरः परैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yaṁ jitvā tapyate paścādāpanna iva durmatiḥ ,kathaṁ manyeta vijayaṁ tato jitataraḥ paraiḥ.
english: When a person, after conquering someone, repents afterward like a fool who has fallen into misfortune, how can he consider that a victory? He is, in fact, more truly defeated than if he had been conquered by others.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. When a person, after conquering someone, repents afterward like a fool who has fallen into misfortune, how can he consider that a victory? He is, in fact, more truly defeated than if he had been conquered by others.

sanskrit: येषामर्थाय पापस्य धिग्जयस्य सुहृद्वधे ।निर्जितैरप्रमत्तैर्हि विजिता जितकाशिनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yeṣāmarthāya pāpasya dhigjayasya suhṛdvadhe ,nirjitairapramattairhi vijitā jitakāśinaḥ.
english: Shame on such a sinful victory, which is achieved in the slaughter of kinsmen and undertaken for the sake of those (wicked ones)! Indeed, those who appear victorious are truly vanquished by the (physically) defeated but morally vigilant ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. Shame on such a sinful victory, which is achieved in the slaughter of kinsmen and undertaken for the sake of those (wicked ones)! Indeed, those who appear victorious are truly vanquished by the (physically) defeated but morally vigilant ones.

sanskrit: कर्णिनालीकदंष्ट्रस्य खड्गजिह्वस्य संयुगे ।चापव्यात्तस्य रौद्रस्य ज्यातलस्वननादिनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: karṇinālīkadaṁṣṭrasya khaḍgajihvasya saṁyuge ,cāpavyāttasya raudrasya jyātalasvananādinaḥ.
english: In battle (saṃyuga), of the fierce one, whose tusks are like arrows and spears, whose tongue is like swords, whose mouth is opened wide by the bow, and who roars with the sound of bowstrings and hand-slaps.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. In battle (saṃyuga), of the fierce one, whose tusks are like arrows and spears, whose tongue is like swords, whose mouth is opened wide by the bow, and who roars with the sound of bowstrings and hand-slaps.

sanskrit: क्रुद्धस्य नरसिंहस्य संग्रामेष्वपलायिनः ।ये व्यमुच्यन्त कर्णस्य प्रमादात्त इमे हताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kruddhasya narasiṁhasya saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinaḥ ,ye vyamucyanta karṇasya pramādātta ime hatāḥ.
english: Of the enraged man-lion (Narasimha), who never retreats from battles. Those who escaped Karna due to his oversight (pramāda), those very ones have now been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. Of the enraged man-lion (Narasimha), who never retreats from battles. Those who escaped Karna due to his oversight (pramāda), those very ones have now been killed.

sanskrit: रथह्रदं शरवर्षोर्मिमन्तं रत्नाचितं वाहनराजियुक्तम् ।शक्त्यृष्टिमीनध्वजनागनक्रं शरासनावर्तमहेषुफेनम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: rathahradaṁ śaravarṣormimantaṁ; ratnācitaṁ vāhanarājiyuktam ,śaktyṛṣṭimīnadhvajanāganakraṁ; śarāsanāvartamaheṣuphenam.
english: (Describing an ocean-like battlefield) having chariots as deep pools, having waves of arrow-showers, strewn with gems, endowed with rows of conveyances, having spears and javelins as fish, and banners as crocodiles, and having whirlpools of bows and foam of great arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. (Describing an ocean-like battlefield) having chariots as deep pools, having waves of arrow-showers, strewn with gems, endowed with rows of conveyances, having spears and javelins as fish, and banners as crocodiles, and having whirlpools of bows and foam of great arrows.

sanskrit: संग्रामचन्द्रोदयवेगवेलं द्रोणार्णवं ज्यातलनेमिघोषम् ।ये तेरुरुच्चावचशस्त्रनौभिस्ते राजपुत्रा निहताः प्रमादात् ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁgrāmacandrodayavegavelaṁ; droṇārṇavaṁ jyātalanemighoṣam ,ye teruruccāvacaśastranaubhi;ste rājaputrā nihatāḥ pramādāt.
english: And of the Drona-ocean (droṇārṇava), whose swift tide (vela) is like the rising moon (candrodaya) of battle (saṃgrāma), and whose roar (ghoṣa) is the combined sound of bowstrings (jyā), hand-slaps (tala), and chariot wheels (nemi). Those princes who crossed it with various weapon-boats, those very ones were killed due to carelessness (pramāda).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. And of the Drona-ocean (droṇārṇava), whose swift tide (vela) is like the rising moon (candrodaya) of battle (saṃgrāma), and whose roar (ghoṣa) is the combined sound of bowstrings (jyā), hand-slaps (tala), and chariot wheels (nemi). Those princes who crossed it with various weapon-boats, those very ones were killed due to carelessness (pramāda).

sanskrit: न हि प्रमादात्परमोऽस्ति कश्चिद्वधो नराणामिह जीवलोके ।प्रमत्तमर्था हि नरं समन्तात्त्यजन्त्यनर्थाश्च समाविशन्ति ॥१९॥
transliteration: na hi pramādātparamo'sti kaści;dvadho narāṇāmiha jīvaloke ,pramattamarthā hi naraṁ samantā;ttyajantyanarthāśca samāviśanti.
english: Indeed, there is no greater destruction for human beings in this world than that caused by heedlessness (pramāda). For, possessions truly abandon a negligent person from all sides, and misfortunes completely overtake him.
