search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. Indeed, there is no greater destruction for human beings in this world than that caused by heedlessness (pramāda). For, possessions truly abandon a negligent person from all sides, and misfortunes completely overtake him.

sanskrit: ध्वजोत्तमाग्रोच्छ्रितधूमकेतुं शरार्चिषं कोपमहासमीरम् ।महाधनुर्ज्यातलनेमिघोषं तनुत्रनानाविधशस्त्रहोमम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: dhvajottamāgrocchritadhūmaketuṁ; śarārciṣaṁ kopamahāsamīram ,mahādhanurjyātalanemighoṣaṁ; tanutranānāvidhaśastrahomam.
english: This describes a formidable entity, likened to a destructive fire: one whose banner of smoke rises high from the top of an excellent flag, whose flames are arrows, whose great wind is wrath, whose roar is the sound from mighty bows, their bowstrings, and the striking of palms, and whose sacrificial offering consists of various kinds of weapons (falling) upon armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. This describes a formidable entity, likened to a destructive fire: one whose banner of smoke rises high from the top of an excellent flag, whose flames are arrows, whose great wind is wrath, whose roar is the sound from mighty bows, their bowstrings, and the striking of palms, and whose sacrificial offering consists of various kinds of weapons (falling) upon armor.

sanskrit: महाचमूकक्षवराभिपन्नं महाहवे भीष्ममहादवाग्निम् ।ये सेहुरात्तायतशस्त्रवेगं ते राजपुत्रा निहताः प्रमादात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: mahācamūkakṣavarābhipannaṁ; mahāhave bhīṣmamahādavāgnim ,ye sehurāttāyataśastravegaṁ; te rājaputrā nihatāḥ pramādāt.
english: This describes Bhishma, the great forest fire of the mighty battle, by whom the excellent warriors of the great army-thicket were afflicted/overcome. Those princes who endured (his) fierce and extended force of weapons, were (ultimately) destroyed due to heedlessness (pramāda).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. This describes Bhishma, the great forest fire of the mighty battle, by whom the excellent warriors of the great army-thicket were afflicted/overcome. Those princes who endured (his) fierce and extended force of weapons, were (ultimately) destroyed due to heedlessness (pramāda).

sanskrit: न हि प्रमत्तेन नरेण लभ्या विद्या तपः श्रीर्विपुलं यशो वा ।पश्याप्रमादेन निहत्य शत्रून्सर्वान्महेन्द्रं सुखमेधमानम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: na hi pramattena nareṇa labhyā; vidyā tapaḥ śrīrvipulaṁ yaśo vā ,paśyāpramādena nihatya śatrū;nsarvānmahendraṁ sukhamedhamānam.
english: Indeed, knowledge, spiritual austerity (tapas), glory, or vast fame cannot be attained by a negligent person. Observe Mahendra (Indra), who, by diligently (apramāda) destroying all his enemies, thrives in happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. Indeed, knowledge, spiritual austerity (tapas), glory, or vast fame cannot be attained by a negligent person. Observe Mahendra (Indra), who, by diligently (apramāda) destroying all his enemies, thrives in happiness.

sanskrit: इन्द्रोपमान्पार्थिवपुत्रपौत्रान्पश्याविशेषेण हतान्प्रमादात् ।तीर्त्वा समुद्रं वणिजः समृद्धाः सन्नाः कुनद्यामिव हेलमानाः ।अमर्षितैर्ये निहताः शयाना निःसंशयं ते त्रिदिवं प्रपन्नाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: indropamānpārthivaputrapautrā;npaśyāviśeṣeṇa hatānpramādāt ,tīrtvā samudraṁ vaṇijaḥ samṛddhāḥ; sannāḥ kunadyāmiva helamānāḥ ,amarṣitairye nihatāḥ śayānā; niḥsaṁśayaṁ te tridivaṁ prapannāḥ.
english: Behold these royal sons and grandsons, mighty like Indra, slain indiscriminately due to recklessness! They are like prosperous merchants who, having successfully crossed the vast ocean, carelessly drown in a small, wretched river. Without a doubt, those who were killed by the enraged (Pandavas) and now lie dead have attained heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. Behold these royal sons and grandsons, mighty like Indra, slain indiscriminately due to recklessness! They are like prosperous merchants who, having successfully crossed the vast ocean, carelessly drown in a small, wretched river. Without a doubt, those who were killed by the enraged (Pandavas) and now lie dead have attained heaven.

sanskrit: कृष्णां नु शोचामि कथं न साध्वीं शोकार्णवे साद्य विनङ्क्ष्यतीति ।भ्रातॄंश्च पुत्रांश्च हतान्निशम्य पाञ्चालराजं पितरं च वृद्धम् ।ध्रुवं विसंज्ञा पतिता पृथिव्यां सा शेष्यते शोककृशाङ्गयष्टिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇāṁ nu śocāmi kathaṁ na sādhvīṁ; śokārṇave sādya vinaṅkṣyatīti ,bhrātṝṁśca putrāṁśca hatānniśamya; pāñcālarājaṁ pitaraṁ ca vṛddham ,dhruvaṁ visaṁjñā patitā pṛthivyāṁ; sā śeṣyate śokakṛśāṅgayaṣṭiḥ.
english: How can I not grieve for the virtuous Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī)? I fear she will certainly perish today in this ocean of sorrow (śokārṇava). Upon learning that her brothers, her sons, and her aged father, the king of Pañcāla, have been killed, she will undoubtedly fall unconscious to the earth, her body-frame emaciated by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 24. How can I not grieve for the virtuous Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī)? I fear she will certainly perish today in this ocean of sorrow (śokārṇava). Upon learning that her brothers, her sons, and her aged father, the king of Pañcāla, have been killed, she will undoubtedly fall unconscious to the earth, her body-frame emaciated by grief.

sanskrit: तच्छोकजं दुःखमपारयन्ती कथं भविष्यत्युचिता सुखानाम् ।पुत्रक्षयभ्रातृवधप्रणुन्ना प्रदह्यमानेव हुताशनेन ॥२५॥
transliteration: tacchokajaṁ duḥkhamapārayantī; kathaṁ bhaviṣyatyucitā sukhānām ,putrakṣayabhrātṛvadhapraṇunnā; pradahyamāneva hutāśanena.
english: How will she, unable to bear such profound suffering born of grief, ever again be fit for happiness? Tormented by the destruction of her sons and the killing of her brothers, she will be as if consumed by a blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 25. How will she, unable to bear such profound suffering born of grief, ever again be fit for happiness? Tormented by the destruction of her sons and the killing of her brothers, she will be as if consumed by a blazing fire.

sanskrit: इत्येवमार्तः परिदेवयन्स राजा कुरूणां नकुलं बभाषे ।गच्छानयैनामिह मन्दभाग्यां समातृपक्षामिति राजपुत्रीम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityevamārtaḥ paridevayansa; rājā kurūṇāṁ nakulaṁ babhāṣe ,gacchānayaināmiha mandabhāgyāṁ; samātṛpakṣāmiti rājaputrīm.
english: Thus, lamenting in this distressed manner, that king of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke to Nakula: 'Go, and bring here that unfortunate princess (Subhadrā), along with her maternal relatives!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 26. Thus, lamenting in this distressed manner, that king of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke to Nakula: 'Go, and bring here that unfortunate princess (Subhadrā), along with her maternal relatives!'

sanskrit: माद्रीसुतस्तत्परिगृह्य वाक्यं धर्मेण धर्मप्रतिमस्य राज्ञः ।ययौ रथेनालयमाशु देव्याः पाञ्चालराजस्य च यत्र दाराः ॥२७॥
transliteration: mādrīsutastatparigṛhya vākyaṁ; dharmeṇa dharmapratimasya rājñaḥ ,yayau rathenālayamāśu devyāḥ; pāñcālarājasya ca yatra dārāḥ.
english: The son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), having accepted that command from the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was as righteous as the very constitution (dharma), quickly went by chariot to the abode where the wives of the Pañcāla king and the goddess (Draupadī) were.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 27. The son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), having accepted that command from the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was as righteous as the very constitution (dharma), quickly went by chariot to the abode where the wives of the Pañcāla king and the goddess (Draupadī) were.

sanskrit: प्रस्थाप्य माद्रीसुतमाजमीढः शोकार्दितस्तैः सहितः सुहृद्भिः ।रोरूयमाणः प्रययौ सुतानामायोधनं भूतगणानुकीर्णम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: prasthāpya mādrīsutamājamīḍhaḥ; śokārditastaiḥ sahitaḥ suhṛdbhiḥ ,rorūyamāṇaḥ prayayau sutānā;māyodhanaṁ bhūtagaṇānukīrṇam.
english: Having sent forth the son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), the descendant of Ajamiḍha (Yudhiṣṭhira), afflicted by sorrow and accompanied by his friends, went forth wailing loudly to the battlefield of his sons, which was strewn with hosts of spirits.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 28. Having sent forth the son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), the descendant of Ajamiḍha (Yudhiṣṭhira), afflicted by sorrow and accompanied by his friends, went forth wailing loudly to the battlefield of his sons, which was strewn with hosts of spirits.

sanskrit: स तत्प्रविश्याशिवमुग्ररूपं ददर्श पुत्रान्सुहृदः सखींश्च ।भूमौ शयानान्रुधिरार्द्रगात्रान्विभिन्नभग्नापहृतोत्तमाङ्गान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tatpraviśyāśivamugrarūpaṁ; dadarśa putrānsuhṛdaḥ sakhīṁśca ,bhūmau śayānānrudhirārdragātrā;nvibhinnabhagnāpahṛtottamāṅgān.
english: Having entered that inauspicious and dreadful place, he saw his sons, friends, and companions lying on the ground, their bodies drenched in blood, and their heads shattered, broken, or severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 29. Having entered that inauspicious and dreadful place, he saw his sons, friends, and companions lying on the ground, their bodies drenched in blood, and their heads shattered, broken, or severed.

sanskrit: स तांस्तु दृष्ट्वा भृशमार्तरूपो युधिष्ठिरो धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठः ।उच्चैः प्रचुक्रोश च कौरवाग्र्यः पपात चोर्व्यां सगणो विसंज्ञः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa tāṁstu dṛṣṭvā bhṛśamārtarūpo; yudhiṣṭhiro dharmabhṛtāṁ variṣṭhaḥ ,uccaiḥ pracukrośa ca kauravāgryaḥ; papāta corvyāṁ sagaṇo visaṁjñaḥ.
english: Indeed, having seen them, Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) and the chief of the Kauravas, appeared exceedingly distressed. He cried out loudly and, along with his retinue, fell unconscious to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 30. Indeed, having seen them, Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) and the chief of the Kauravas, appeared exceedingly distressed. He cried out loudly and, along with his retinue, fell unconscious to the ground.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स दृष्ट्वा निहतान्संख्ये पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्सखींस्तथा ।महादुःखपरीतात्मा बभूव जनमेजय ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa dṛṣṭvā nihatānsaṁkhye putrānbhrātṝnsakhīṁstathā ,mahāduḥkhaparītātmā babhūva janamejaya.
english: Vaiśampayana said: Janamejaya, upon seeing his sons, brothers, and friends slain in battle, became deeply overwhelmed with great sorrow (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: Janamejaya, upon seeing his sons, brothers, and friends slain in battle, became deeply overwhelmed with great sorrow (ātman).

sanskrit: ततस्तस्य महाञ्शोकः प्रादुरासीन्महात्मनः ।स्मरतः पुत्रपौत्राणां भ्रातॄणां स्वजनस्य ह ॥२॥
transliteration: tatastasya mahāñśokaḥ prādurāsīnmahātmanaḥ ,smarataḥ putrapautrāṇāṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ svajanasya ha.
english: Then, a great sorrow arose in that great-souled (ātman) Janamejaya, as he remembered his sons, grandsons, brothers, and his own kin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. Then, a great sorrow arose in that great-souled (ātman) Janamejaya, as he remembered his sons, grandsons, brothers, and his own kin.

sanskrit: तमश्रुपरिपूर्णाक्षं वेपमानमचेतसम् ।सुहृदो भृशसंविग्नाः सान्त्वयां चक्रिरे तदा ॥३॥
transliteration: tamaśruparipūrṇākṣaṁ vepamānamacetasam ,suhṛdo bhṛśasaṁvignāḥ sāntvayāṁ cakrire tadā.
english: Then, his greatly distressed friends consoled him, who was trembling, senseless, and whose eyes were filled with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. Then, his greatly distressed friends consoled him, who was trembling, senseless, and whose eyes were filled with tears.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्मिन्क्षणे काल्ये रथेनादित्यवर्चसा ।नकुलः कृष्णया सार्धमुपायात्परमार्तया ॥४॥
transliteration: tatastasminkṣaṇe kālye rathenādityavarcasā ,nakulaḥ kṛṣṇayā sārdhamupāyātparamārtayā.
english: Then, at that opportune moment, Nakula arrived by a chariot gleaming like the sun, accompanied by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was extremely distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. Then, at that opportune moment, Nakula arrived by a chariot gleaming like the sun, accompanied by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was extremely distressed.

sanskrit: उपप्लव्यगता सा तु श्रुत्वा सुमहदप्रियम् ।तदा विनाशं पुत्राणां सर्वेषां व्यथिताभवत् ॥५॥
transliteration: upaplavyagatā sā tu śrutvā sumahadapriyam ,tadā vināśaṁ putrāṇāṁ sarveṣāṁ vyathitābhavat.
english: But she, while in Upaplavya, upon hearing the very great misfortune of the destruction of all her sons, became greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. But she, while in Upaplavya, upon hearing the very great misfortune of the destruction of all her sons, became greatly distressed.

sanskrit: कम्पमानेव कदली वातेनाभिसमीरिता ।कृष्णा राजानमासाद्य शोकार्ता न्यपतद्भुवि ॥६॥
transliteration: kampamāneva kadalī vātenābhisamīritā ,kṛṣṇā rājānamāsādya śokārtā nyapatadbhuvi.
english: Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), overwhelmed by grief, approached the king and fell to the ground, like a banana tree swayed intensely by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), overwhelmed by grief, approached the king and fell to the ground, like a banana tree swayed intensely by the wind.

sanskrit: बभूव वदनं तस्याः सहसा शोककर्शितम् ।फुल्लपद्मपलाशाक्ष्यास्तमोध्वस्त इवांशुमान् ॥७॥
transliteration: babhūva vadanaṁ tasyāḥ sahasā śokakarśitam ,phullapadmapalāśākṣyāstamodhvasta ivāṁśumān.
english: Her face, belonging to one with eyes like full-blown lotus petals, suddenly became emaciated by grief, resembling the sun eclipsed by darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. Her face, belonging to one with eyes like full-blown lotus petals, suddenly became emaciated by grief, resembling the sun eclipsed by darkness.

sanskrit: ततस्तां पतितां दृष्ट्वा संरम्भी सत्यविक्रमः ।बाहुभ्यां परिजग्राह समुपेत्य वृकोदरः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatastāṁ patitāṁ dṛṣṭvā saṁrambhī satyavikramaḥ ,bāhubhyāṁ parijagrāha samupetya vṛkodaraḥ.
english: Then Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who was impetuous and truly mighty, having approached her and seen her fallen, embraced her with both arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. Then Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who was impetuous and truly mighty, having approached her and seen her fallen, embraced her with both arms.

sanskrit: सा समाश्वासिता तेन भीमसेनेन भामिनी ।रुदती पाण्डवं कृष्णा सहभ्रातरमब्रवीत् ॥९॥
transliteration: sā samāśvāsitā tena bhīmasenena bhāminī ,rudatī pāṇḍavaṁ kṛṣṇā sahabhrātaramabravīt.
english: Having been comforted by Bhīmasena, the beautiful Draupadī, weeping, spoke to the Pāṇḍava [Yudhiṣṭhira] who was accompanied by his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. Having been comforted by Bhīmasena, the beautiful Draupadī, weeping, spoke to the Pāṇḍava [Yudhiṣṭhira] who was accompanied by his brothers.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या राजंस्त्वमद्येमामखिलां भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।आत्मजान्क्षत्रधर्मेण संप्रदाय यमाय वै ॥१०॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā rājaṁstvamadyemāmakhilāṁ bhokṣyase mahīm ,ātmajānkṣatradharmeṇa saṁpradāya yamāya vai.
english: O King, fortunately, you will today rule this entire earth, having consigned your sons to Yama, the god of death, in accordance with the warrior's code (kṣatra-dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. O King, fortunately, you will today rule this entire earth, having consigned your sons to Yama, the god of death, in accordance with the warrior's code (kṣatra-dharma).

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या त्वं पार्थ कुशली मत्तमातङ्गगामिनम् ।अवाप्य पृथिवीं कृत्स्नां सौभद्रं न स्मरिष्यसि ॥११॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tvaṁ pārtha kuśalī mattamātaṅgagāminam ,avāpya pṛthivīṁ kṛtsnāṁ saubhadraṁ na smariṣyasi.
english: Fortunately, O Pārtha (Arjuna), you are well! Having regained the entire earth, you will not remember Saubhadra [Abhimanyu], who walked with the gait of a furious elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. Fortunately, O Pārtha (Arjuna), you are well! Having regained the entire earth, you will not remember Saubhadra [Abhimanyu], who walked with the gait of a furious elephant.

sanskrit: आत्मजांस्तेन धर्मेण श्रुत्वा शूरान्निपातितान् ।उपप्लव्ये मया सार्धं दिष्ट्या त्वं न स्मरिष्यसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: ātmajāṁstena dharmeṇa śrutvā śūrānnipātitān ,upaplavye mayā sārdhaṁ diṣṭyā tvaṁ na smariṣyasi.
english: Fortunately, after hearing that your brave sons were struck down by that very law (dharma), you will not remember them, though you were with me at Upaplavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. Fortunately, after hearing that your brave sons were struck down by that very law (dharma), you will not remember them, though you were with me at Upaplavya.

sanskrit: प्रसुप्तानां वधं श्रुत्वा द्रौणिना पापकर्मणा ।शोकस्तपति मां पार्थ हुताशन इवाशयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: prasuptānāṁ vadhaṁ śrutvā drauṇinā pāpakarmaṇā ,śokastapati māṁ pārtha hutāśana ivāśayam.
english: Having heard of the slaughter of my sleeping sons by Drauṇi, that perpetrator of sinful acts, grief consumes me, O Pārtha, just as fire consumes a dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. Having heard of the slaughter of my sleeping sons by Drauṇi, that perpetrator of sinful acts, grief consumes me, O Pārtha, just as fire consumes a dwelling.

sanskrit: तस्य पापकृतो द्रौणेर्न चेदद्य त्वया मृधे ।ह्रियते सानुबन्धस्य युधि विक्रम्य जीवितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasya pāpakṛto drauṇerna cedadya tvayā mṛdhe ,hriyate sānubandhasya yudhi vikramya jīvitam.
english: If today, having displayed valor in battle, the life of that sinful Drauṇi, along with his kinsmen, is not taken by you in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. If today, having displayed valor in battle, the life of that sinful Drauṇi, along with his kinsmen, is not taken by you in combat.

sanskrit: इहैव प्रायमासिष्ये तन्निबोधत पाण्डवाः ।न चेत्फलमवाप्नोति द्रौणिः पापस्य कर्मणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ihaiva prāyamāsiṣye tannibodhata pāṇḍavāḥ ,na cetphalamavāpnoti drauṇiḥ pāpasya karmaṇaḥ.
english: I will undertake a fast unto death right here. Understand this, O Pāṇḍavas: if Drauṇi does not obtain the consequence of his sinful deed (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. I will undertake a fast unto death right here. Understand this, O Pāṇḍavas: if Drauṇi does not obtain the consequence of his sinful deed (karma).

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा ततः कृष्णा पाण्डवं प्रत्युपाविशत् ।युधिष्ठिरं याज्ञसेनी धर्मराजं यशस्विनी ॥१६॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tataḥ kṛṣṇā pāṇḍavaṁ pratyupāviśat ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ yājñasenī dharmarājaṁ yaśasvinī.
english: Having spoken thus, Kṛṣṇā, the glorious daughter of Yajñasena (Yājñasenī), then sat down facing the Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. Having spoken thus, Kṛṣṇā, the glorious daughter of Yajñasena (Yājñasenī), then sat down facing the Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja).

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वोपविष्टां राजर्षिः पाण्डवो महिषीं प्रियाम् ।प्रत्युवाच स धर्मात्मा द्रौपदीं चारुदर्शनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvopaviṣṭāṁ rājarṣiḥ pāṇḍavo mahiṣīṁ priyām ,pratyuvāca sa dharmātmā draupadīṁ cārudarśanām.
english: The royal sage (rājarṣi) and Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira, whose soul was devoted to righteousness (dharma), having seen his beloved queen Draupadī, who was seated and beautiful to behold, replied to her.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. The royal sage (rājarṣi) and Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira, whose soul was devoted to righteousness (dharma), having seen his beloved queen Draupadī, who was seated and beautiful to behold, replied to her.

sanskrit: धर्म्यं धर्मेण धर्मज्ञे प्राप्तास्ते निधनं शुभे ।पुत्रास्ते भ्रातरश्चैव तान्न शोचितुमर्हसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: dharmyaṁ dharmeṇa dharmajñe prāptāste nidhanaṁ śubhe ,putrāste bhrātaraścaiva tānna śocitumarhasi.
english: O knower of righteousness (dharma), O auspicious one, your sons and brothers indeed met a righteous (dharmya) death according to natural law (dharma). You should not grieve for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. O knower of righteousness (dharma), O auspicious one, your sons and brothers indeed met a righteous (dharmya) death according to natural law (dharma). You should not grieve for them.

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रः स कल्याणि वनं दूरमितो गतः ।तस्य त्वं पातनं संख्ये कथं ज्ञास्यसि शोभने ॥१९॥
transliteration: droṇaputraḥ sa kalyāṇi vanaṁ dūramito gataḥ ,tasya tvaṁ pātanaṁ saṁkhye kathaṁ jñāsyasi śobhane.
english: O blessed one (kalyāṇi), that son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) has gone far from here into the forest. O beautiful one (śobhane), how will you come to know of his demise in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. O blessed one (kalyāṇi), that son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) has gone far from here into the forest. O beautiful one (śobhane), how will you come to know of his demise in battle?

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।द्रोणपुत्रस्य सहजो मणिः शिरसि मे श्रुतः ।निहत्य संख्ये तं पापं पश्येयं मणिमाहृतम् ।राजञ्शिरसि तं कृत्वा जीवेयमिति मे मतिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,droṇaputrasya sahajo maṇiḥ śirasi me śrutaḥ ,nihatya saṁkhye taṁ pāpaṁ paśyeyaṁ maṇimāhṛtam ,rājañśirasi taṁ kṛtvā jīveyamiti me matiḥ.
english: Draupadī replied: "I have heard that the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) has an inherent gem (maṇi) on his head. Having slain that sinner in battle, I wish to see the gem brought here. O King, it is my resolve (mati) that I would live by placing that (gem) on my head."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 20. Draupadī replied: "I have heard that the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) has an inherent gem (maṇi) on his head. Having slain that sinner in battle, I wish to see the gem brought here. O King, it is my resolve (mati) that I would live by placing that (gem) on my head."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा पाण्डवं कृष्णा राजानं चारुदर्शना ।भीमसेनमथाभ्येत्य कुपिता वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā pāṇḍavaṁ kṛṣṇā rājānaṁ cārudarśanā ,bhīmasenamathābhyetya kupitā vākyamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to the king, Pāṇḍu's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), Kṛṣṇā, who possessed beautiful eyes, then approached Bhīmasena and, enraged, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to the king, Pāṇḍu's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), Kṛṣṇā, who possessed beautiful eyes, then approached Bhīmasena and, enraged, spoke these words.

sanskrit: त्रातुमर्हसि मां भीम क्षत्रधर्ममनुस्मरन् ।जहि तं पापकर्माणं शम्बरं मघवानिव ।न हि ते विक्रमे तुल्यः पुमानस्तीह कश्चन ॥२२॥
transliteration: trātumarhasi māṁ bhīma kṣatradharmamanusmaran ,jahi taṁ pāpakarmāṇaṁ śambaraṁ maghavāniva ,na hi te vikrame tulyaḥ pumānastīha kaścana.
english: “O Bhīma, remembering your warrior's code (kṣatradharma), you ought to protect me! Slay that evil-doer, Śambara, just as Maghavan (Indra) did. Indeed, there is no man here equal to your valor.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 22. “O Bhīma, remembering your warrior's code (kṣatradharma), you ought to protect me! Slay that evil-doer, Śambara, just as Maghavan (Indra) did. Indeed, there is no man here equal to your valor.”

sanskrit: श्रुतं तत्सर्वलोकेषु परमव्यसने यथा ।द्वीपोऽभूस्त्वं हि पार्थानां नगरे वारणावते ।हिडिम्बदर्शने चैव तथा त्वमभवो गतिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: śrutaṁ tatsarvalokeṣu paramavyasane yathā ,dvīpo'bhūstvaṁ hi pārthānāṁ nagare vāraṇāvate ,hiḍimbadarśane caiva tathā tvamabhavo gatiḥ.
english: “It is indeed well-known in all worlds how, during that great distress, you became a refuge (dvīpa) for the Pārthas in the city of Vāraṇāvata. And similarly, you became their protector (gati) at the encounter with Hiḍimba.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 23. “It is indeed well-known in all worlds how, during that great distress, you became a refuge (dvīpa) for the Pārthas in the city of Vāraṇāvata. And similarly, you became their protector (gati) at the encounter with Hiḍimba.”

sanskrit: तथा विराटनगरे कीचकेन भृशार्दिताम् ।मामप्युद्धृतवान्कृच्छ्रात्पौलोमीं मघवानिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: tathā virāṭanagare kīcakena bhṛśārditām ,māmapyuddhṛtavānkṛcchrātpaulomīṁ maghavāniva.
english: “Similarly, in the city of Virāṭa, you also rescued me from distress, who was greatly afflicted by Kīcaka, just as Maghavan (Indra) rescued Paulomī.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 24. “Similarly, in the city of Virāṭa, you also rescued me from distress, who was greatly afflicted by Kīcaka, just as Maghavan (Indra) rescued Paulomī.”

sanskrit: यथैतान्यकृथाः पार्थ महाकर्माणि वै पुरा ।तथा द्रौणिममित्रघ्न विनिहत्य सुखी भव ॥२५॥
transliteration: yathaitānyakṛthāḥ pārtha mahākarmāṇi vai purā ,tathā drauṇimamitraghna vinihatya sukhī bhava.
english: Just as you, O Pārtha, performed these great deeds in the past, so too, O slayer of foes, having slain Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), be happy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 25. Just as you, O Pārtha, performed these great deeds in the past, so too, O slayer of foes, having slain Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), be happy.

sanskrit: तस्या बहुविधं दुःखान्निशम्य परिदेवितम् ।नामर्षयत कौन्तेयो भीमसेनो महाबलः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasyā bahuvidhaṁ duḥkhānniśamya paridevitam ,nāmarṣayata kaunteyo bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ.
english: Having heard her varied lamentation arising from suffering, Bhīmasena, the mighty son of Kuntī, did not tolerate it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 26. Having heard her varied lamentation arising from suffering, Bhīmasena, the mighty son of Kuntī, did not tolerate it.

sanskrit: स काञ्चनविचित्राङ्गमारुरोह महारथम् ।आदाय रुचिरं चित्रं समार्गणगुणं धनुः ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa kāñcanavicitrāṅgamāruroha mahāratham ,ādāya ruciraṁ citraṁ samārgaṇaguṇaṁ dhanuḥ.
english: He ascended the great chariot, whose parts were variegated with gold, taking up his beautiful, wondrous bow, equipped with arrows and string.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 27. He ascended the great chariot, whose parts were variegated with gold, taking up his beautiful, wondrous bow, equipped with arrows and string.

sanskrit: नकुलं सारथिं कृत्वा द्रोणपुत्रवधे वृतः ।विस्फार्य सशरं चापं तूर्णमश्वानचोदयत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ sārathiṁ kṛtvā droṇaputravadhe vṛtaḥ ,visphārya saśaraṁ cāpaṁ tūrṇamaśvānacodayat.
english: Having made Nakula his charioteer, and being intent on killing Droṇa's son, he swiftly urged the horses after stretching his arrow-equipped bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 28. Having made Nakula his charioteer, and being intent on killing Droṇa's son, he swiftly urged the horses after stretching his arrow-equipped bow.

sanskrit: ते हयाः पुरुषव्याघ्र चोदिता वातरंहसः ।वेगेन त्वरिता जग्मुर्हरयः शीघ्रगामिनः ॥२९॥
transliteration: te hayāḥ puruṣavyāghra coditā vātaraṁhasaḥ ,vegena tvaritā jagmurharayaḥ śīghragāminaḥ.
english: O tiger among men, those horses, spurred on and swift as the wind, moved quickly, resembling swift-moving lions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 29. O tiger among men, those horses, spurred on and swift as the wind, moved quickly, resembling swift-moving lions.

sanskrit: शिबिरात्स्वाद्गृहीत्वा स रथस्य पदमच्युतः ।द्रोणपुत्ररथस्याशु ययौ मार्गेण वीर्यवान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: śibirātsvādgṛhītvā sa rathasya padamacyutaḥ ,droṇaputrarathasyāśu yayau mārgeṇa vīryavān.
english: From his own camp, that powerful and unwavering (acyuta) one, taking the track of Dronaputra's chariot, swiftly followed its path.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 30. From his own camp, that powerful and unwavering (acyuta) one, taking the track of Dronaputra's chariot, swiftly followed its path.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्प्रयाते दुर्धर्षे यदूनामृषभस्ततः ।अब्रवीत्पुण्डरीकाक्षः कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminprayāte durdharṣe yadūnāmṛṣabhastataḥ ,abravītpuṇḍarīkākṣaḥ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, after that unconquerable one had departed, the chief among the Yadus, the lotus-eyed (puṇḍarīkākṣa) Krishna, spoke to Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, after that unconquerable one had departed, the chief among the Yadus, the lotus-eyed (puṇḍarīkākṣa) Krishna, spoke to Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti.

sanskrit: एष पाण्डव ते भ्राता पुत्रशोकमपारयन् ।जिघांसुर्द्रौणिमाक्रन्दे याति भारत भारतः ॥२॥
transliteration: eṣa pāṇḍava te bhrātā putraśokamapārayan ,jighāṁsurdrauṇimākrande yāti bhārata bhārataḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍava, your brother, unable to bear the grief for his son, is going to the battlefield, intending to kill Drauṇi, O Bhārata, that great descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. O Pāṇḍava, your brother, unable to bear the grief for his son, is going to the battlefield, intending to kill Drauṇi, O Bhārata, that great descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: भीमः प्रियस्ते सर्वेभ्यो भ्रातृभ्यो भरतर्षभ ।तं कृच्छ्रगतमद्य त्वं कस्मान्नाभ्यवपद्यसे ॥३॥
transliteration: bhīmaḥ priyaste sarvebhyo bhrātṛbhyo bharatarṣabha ,taṁ kṛcchragatamadya tvaṁ kasmānnābhyavapadyase.
english: O best among the Bharatas, Bhima is dearer to you than all (your) brothers. Why do you not come to his aid today, now that he has fallen into such distress?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. O best among the Bharatas, Bhima is dearer to you than all (your) brothers. Why do you not come to his aid today, now that he has fallen into such distress?

sanskrit: यत्तदाचष्ट पुत्राय द्रोणः परपुरंजयः ।अस्त्रं ब्रह्मशिरो नाम दहेद्यत्पृथिवीमपि ॥४॥
transliteration: yattadācaṣṭa putrāya droṇaḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ ,astraṁ brahmaśiro nāma dahedyatpṛthivīmapi.
english: Drona, the conqueror of enemy cities, then taught his son about the weapon named Brahmasiras, which has the power to incinerate even the entire earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Drona, the conqueror of enemy cities, then taught his son about the weapon named Brahmasiras, which has the power to incinerate even the entire earth.

sanskrit: तन्महात्मा महाभागः केतुः सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।प्रत्यपादयदाचार्यः प्रीयमाणो धनंजयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tanmahātmā mahābhāgaḥ ketuḥ sarvadhanuṣmatām ,pratyapādayadācāryaḥ prīyamāṇo dhanaṁjayam.
english: That great-souled, illustrious teacher, who was the foremost among all archers, being pleased, imparted that (weapon) to Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. That great-souled, illustrious teacher, who was the foremost among all archers, being pleased, imparted that (weapon) to Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तत्पुत्रोऽस्यैवमेवैनमन्वयाचदमर्षणः ।ततः प्रोवाच पुत्राय नातिहृष्टमना इव ॥६॥
transliteration: tatputro'syaivamevainamanvayācadamarṣaṇaḥ ,tataḥ provāca putrāya nātihṛṣṭamanā iva.
english: Drona's son (Ashvatthama), being impatient, requested that very (weapon) from him in the same manner. Then (Drona) spoke to his son, appearing as if his mind was not greatly pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. Drona's son (Ashvatthama), being impatient, requested that very (weapon) from him in the same manner. Then (Drona) spoke to his son, appearing as if his mind was not greatly pleased.

sanskrit: विदितं चापलं ह्यासीदात्मजस्य महात्मनः ।सर्वधर्मविदाचार्यो नान्विषत्सततं सुतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: viditaṁ cāpalaṁ hyāsīdātmajasya mahātmanaḥ ,sarvadharmavidācāryo nānviṣatsatataṁ sutam.
english: The teacher, who understood all aspects of natural law (dharma), was indeed aware of the impetuous nature of his great-souled son. Therefore, he did not constantly pursue his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. The teacher, who understood all aspects of natural law (dharma), was indeed aware of the impetuous nature of his great-souled son. Therefore, he did not constantly pursue his son.

sanskrit: परमापद्गतेनापि न स्म तात त्वया रणे ।इदमस्त्रं प्रयोक्तव्यं मानुषेषु विशेषतः ॥८॥
transliteration: paramāpadgatenāpi na sma tāta tvayā raṇe ,idamastraṁ prayoktavyaṁ mānuṣeṣu viśeṣataḥ.
english: My dear son, even when you are in extreme peril in battle, this weapon should certainly not be employed by you, especially against human beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. My dear son, even when you are in extreme peril in battle, this weapon should certainly not be employed by you, especially against human beings.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवान्गुरुः पुत्रं द्रोणः पश्चादथोक्तवान् ।न त्वं जातु सतां मार्गे स्थातेति पुरुषर्षभ ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktavānguruḥ putraṁ droṇaḥ paścādathoktavān ,na tvaṁ jātu satāṁ mārge sthāteti puruṣarṣabha.
english: Thus spoke the teacher Droṇa to his son. Afterward, he also declared, "O best among men (puruṣa), you will certainly never remain on the path of the virtuous!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. Thus spoke the teacher Droṇa to his son. Afterward, he also declared, "O best among men (puruṣa), you will certainly never remain on the path of the virtuous!"

sanskrit: स तदाज्ञाय दुष्टात्मा पितुर्वचनमप्रियम् ।निराशः सर्वकल्याणैः शोचन्पर्यपतन्महीम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa tadājñāya duṣṭātmā piturvacanamapriyam ,nirāśaḥ sarvakalyāṇaiḥ śocanparyapatanmahīm.
english: He, whose nature (ātman) was corrupt, understood his father's unpleasant words. Despairing of all good fortune, he lamented and wandered the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. He, whose nature (ātman) was corrupt, understood his father's unpleasant words. Despairing of all good fortune, he lamented and wandered the earth.

sanskrit: ततस्तदा कुरुश्रेष्ठ वनस्थे त्वयि भारत ।अवसद्द्वारकामेत्य वृष्णिभिः परमार्चितः ॥११॥
transliteration: tatastadā kuruśreṣṭha vanasthe tvayi bhārata ,avasaddvārakāmetya vṛṣṇibhiḥ paramārcitaḥ.
english: O best of the Kurus, O Bhārata, at that time, while you were dwelling in the forest, he arrived in Dvārakā and resided there, highly honored by the Vṛṣṇis.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. O best of the Kurus, O Bhārata, at that time, while you were dwelling in the forest, he arrived in Dvārakā and resided there, highly honored by the Vṛṣṇis.

sanskrit: स कदाचित्समुद्रान्ते वसन्द्रारवतीमनु ।एक एकं समागम्य मामुवाच हसन्निव ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa kadācitsamudrānte vasandrāravatīmanu ,eka ekaṁ samāgamya māmuvāca hasanniva.
english: Once, while dwelling near the sea-shore (samudrānta) adjacent to Dvāravatī, he, having met me alone, spoke to me as if smiling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. Once, while dwelling near the sea-shore (samudrānta) adjacent to Dvāravatī, he, having met me alone, spoke to me as if smiling.

sanskrit: यत्तदुग्रं तपः कृष्ण चरन्सत्यपराक्रमः ।अगस्त्याद्भारताचार्यः प्रत्यपद्यत मे पिता ॥१३॥
transliteration: yattadugraṁ tapaḥ kṛṣṇa caransatyaparākramaḥ ,agastyādbhāratācāryaḥ pratyapadyata me pitā.
english: O Krishna, O teacher of the Bhāratas, my father, whose might (parākrama) was truly great, performed severe austerity (tapas) and obtained that (power/knowledge/weapon, implied) from Agastya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. O Krishna, O teacher of the Bhāratas, my father, whose might (parākrama) was truly great, performed severe austerity (tapas) and obtained that (power/knowledge/weapon, implied) from Agastya.

sanskrit: अस्त्रं ब्रह्मशिरो नाम देवगन्धर्वपूजितम् ।तदद्य मयि दाशार्ह यथा पितरि मे तथा ॥१४॥
transliteration: astraṁ brahmaśiro nāma devagandharvapūjitam ,tadadya mayi dāśārha yathā pitari me tathā.
english: O Daśārha, that weapon named Brahmaśiras, revered by gods and gandharvas, is now within me, just as it was with my father.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. O Daśārha, that weapon named Brahmaśiras, revered by gods and gandharvas, is now within me, just as it was with my father.

sanskrit: अस्मत्तस्तदुपादाय दिव्यमस्त्रं यदूत्तम ।ममाप्यस्त्रं प्रयच्छ त्वं चक्रं रिपुहरं रणे ॥१५॥
transliteration: asmattastadupādāya divyamastraṁ yadūttama ,mamāpyastraṁ prayaccha tvaṁ cakraṁ ripuharaṁ raṇe.
english: O best among the Yadus, after taking that divine weapon from me, you should also give me a weapon - the discus that destroys enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. O best among the Yadus, after taking that divine weapon from me, you should also give me a weapon - the discus that destroys enemies in battle.

sanskrit: स राजन्प्रीयमाणेन मयाप्युक्तः कृताञ्जलिः ।याचमानः प्रयत्नेन मत्तोऽस्त्रं भरतर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa rājanprīyamāṇena mayāpyuktaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ ,yācamānaḥ prayatnena matto'straṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O King, O best among the Bhāratas, he (Arjuna), with folded hands, earnestly requested a weapon from me, and I, being pleased, also spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. O King, O best among the Bhāratas, he (Arjuna), with folded hands, earnestly requested a weapon from me, and I, being pleased, also spoke to him.

sanskrit: देवदानवगन्धर्वमनुष्यपतगोरगाः ।न समा मम वीर्यस्य शतांशेनापि पिण्डिताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: devadānavagandharvamanuṣyapatagoragāḥ ,na samā mama vīryasya śatāṁśenāpi piṇḍitāḥ.
english: Gods, demons, Gandharvas, humans, birds, and serpents, even when gathered together, are not equal to a hundredth part of my power.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. Gods, demons, Gandharvas, humans, birds, and serpents, even when gathered together, are not equal to a hundredth part of my power.

sanskrit: इदं धनुरियं शक्तिरिदं चक्रमियं गदा ।यद्यदिच्छसि चेदस्त्रं मत्तस्तत्तद्ददानि ते ॥१८॥
transliteration: idaṁ dhanuriyaṁ śaktiridaṁ cakramiyaṁ gadā ,yadyadicchasi cedastraṁ mattastattaddadāni te.
english: Here is this bow, this spear (śakti), this discus, and this mace. If you desire any (such) weapon from me, I shall give that to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. Here is this bow, this spear (śakti), this discus, and this mace. If you desire any (such) weapon from me, I shall give that to you.

sanskrit: यच्छक्नोषि समुद्यन्तुं प्रयोक्तुमपि वा रणे ।तद्गृहाण विनास्त्रेण यन्मे दातुमभीप्ससि ॥१९॥
transliteration: yacchaknoṣi samudyantuṁ prayoktumapi vā raṇe ,tadgṛhāṇa vināstreṇa yanme dātumabhīpsasi.
english: Whatever you are capable of raising and employing in battle, take that without using any (other) weapon, if you wish to offer it to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. Whatever you are capable of raising and employing in battle, take that without using any (other) weapon, if you wish to offer it to me.

sanskrit: स सुनाभं सहस्रारं वज्रनाभमयस्मयम् ।वव्रे चक्रं महाबाहो स्पर्धमानो मया सह ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa sunābhaṁ sahasrāraṁ vajranābhamayasmayam ,vavre cakraṁ mahābāho spardhamāno mayā saha.
english: O mighty-armed one, competing with me, he chose the discus (chakra) which had a beautiful hub, a thousand spokes, and a hub as hard as a thunderbolt (vajra) and made of iron.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. O mighty-armed one, competing with me, he chose the discus (chakra) which had a beautiful hub, a thousand spokes, and a hub as hard as a thunderbolt (vajra) and made of iron.

sanskrit: गृहाण चक्रमित्युक्तो मया तु तदनन्तरम् ।जग्राहोपेत्य सहसा चक्रं सव्येन पाणिना ।न चैतदशकत्स्थानात्संचालयितुमच्युत ॥२१॥
transliteration: gṛhāṇa cakramityukto mayā tu tadanantaram ,jagrāhopetya sahasā cakraṁ savyena pāṇinā ,na caitadaśakatsthānātsaṁcālayitumacyuta.
english: Then, upon being told by me, 'Take the discus (chakra)', he quickly approached and grasped the discus (chakra) with his left hand. But, O Acyuta, he was unable to move it from its spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. Then, upon being told by me, 'Take the discus (chakra)', he quickly approached and grasped the discus (chakra) with his left hand. But, O Acyuta, he was unable to move it from its spot.

sanskrit: अथ तद्दक्षिणेनापि ग्रहीतुमुपचक्रमे ।सर्वयत्नेन तेनापि गृह्णन्नेतदकल्पयत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: atha taddakṣiṇenāpi grahītumupacakrame ,sarvayatnena tenāpi gṛhṇannetadakalpayat.
english: Then he also began to grasp that (discus) with his right hand. Even with all his effort, while grasping it, he succeeded in this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. Then he also began to grasp that (discus) with his right hand. Even with all his effort, while grasping it, he succeeded in this.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वबलेनापि यच्चैतन्न शशाक सः ।उद्धर्तुं वा चालयितुं द्रौणिः परमदुर्मनाः ।कृत्वा यत्नं परं श्रान्तः स न्यवर्तत भारत ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarvabalenāpi yaccaitanna śaśāka saḥ ,uddhartuṁ vā cālayituṁ drauṇiḥ paramadurmanāḥ ,kṛtvā yatnaṁ paraṁ śrāntaḥ sa nyavartata bhārata.
english: Then, even with all his might, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), greatly disheartened, was unable to lift or move it. Having made a supreme effort and become exhausted, he turned back, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. Then, even with all his might, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), greatly disheartened, was unable to lift or move it. Having made a supreme effort and become exhausted, he turned back, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: निवृत्तमथ तं तस्मादभिप्रायाद्विचेतसम् ।अहमामन्त्र्य सुस्निग्धमश्वत्थामानमब्रुवम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: nivṛttamatha taṁ tasmādabhiprāyādvicetasam ,ahamāmantrya susnigdhamaśvatthāmānamabruvam.
english: Then, I addressed Aśvatthāman, who was bewildered and had returned from that intention, very affectionately, and spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 24. Then, I addressed Aśvatthāman, who was bewildered and had returned from that intention, very affectionately, and spoke to him.

sanskrit: यः स देवमनुष्येषु प्रमाणं परमं गतः ।गाण्डीवधन्वा श्वेताश्वः कपिप्रवरकेतनः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yaḥ sa devamanuṣyeṣu pramāṇaṁ paramaṁ gataḥ ,gāṇḍīvadhanvā śvetāśvaḥ kapipravaraketanaḥ.
english: He who among gods and men has reached the highest standard (of excellence), who bears the Gāṇḍīva bow, has white horses, and whose banner is marked by the chief of monkeys.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 25. He who among gods and men has reached the highest standard (of excellence), who bears the Gāṇḍīva bow, has white horses, and whose banner is marked by the chief of monkeys.

sanskrit: यः साक्षाद्देवदेवेशं शितिकण्ठमुमापतिम् ।द्वंद्वयुद्धे पराजिष्णुस्तोषयामास शंकरम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yaḥ sākṣāddevadeveśaṁ śitikaṇṭhamumāpatim ,dvaṁdvayuddhe parājiṣṇustoṣayāmāsa śaṁkaram.
english: He who directly pleased Śaṅkara, the Lord of lords among gods, the blue-throated (Śitikaṇṭha), the husband of Umā, by being victorious in a duel.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 26. He who directly pleased Śaṅkara, the Lord of lords among gods, the blue-throated (Śitikaṇṭha), the husband of Umā, by being victorious in a duel.

sanskrit: यस्मात्प्रियतरो नास्ति ममान्यः पुरुषो भुवि ।नादेयं यस्य मे किंचिदपि दाराः सुतास्तथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: yasmātpriyataro nāsti mamānyaḥ puruṣo bhuvi ,nādeyaṁ yasya me kiṁcidapi dārāḥ sutāstathā.
english: There is no other person (puruṣa) on this earth dearer to me than him. For his sake, there is nothing I would not give, not even my wife and children.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 27. There is no other person (puruṣa) on this earth dearer to me than him. For his sake, there is nothing I would not give, not even my wife and children.

sanskrit: तेनापि सुहृदा ब्रह्मन्पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।नोक्तपूर्वमिदं वाक्यं यत्त्वं मामभिभाषसे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tenāpi suhṛdā brahmanpārthenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,noktapūrvamidaṁ vākyaṁ yattvaṁ māmabhibhāṣase.
english: O Brahmin, even by that friend Pārtha, whose deeds (karma) are unblemished, this statement that you are now speaking to me has never been uttered before.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 28. O Brahmin, even by that friend Pārtha, whose deeds (karma) are unblemished, this statement that you are now speaking to me has never been uttered before.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मचर्यं महद्घोरं चीर्त्वा द्वादशवार्षिकम् ।हिमवत्पार्श्वमभ्येत्य यो मया तपसार्चितः ॥२९॥
transliteration: brahmacaryaṁ mahadghoraṁ cīrtvā dvādaśavārṣikam ,himavatpārśvamabhyetya yo mayā tapasārcitaḥ.
english: Having completed a great and severe twelve-year period of student life (brahmacarya), and having approached the slopes of the Himavat (Himalayas), he whom I worshipped with austerity (tapas)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 29. Having completed a great and severe twelve-year period of student life (brahmacarya), and having approached the slopes of the Himavat (Himalayas), he whom I worshipped with austerity (tapas)...

sanskrit: समानव्रतचारिण्यां रुक्मिण्यां योऽन्वजायत ।सनत्कुमारस्तेजस्वी प्रद्युम्नो नाम मे सुतः ॥३०॥
transliteration: samānavratacāriṇyāṁ rukmiṇyāṁ yo'nvajāyata ,sanatkumārastejasvī pradyumno nāma me sutaḥ.
english: He who was born from Rukmiṇī, my wife who observes similar vows (vrata), is my brilliant and resplendent son (suta) named Pradyumna, (who is) Sanatkumāra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 30. He who was born from Rukmiṇī, my wife who observes similar vows (vrata), is my brilliant and resplendent son (suta) named Pradyumna, (who is) Sanatkumāra.

sanskrit: तेनाप्येतन्महद्दिव्यं चक्रमप्रतिमं मम ।न प्रार्थितमभून्मूढ यदिदं प्रार्थितं त्वया ॥३१॥
transliteration: tenāpyetanmahaddivyaṁ cakramapratimaṁ mama ,na prārthitamabhūnmūḍha yadidaṁ prārthitaṁ tvayā.
english: O fool, even this great, divine, and unrivaled discus (cakra) of mine has never been requested by anyone before, yet this is what you have sought.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 31. O fool, even this great, divine, and unrivaled discus (cakra) of mine has never been requested by anyone before, yet this is what you have sought.

sanskrit: रामेणातिबलेनैतन्नोक्तपूर्वं कदाचन ।न गदेन न साम्बेन यदिदं प्रार्थितं त्वया ॥३२॥
transliteration: rāmeṇātibalenaitannoktapūrvaṁ kadācana ,na gadena na sāmbena yadidaṁ prārthitaṁ tvayā.
english: Even by the supremely powerful Rāma, this has never been requested before; nor by Gada, nor by Sāmba; yet this is what you have sought.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 32. Even by the supremely powerful Rāma, this has never been requested before; nor by Gada, nor by Sāmba; yet this is what you have sought.

sanskrit: द्वारकावासिभिश्चान्यैर्वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथैः ।नोक्तपूर्वमिदं जातु यदिदं प्रार्थितं त्वया ॥३३॥
transliteration: dvārakāvāsibhiścānyairvṛṣṇyandhakamahārathaiḥ ,noktapūrvamidaṁ jātu yadidaṁ prārthitaṁ tvayā.
english: Nor by the residents of Dvārakā, nor by other great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, has this ever been requested, yet this is what you have sought.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 33. Nor by the residents of Dvārakā, nor by other great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, has this ever been requested, yet this is what you have sought.

sanskrit: भारताचार्यपुत्रः सन्मानितः सर्वयादवैः ।चक्रेण रथिनां श्रेष्ठ किं नु तात युयुत्ससे ॥३४॥
transliteration: bhāratācāryaputraḥ sanmānitaḥ sarvayādavaiḥ ,cakreṇa rathināṁ śreṣṭha kiṁ nu tāta yuyutsase.
english: You are the son of the Bhārata teacher (Droṇa), honored by all the Yadavas. O best among charioteers, dear one, why indeed do you wish to fight (with my discus)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 34. You are the son of the Bhārata teacher (Droṇa), honored by all the Yadavas. O best among charioteers, dear one, why indeed do you wish to fight (with my discus)?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तो मया द्रौणिर्मामिदं प्रत्युवाच ह ।प्रयुज्य भवते पूजां योत्स्ये कृष्ण त्वयेत्युत ॥३५॥
transliteration: evamukto mayā drauṇirmāmidaṁ pratyuvāca ha ,prayujya bhavate pūjāṁ yotsye kṛṣṇa tvayetyuta.
english: When I addressed him thus, Drauni replied to me, 'Having first offered you worship, Krishna, I will indeed fight with you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 35. When I addressed him thus, Drauni replied to me, 'Having first offered you worship, Krishna, I will indeed fight with you.'

sanskrit: ततस्ते प्रार्थितं चक्रं देवदानवपूजितम् ।अजेयः स्यामिति विभो सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataste prārthitaṁ cakraṁ devadānavapūjitam ,ajeyaḥ syāmiti vibho satyametadbravīmi te.
english: Then, regarding the discus, worshipped by gods and demons, which was prayed for by him (Drauni), he said to me, 'O Lord (Krishna), may I be unconquerable!' This truth I (Krishna) declare to you (Yudhisthira).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 36. Then, regarding the discus, worshipped by gods and demons, which was prayed for by him (Drauni), he said to me, 'O Lord (Krishna), may I be unconquerable!' This truth I (Krishna) declare to you (Yudhisthira).

sanskrit: त्वत्तोऽहं दुर्लभं काममनवाप्यैव केशव ।प्रतियास्यामि गोविन्द शिवेनाभिवदस्व माम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: tvatto'haṁ durlabhaṁ kāmamanavāpyaiva keśava ,pratiyāsyāmi govinda śivenābhivadasva mām.
english: O Keśava, since I have not obtained this difficult desire from you, I will return, O Govinda. Bid me farewell with blessings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 37. O Keśava, since I have not obtained this difficult desire from you, I will return, O Govinda. Bid me farewell with blessings.

sanskrit: एतत्सुनाभं वृष्णीनामृषभेण त्वया धृतम् ।चक्रमप्रतिचक्रेण भुवि नान्योऽभिपद्यते ॥३८॥
transliteration: etatsunābhaṁ vṛṣṇīnāmṛṣabheṇa tvayā dhṛtam ,cakramapraticakreṇa bhuvi nānyo'bhipadyate.
english: This discus (Sudarśana), which has an excellent hub, is held by you, the foremost of the Vṛṣṇis, who possesses an unparalleled discus. No one else on earth can obtain it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 38. This discus (Sudarśana), which has an excellent hub, is held by you, the foremost of the Vṛṣṇis, who possesses an unparalleled discus. No one else on earth can obtain it.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा द्रौणिर्मां युग्यमश्वान्धनानि च ।आदायोपययौ बालो रत्नानि विविधानि च ॥३९॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā drauṇirmāṁ yugyamaśvāndhanāni ca ,ādāyopayayau bālo ratnāni vividhāni ca.
english: Having said this much, Droṇi, acting rashly like a youth, took me, along with the chariots, horses, wealth, and various jewels, and departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 39. Having said this much, Droṇi, acting rashly like a youth, took me, along with the chariots, horses, wealth, and various jewels, and departed.

sanskrit: स संरम्भी दुरात्मा च चपलः क्रूर एव च ।वेद चास्त्रं ब्रह्मशिरस्तस्माद्रक्ष्यो वृकोदरः ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa saṁrambhī durātmā ca capalaḥ krūra eva ca ,veda cāstraṁ brahmaśirastasmādrakṣyo vṛkodaraḥ.
english: He is impetuous, evil-minded, fickle, and indeed cruel. Moreover, he knows the "brahmaśiras" weapon. Therefore, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) must be protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 40. He is impetuous, evil-minded, fickle, and indeed cruel. Moreover, he knows the "brahmaśiras" weapon. Therefore, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) must be protected.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा युधां श्रेष्ठः सर्वयादवनन्दनः ।सर्वायुधवरोपेतमारुरोह महारथम् ।युक्तं परमकाम्बोजैस्तुरगैर्हेममालिभिः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ sarvayādavanandanaḥ ,sarvāyudhavaropetamāruroha mahāratham ,yuktaṁ paramakāmbojaisturagairhemamālibhiḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the foremost among warriors, the joy of all the Yadus (Kṛṣṇa), ascended his great chariot. This chariot was equipped with all excellent weapons and yoked with supreme Kāmboja horses, adorned with golden garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the foremost among warriors, the joy of all the Yadus (Kṛṣṇa), ascended his great chariot. This chariot was equipped with all excellent weapons and yoked with supreme Kāmboja horses, adorned with golden garlands.

sanskrit: आदित्योदयवर्णस्य धुरं रथवरस्य तु ।दक्षिणामवहत्सैन्यः सुग्रीवः सव्यतोऽवहत् ।पार्ष्णिवाहौ तु तस्यास्तां मेघपुष्पबलाहकौ ॥२॥
transliteration: ādityodayavarṇasya dhuraṁ rathavarasya tu ,dakṣiṇāmavahatsainyaḥ sugrīvaḥ savyato'vahat ,pārṣṇivāhau tu tasyāstāṁ meghapuṣpabalāhakau.
english: Sainya pulled the right side of the yoke of that excellent chariot, which shone with the color of the rising sun, while Sugrīva pulled from the left. Indeed, Meghapuṣpa and Balāhaka were the two rear horses of that chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. Sainya pulled the right side of the yoke of that excellent chariot, which shone with the color of the rising sun, while Sugrīva pulled from the left. Indeed, Meghapuṣpa and Balāhaka were the two rear horses of that chariot.

sanskrit: विश्वकर्मकृता दिव्या नानारत्नविभूषिता ।उच्छ्रितेव रथे माया ध्वजयष्टिरदृश्यत ॥३॥
transliteration: viśvakarmakṛtā divyā nānāratnavibhūṣitā ,ucchriteva rathe māyā dhvajayaṣṭiradṛśyata.
english: A divine flagpole, crafted by Viśvakarman and adorned with various gems, was seen on the chariot, towering high like a miraculous (māyā) manifestation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. A divine flagpole, crafted by Viśvakarman and adorned with various gems, was seen on the chariot, towering high like a miraculous (māyā) manifestation.

sanskrit: वैनतेयः स्थितस्तस्यां प्रभामण्डलरश्मिवान् ।तस्य सत्यवतः केतुर्भुजगारिरदृश्यत ॥४॥
transliteration: vainateyaḥ sthitastasyāṁ prabhāmaṇḍalaraśmivān ,tasya satyavataḥ keturbhujagāriradṛśyata.
english: Garuda, son of Vinatā, appeared on that flagpole, radiating with the splendor of his aura. This enemy of serpents (Garuda) was seen as the emblem of the truthful Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. Garuda, son of Vinatā, appeared on that flagpole, radiating with the splendor of his aura. This enemy of serpents (Garuda) was seen as the emblem of the truthful Arjuna.

sanskrit: अन्वारोहद्धृषीकेशः केतुः सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।अर्जुनः सत्यकर्मा च कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥५॥
transliteration: anvārohaddhṛṣīkeśaḥ ketuḥ sarvadhanuṣmatām ,arjunaḥ satyakarmā ca kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) ascended (the chariot). Arjuna, the foremost among all archers and whose actions are righteous, and Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of Kurus, [were also present].
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) ascended (the chariot). Arjuna, the foremost among all archers and whose actions are righteous, and Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of Kurus, [were also present].

sanskrit: अशोभेतां महात्मानौ दाशार्हमभितः स्थितौ ।रथस्थं शार्ङ्गधन्वानमश्विनाविव वासवम् ॥६॥
transliteration: aśobhetāṁ mahātmānau dāśārhamabhitaḥ sthitau ,rathasthaṁ śārṅgadhanvānamaśvināviva vāsavam.
english: The two great souls (Arjuna and Yudhiṣṭhira), standing on either side of Daśārha (Krishna), who was situated on the chariot holding the Śārṅga bow, shone splendidly like the two Aśvins surrounding Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. The two great souls (Arjuna and Yudhiṣṭhira), standing on either side of Daśārha (Krishna), who was situated on the chariot holding the Śārṅga bow, shone splendidly like the two Aśvins surrounding Indra.

sanskrit: तावुपारोप्य दाशार्हः स्यन्दनं लोकपूजितम् ।प्रतोदेन जवोपेतान्परमाश्वानचोदयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tāvupāropya dāśārhaḥ syandanaṁ lokapūjitam ,pratodena javopetānparamāśvānacodayat.
english: The descendant of Daśārha (Krishna), having seated those two upon the world-honored chariot, urged on the exceedingly swift horses with a goad.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. The descendant of Daśārha (Krishna), having seated those two upon the world-honored chariot, urged on the exceedingly swift horses with a goad.

sanskrit: ते हयाः सहसोत्पेतुर्गृहीत्वा स्यन्दनोत्तमम् ।आस्थितं पाण्डवेयाभ्यां यदूनामृषभेण च ॥८॥
transliteration: te hayāḥ sahasotpeturgṛhītvā syandanottamam ,āsthitaṁ pāṇḍaveyābhyāṁ yadūnāmṛṣabheṇa ca.
english: Those horses suddenly sprang forth, carrying the excellent chariot which was occupied by the two sons of Pāṇḍu and the bull (best) of the Yadus (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. Those horses suddenly sprang forth, carrying the excellent chariot which was occupied by the two sons of Pāṇḍu and the bull (best) of the Yadus (Krishna).

sanskrit: वहतां शार्ङ्गधन्वानमश्वानां शीघ्रगामिनाम् ।प्रादुरासीन्महाञ्शब्दः पक्षिणां पततामिव ॥९॥
transliteration: vahatāṁ śārṅgadhanvānamaśvānāṁ śīghragāminām ,prādurāsīnmahāñśabdaḥ pakṣiṇāṁ patatāmiva.
english: A great sound arose, like that of flying birds, from the swiftly moving horses that were carrying the wielder of the Śārṅga bow (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. A great sound arose, like that of flying birds, from the swiftly moving horses that were carrying the wielder of the Śārṅga bow (Krishna).

sanskrit: ते समार्छन्नरव्याघ्राः क्षणेन भरतर्षभ ।भीमसेनं महेष्वासं समनुद्रुत्य वेगिताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: te samārchannaravyāghrāḥ kṣaṇena bharatarṣabha ,bhīmasenaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ samanudrutya vegitāḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, those swift heroes, tiger-like among men, having sped towards Bhimasena, the great archer, reached him in an instant.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. O best among the Bharatas, those swift heroes, tiger-like among men, having sped towards Bhimasena, the great archer, reached him in an instant.

sanskrit: क्रोधदीप्तं तु कौन्तेयं द्विषदर्थे समुद्यतम् ।नाशक्नुवन्वारयितुं समेत्यापि महारथाः ॥११॥
transliteration: krodhadīptaṁ tu kaunteyaṁ dviṣadarthe samudyatam ,nāśaknuvanvārayituṁ sametyāpi mahārathāḥ.
english: Even after gathering, the great charioteers were unable to stop Arjuna, Kunti's son, who was ablaze with wrath and prepared to strike against his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Even after gathering, the great charioteers were unable to stop Arjuna, Kunti's son, who was ablaze with wrath and prepared to strike against his enemies.

sanskrit: स तेषां प्रेक्षतामेव श्रीमतां दृढधन्विनाम् ।ययौ भागिरथीकच्छं हरिभिर्भृशवेगितैः ।यत्र स्म श्रूयते द्रौणिः पुत्रहन्ता महात्मनाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa teṣāṁ prekṣatāmeva śrīmatāṁ dṛḍhadhanvinām ,yayau bhāgirathīkacchaṁ haribhirbhṛśavegitaiḥ ,yatra sma śrūyate drauṇiḥ putrahantā mahātmanām.
english: While those glorious, strong-bowed warriors merely watched, he (Arjuna) went to the banks of the Ganges, carried by exceedingly swift horses. It was there that Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), the killer of the great-souled ones' (Pāṇḍavas') sons, was said to be.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. While those glorious, strong-bowed warriors merely watched, he (Arjuna) went to the banks of the Ganges, carried by exceedingly swift horses. It was there that Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), the killer of the great-souled ones' (Pāṇḍavas') sons, was said to be.

sanskrit: स ददर्श महात्मानमुदकान्ते यशस्विनम् ।कृष्णद्वैपायनं व्यासमासीनमृषिभिः सह ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa dadarśa mahātmānamudakānte yaśasvinam ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaṁ vyāsamāsīnamṛṣibhiḥ saha.
english: He (Arjuna) saw the illustrious Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana Vyāsa, that great soul (ātman), seated at the water's edge along with other sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. He (Arjuna) saw the illustrious Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana Vyāsa, that great soul (ātman), seated at the water's edge along with other sages.

sanskrit: तं चैव क्रूरकर्माणं घृताक्तं कुशचीरिणम् ।रजसा ध्वस्तकेशान्तं ददर्श द्रौणिमन्तिके ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ caiva krūrakarmāṇaṁ ghṛtāktaṁ kuśacīriṇam ,rajasā dhvastakeśāntaṁ dadarśa drauṇimantike.
english: And there, nearby, he (Arjuna) saw Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), who had performed cruel deeds, smeared with ghee (ghṛta), wearing garments of kuśa grass, with the ends of his hair disheveled by dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. And there, nearby, he (Arjuna) saw Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), who had performed cruel deeds, smeared with ghee (ghṛta), wearing garments of kuśa grass, with the ends of his hair disheveled by dust.

sanskrit: तमभ्यधावत्कौन्तेयः प्रगृह्य सशरं धनुः ।भीमसेनो महाबाहुस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamabhyadhāvatkaunteyaḥ pragṛhya saśaraṁ dhanuḥ ,bhīmaseno mahābāhustiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: The son of Kunti (Arjuna), having grasped his bow with an arrow, ran towards him. And the mighty-armed Bhimasena exclaimed, "Stop! Stop!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. The son of Kunti (Arjuna), having grasped his bow with an arrow, ran towards him. And the mighty-armed Bhimasena exclaimed, "Stop! Stop!"

sanskrit: स दृष्ट्वा भीमधन्वानं प्रगृहीतशरासनम् ।भ्रातरौ पृष्ठतश्चास्य जनार्दनरथे स्थितौ ।व्यथितात्माभवद्द्रौणिः प्राप्तं चेदममन्यत ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa dṛṣṭvā bhīmadhanvānaṁ pragṛhītaśarāsanam ,bhrātarau pṛṣṭhataścāsya janārdanarathe sthitau ,vyathitātmābhavaddrauṇiḥ prāptaṁ cedamamanyata.
english: Drauni, seeing Arjuna, who held a formidable bow and had grasped his weapon, and seeing the two brothers (Arjuna and Bhima) standing behind him on Janardana's (Krishna's) chariot, became distressed in spirit (ātman). He believed that his doom had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Drauni, seeing Arjuna, who held a formidable bow and had grasped his weapon, and seeing the two brothers (Arjuna and Bhima) standing behind him on Janardana's (Krishna's) chariot, became distressed in spirit (ātman). He believed that his doom had arrived.

sanskrit: स तद्दिव्यमदीनात्मा परमास्त्रमचिन्तयत् ।जग्राह च स चैषीकां द्रौणिः सव्येन पाणिना ।स तामापदमासाद्य दिव्यमस्त्रमुदीरयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa taddivyamadīnātmā paramāstramacintayat ,jagrāha ca sa caiṣīkāṁ drauṇiḥ savyena pāṇinā ,sa tāmāpadamāsādya divyamastramudīrayat.
english: Undaunted in spirit (ātman), Drauni contemplated that divine, supreme weapon (astra). He then, with his left hand, picked up a blade of grass. Having thus encountered that dire calamity, he invoked the divine weapon (astra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. Undaunted in spirit (ātman), Drauni contemplated that divine, supreme weapon (astra). He then, with his left hand, picked up a blade of grass. Having thus encountered that dire calamity, he invoked the divine weapon (astra).

sanskrit: अमृष्यमाणस्ताञ्शूरान्दिव्यायुधधरान्स्थितान् ।अपाण्डवायेति रुषा व्यसृजद्दारुणं वचः ॥१८॥
transliteration: amṛṣyamāṇastāñśūrāndivyāyudhadharānsthitān ,apāṇḍavāyeti ruṣā vyasṛjaddāruṇaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Unable to bear the sight of those heroes, standing there wielding divine weapons, he furiously uttered the terrible words, "For the extermination of the Pāṇḍavas!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. Unable to bear the sight of those heroes, standing there wielding divine weapons, he furiously uttered the terrible words, "For the extermination of the Pāṇḍavas!"

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा राजशार्दूल द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।सर्वलोकप्रमोहार्थं तदस्त्रं प्रमुमोच ह ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityuktvā rājaśārdūla droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,sarvalokapramohārthaṁ tadastraṁ pramumoca ha.
english: Having spoken thus, O tiger among kings, the powerful son of Droṇa released that weapon to delude all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. Having spoken thus, O tiger among kings, the powerful son of Droṇa released that weapon to delude all people.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्यामिषीकायां पावकः समजायत ।प्रधक्ष्यन्निव लोकांस्त्रीन्कालान्तकयमोपमः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tatastasyāmiṣīkāyāṁ pāvakaḥ samajāyata ,pradhakṣyanniva lokāṁstrīnkālāntakayamopamaḥ.
english: Then, from that blade of grass, fire emerged, similar to Yama, the Destroyer at the end of time, as if it would consume the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. Then, from that blade of grass, fire emerged, similar to Yama, the Destroyer at the end of time, as if it would consume the three worlds.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इङ्गितेनैव दाशार्हस्तमभिप्रायमादितः ।द्रौणेर्बुद्ध्वा महाबाहुरर्जुनं प्रत्यभाषत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,iṅgitenaiva dāśārhastamabhiprāyamāditaḥ ,drauṇerbuddhvā mahābāhurarjunaṁ pratyabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The mighty-armed (Krishna), a descendant of Daśārha, having understood Droṇa's son's intention from the very beginning merely by a hint, then spoke to Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: The mighty-armed (Krishna), a descendant of Daśārha, having understood Droṇa's son's intention from the very beginning merely by a hint, then spoke to Arjuna.

sanskrit: अर्जुनार्जुन यद्दिव्यमस्त्रं ते हृदि वर्तते ।द्रोणोपदिष्टं तस्यायं कालः संप्रति पाण्डव ॥२॥
transliteration: arjunārjuna yaddivyamastraṁ te hṛdi vartate ,droṇopadiṣṭaṁ tasyāyaṁ kālaḥ saṁprati pāṇḍava.
english: Arjuna, Arjuna! The divine weapon residing in your heart, which was taught by Droṇa – this is its moment now, O Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. Arjuna, Arjuna! The divine weapon residing in your heart, which was taught by Droṇa – this is its moment now, O Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: भ्रातॄणामात्मनश्चैव परित्राणाय भारत ।विसृजैतत्त्वमप्याजावस्त्रमस्त्रनिवारणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: bhrātṝṇāmātmanaścaiva paritrāṇāya bhārata ,visṛjaitattvamapyājāvastramastranivāraṇam.
english: O Bhārata (Arjuna), for the protection of your brothers and yourself (ātman), you too should release this weapon, which repels other weapons, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. O Bhārata (Arjuna), for the protection of your brothers and yourself (ātman), you too should release this weapon, which repels other weapons, in battle.

sanskrit: केशवेनैवमुक्तस्तु पाण्डवः परवीरहा ।अवातरद्रथात्तूर्णं प्रगृह्य सशरं धनुः ॥४॥
transliteration: keśavenaivamuktastu pāṇḍavaḥ paravīrahā ,avātaradrathāttūrṇaṁ pragṛhya saśaraṁ dhanuḥ.
english: But the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), slayer of enemy heroes, thus addressed by Keśava (Krishna), quickly descended from the chariot, grasping his bow with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. But the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), slayer of enemy heroes, thus addressed by Keśava (Krishna), quickly descended from the chariot, grasping his bow with arrows.

sanskrit: पूर्वमाचार्यपुत्राय ततोऽनन्तरमात्मने ।भ्रातृभ्यश्चैव सर्वेभ्यः स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा परंतपः ॥५॥
transliteration: pūrvamācāryaputrāya tato'nantaramātmane ,bhrātṛbhyaścaiva sarvebhyaḥ svastītyuktvā paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Having first said "blessings be" (svasti) to the preceptor's son, then immediately afterwards to himself (ātman), and also to all his brothers, the tormentor of foes (Arjuna) then...
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. Having first said "blessings be" (svasti) to the preceptor's son, then immediately afterwards to himself (ātman), and also to all his brothers, the tormentor of foes (Arjuna) then...

sanskrit: देवताभ्यो नमस्कृत्य गुरुभ्यश्चैव सर्वशः ।उत्ससर्ज शिवं ध्यायन्नस्त्रमस्त्रेण शाम्यताम् ॥६॥
transliteration: devatābhyo namaskṛtya gurubhyaścaiva sarvaśaḥ ,utsasarja śivaṁ dhyāyannastramastreṇa śāmyatām.
english: Having bowed to the deities and indeed to all the preceptors (guru) in every way, he (Arjuna) released the weapon, meditating (dhyāna) on Śiva, (thinking) "May this weapon be appeased by (my) weapon."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. Having bowed to the deities and indeed to all the preceptors (guru) in every way, he (Arjuna) released the weapon, meditating (dhyāna) on Śiva, (thinking) "May this weapon be appeased by (my) weapon."

sanskrit: ततस्तदस्त्रं सहसा सृष्टं गाण्डीवधन्वना ।प्रजज्वाल महार्चिष्मद्युगान्तानलसंनिभम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tatastadastraṁ sahasā sṛṣṭaṁ gāṇḍīvadhanvanā ,prajajvāla mahārciṣmadyugāntānalasaṁnibham.
english: Then that weapon, suddenly released by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, blazed with immense flames, resembling the cosmic fire at the dissolution of an age.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. Then that weapon, suddenly released by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, blazed with immense flames, resembling the cosmic fire at the dissolution of an age.

sanskrit: तथैव द्रोणपुत्रस्य तदस्त्रं तिग्मतेजसः ।प्रजज्वाल महाज्वालं तेजोमण्डलसंवृतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tathaiva droṇaputrasya tadastraṁ tigmatejasaḥ ,prajajvāla mahājvālaṁ tejomaṇḍalasaṁvṛtam.
english: And similarly, that weapon of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), possessing fierce radiance, blazed with enormous flames, enveloped by a circle of light.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. And similarly, that weapon of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), possessing fierce radiance, blazed with enormous flames, enveloped by a circle of light.

sanskrit: निर्घाता बहवश्चासन्पेतुरुल्काः सहस्रशः ।महद्भयं च भूतानां सर्वेषां समजायत ॥९॥
transliteration: nirghātā bahavaścāsanpeturulkāḥ sahasraśaḥ ,mahadbhayaṁ ca bhūtānāṁ sarveṣāṁ samajāyata.
english: Many thunderbolts occurred, and thousands of meteors fell. A great fear also arose among all creatures (bhūta).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. Many thunderbolts occurred, and thousands of meteors fell. A great fear also arose among all creatures (bhūta).

sanskrit: सशब्दमभवद्व्योम ज्वालामालाकुलं भृशम् ।चचाल च मही कृत्स्ना सपर्वतवनद्रुमा ॥१०॥
transliteration: saśabdamabhavadvyoma jvālāmālākulaṁ bhṛśam ,cacāla ca mahī kṛtsnā saparvatavanadrumā.
english: The sky became filled with sound and intensely agitated by garlands of flames. And the entire earth (mahī), along with its mountains, forests, and trees, trembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. The sky became filled with sound and intensely agitated by garlands of flames. And the entire earth (mahī), along with its mountains, forests, and trees, trembled.

sanskrit: ते अस्त्रे तेजसा लोकांस्तापयन्ती व्यवस्थिते ।महर्षी सहितौ तत्र दर्शयामासतुस्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: te astre tejasā lokāṁstāpayantī vyavasthite ,maharṣī sahitau tatra darśayāmāsatustadā.
english: As those two weapons, deployed and scorching the worlds with their fierce power, the two great sages appeared there together at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. As those two weapons, deployed and scorching the worlds with their fierce power, the two great sages appeared there together at that time.

sanskrit: नारदः स च धर्मात्मा भरतानां पितामहः ।उभौ शमयितुं वीरौ भारद्वाजधनंजयौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: nāradaḥ sa ca dharmātmā bharatānāṁ pitāmahaḥ ,ubhau śamayituṁ vīrau bhāradvājadhanaṁjayau.
english: And that righteous (dharma) Nārada, along with the venerable patriarch (pitāmaha) of the Bhāratas, appeared to pacify both heroes, Bharadvāja (Droṇa) and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. And that righteous (dharma) Nārada, along with the venerable patriarch (pitāmaha) of the Bhāratas, appeared to pacify both heroes, Bharadvāja (Droṇa) and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तौ मुनी सर्वधर्मज्ञौ सर्वभूतहितैषिणौ ।दीप्तयोरस्त्रयोर्मध्ये स्थितौ परमतेजसौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: tau munī sarvadharmajñau sarvabhūtahitaiṣiṇau ,dīptayorastrayormadhye sthitau paramatejasau.
english: Those two sages, who knew all aspects of natural law (dharma) and desired the welfare of all beings, stood with supreme spiritual power amidst the two blazing weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. Those two sages, who knew all aspects of natural law (dharma) and desired the welfare of all beings, stood with supreme spiritual power amidst the two blazing weapons.

sanskrit: तदन्तरमनाधृष्यावुपगम्य यशस्विनौ ।आस्तामृषिवरौ तत्र ज्वलिताविव पावकौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: tadantaramanādhṛṣyāvupagamya yaśasvinau ,āstāmṛṣivarau tatra jvalitāviva pāvakau.
english: Then, having approached, those two unassailable and glorious foremost sages (ṛṣivara) were present there, appearing like two blazing fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. Then, having approached, those two unassailable and glorious foremost sages (ṛṣivara) were present there, appearing like two blazing fires.

sanskrit: प्राणभृद्भिरनाधृष्यौ देवदानवसंमतौ ।अस्त्रतेजः शमयितुं लोकानां हितकाम्यया ॥१५॥
transliteration: prāṇabhṛdbhiranādhṛṣyau devadānavasaṁmatau ,astratejaḥ śamayituṁ lokānāṁ hitakāmyayā.
english: Unassailable by any living beings, and revered by both gods and demons, they [came] to pacify the power of the weapon, out of a desire for the welfare of the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. Unassailable by any living beings, and revered by both gods and demons, they [came] to pacify the power of the weapon, out of a desire for the welfare of the worlds.

sanskrit: ऋषी ऊचतुः ।नानाशस्त्रविदः पूर्वे येऽप्यतीता महारथाः ।नैतदस्त्रं मनुष्येषु तैः प्रयुक्तं कथंचन ॥१६॥
transliteration: ṛṣī ūcatuḥ ,nānāśastravidaḥ pūrve ye'pyatītā mahārathāḥ ,naitadastraṁ manuṣyeṣu taiḥ prayuktaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: The two sages said: "This weapon was never used among humans by any of the great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) of the past, even those who were expert in various weapons."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. The two sages said: "This weapon was never used among humans by any of the great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) of the past, even those who were expert in various weapons."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।दृष्ट्वैव नरशार्दूलस्तावग्निसमतेजसौ ।संजहार शरं दिव्यं त्वरमाणो धनंजयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dṛṣṭvaiva naraśārdūlastāvagnisamatejasau ,saṁjahāra śaraṁ divyaṁ tvaramāṇo dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Upon seeing those two tiger-like men, whose radiance (tejas) was like fire, Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya) swiftly withdrew his divine arrow."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "Upon seeing those two tiger-like men, whose radiance (tejas) was like fire, Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya) swiftly withdrew his divine arrow."

sanskrit: उवाच वदतां श्रेष्ठस्तावृषी प्राञ्जलिस्तदा ।प्रयुक्तमस्त्रमस्त्रेण शाम्यतामिति वै मया ॥२॥
transliteration: uvāca vadatāṁ śreṣṭhastāvṛṣī prāñjalistadā ,prayuktamastramastreṇa śāmyatāmiti vai mayā.
english: Then Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya), the foremost of speakers, with folded hands, said to those two sages: "Indeed, let this weapon, which has been discharged by me with another weapon, be pacified."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Then Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya), the foremost of speakers, with folded hands, said to those two sages: "Indeed, let this weapon, which has been discharged by me with another weapon, be pacified."

sanskrit: संहृते परमास्त्रेऽस्मिन्सर्वानस्मानशेषतः ।पापकर्मा ध्रुवं द्रौणिः प्रधक्ष्यत्यस्त्रतेजसा ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁhṛte paramāstre'sminsarvānasmānaśeṣataḥ ,pāpakarmā dhruvaṁ drauṇiḥ pradhakṣyatyastratejasā.
english: When this supreme weapon is withdrawn, the sinful Drauni, whose actions (karma) are evil, will surely burn all of us completely with the weapon's potent energy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. When this supreme weapon is withdrawn, the sinful Drauni, whose actions (karma) are evil, will surely burn all of us completely with the weapon's potent energy.

sanskrit: अत्र यद्धितमस्माकं लोकानां चैव सर्वथा ।भवन्तौ देवसंकाशौ तथा संहर्तुमर्हतः ॥४॥
transliteration: atra yaddhitamasmākaṁ lokānāṁ caiva sarvathā ,bhavantau devasaṁkāśau tathā saṁhartumarhataḥ.
english: Whatever is beneficial here for us and for all beings, you two, who are like gods, should accordingly withdraw (the weapon).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. Whatever is beneficial here for us and for all beings, you two, who are like gods, should accordingly withdraw (the weapon).

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा संजहारास्त्रं पुनरेव धनंजयः ।संहारो दुष्करस्तस्य देवैरपि हि संयुगे ॥५॥
transliteration: ityuktvā saṁjahārāstraṁ punareva dhanaṁjayaḥ ,saṁhāro duṣkarastasya devairapi hi saṁyuge.
english: Having spoken thus, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) then withdrew the weapon again. Indeed, its withdrawal was difficult even for the gods in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. Having spoken thus, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) then withdrew the weapon again. Indeed, its withdrawal was difficult even for the gods in battle.

sanskrit: विसृष्टस्य रणे तस्य परमास्त्रस्य संग्रहे ।न शक्तः पाण्डवादन्यः साक्षादपि शतक्रतुः ॥६॥
transliteration: visṛṣṭasya raṇe tasya paramāstrasya saṁgrahe ,na śaktaḥ pāṇḍavādanyaḥ sākṣādapi śatakratuḥ.
english: No one other than the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) is capable of controlling that supreme weapon (astra) which was unleashed in battle, not even Indra (Śatakratu) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. No one other than the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) is capable of controlling that supreme weapon (astra) which was unleashed in battle, not even Indra (Śatakratu) himself.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मतेजोभवं तद्धि विसृष्टमकृतात्मना ।न शक्यमावर्तयितुं ब्रह्मचारिव्रतादृते ॥७॥
transliteration: brahmatejobhavaṁ taddhi visṛṣṭamakṛtātmanā ,na śakyamāvartayituṁ brahmacārivratādṛte.
english: That (weapon), which is born from the power of Brahman (brahman) and has been released by someone whose self (ātman) is not disciplined, certainly cannot be recalled without adhering to the vow of celibacy (brahmacarya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. That (weapon), which is born from the power of Brahman (brahman) and has been released by someone whose self (ātman) is not disciplined, certainly cannot be recalled without adhering to the vow of celibacy (brahmacarya).

sanskrit: अचीर्णब्रह्मचर्यो यः सृष्ट्वावर्तयते पुनः ।तदस्त्रं सानुबन्धस्य मूर्धानं तस्य कृन्तति ॥८॥
transliteration: acīrṇabrahmacaryo yaḥ sṛṣṭvāvartayate punaḥ ,tadastraṁ sānubandhasya mūrdhānaṁ tasya kṛntati.
english: Indeed, the weapon that has been released by someone who has not observed the vow of celibacy (brahmacarya) and then tries to recall it, that very weapon severs the head of that person, along with all its associated consequences.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. Indeed, the weapon that has been released by someone who has not observed the vow of celibacy (brahmacarya) and then tries to recall it, that very weapon severs the head of that person, along with all its associated consequences.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मचारी व्रती चापि दुरवापमवाप्य तत् ।परमव्यसनार्तोऽपि नार्जुनोऽस्त्रं व्यमुञ्चत ॥९॥
transliteration: brahmacārī vratī cāpi duravāpamavāpya tat ,paramavyasanārto'pi nārjuno'straṁ vyamuñcata.
english: Even a celibate (brahmacārin) who observes sacred vows, having acquired that (weapon) which is difficult to obtain, Arjuna still did not release the weapon, even when afflicted by the most extreme distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. Even a celibate (brahmacārin) who observes sacred vows, having acquired that (weapon) which is difficult to obtain, Arjuna still did not release the weapon, even when afflicted by the most extreme distress.

sanskrit: सत्यव्रतधरः शूरो ब्रह्मचारी च पाण्डवः ।गुरुवर्ती च तेनास्त्रं संजहारार्जुनः पुनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: satyavratadharaḥ śūro brahmacārī ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,guruvartī ca tenāstraṁ saṁjahārārjunaḥ punaḥ.
english: That brave Pandava (Arjuna), who was a keeper of truth-vows, a celibate (brahmacārin), and obedient to his guru, therefore withdrew the weapon again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. That brave Pandava (Arjuna), who was a keeper of truth-vows, a celibate (brahmacārin), and obedient to his guru, therefore withdrew the weapon again.
