sanskrit: द्रौणिरप्यथ संप्रेक्ष्य तावृषी पुरतः स्थितौ ।न शशाक पुनर्घोरमस्त्रं संहर्तुमाहवे ॥११॥
transliteration: drauṇirapyatha saṁprekṣya tāvṛṣī purataḥ sthitau ,na śaśāka punarghoramastraṁ saṁhartumāhave.
english: Drauṇi, upon seeing those two sages standing before him, was then unable to withdraw the dreadful weapon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Drauṇi, upon seeing those two sages standing before him, was then unable to withdraw the dreadful weapon in battle.

sanskrit: अशक्तः प्रतिसंहारे परमास्त्रस्य संयुगे ।द्रौणिर्दीनमना राजन्द्वैपायनमभाषत ॥१२॥
transliteration: aśaktaḥ pratisaṁhāre paramāstrasya saṁyuge ,drauṇirdīnamanā rājandvaipāyanamabhāṣata.
english: O king, Drauṇi, dejected in mind and powerless to withdraw the supreme weapon in battle, spoke to Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. O king, Drauṇi, dejected in mind and powerless to withdraw the supreme weapon in battle, spoke to Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa).

sanskrit: उत्तमव्यसनार्तेन प्राणत्राणमभीप्सुना ।मयैतदस्त्रमुत्सृष्टं भीमसेनभयान्मुने ॥१३॥
transliteration: uttamavyasanārtena prāṇatrāṇamabhīpsunā ,mayaitadastramutsṛṣṭaṁ bhīmasenabhayānmune.
english: O sage, I, afflicted by extreme calamity and desiring to save my life, released this weapon out of fear of Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. O sage, I, afflicted by extreme calamity and desiring to save my life, released this weapon out of fear of Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: अधर्मश्च कृतोऽनेन धार्तराष्ट्रं जिघांसता ।मिथ्याचारेण भगवन्भीमसेनेन संयुगे ॥१४॥
transliteration: adharmaśca kṛto'nena dhārtarāṣṭraṁ jighāṁsatā ,mithyācāreṇa bhagavanbhīmasenena saṁyuge.
english: O revered one, an unrighteous act (adharma) was committed in battle by this Bhīmasena, who, acting deceitfully, desired to kill Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. O revered one, an unrighteous act (adharma) was committed in battle by this Bhīmasena, who, acting deceitfully, desired to kill Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭra).

sanskrit: अतः सृष्टमिदं ब्रह्मन्मयास्त्रमकृतात्मना ।तस्य भूयोऽद्य संहारं कर्तुं नाहमिहोत्सहे ॥१५॥
transliteration: ataḥ sṛṣṭamidaṁ brahmanmayāstramakṛtātmanā ,tasya bhūyo'dya saṁhāraṁ kartuṁ nāhamihotsahe.
english: O Brahmin, this weapon was unleashed by me, an undisciplined (akṛtātman) person. Now, I am unable to withdraw its destruction again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. O Brahmin, this weapon was unleashed by me, an undisciplined (akṛtātman) person. Now, I am unable to withdraw its destruction again.

sanskrit: विसृष्टं हि मया दिव्यमेतदस्त्रं दुरासदम् ।अपाण्डवायेति मुने वह्नितेजोऽनुमन्त्र्य वै ॥१६॥
transliteration: visṛṣṭaṁ hi mayā divyametadastraṁ durāsadam ,apāṇḍavāyeti mune vahnitejo'numantrya vai.
english: O sage, indeed, this divine and irresistible weapon was unleashed by me, consecrating it with fiery energy and declaring, "May there be no Pāṇḍavas!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. O sage, indeed, this divine and irresistible weapon was unleashed by me, consecrating it with fiery energy and declaring, "May there be no Pāṇḍavas!"

sanskrit: तदिदं पाण्डवेयानामन्तकायाभिसंहितम् ।अद्य पाण्डुसुतान्सर्वाञ्जीविताद्भ्रंशयिष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ pāṇḍaveyānāmantakāyābhisaṁhitam ,adya pāṇḍusutānsarvāñjīvitādbhraṁśayiṣyati.
english: This weapon, aimed at the destruction of the Pāṇḍavas, will today certainly deprive all the sons of Pāṇḍu of their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. This weapon, aimed at the destruction of the Pāṇḍavas, will today certainly deprive all the sons of Pāṇḍu of their lives.

sanskrit: कृतं पापमिदं ब्रह्मन्रोषाविष्टेन चेतसा ।वधमाशास्य पार्थानां मयास्त्रं सृजता रणे ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṛtaṁ pāpamidaṁ brahmanroṣāviṣṭena cetasā ,vadhamāśāsya pārthānāṁ mayāstraṁ sṛjatā raṇe.
english: O Brahmin, this sin was committed by me, with a mind overcome by rage, desiring the slaughter of the Pārthas while unleashing the weapon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. O Brahmin, this sin was committed by me, with a mind overcome by rage, desiring the slaughter of the Pārthas while unleashing the weapon in battle.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।अस्त्रं ब्रह्मशिरस्तात विद्वान्पार्थो धनंजयः ।उत्सृष्टवान्न रोषेण न वधाय तवाहवे ॥१९॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,astraṁ brahmaśirastāta vidvānpārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ ,utsṛṣṭavānna roṣeṇa na vadhāya tavāhave.
english: Vyasa said: "My dear child (tāta), the discerning Pārtha Dhananjaya released the Brahmaśiras missile not out of anger, nor with the intent to kill you (Ashwatthama) in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. Vyasa said: "My dear child (tāta), the discerning Pārtha Dhananjaya released the Brahmaśiras missile not out of anger, nor with the intent to kill you (Ashwatthama) in battle."

sanskrit: अस्त्रमस्त्रेण तु रणे तव संशमयिष्यता ।विसृष्टमर्जुनेनेदं पुनश्च प्रतिसंहृतम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: astramastreṇa tu raṇe tava saṁśamayiṣyatā ,visṛṣṭamarjunenedaṁ punaśca pratisaṁhṛtam.
english: But this missile was released by Arjuna in battle to pacify your (Ashwatthama's) weapon, and it was also withdrawn (pratisaṃhṛtam) by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. But this missile was released by Arjuna in battle to pacify your (Ashwatthama's) weapon, and it was also withdrawn (pratisaṃhṛtam) by him.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मास्त्रमप्यवाप्यैतदुपदेशात्पितुस्तव ।क्षत्रधर्मान्महाबाहुर्नाकम्पत धनंजयः ॥२१॥
transliteration: brahmāstramapyavāpyaitadupadeśātpitustava ,kṣatradharmānmahābāhurnākampata dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Even having obtained this Brahma weapon (brahmāstra) through the instruction (upadeśa) of his teacher (guru) – Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the mighty-armed, did not waver from the natural law (dharma) of a kṣatriya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. Even having obtained this Brahma weapon (brahmāstra) through the instruction (upadeśa) of his teacher (guru) – Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the mighty-armed, did not waver from the natural law (dharma) of a kṣatriya.

sanskrit: एवं धृतिमतः साधोः सर्वास्त्रविदुषः सतः ।सभ्रातृबन्धोः कस्मात्त्वं वधमस्य चिकीर्षसि ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṁ dhṛtimataḥ sādhoḥ sarvāstraviduṣaḥ sataḥ ,sabhrātṛbandhoḥ kasmāttvaṁ vadhamasya cikīrṣasi.
english: So, why do you desire to kill him, this resolute and righteous (sat) man, who is skilled in all weapons, and who has his brothers and kinsmen (bandhu)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. So, why do you desire to kill him, this resolute and righteous (sat) man, who is skilled in all weapons, and who has his brothers and kinsmen (bandhu)?

sanskrit: अस्त्रं ब्रह्मशिरो यत्र परमास्त्रेण वध्यते ।समा द्वादश पर्जन्यस्तद्राष्ट्रं नाभिवर्षति ॥२३॥
transliteration: astraṁ brahmaśiro yatra paramāstreṇa vadhyate ,samā dvādaśa parjanyastadrāṣṭraṁ nābhivarṣati.
english: Where the Brahmashira (astra) weapon is counteracted by a supreme weapon, for twelve years the rain god (parjanya) does not shower upon that kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. Where the Brahmashira (astra) weapon is counteracted by a supreme weapon, for twelve years the rain god (parjanya) does not shower upon that kingdom.

sanskrit: एतदर्थं महाबाहुः शक्तिमानपि पाण्डवः ।न विहन्त्येतदस्त्रं ते प्रजाहितचिकीर्षया ॥२४॥
transliteration: etadarthaṁ mahābāhuḥ śaktimānapi pāṇḍavaḥ ,na vihantyetadastraṁ te prajāhitacikīrṣayā.
english: For this reason, the mighty-armed Pandava, even though powerful, will not destroy this weapon of yours, out of his desire to secure the welfare of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. For this reason, the mighty-armed Pandava, even though powerful, will not destroy this weapon of yours, out of his desire to secure the welfare of the people.

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्त्वं च राष्ट्रं च सदा संरक्ष्यमेव नः ।तस्मात्संहर दिव्यं त्वमस्त्रमेतन्महाभुज ॥२५॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstvaṁ ca rāṣṭraṁ ca sadā saṁrakṣyameva naḥ ,tasmātsaṁhara divyaṁ tvamastrametanmahābhuja.
english: The Pandavas, you, and the kingdom are always to be protected by us. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, withdraw this divine weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. The Pandavas, you, and the kingdom are always to be protected by us. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, withdraw this divine weapon.

sanskrit: अरोषस्तव चैवास्तु पार्थाः सन्तु निरामयाः ।न ह्यधर्मेण राजर्षिः पाण्डवो जेतुमिच्छति ॥२६॥
transliteration: aroṣastava caivāstu pārthāḥ santu nirāmayāḥ ,na hyadharmeṇa rājarṣiḥ pāṇḍavo jetumicchati.
english: Let your anger cease, and may the Pārthas be free from affliction. For the royal sage Pandava does not wish to conquer through unrighteousness (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. Let your anger cease, and may the Pārthas be free from affliction. For the royal sage Pandava does not wish to conquer through unrighteousness (adharma).

sanskrit: मणिं चैतं प्रयच्छैभ्यो यस्ते शिरसि तिष्ठति ।एतदादाय ते प्राणान्प्रतिदास्यन्ति पाण्डवाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: maṇiṁ caitaṁ prayacchaibhyo yaste śirasi tiṣṭhati ,etadādāya te prāṇānpratidāsyanti pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: And give this gem, which is on your head, to them. The Pāṇḍavas, taking this, will return your lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 27. And give this gem, which is on your head, to them. The Pāṇḍavas, taking this, will return your lives.

sanskrit: द्रौणिरुवाच ।पाण्डवैर्यानि रत्नानि यच्चान्यत्कौरवैर्धनम् ।अवाप्तानीह तेभ्योऽयं मणिर्मम विशिष्यते ॥२८॥
transliteration: drauṇiruvāca ,pāṇḍavairyāni ratnāni yaccānyatkauravairdhanam ,avāptānīha tebhyo'yaṁ maṇirmama viśiṣyate.
english: Drauṇi said: 'This gem of mine surpasses whatever jewels were acquired by the Pāṇḍavas and whatever other wealth was acquired by the Kauravas here.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 28. Drauṇi said: 'This gem of mine surpasses whatever jewels were acquired by the Pāṇḍavas and whatever other wealth was acquired by the Kauravas here.'

sanskrit: यमाबध्य भयं नास्ति शस्त्रव्याधिक्षुधाश्रयम् ।देवेभ्यो दानवेभ्यो वा नागेभ्यो वा कथंचन ॥२९॥
transliteration: yamābadhya bhayaṁ nāsti śastravyādhikṣudhāśrayam ,devebhyo dānavebhyo vā nāgebhyo vā kathaṁcana.
english: When this gem is worn, there is no fear (bhaya) of weapons, disease, or hunger, nor any fear (bhaya) from gods, demons, or serpents whatsoever.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 29. When this gem is worn, there is no fear (bhaya) of weapons, disease, or hunger, nor any fear (bhaya) from gods, demons, or serpents whatsoever.

sanskrit: न च रक्षोगणभयं न तस्करभयं तथा ।एवंवीर्यो मणिरयं न मे त्याज्यः कथंचन ॥३०॥
transliteration: na ca rakṣogaṇabhayaṁ na taskarabhayaṁ tathā ,evaṁvīryo maṇirayaṁ na me tyājyaḥ kathaṁcana.
english: Moreover, there is no fear (bhaya) from hordes of Rākṣasas, nor from thieves. This gem (maṇi), possessing such power, must not be given up by me under any circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 30. Moreover, there is no fear (bhaya) from hordes of Rākṣasas, nor from thieves. This gem (maṇi), possessing such power, must not be given up by me under any circumstances.

sanskrit: यत्तु मे भगवानाह तन्मे कार्यमनन्तरम् ।अयं मणिरयं चाहमिषीका निपतिष्यति ।गर्भेषु पाण्डवेयानाममोघं चैतदुद्यतम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: yattu me bhagavānāha tanme kāryamanantaram ,ayaṁ maṇirayaṁ cāhamiṣīkā nipatiṣyati ,garbheṣu pāṇḍaveyānāmamoghaṁ caitadudyatam.
english: Whatever the revered Lord (Kṛṣṇa) has commanded me, that I must carry out immediately. This jewel (my head-jewel) [is here], and I myself will fall like a mere reed [without it]. And this [weapon], directed at the wombs of the Pāṇḍavas, is certainly infallible.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 31. Whatever the revered Lord (Kṛṣṇa) has commanded me, that I must carry out immediately. This jewel (my head-jewel) [is here], and I myself will fall like a mere reed [without it]. And this [weapon], directed at the wombs of the Pāṇḍavas, is certainly infallible.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।एवं कुरु न चान्या ते बुद्धिः कार्या कदाचन ।गर्भेषु पाण्डवेयानां विसृज्यैतदुपारम ॥३२॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,evaṁ kuru na cānyā te buddhiḥ kāryā kadācana ,garbheṣu pāṇḍaveyānāṁ visṛjyaitadupārama.
english: Vyāsa said, "Do thus, and never entertain any other intention (buddhi). Having released this [weapon's heat] into the wombs of the Pāṇḍavas, then desist."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 32. Vyāsa said, "Do thus, and never entertain any other intention (buddhi). Having released this [weapon's heat] into the wombs of the Pāṇḍavas, then desist."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः परममस्त्रं तदश्वत्थामा भृशातुरः ।द्वैपायनवचः श्रुत्वा गर्भेषु प्रमुमोच ह ॥३३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ paramamastraṁ tadaśvatthāmā bhṛśāturaḥ ,dvaipāyanavacaḥ śrutvā garbheṣu pramumoca ha.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Then, that supreme weapon (Brahmāstra), Aśvatthāmā, who was exceedingly distressed, having heard the words of Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), indeed released it into the wombs."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 33. Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Then, that supreme weapon (Brahmāstra), Aśvatthāmā, who was exceedingly distressed, having heard the words of Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), indeed released it into the wombs."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तदाज्ञाय हृषीकेशो विसृष्टं पापकर्मणा ।हृष्यमाण इदं वाक्यं द्रौणिं प्रत्यब्रवीत्तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tadājñāya hṛṣīkeśo visṛṣṭaṁ pāpakarmaṇā ,hṛṣyamāṇa idaṁ vākyaṁ drauṇiṁ pratyabravīttadā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Then, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), having recognized that [weapon] which had been released by the evil-doer (Aśvatthāmā), spoke these words to the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) while rejoicing."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Then, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), having recognized that [weapon] which had been released by the evil-doer (Aśvatthāmā), spoke these words to the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) while rejoicing."

sanskrit: विराटस्य सुतां पूर्वं स्नुषां गाण्डीवधन्वनः ।उपप्लव्यगतां दृष्ट्वा व्रतवान्ब्राह्मणोऽब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: virāṭasya sutāṁ pūrvaṁ snuṣāṁ gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ ,upaplavyagatāṁ dṛṣṭvā vratavānbrāhmaṇo'bravīt.
english: A vow-observing Brahmin, seeing Virata's daughter  -  who was formerly the daughter-in-law of the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Gāṇḍīvadhanvan)  -  having gone to Upaplavya, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. A vow-observing Brahmin, seeing Virata's daughter  -  who was formerly the daughter-in-law of the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Gāṇḍīvadhanvan)  -  having gone to Upaplavya, spoke.

sanskrit: परिक्षीणेषु कुरुषु पुत्रस्तव जनिष्यति ।एतदस्य परिक्षित्त्वं गर्भस्थस्य भविष्यति ॥३॥
transliteration: parikṣīṇeṣu kuruṣu putrastava janiṣyati ,etadasya parikṣittvaṁ garbhasthasya bhaviṣyati.
english: When the Kuru lineage is completely diminished, your son will be born. This state of being observed and protected (parikṣittvam) will be his even while he is in the womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. When the Kuru lineage is completely diminished, your son will be born. This state of being observed and protected (parikṣittvam) will be his even while he is in the womb.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं साधोः सत्यमेव भविष्यति ।परिक्षिद्भविता ह्येषां पुनर्वंशकरः सुतः ॥४॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ sādhoḥ satyameva bhaviṣyati ,parikṣidbhavitā hyeṣāṁ punarvaṁśakaraḥ sutaḥ.
english: That statement of the good man (sādhu) will indeed come true. This son will be Parikṣit, who will restore their lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. That statement of the good man (sādhu) will indeed come true. This son will be Parikṣit, who will restore their lineage.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवाणं गोविन्दं सात्वतप्रवरं तदा ।द्रौणिः परमसंरब्धः प्रत्युवाचेदमुत्तरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvāṇaṁ govindaṁ sātvatapravaraṁ tadā ,drauṇiḥ paramasaṁrabdhaḥ pratyuvācedamuttaram.
english: Then Drauni, Droṇa's son, greatly enraged, spoke this reply to Govinda, the foremost among the Sātvatas, who was speaking thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. Then Drauni, Droṇa's son, greatly enraged, spoke this reply to Govinda, the foremost among the Sātvatas, who was speaking thus.

sanskrit: नैतदेवं यथात्थ त्वं पक्षपातेन केशव ।वचनं पुण्डरीकाक्ष न च मद्वाक्यमन्यथा ॥६॥
transliteration: naitadevaṁ yathāttha tvaṁ pakṣapātena keśava ,vacanaṁ puṇḍarīkākṣa na ca madvākyamanyathā.
english: O Keśava, lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), this is not as you state, influenced by partiality. My word is not otherwise (not false).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. O Keśava, lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), this is not as you state, influenced by partiality. My word is not otherwise (not false).

sanskrit: पतिष्यत्येतदस्त्रं हि गर्भे तस्या मयोद्यतम् ।विराटदुहितुः कृष्ण यां त्वं रक्षितुमिच्छसि ॥७॥
transliteration: patiṣyatyetadastraṁ hi garbhe tasyā mayodyatam ,virāṭaduhituḥ kṛṣṇa yāṁ tvaṁ rakṣitumicchasi.
english: O Krishna, this weapon, indeed, having been handled by me (in an attempt to stop it), will inevitably affect the womb of Virāṭa's daughter, whom you desire to protect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. O Krishna, this weapon, indeed, having been handled by me (in an attempt to stop it), will inevitably affect the womb of Virāṭa's daughter, whom you desire to protect.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अमोघः परमास्त्रस्य पातस्तस्य भविष्यति ।स तु गर्भो मृतो जातो दीर्घमायुरवाप्स्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,amoghaḥ paramāstrasya pātastasya bhaviṣyati ,sa tu garbho mṛto jāto dīrghamāyuravāpsyati.
english: Vāsudeva said: "The impact (pāta) of that supreme weapon (paramāstra) will certainly be unfailing. But that fetus, though born dead, will attain a long life."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. Vāsudeva said: "The impact (pāta) of that supreme weapon (paramāstra) will certainly be unfailing. But that fetus, though born dead, will attain a long life."

sanskrit: त्वां तु कापुरुषं पापं विदुः सर्वे मनीषिणः ।असकृत्पापकर्माणं बालजीवितघातकम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tvāṁ tu kāpuruṣaṁ pāpaṁ viduḥ sarve manīṣiṇaḥ ,asakṛtpāpakarmāṇaṁ bālajīvitaghātakam.
english: But all thoughtful persons (manīṣiṇaḥ) know you to be a wicked (kāpuruṣa), sinful man, one whose actions are repeatedly evil, and a killer of children.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. But all thoughtful persons (manīṣiṇaḥ) know you to be a wicked (kāpuruṣa), sinful man, one whose actions are repeatedly evil, and a killer of children.

sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमस्य पापस्य कर्मणः फलमाप्नुहि ।त्रीणि वर्षसहस्राणि चरिष्यसि महीमिमाम् ।अप्राप्नुवन्क्वचित्कांचित्संविदं जातु केनचित् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasmāttvamasya pāpasya karmaṇaḥ phalamāpnuhi ,trīṇi varṣasahasrāṇi cariṣyasi mahīmimām ,aprāpnuvankvacitkāṁcitsaṁvidaṁ jātu kenacit.
english: Therefore, you shall experience the consequence (phala) of this sinful act (karma). For three thousand years, you will wander this earth, never at any time finding any understanding or connection with anyone anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. Therefore, you shall experience the consequence (phala) of this sinful act (karma). For three thousand years, you will wander this earth, never at any time finding any understanding or connection with anyone anywhere.

sanskrit: निर्जनानसहायस्त्वं देशान्प्रविचरिष्यसि ।भवित्री न हि ते क्षुद्र जनमध्येषु संस्थितिः ॥११॥
transliteration: nirjanānasahāyastvaṁ deśānpravicariṣyasi ,bhavitrī na hi te kṣudra janamadhyeṣu saṁsthitiḥ.
english: You will wander through desolate and friendless lands. Oh wretched one, there will certainly be no dwelling for you among people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. You will wander through desolate and friendless lands. Oh wretched one, there will certainly be no dwelling for you among people.

sanskrit: पूयशोणितगन्धी च दुर्गकान्तारसंश्रयः ।विचरिष्यसि पापात्मन्सर्वव्याधिसमन्वितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pūyaśoṇitagandhī ca durgakāntārasaṁśrayaḥ ,vicariṣyasi pāpātmansarvavyādhisamanvitaḥ.
english: And, O evil-souled one (pāpātman), you will wander, smelling of pus and blood, dwelling in inaccessible forests, and afflicted with all diseases.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. And, O evil-souled one (pāpātman), you will wander, smelling of pus and blood, dwelling in inaccessible forests, and afflicted with all diseases.

sanskrit: वयः प्राप्य परिक्षित्तु वेदव्रतमवाप्य च ।कृपाच्छारद्वताद्वीरः सर्वास्त्राण्युपलप्स्यते ॥१३॥
transliteration: vayaḥ prāpya parikṣittu vedavratamavāpya ca ,kṛpācchāradvatādvīraḥ sarvāstrāṇyupalapsyate.
english: But Parikṣit, having reached maturity and having accomplished his Vedic vows, that hero will obtain all weapons from Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. But Parikṣit, having reached maturity and having accomplished his Vedic vows, that hero will obtain all weapons from Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan.

sanskrit: विदित्वा परमास्त्राणि क्षत्रधर्मव्रते स्थितः ।षष्टिं वर्षाणि धर्मात्मा वसुधां पालयिष्यति ॥१४॥
transliteration: viditvā paramāstrāṇi kṣatradharmavrate sthitaḥ ,ṣaṣṭiṁ varṣāṇi dharmātmā vasudhāṁ pālayiṣyati.
english: Having understood the supreme weapons and being established in the vow of a warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma), the righteous soul (dharmātmā) will rule the earth for sixty years.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. Having understood the supreme weapons and being established in the vow of a warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma), the righteous soul (dharmātmā) will rule the earth for sixty years.

sanskrit: इतश्चोर्ध्वं महाबाहुः कुरुराजो भविष्यति ।परिक्षिन्नाम नृपतिर्मिषतस्ते सुदुर्मते ।पश्य मे तपसो वीर्यं सत्यस्य च नराधम ॥१५॥
transliteration: itaścordhvaṁ mahābāhuḥ kururājo bhaviṣyati ,parikṣinnāma nṛpatirmiṣataste sudurmate ,paśya me tapaso vīryaṁ satyasya ca narādhama.
english: From now onwards, a mighty-armed king of the Kurus named Parikṣit will rule, while you are watching, O evil-minded one! O vilest among men, behold the power of my asceticism (tapas) and truth!
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. From now onwards, a mighty-armed king of the Kurus named Parikṣit will rule, while you are watching, O evil-minded one! O vilest among men, behold the power of my asceticism (tapas) and truth!

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।यस्मादनादृत्य कृतं त्वयास्मान्कर्म दारुणम् ।ब्राह्मणस्य सतश्चैव यस्मात्ते वृत्तमीदृशम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,yasmādanādṛtya kṛtaṁ tvayāsmānkarma dāruṇam ,brāhmaṇasya sataścaiva yasmātte vṛttamīdṛśam.
english: Vyāsa said: Because you, disregarding us, have committed this dreadful act (karma); and because such conduct has been yours, even though you are a Brahmin...
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. Vyāsa said: Because you, disregarding us, have committed this dreadful act (karma); and because such conduct has been yours, even though you are a Brahmin...

sanskrit: तस्माद्यद्देवकीपुत्र उक्तवानुत्तमं वचः ।असंशयं ते तद्भावि क्षुद्रकर्मन्व्रजाश्वितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasmādyaddevakīputra uktavānuttamaṁ vacaḥ ,asaṁśayaṁ te tadbhāvi kṣudrakarmanvrajāśvitaḥ.
english: Therefore, what the son of Devakī (Kṛṣṇa) has spoken, that supreme word will undoubtedly come to pass for you, O mean-spirited one. Go quickly!
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. Therefore, what the son of Devakī (Kṛṣṇa) has spoken, that supreme word will undoubtedly come to pass for you, O mean-spirited one. Go quickly!

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामोवाच ।सहैव भवता ब्रह्मन्स्थास्यामि पुरुषेष्वहम् ।सत्यवागस्तु भगवानयं च पुरुषोत्तमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmovāca ,sahaiva bhavatā brahmansthāsyāmi puruṣeṣvaham ,satyavāgastu bhagavānayaṁ ca puruṣottamaḥ.
english: Aśvatthāman said: "O Brāhmaṇa, I will surely remain among these people (puruṣa) with you. And may this divine (bhagavān) supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama) be true to his word."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. Aśvatthāman said: "O Brāhmaṇa, I will surely remain among these people (puruṣa) with you. And may this divine (bhagavān) supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama) be true to his word."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रदायाथ मणिं द्रौणिः पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।जगाम विमनास्तेषां सर्वेषां पश्यतां वनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pradāyātha maṇiṁ drauṇiḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,jagāma vimanāsteṣāṁ sarveṣāṁ paśyatāṁ vanam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Then, having handed over the jewel to the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), disheartened, went to the forest while all of them watched."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. Vaiśampāyana said: "Then, having handed over the jewel to the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), disheartened, went to the forest while all of them watched."

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्चापि गोविन्दं पुरस्कृत्य हतद्विषः ।कृष्णद्वैपायनं चैव नारदं च महामुनिम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāścāpi govindaṁ puraskṛtya hatadviṣaḥ ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaṁ caiva nāradaṁ ca mahāmunim.
english: And the Pāṇḍavas, who had vanquished their enemies, also placing Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) at their head, and Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and the great sage Nārada...
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. And the Pāṇḍavas, who had vanquished their enemies, also placing Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) at their head, and Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and the great sage Nārada...

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रस्य सहजं मणिमादाय सत्वराः ।द्रौपदीमभ्यधावन्त प्रायोपेतां मनस्विनीम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: droṇaputrasya sahajaṁ maṇimādāya satvarāḥ ,draupadīmabhyadhāvanta prāyopetāṁ manasvinīm.
english: Having taken the jewel (maṇi) that was naturally born with Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), they swiftly rushed towards Draupadī, who was high-minded and preparing for a fast unto death (prāyopetā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. Having taken the jewel (maṇi) that was naturally born with Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), they swiftly rushed towards Draupadī, who was high-minded and preparing for a fast unto death (prāyopetā).

sanskrit: ततस्ते पुरुषव्याघ्राः सदश्वैरनिलोपमैः ।अभ्ययुः सहदाशार्हाः शिबिरं पुनरेव ह ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataste puruṣavyāghrāḥ sadaśvairanilopamaiḥ ,abhyayuḥ sahadāśārhāḥ śibiraṁ punareva ha.
english: Then, those foremost among men, accompanied by the Daśārhas (Krishna and his relatives), returned to the camp on excellent horses swift as the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. Then, those foremost among men, accompanied by the Daśārhas (Krishna and his relatives), returned to the camp on excellent horses swift as the wind.

sanskrit: अवतीर्य रथाभ्यां तु त्वरमाणा महारथाः ।ददृशुर्द्रौपदीं कृष्णामार्तामार्ततराः स्वयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: avatīrya rathābhyāṁ tu tvaramāṇā mahārathāḥ ,dadṛśurdraupadīṁ kṛṣṇāmārtāmārtatarāḥ svayam.
english: And having quickly dismounted from their two chariots, those great chariot warriors (mahārathāḥ) themselves became even more distressed upon seeing Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), who was already suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. And having quickly dismounted from their two chariots, those great chariot warriors (mahārathāḥ) themselves became even more distressed upon seeing Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), who was already suffering.

sanskrit: तामुपेत्य निरानन्दां दुःखशोकसमन्विताम् ।परिवार्य व्यतिष्ठन्त पाण्डवाः सहकेशवाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tāmupetya nirānandāṁ duḥkhaśokasamanvitām ,parivārya vyatiṣṭhanta pāṇḍavāḥ sahakeśavāḥ.
english: Having approached her, who was joyless and overcome with sorrow and grief, the Pāṇḍavas, accompanied by Keśava (Krishna), stood surrounding her.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. Having approached her, who was joyless and overcome with sorrow and grief, the Pāṇḍavas, accompanied by Keśava (Krishna), stood surrounding her.

sanskrit: ततो राज्ञाभ्यनुज्ञातो भीमसेनो महाबलः ।प्रददौ तु मणिं दिव्यं वचनं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato rājñābhyanujñāto bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,pradadau tu maṇiṁ divyaṁ vacanaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: Then, having been permitted by the king, Bhīmasena, who was of great strength, presented the divine jewel and spoke the following words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. Then, having been permitted by the king, Bhīmasena, who was of great strength, presented the divine jewel and spoke the following words.

sanskrit: अयं भद्रे तव मणिः पुत्रहन्ता जितः स ते ।उत्तिष्ठ शोकमुत्सृज्य क्षत्रधर्ममनुस्मर ॥२६॥
transliteration: ayaṁ bhadre tava maṇiḥ putrahantā jitaḥ sa te ,uttiṣṭha śokamutsṛjya kṣatradharmamanusmara.
english: O gentle lady, this gem, which belonged to the slayer of your sons, has been won by you. Rise up, casting aside your grief, and recall the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. O gentle lady, this gem, which belonged to the slayer of your sons, has been won by you. Rise up, casting aside your grief, and recall the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior.

sanskrit: प्रयाणे वासुदेवस्य शमार्थमसितेक्षणे ।यान्युक्तानि त्वया भीरु वाक्यानि मधुघातिनः ॥२७॥
transliteration: prayāṇe vāsudevasya śamārthamasitekṣaṇe ,yānyuktāni tvayā bhīru vākyāni madhughātinaḥ.
english: O dark-eyed one, O timid one, (remember) those words which were spoken by you to Vāsudeva, the slayer of Madhu, for the purpose of peace, at his departure.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. O dark-eyed one, O timid one, (remember) those words which were spoken by you to Vāsudeva, the slayer of Madhu, for the purpose of peace, at his departure.

sanskrit: नैव मे पतयः सन्ति न पुत्रा भ्रातरो न च ।नैव त्वमपि गोविन्द शममिच्छति राजनि ॥२८॥
transliteration: naiva me patayaḥ santi na putrā bhrātaro na ca ,naiva tvamapi govinda śamamicchati rājani.
english: My husbands certainly do not exist, nor sons, nor brothers. And, O Govinda, do even you (Kṛṣṇa) not desire peace for King Yudhiṣṭhira?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. My husbands certainly do not exist, nor sons, nor brothers. And, O Govinda, do even you (Kṛṣṇa) not desire peace for King Yudhiṣṭhira?

sanskrit: उक्तवत्यसि धीराणि वाक्यानि पुरुषोत्तमम् ।क्षत्रधर्मानुरूपाणि तानि संस्मर्तुमर्हसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: uktavatyasi dhīrāṇi vākyāni puruṣottamam ,kṣatradharmānurūpāṇi tāni saṁsmartumarhasi.
english: You spoke firm words to the supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama), words that were in accordance with the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior. You ought to remember those (words).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. You spoke firm words to the supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama), words that were in accordance with the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior. You ought to remember those (words).

sanskrit: हतो दुर्योधनः पापो राज्यस्य परिपन्थकः ।दुःशासनस्य रुधिरं पीतं विस्फुरतो मया ॥३०॥
transliteration: hato duryodhanaḥ pāpo rājyasya paripanthakaḥ ,duḥśāsanasya rudhiraṁ pītaṁ visphurato mayā.
english: The wicked Duryodhana, the enemy of the kingdom, has been killed. The blood of Duḥśāsana, while he was struggling, has been drunk by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. The wicked Duryodhana, the enemy of the kingdom, has been killed. The blood of Duḥśāsana, while he was struggling, has been drunk by me.

sanskrit: वैरस्य गतमानृण्यं न स्म वाच्या विवक्षताम् ।जित्वा मुक्तो द्रोणपुत्रो ब्राह्मण्याद्गौरवेण च ॥३१॥
transliteration: vairasya gatamānṛṇyaṁ na sma vācyā vivakṣatām ,jitvā mukto droṇaputro brāhmaṇyādgauraveṇa ca.
english: Our obligation regarding the enmity has been fulfilled. No reproach should be uttered by those who wish to speak. Drona's son was released after being defeated, due to his Brahminhood and (our) respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. Our obligation regarding the enmity has been fulfilled. No reproach should be uttered by those who wish to speak. Drona's son was released after being defeated, due to his Brahminhood and (our) respect.

sanskrit: यशोऽस्य पातितं देवि शरीरं त्ववशेषितम् ।वियोजितश्च मणिना न्यासितश्चायुधं भुवि ॥३२॥
transliteration: yaśo'sya pātitaṁ devi śarīraṁ tvavaśeṣitam ,viyojitaśca maṇinā nyāsitaścāyudhaṁ bhuvi.
english: O queen, his glory has been brought down, but his body has been spared. He has been deprived of his jewel, and his weapon has been laid down on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. O queen, his glory has been brought down, but his body has been spared. He has been deprived of his jewel, and his weapon has been laid down on the ground.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।केवलानृण्यमाप्तास्मि गुरुपुत्रो गुरुर्मम ।शिरस्येतं मणिं राजा प्रतिबध्नातु भारत ॥३३॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,kevalānṛṇyamāptāsmi guruputro gururmama ,śirasyetaṁ maṇiṁ rājā pratibadhnātu bhārata.
english: Draupadi said: "I have achieved only the fulfillment of my obligation. The guru's son (Aśvatthāman) is my (respected) teacher (guru). O King, O descendant of Bharata, may you fasten this jewel on your head!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. Draupadi said: "I have achieved only the fulfillment of my obligation. The guru's son (Aśvatthāman) is my (respected) teacher (guru). O King, O descendant of Bharata, may you fasten this jewel on your head!"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं गृहीत्वा ततो राजा शिरस्येवाकरोत्तदा ।गुरोरुच्छिष्टमित्येव द्रौपद्या वचनादपि ॥३४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ gṛhītvā tato rājā śirasyevākarottadā ,gurorucchiṣṭamityeva draupadyā vacanādapi.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then the king, taking it, placed it on his head, considering it a remnant (ucchiṣṭa) from the preceptor (guru), and also complying with Draupadī's request.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. Vaiśampāyana said: Then the king, taking it, placed it on his head, considering it a remnant (ucchiṣṭa) from the preceptor (guru), and also complying with Draupadī's request.

sanskrit: ततो दिव्यं मणिवरं शिरसा धारयन्प्रभुः ।शुशुभे स महाराजः सचन्द्र इव पर्वतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tato divyaṁ maṇivaraṁ śirasā dhārayanprabhuḥ ,śuśubhe sa mahārājaḥ sacandra iva parvataḥ.
english: Then, bearing the excellent divine jewel on his head, that great king (prabhu) shone like a mountain adorned with the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. Then, bearing the excellent divine jewel on his head, that great king (prabhu) shone like a mountain adorned with the moon.

sanskrit: उत्तस्थौ पुत्रशोकार्ता ततः कृष्णा मनस्विनी ।कृष्णं चापि महाबाहुं पर्यपृच्छत धर्मराट् ॥३६॥
transliteration: uttasthau putraśokārtā tataḥ kṛṣṇā manasvinī ,kṛṣṇaṁ cāpi mahābāhuṁ paryapṛcchata dharmarāṭ.
english: Then, the high-minded Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), afflicted with grief for her sons, arose. And the king of (natural law) (dharmarāṭ), Yudhishthira, also questioned the mighty-armed Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. Then, the high-minded Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), afflicted with grief for her sons, arose. And the king of (natural law) (dharmarāṭ), Yudhishthira, also questioned the mighty-armed Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।हतेषु सर्वसैन्येषु सौप्तिके तै रथैस्त्रिभिः ।शोचन्युधिष्ठिरो राजा दाशार्हमिदमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,hateṣu sarvasainyeṣu sauptike tai rathaistribhiḥ ,śocanyudhiṣṭhiro rājā dāśārhamidamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When all the armies had been killed in the night attack (sauptika) by those three charioteers, King Yudhishthira, grieving, spoke this to Kṛṣṇa (Dāśārha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When all the armies had been killed in the night attack (sauptika) by those three charioteers, King Yudhishthira, grieving, spoke this to Kṛṣṇa (Dāśārha).

sanskrit: कथं नु कृष्ण पापेन क्षुद्रेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।द्रौणिना निहताः सर्वे मम पुत्रा महारथाः ॥२॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu kṛṣṇa pāpena kṣudreṇākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,drauṇinā nihatāḥ sarve mama putrā mahārathāḥ.
english: How then, Krishna, were all my great chariot-warrior sons killed by Droṇa's son, Aśvatthāman, that sinful, ignoble man whose deeds were unhindered?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. How then, Krishna, were all my great chariot-warrior sons killed by Droṇa's son, Aśvatthāman, that sinful, ignoble man whose deeds were unhindered?

sanskrit: तथा कृतास्त्रा विक्रान्ताः सहस्रशतयोधिनः ।द्रुपदस्यात्मजाश्चैव द्रोणपुत्रेण पातिताः ॥३॥
transliteration: tathā kṛtāstrā vikrāntāḥ sahasraśatayodhinaḥ ,drupadasyātmajāścaiva droṇaputreṇa pātitāḥ.
english: Similarly, the sons of Drupada, who were skilled in weaponry, valorous, and able to fight hundreds of thousands, were also slain by Droṇa's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. Similarly, the sons of Drupada, who were skilled in weaponry, valorous, and able to fight hundreds of thousands, were also slain by Droṇa's son.

sanskrit: यस्य द्रोणो महेष्वासो न प्रादादाहवे मुखम् ।तं जघ्ने रथिनां श्रेष्ठं धृष्टद्युम्नं कथं नु सः ॥४॥
transliteration: yasya droṇo maheṣvāso na prādādāhave mukham ,taṁ jaghne rathināṁ śreṣṭhaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ kathaṁ nu saḥ.
english: How then did he (Aśvatthāman) kill Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the foremost among chariot-warriors, whom even Droṇa, the great archer, would not directly confront in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. How then did he (Aśvatthāman) kill Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the foremost among chariot-warriors, whom even Droṇa, the great archer, would not directly confront in battle?

sanskrit: किं नु तेन कृतं कर्म तथायुक्तं नरर्षभ ।यदेकः शिबिरं सर्वमवधीन्नो गुरोः सुतः ॥५॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu tena kṛtaṁ karma tathāyuktaṁ nararṣabha ,yadekaḥ śibiraṁ sarvamavadhīnno guroḥ sutaḥ.
english: O chief of men (Krishna), what sort of action did he perform, so terribly effective, that our preceptor's son alone destroyed the entire camp?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. O chief of men (Krishna), what sort of action did he perform, so terribly effective, that our preceptor's son alone destroyed the entire camp?

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।नूनं स देवदेवानामीश्वरेश्वरमव्ययम् ।जगाम शरणं द्रौणिरेकस्तेनावधीद्बहून् ॥६॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,nūnaṁ sa devadevānāmīśvareśvaramavyayam ,jagāma śaraṇaṁ drauṇirekastenāvadhīdbahūn.
english: Vāsudeva said: Surely, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), though alone, took refuge with the immutable Lord of lords, the God of gods. By the power granted by Him, he (Drauṇi) killed many.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. Vāsudeva said: Surely, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), though alone, took refuge with the immutable Lord of lords, the God of gods. By the power granted by Him, he (Drauṇi) killed many.

sanskrit: प्रसन्नो हि महादेवो दद्यादमरतामपि ।वीर्यं च गिरिशो दद्याद्येनेन्द्रमपि शातयेत् ॥७॥
transliteration: prasanno hi mahādevo dadyādamaratāmapi ,vīryaṁ ca giriśo dadyādyenendramapi śātayet.
english: For indeed, the gratified great God (Mahādeva) can bestow even immortality. And Giriśa (Shiva) can grant such immense power (vīrya) that with it, one could even defeat Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. For indeed, the gratified great God (Mahādeva) can bestow even immortality. And Giriśa (Shiva) can grant such immense power (vīrya) that with it, one could even defeat Indra.

sanskrit: वेदाहं हि महादेवं तत्त्वेन भरतर्षभ ।यानि चास्य पुराणानि कर्माणि विविधान्युत ॥८॥
transliteration: vedāhaṁ hi mahādevaṁ tattvena bharatarṣabha ,yāni cāsya purāṇāni karmāṇi vividhānyuta.
english: Indeed, O best of Bhāratas, I truly understand the great God (Mahādeva) in his true essence (tattva). I also know all His ancient and diverse actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. Indeed, O best of Bhāratas, I truly understand the great God (Mahādeva) in his true essence (tattva). I also know all His ancient and diverse actions (karma).

sanskrit: आदिरेष हि भूतानां मध्यमन्तश्च भारत ।विचेष्टते जगच्चेदं सर्वमस्यैव कर्मणा ॥९॥
transliteration: ādireṣa hi bhūtānāṁ madhyamantaśca bhārata ,viceṣṭate jagaccedaṁ sarvamasyaiva karmaṇā.
english: Indeed, O Bhārata, He (Mahādeva) is the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings. This entire universe moves and functions solely through His own action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. Indeed, O Bhārata, He (Mahādeva) is the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings. This entire universe moves and functions solely through His own action (karma).

sanskrit: एवं सिसृक्षुर्भूतानि ददर्श प्रथमं विभुः ।पितामहोऽब्रवीच्चैनं भूतानि सृज माचिरम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ sisṛkṣurbhūtāni dadarśa prathamaṁ vibhuḥ ,pitāmaho'bravīccainaṁ bhūtāni sṛja māciram.
english: Thus, the powerful lord (Brahma), desiring to create beings, first beheld him. The grandfather (Brahma) then said to him, 'Create beings without delay!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. Thus, the powerful lord (Brahma), desiring to create beings, first beheld him. The grandfather (Brahma) then said to him, 'Create beings without delay!'

sanskrit: हरिकेशस्तथेत्युक्त्वा भूतानां दोषदर्शिवान् ।दीर्घकालं तपस्तेपे मग्नोऽम्भसि महातपाः ॥११॥
transliteration: harikeśastathetyuktvā bhūtānāṁ doṣadarśivān ,dīrghakālaṁ tapastepe magno'mbhasi mahātapāḥ.
english: Having said 'So be it,' Hari Keśa, who perceived the faults of beings, then performed severe penance (tapas) for a long time, submerged in water, he of great austerity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. Having said 'So be it,' Hari Keśa, who perceived the faults of beings, then performed severe penance (tapas) for a long time, submerged in water, he of great austerity.

sanskrit: सुमहान्तं ततः कालं प्रतीक्ष्यैनं पितामहः ।स्रष्टारं सर्वभूतानां ससर्ज मनसापरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: sumahāntaṁ tataḥ kālaṁ pratīkṣyainaṁ pitāmahaḥ ,sraṣṭāraṁ sarvabhūtānāṁ sasarja manasāparam.
english: Then, after waiting for a very long time for him, the grandfather (Brahma) mentally created another creator of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. Then, after waiting for a very long time for him, the grandfather (Brahma) mentally created another creator of all beings.

sanskrit: सोऽब्रवीत्पितरं दृष्ट्वा गिरिशं मग्नमम्भसि ।यदि मे नाग्रजस्त्वन्यस्ततः स्रक्ष्याम्यहं प्रजाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: so'bravītpitaraṁ dṛṣṭvā giriśaṁ magnamambhasi ,yadi me nāgrajastvanyastataḥ srakṣyāmyahaṁ prajāḥ.
english: Having seen his father, Giriśa, submerged in water, he (the new creator) said, 'If there is no elder brother for me other than you, then I shall create beings.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. Having seen his father, Giriśa, submerged in water, he (the new creator) said, 'If there is no elder brother for me other than you, then I shall create beings.'

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्पिता नास्ति त्वदन्यः पुरुषोऽग्रजः ।स्थाणुरेष जले मग्नो विस्रब्धः कुरु वै कृतिम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tamabravītpitā nāsti tvadanyaḥ puruṣo'grajaḥ ,sthāṇureṣa jale magno visrabdhaḥ kuru vai kṛtim.
english: His father said to him: "There is no elder person (puruṣa) other than you. This one is like a pillar, submerged in water; therefore, confidently perform your task."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. His father said to him: "There is no elder person (puruṣa) other than you. This one is like a pillar, submerged in water; therefore, confidently perform your task."

sanskrit: स भूतान्यसृजत्सप्त दक्षादींस्तु प्रजापतीन् ।यैरिमं व्यकरोत्सर्वं भूतग्रामं चतुर्विधम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa bhūtānyasṛjatsapta dakṣādīṁstu prajāpatīn ,yairimaṁ vyakarotsarvaṁ bhūtagrāmaṁ caturvidham.
english: He created seven beings - namely, the Prajāpatis headed by Dakṣa - by whom he diversified this entire four-fold aggregate of creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. He created seven beings - namely, the Prajāpatis headed by Dakṣa - by whom he diversified this entire four-fold aggregate of creatures.

sanskrit: ताः सृष्टमात्राः क्षुधिताः प्रजाः सर्वाः प्रजापतिम् ।बिभक्षयिषवो राजन्सहसा प्राद्रवंस्तदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: tāḥ sṛṣṭamātrāḥ kṣudhitāḥ prajāḥ sarvāḥ prajāpatim ,bibhakṣayiṣavo rājansahasā prādravaṁstadā.
english: O King, those creatures, all of them, as soon as they were created, became hungry and, desiring to devour the Prajāpati, suddenly rushed towards him then.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. O King, those creatures, all of them, as soon as they were created, became hungry and, desiring to devour the Prajāpati, suddenly rushed towards him then.

sanskrit: स भक्ष्यमाणस्त्राणार्थी पितामहमुपाद्रवत् ।आभ्यो मां भगवान्पातु वृत्तिरासां विधीयताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa bhakṣyamāṇastrāṇārthī pitāmahamupādravat ,ābhyo māṁ bhagavānpātu vṛttirāsāṁ vidhīyatām.
english: Being devoured, he, seeking protection, rushed to the Grandfather (Brahmā). (He pleaded): "May you, revered lord, protect me from these! Let a means of subsistence be provided for them!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. Being devoured, he, seeking protection, rushed to the Grandfather (Brahmā). (He pleaded): "May you, revered lord, protect me from these! Let a means of subsistence be provided for them!"

sanskrit: ततस्ताभ्यो ददावन्नमोषधीः स्थावराणि च ।जङ्गमानि च भूतानि दुर्बलानि बलीयसाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatastābhyo dadāvannamoṣadhīḥ sthāvarāṇi ca ,jaṅgamāni ca bhūtāni durbalāni balīyasām.
english: Then he gave them food, as well as herbs and immobile things. He also provided mobile creatures, the weak ones, for the strong.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. Then he gave them food, as well as herbs and immobile things. He also provided mobile creatures, the weak ones, for the strong.

sanskrit: विहितान्नाः प्रजास्तास्तु जग्मुस्तुष्टा यथागतम् ।ततो ववृधिरे राजन्प्रीतिमत्यः स्वयोनिषु ॥१९॥
transliteration: vihitānnāḥ prajāstāstu jagmustuṣṭā yathāgatam ,tato vavṛdhire rājanprītimatyaḥ svayoniṣu.
english: But those creatures, for whom food had been provided, went away satisfied, each to their own abode. Then, O king, they greatly increased, becoming affectionate towards their own species.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. But those creatures, for whom food had been provided, went away satisfied, each to their own abode. Then, O king, they greatly increased, becoming affectionate towards their own species.

sanskrit: भूतग्रामे विवृद्धे तु तुष्टे लोकगुरावपि ।उदतिष्ठज्जलाज्ज्येष्ठः प्रजाश्चेमा ददर्श सः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhūtagrāme vivṛddhe tu tuṣṭe lokagurāvapi ,udatiṣṭhajjalājjyeṣṭhaḥ prajāścemā dadarśa saḥ.
english: And when the multitude of beings (bhūta) had greatly increased, and even the teacher of the worlds was satisfied, then the eldest (Brahmā) arose from the water, and he saw these creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. And when the multitude of beings (bhūta) had greatly increased, and even the teacher of the worlds was satisfied, then the eldest (Brahmā) arose from the water, and he saw these creatures.

sanskrit: बहुरूपाः प्रजा दृष्ट्वा विवृद्धाः स्वेन तेजसा ।चुक्रोध भगवान्रुद्रो लिङ्गं स्वं चाप्यविध्यत ॥२१॥
transliteration: bahurūpāḥ prajā dṛṣṭvā vivṛddhāḥ svena tejasā ,cukrodha bhagavānrudro liṅgaṁ svaṁ cāpyavidhyata.
english: Having seen the diverse forms of creatures, grown by their own power, the venerable Rudra became angry. He then pierced his own emblem (liṅga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. Having seen the diverse forms of creatures, grown by their own power, the venerable Rudra became angry. He then pierced his own emblem (liṅga).

sanskrit: तत्प्रविद्धं तदा भूमौ तथैव प्रत्यतिष्ठत ।तमुवाचाव्ययो ब्रह्मा वचोभिः शमयन्निव ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatpraviddhaṁ tadā bhūmau tathaiva pratyatiṣṭhata ,tamuvācāvyayo brahmā vacobhiḥ śamayanniva.
english: That which had been cast then became firmly established on the ground. The imperishable Brahmā then spoke to him, as if calming him with words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. That which had been cast then became firmly established on the ground. The imperishable Brahmā then spoke to him, as if calming him with words.

sanskrit: किं कृतं सलिले शर्व चिरकालं स्थितेन ते ।किमर्थं चैतदुत्पाट्य भूमौ लिङ्गं प्रवेरितम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: kiṁ kṛtaṁ salile śarva cirakālaṁ sthitena te ,kimarthaṁ caitadutpāṭya bhūmau liṅgaṁ praveritam.
english: O Śarva, what have you done, you who remained for a long time in the water? And for what purpose has this liṅga (sacred emblem), after being uprooted, been cast upon the ground?
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. O Śarva, what have you done, you who remained for a long time in the water? And for what purpose has this liṅga (sacred emblem), after being uprooted, been cast upon the ground?

sanskrit: सोऽब्रवीज्जातसंरम्भस्तदा लोकगुरुर्गुरुम् ।प्रजाः सृष्टाः परेणेमाः किं करिष्याम्यनेन वै ॥२४॥
transliteration: so'bravījjātasaṁrambhastadā lokagururgurum ,prajāḥ sṛṣṭāḥ pareṇemāḥ kiṁ kariṣyāmyanena vai.
english: Then, becoming enraged, he (Śiva), the teacher of the world, spoke to the teacher (Brahmā): "These beings have been created by another; what shall I indeed do with this (liṅga)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. Then, becoming enraged, he (Śiva), the teacher of the world, spoke to the teacher (Brahmā): "These beings have been created by another; what shall I indeed do with this (liṅga)?"

sanskrit: तपसाधिगतं चान्नं प्रजार्थं मे पितामह ।ओषध्यः परिवर्तेरन्यथैव सततं प्रजाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tapasādhigataṁ cānnaṁ prajārthaṁ me pitāmaha ,oṣadhyaḥ parivarteranyathaiva satataṁ prajāḥ.
english: "And O Grandfather, the food (nourishment) obtained by me through spiritual austerity (tapas) is for the sake of creatures. Just as plants continually regenerate, so too do creatures (need sustenance)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. "And O Grandfather, the food (nourishment) obtained by me through spiritual austerity (tapas) is for the sake of creatures. Just as plants continually regenerate, so too do creatures (need sustenance)."

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु संक्रुद्धो जगाम विमना भवः ।गिरेर्मुञ्जवतः पादं तपस्तप्तुं महातपाः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu saṁkruddho jagāma vimanā bhavaḥ ,girermuñjavataḥ pādaṁ tapastaptuṁ mahātapāḥ.
english: Having thus spoken, Bhava, a great ascetic (mahātapaḥ), greatly enraged and with a disturbed mind, went to the foot of Mount Muñjavat to perform asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. Having thus spoken, Bhava, a great ascetic (mahātapaḥ), greatly enraged and with a disturbed mind, went to the foot of Mount Muñjavat to perform asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।ततो देवयुगेऽतीते देवा वै समकल्पयन् ।यज्ञं वेदप्रमाणेन विधिवद्यष्टुमीप्सवः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tato devayuge'tīte devā vai samakalpayan ,yajñaṁ vedapramāṇena vidhivadyaṣṭumīpsavaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: "Then, after a divine age (yuga) had passed, the gods resolved to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) according to Vedic injunctions, desiring to worship properly."
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: "Then, after a divine age (yuga) had passed, the gods resolved to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) according to Vedic injunctions, desiring to worship properly."

sanskrit: कल्पयामासुरव्यग्रा देशान्यज्ञोचितांस्ततः ।भागार्हा देवताश्चैव यज्ञियं द्रव्यमेव च ॥२॥
transliteration: kalpayāmāsuravyagrā deśānyajñocitāṁstataḥ ,bhāgārhā devatāścaiva yajñiyaṁ dravyameva ca.
english: Then, being attentive, they arranged the places suitable for the performance of the Vedic ritual (yajña), as well as the deities deserving of a share, and the sacrificial materials.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. Then, being attentive, they arranged the places suitable for the performance of the Vedic ritual (yajña), as well as the deities deserving of a share, and the sacrificial materials.

sanskrit: ता वै रुद्रमजानन्त्यो याथातथ्येन देवताः ।नाकल्पयन्त देवस्य स्थाणोर्भागं नराधिप ॥३॥
transliteration: tā vai rudramajānantyo yāthātathyena devatāḥ ,nākalpayanta devasya sthāṇorbhāgaṁ narādhipa.
english: O king (narādhipa), those deities, not truly understanding Rudra (yāthātathya), did not allot a share to the god Sthāṇu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. O king (narādhipa), those deities, not truly understanding Rudra (yāthātathya), did not allot a share to the god Sthāṇu.

sanskrit: सोऽकल्प्यमाने भागे तु कृत्तिवासा मखेऽमरैः ।तरसा भागमन्विच्छन्धनुरादौ ससर्ज ह ॥४॥
transliteration: so'kalpyamāne bhāge tu kṛttivāsā makhe'maraiḥ ,tarasā bhāgamanvicchandhanurādau sasarja ha.
english: When the gods did not allot him a share in the Vedic ritual (makha), Shiva (Kṛttivāsā), seeking his portion by force, indeed first fashioned a bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. When the gods did not allot him a share in the Vedic ritual (makha), Shiva (Kṛttivāsā), seeking his portion by force, indeed first fashioned a bow.

sanskrit: लोकयज्ञः क्रियायज्ञो गृहयज्ञः सनातनः ।पञ्चभूतमयो यज्ञो नृयज्ञश्चैव पञ्चमः ॥५॥
transliteration: lokayajñaḥ kriyāyajño gṛhayajñaḥ sanātanaḥ ,pañcabhūtamayo yajño nṛyajñaścaiva pañcamaḥ.
english: There is the world-sacrifice (lokayajña), the action-sacrifice (kriyāyajña), the domestic sacrifice (gṛhayajña), which is eternal (sanātana), and the Vedic ritual (yajña) made of the five elements (pañcabhūtamaya); the human-sacrifice (nṛyajña) is indeed the fifth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. There is the world-sacrifice (lokayajña), the action-sacrifice (kriyāyajña), the domestic sacrifice (gṛhayajña), which is eternal (sanātana), and the Vedic ritual (yajña) made of the five elements (pañcabhūtamaya); the human-sacrifice (nṛyajña) is indeed the fifth.

sanskrit: लोकयज्ञेन यज्ञैषी कपर्दी विदधे धनुः ।धनुः सृष्टमभूत्तस्य पञ्चकिष्कुप्रमाणतः ॥६॥
transliteration: lokayajñena yajñaiṣī kapardī vidadhe dhanuḥ ,dhanuḥ sṛṣṭamabhūttasya pañcakiṣkupramāṇataḥ.
english: The one with matted hair (kapardī), desirous of Vedic ritual (yajña) through the world-sacrifice (lokayajña), created a bow. That bow, once created, became five kiṣkus in length.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. The one with matted hair (kapardī), desirous of Vedic ritual (yajña) through the world-sacrifice (lokayajña), created a bow. That bow, once created, became five kiṣkus in length.

sanskrit: वषट्कारोऽभवज्ज्या तु धनुषस्तस्य भारत ।यज्ञाङ्गानि च चत्वारि तस्य संहननेऽभवन् ॥७॥
transliteration: vaṣaṭkāro'bhavajjyā tu dhanuṣastasya bhārata ,yajñāṅgāni ca catvāri tasya saṁhanane'bhavan.
english: O bhārata, the vaṣaṭkāra (the exclamation of 'vaṣaṭ') became the bowstring of that bow. And four components of the Vedic ritual (yajña) were used in its construction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. O bhārata, the vaṣaṭkāra (the exclamation of 'vaṣaṭ') became the bowstring of that bow. And four components of the Vedic ritual (yajña) were used in its construction.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महादेवस्तदुपादाय कार्मुकम् ।आजगामाथ तत्रैव यत्र देवाः समीजिरे ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahādevastadupādāya kārmukam ,ājagāmātha tatraiva yatra devāḥ samījire.
english: Then, the enraged Mahadeva, taking up that bow, came right to the place where the gods were performing the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. Then, the enraged Mahadeva, taking up that bow, came right to the place where the gods were performing the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तमात्तकार्मुकं दृष्ट्वा ब्रह्मचारिणमव्ययम् ।विव्यथे पृथिवी देवी पर्वताश्च चकम्पिरे ॥९॥
transliteration: tamāttakārmukaṁ dṛṣṭvā brahmacāriṇamavyayam ,vivyathe pṛthivī devī parvatāśca cakampire.
english: Having seen him - the immutable one (avyayam) holding a bow in the form of a brahmacārin - the Earth goddess became distressed, and the mountains trembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. Having seen him - the immutable one (avyayam) holding a bow in the form of a brahmacārin - the Earth goddess became distressed, and the mountains trembled.

sanskrit: न ववौ पवनश्चैव नाग्निर्जज्वाल चैधितः ।व्यभ्रमच्चापि संविग्नं दिवि नक्षत्रमण्डलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: na vavau pavanaścaiva nāgnirjajvāla caidhitaḥ ,vyabhramaccāpi saṁvignaṁ divi nakṣatramaṇḍalam.
english: The wind did not blow, and the kindled fire did not blaze. Also, the agitated host of stars in the sky became disordered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. The wind did not blow, and the kindled fire did not blaze. Also, the agitated host of stars in the sky became disordered.

sanskrit: न बभौ भास्करश्चापि सोमः श्रीमुक्तमण्डलः ।तिमिरेणाकुलं सर्वमाकाशं चाभवद्वृतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: na babhau bhāskaraścāpi somaḥ śrīmuktamaṇḍalaḥ ,timireṇākulaṁ sarvamākāśaṁ cābhavadvṛtam.
english: The sun did not shine, and the moon's orb, deprived of splendor, also did not shine. Moreover, the entire sky became enveloped and filled with darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. The sun did not shine, and the moon's orb, deprived of splendor, also did not shine. Moreover, the entire sky became enveloped and filled with darkness.

sanskrit: अभिभूतास्ततो देवा विषयान्न प्रजज्ञिरे ।न प्रत्यभाच्च यज्ञस्तान्वेदा बभ्रंशिरे तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhibhūtāstato devā viṣayānna prajajñire ,na pratyabhācca yajñastānvedā babhraṁśire tadā.
english: Overwhelmed, the gods then could not perceive the sensory objects. The Vedic ritual (yajña) also ceased to be clear to them, and at that time, the Vedas slipped away.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. Overwhelmed, the gods then could not perceive the sensory objects. The Vedic ritual (yajña) also ceased to be clear to them, and at that time, the Vedas slipped away.

sanskrit: ततः स यज्ञं रौद्रेण विव्याध हृदि पत्रिणा ।अपक्रान्तस्ततो यज्ञो मृगो भूत्वा सपावकः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa yajñaṁ raudreṇa vivyādha hṛdi patriṇā ,apakrāntastato yajño mṛgo bhūtvā sapāvakaḥ.
english: Then, with a formidable arrow, he pierced the Vedic ritual (yajña) in the heart. Subsequently, the Vedic ritual (yajña) transformed into a deer and fled, accompanied by fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. Then, with a formidable arrow, he pierced the Vedic ritual (yajña) in the heart. Subsequently, the Vedic ritual (yajña) transformed into a deer and fled, accompanied by fire.

sanskrit: स तु तेनैव रूपेण दिवं प्राप्य व्यरोचत ।अन्वीयमानो रुद्रेण युधिष्ठिर नभस्तले ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa tu tenaiva rūpeṇa divaṁ prāpya vyarocata ,anvīyamāno rudreṇa yudhiṣṭhira nabhastale.
english: But that very Vedic ritual (yajña), O Yudhiṣṭhira, having reached heaven in that same form, shone brightly, while being pursued by Rudra in the celestial realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. But that very Vedic ritual (yajña), O Yudhiṣṭhira, having reached heaven in that same form, shone brightly, while being pursued by Rudra in the celestial realm.

sanskrit: अपक्रान्ते ततो यज्ञे संज्ञा न प्रत्यभात्सुरान् ।नष्टसंज्ञेषु देवेषु न प्रज्ञायत किंचन ॥१५॥
transliteration: apakrānte tato yajñe saṁjñā na pratyabhātsurān ,naṣṭasaṁjñeṣu deveṣu na prajñāyata kiṁcana.
english: After the Vedic ritual (yajña) had fled, then awareness did not return to the gods. With the gods having lost their senses, nothing at all could be comprehended.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. After the Vedic ritual (yajña) had fled, then awareness did not return to the gods. With the gods having lost their senses, nothing at all could be comprehended.

sanskrit: त्र्यम्बकः सवितुर्बाहू भगस्य नयने तथा ।पूष्णश्च दशनान्क्रुद्धो धनुष्कोट्या व्यशातयत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tryambakaḥ saviturbāhū bhagasya nayane tathā ,pūṣṇaśca daśanānkruddho dhanuṣkoṭyā vyaśātayat.
english: Enraged, Tryambaka (Śiva) cut off the two arms of Savitṛ, and similarly the two eyes of Bhaga, and the teeth of Pūṣan, with the tip of his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. Enraged, Tryambaka (Śiva) cut off the two arms of Savitṛ, and similarly the two eyes of Bhaga, and the teeth of Pūṣan, with the tip of his bow.

sanskrit: प्राद्रवन्त ततो देवा यज्ञाङ्गानि च सर्वशः ।केचित्तत्रैव घूर्णन्तो गतासव इवाभवन् ॥१७॥
transliteration: prādravanta tato devā yajñāṅgāni ca sarvaśaḥ ,kecittatraiva ghūrṇanto gatāsava ivābhavan.
english: Then, the gods and all the components of the Vedic ritual (yajña) fled in every direction. Some, right there, reeled and became as if lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. Then, the gods and all the components of the Vedic ritual (yajña) fled in every direction. Some, right there, reeled and became as if lifeless.

sanskrit: स तु विद्राव्य तत्सर्वं शितिकण्ठोऽवहस्य च ।अवष्टभ्य धनुष्कोटिं रुरोध विबुधांस्ततः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa tu vidrāvya tatsarvaṁ śitikaṇṭho'vahasya ca ,avaṣṭabhya dhanuṣkoṭiṁ rurodha vibudhāṁstataḥ.
english: But he, Śitikaṇṭha (Śiva), having driven all of them away and having laughed, then obstructed the gods by holding the tip of his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. But he, Śitikaṇṭha (Śiva), having driven all of them away and having laughed, then obstructed the gods by holding the tip of his bow.

sanskrit: ततो वागमरैरुक्ता ज्यां तस्य धनुषोऽच्छिनत् ।अथ तत्सहसा राजंश्छिन्नज्यं विस्फुरद्धनुः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tato vāgamarairuktā jyāṁ tasya dhanuṣo'cchinat ,atha tatsahasā rājaṁśchinnajyaṁ visphuraddhanuḥ.
english: Then a voice, uttered by the immortals, cut the string of his bow. Thereupon, O king, that bow, its string severed, suddenly quivered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. Then a voice, uttered by the immortals, cut the string of his bow. Thereupon, O king, that bow, its string severed, suddenly quivered.

sanskrit: ततो विधनुषं देवा देवश्रेष्ठमुपागमन् ।शरणं सह यज्ञेन प्रसादं चाकरोत्प्रभुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato vidhanuṣaṁ devā devaśreṣṭhamupāgaman ,śaraṇaṁ saha yajñena prasādaṁ cākarotprabhuḥ.
english: Then the gods approached the best of gods, who was without a bow, seeking refuge. And the Lord, along with the Vedic ritual (yajña), bestowed his grace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. Then the gods approached the best of gods, who was without a bow, seeking refuge. And the Lord, along with the Vedic ritual (yajña), bestowed his grace.

sanskrit: ततः प्रसन्नो भगवान्प्रास्यत्कोपं जलाशये ।स जलं पावको भूत्वा शोषयत्यनिशं प्रभो ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ prasanno bhagavānprāsyatkopaṁ jalāśaye ,sa jalaṁ pāvako bhūtvā śoṣayatyaniśaṁ prabho.
english: Then the pleased Lord (Bhagavān) cast his anger into a body of water. That (anger), having become fire, constantly dries up the water, O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. Then the pleased Lord (Bhagavān) cast his anger into a body of water. That (anger), having become fire, constantly dries up the water, O lord.

sanskrit: भगस्य नयने चैव बाहू च सवितुस्तथा ।प्रादात्पूष्णश्च दशनान्पुनर्यज्ञं च पाण्डव ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhagasya nayane caiva bāhū ca savitustathā ,prādātpūṣṇaśca daśanānpunaryajñaṁ ca pāṇḍava.
english: O Pāṇḍava, he restored the two eyes of Bhaga, and also the two arms of Savitṛ, and the teeth of Pūṣan. And he reinstated the Vedic ritual (yajña) again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. O Pāṇḍava, he restored the two eyes of Bhaga, and also the two arms of Savitṛ, and the teeth of Pūṣan. And he reinstated the Vedic ritual (yajña) again.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वमिदं स्वस्थं बभूव पुनरेव ह ।सर्वाणि च हवींष्यस्य देवा भागमकल्पयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarvamidaṁ svasthaṁ babhūva punareva ha ,sarvāṇi ca havīṁṣyasya devā bhāgamakalpayan.
english: Then, all this indeed became well again. And the gods allotted his (Shiva's) share from all the offerings (havis).
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. Then, all this indeed became well again. And the gods allotted his (Shiva's) share from all the offerings (havis).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्क्रुद्धेऽभवत्सर्वमस्वस्थं भुवनं विभो ।प्रसन्ने च पुनः स्वस्थं स प्रसन्नोऽस्य वीर्यवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasminkruddhe'bhavatsarvamasvasthaṁ bhuvanaṁ vibho ,prasanne ca punaḥ svasthaṁ sa prasanno'sya vīryavān.
english: O Lord (vibho), when He became enraged, the entire world (bhuvana) became disturbed. And when He was pleased, it became stable again. That pleased one is truly mighty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. O Lord (vibho), when He became enraged, the entire world (bhuvana) became disturbed. And when He was pleased, it became stable again. That pleased one is truly mighty.

sanskrit: ततस्ते निहताः सर्वे तव पुत्रा महारथाः ।अन्ये च बहवः शूराः पाञ्चालाश्च सहानुगाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataste nihatāḥ sarve tava putrā mahārathāḥ ,anye ca bahavaḥ śūrāḥ pāñcālāśca sahānugāḥ.
english: Then all your sons, the great charioteers (mahārathas), were killed, as well as many other heroes and the Pañcālas along with their followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. Then all your sons, the great charioteers (mahārathas), were killed, as well as many other heroes and the Pañcālas along with their followers.

sanskrit: न तन्मनसि कर्तव्यं न हि तद्द्रौणिना कृतम् ।महादेवप्रसादः स कुरु कार्यमनन्तरम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: na tanmanasi kartavyaṁ na hi taddrauṇinā kṛtam ,mahādevaprasādaḥ sa kuru kāryamanantaram.
english: Do not dwell on that (event) in your mind (manasi), for truly, that was not done by Droṇi (Aśvatthāman). It was the grace of Mahādeva (Śiva). Now, attend to the next task.
search_text: Mahabharata Sauptika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. Do not dwell on that (event) in your mind (manasi), for truly, that was not done by Droṇi (Aśvatthāman). It was the grace of Mahādeva (Śiva). Now, attend to the next task.
